english_verse: You surely wouldn't let them be killed when you destroy the evil ones. You are the judge of all the earth, and you do what is right.”
translation_verse: Iyi mpar wo dɛ ebɛyɛ no dɛm, dɛ edze atseneneefo bɛka ndzebɔnyɛfo ho eku hɔn, ma atseneneefo ayɛ dɛ ndzebɔnyɛfo; iyi mpar wo: ana asaase nyinara ɔtsɛmbuafo nnkɛyɛ dza ɔtsen bi a?

english_verse: Abraham answered, “I am nothing more than the dust of the earth. Please forgive me, Lord, for daring to speak to you like this.
translation_verse: Na Abraham buae na ɔsee dɛ, Afei hwɛ, emi a meyɛ dɛtse nye nso, na mesi mo bo dɛ mebɛkasa akyerɛ EWURADZE yi:

english_verse: Then Abraham said, “I don't have any right to ask you, Lord, but what would you do if you find only
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Afei hwɛ, mesi mo bo dɛ mebɛkasa akyerɛ EWURADZE: egyangyanbiara eduonu na wobohu hɔn wɔ hɔ ɛ: Na ɔsee dɛ, Osian eduonu no ntsi mennkɛsɛɛ no.

english_verse: Finally, Abraham said, “Please don't get angry, Lord, if I speak just once more. Suppose you find only
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Ao mma mma EWURADZE no bo mmfuw, na menkasa prɛko pɛ yi ara: egyangyanbiara du na wobohu hɔn wɔ hɔ ɛ; Na ɔsee dɛ, Osian du no ntsi mennkɛsɛɛ no.

english_verse: After speaking with Abraham, the Lord left, and Abraham went back home.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE wiee dɛ ɔnye Abraham kasa ara na ɔkɔr no kwan: na Abraham sanee baa ne tsenabea.

english_verse: That evening, while Lot was sitting near the city gate, the two angels arrived in Sodom. When Lot saw them, he got up, bowed down low,
translation_verse: Na sor abɔfo beenu no baa Sodom ewimbir; nna Lot tse Sodom abotsen ano: na Lot hun hɔn, na ɔsoɛree kehyiaa hɔn kwan; na ɔkotowee dze n’enyim boom famu;

english_verse: and said, “Gentlemen, I am your servant. Please come to my home. You can wash your feet, spend the night, and be on your way in the morning.” They told him, “No, we'll spend the night in the city square.”
translation_verse: na ɔsee dɛ, Afei hwɛ, mo wuranom, meserɛ hom, wɔmman hom akowaa fie, na wɔnkɔsoɛ hɔ anafua yi nyinara, na hom nhohor hom anan ho, na hom asoɛr anapaatu akɔ hom kwan. Na wɔsee dɛ, Dabida; na yɛbɛtsena gua do anafua yi nyinara.

english_verse: But Lot kept insisting, until they finally agreed and went home with him. He quickly baked some bread, cooked a meal, and they ate.
translation_verse: Na ɔserɛɛ hɔn ɔdzendo ara, na wɔmaan ne nkyɛn, na wɔkɔr ne fie; na ɔtoo hɔn pon, na ɔtoo epitsi ma wodzii.

english_verse: Before Lot and his guests could go to bed, every man in Sodom, young and old, came and stood outside his house
translation_verse: Na wonnyae nnkɛdae no, nna kurow no mu mba, mekyerɛ Sodomfo no, botwaa dan no ho hyiae, mbofra nye mpanyin, apaa nyinara mu nkorɔfo nyinara;

english_verse: and started shouting, “Where are your visitors? Send them out, so we can have sex with them!”
translation_verse: na wɔfrɛɛ Lot na wɔsee no dɛ, Mbarimba a wɔbaa wo nkyɛn anafua yi no wɔ hen? Yi hɔn brɛ hɛn, ma yɛnye hɔn nda.

english_verse: Lot went outside and shut the door behind him.
translation_verse: Na Lot puei kɔr hɔn nkyɛn wɔ abow no ano, na ɔtoow abow no mu wɔ n’ekyir.

english_verse: Then he said, “Friends, please don't do such a terrible thing!
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Mo nuanom, meserɛ hom, mma wɔnnyɛ emumuyɛ a ɔtse dɛm.

english_verse: I have two daughters who have never had sex. I'll bring them out, and you can do what you want with them. But don't harm these men. They are guests in my home.”
translation_verse: Afei hwɛ, mowɔ mbabaa beenu a wonnhun banyin: meserɛ hom, wɔmma minyi hɔn mbrɛ hom, na dza homara hwɛ a oye no ara hom nyɛ hɔn: mbarimba yi dze mma wɔnnyɛ hɔn biribiara; susuampa dɛ wɔaba me hatsɛw no nwin ase yi.

english_verse: “Don't get in our way,” the crowd answered. “You're a foreigner. What right do you have to order us around? We'll do worse things to you than we're going to do to them.” The crowd kept arguing with Lot. Finally, they rushed toward the door to break it down.
translation_verse: Na wɔsee dɛ, Gyina nkyɛn. Na wɔsee dɛ, Akowaa kor yi ɔbaa hɛn amanyɛ, na ɔrohwehwɛ ɔayɛ ɔsɛndzifo: afei yɛnye wo bedzi no bɔn akyɛn hɔn. Na wɔhyɛɛ Lot no do dzendzenndzen, na wɔtwee pinii dɛ worubobubu abow no.

english_verse: But the two angels in the house reached out and pulled Lot safely inside.
translation_verse: Na mbarimba no tseen hɔn nsa, na wɔtwee Lot baa hɔn nkyɛn wɔ dan mu na wɔtoow abow no mu.

english_verse: Then they struck blind everyone in the crowd, and none of them could even find the door.
translation_verse: Na wɔdze enyifura baa nkorɔfo a wogyinagyina ofi no n’abow n’ano no hɔn do, nkakraba nye akɛse nyina: ma wɔfonaa dɛ wobohu abow no n’ano kwan. Lot Tu Fi Sodom

english_verse: Lot went to the men who were engaged to his daughters and said, “Hurry up and get out of here! The Lord is going to destroy this city.” But they thought he was joking, and they laughed at him.
translation_verse: Na Lot puei, na ɔkɛkasaa kyerɛɛ ne nsewnom a wɔawarwar ne mbabaa no, na ɔsee dɛ, Hom nsoɛr mfi ha; na EWURADZE rebɛsɛɛ kurow yi. Na ɔyɛɛ ne nsewnom no enyiwa do dɛ obi a oridzi fɛw.

english_verse: Early the next morning the two angels tried to make Lot hurry and leave. They said, “Take your wife and your two daughters and get away from here as fast as you can! If you don't, every one of you will be killed when the Lord destroys the city.”
translation_verse: Na adze kyee no, sor abɔfo no guu Lot do, dɛ, Soɛr, fa wo yer nye wo mbabaa beenu a wɔwɔ ha yi kɔ; amma aannhyew wɔ kurow yi n’emumuyɛ mu.

english_verse: At first, Lot just stood there. But the Lord wanted to save him. So the angels took Lot, his wife, and his two daughters by the hand and led them out of the city.
translation_verse: Na okyinkyimii; na EWURADZE hun no mbɔbɔr ntsi, nkorɔfo no suoo ɔno nye ne yer nye ne mbabaa beenu no hɔn nsa mu na wɔdze hɔn puei, na wɔdze hɔn kesii kurow no n’ekyir.

english_verse: When they were outside, one of the angels said, “Run for your lives! Don't even look back. And don't stop in the valley. Run to the hills, where you'll be safe.”
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ, wɔdze hɔn puei no, wɔsee dɛ, Hom nguan wɔ hom nkwa ntsi; mma hom mmbu hom kɔn ekyir, mma hom nngyina Tɛtɛr yi mu beebiara so; hom guan nkɔ bepɔw no do, ɔno bɛyɛ na hom ahyew.

english_verse: Lot answered, “You have done us a great favor, sir. You have saved our lives, but please don't make us go to the hills. That's too far away. The city will be destroyed before we can get there, and we will be killed when it happens.
translation_verse: Afei hwɛ, w’akowaa enya w’enyim enyimpa, na ama w’ehumbɔbɔr a edze ehu me adɔɔ so, wɔ dɛ agye me nkwa; mbom munnkotum meguan akɔ mbepɔw no do, ɔno bɛyɛ na mbusu no ato me ma meewu.

english_verse: There's a town near here. It's only a small place, but my family and I will be safe, if you let us go there.”
translation_verse: Hwɛ, kurow yi bɛn dɛ mubotum eguan akɔ mu, na osuar. Innhu dɛ osuar? Ao, ma munguan nkɔ hɔ, na me kra benya nkwa.

english_verse: “All right, go there,” he answered. “I won't destroy that town.
translation_verse: Na ɔsee no dɛ, Hwɛ, metsie wo wɔ adze kor yi so mu, dɛ monnkɔbɔ kurow a aka ho asɛm no.

english_verse: Hurry! Run! I can't do anything until you are safely there.” The town was later called Zoar because Lot had said it was small. Sodom and Gomorrah Are Destroyed
translation_verse: Ka woho ɛ, guan kɔ hɔ; na murunntum biribiara yɛ, gyedɛ edu hɔ ana. Dɛm ntsi wɔfrɛɛ kurow no ne dzin Zoar ase nye dɛ kakraba.

english_verse: The sun was coming up as Lot reached the town of Zoar,
translation_verse: Na aber a Lot dur Zoar no, nna ewia epue asaase do.

english_verse: and the Lord sent burning sulfur down like rain on Sodom and Gomorrah.
translation_verse: Nna EWURADZE tɔɔ brestɔn nye ogya fi EWURADZE hɔ wɔ sor guu Sodom nye Gomora do;

english_verse: He destroyed those cities and everyone who lived in them, as well as their land and the trees and grass that grew there.
translation_verse: na ɔbɔɔ nkurow no, nye Tɛtɛr no nyinara, nye hɔn a wɔtse nkurow no mu nyinara, nye dza ofifir asaase no do no.

english_verse: On the way, Lot's wife looked back and was turned into a block of salt.
translation_verse: Na Lot ne yer buu no kɔn ekyir, na ɔdaan nkyensiw.

english_verse: That same morning Abraham got up and went to the place where he had stood and spoken with the Lord.
translation_verse: Na Abraham soɛɛr anapaatu kɔr mbrɛ ogyinae wɔ EWURADZE n’enyim no:

english_verse: He looked down toward Sodom and Gomorrah and saw smoke rising from all over the land—it was like a flaming furnace.
translation_verse: na ɔdaan n’enyi kɔr Sodom nye Gomora, nye Tɛtɛr no mu asaase no nyinara do, na, hwɛ, asaase no do wusiw foow sor dɛ fononoo wusiw.

english_verse: When God destroyed the cities of the valley where Lot lived, he remembered his promise to Abraham and saved Lot from the terrible destruction. Moab and Ammon
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ, aber a Nyankopɔn sɛɛ Tɛtɛr no mu nkurow no, Nyankopɔn kaa Abraham, na aber a ɔbɔɔ nkurow a Lot tse mu no, oyii Lot wɔ ɔsɛɛ no mu. Lot Ne Mbabaa Beenu No

english_verse: Lot was afraid to stay on in Zoar. So he took his two daughters and moved to a cave in the hill country.
translation_verse: Na Lot fii Zoar fowee, na ɔkɛtsenaa bepɔw no do, na ne mbabaa beenu no ka no ho; osiandɛ osuroo dɛ ɔbɛtsena Zoar: na wɔtsenaa ɔbo tu mu.

english_verse: One day his older daughter said to her sister, “Our father is old, and there are no men anywhere for us to marry.
translation_verse: Na abakan no see ekumaba no dɛ, Hɛn egya ayɛ akɔdaa, na ɔbanyin biara nnyi asaase do a ɔbɛba hɛn nkyɛn dɛ mbrɛ asaase nyinara yɛ no:

english_verse: Let's get our father drunk! Then we can sleep with him and have children.”
translation_verse: Bra, ma yɛmma hɛn egya nnom nsa, na yɛnye no nkɛda, ama yeenya hɛn egya no ho mba.

english_verse: That night they got their father drunk, and the older daughter got in bed with him, but he was too drunk even to know she was there.
translation_verse: Na wɔmaa hɔn egya noom nsa anafua no: na abakan no kɔr mu, na ɔnye n’egya kɛdae; na oennhu ne ndaa mu, eso no nsoɛree mu.

english_verse: The next day the older daughter said to her sister, “I slept with my father last night. We'll get him drunk again tonight, so you can sleep with him, and we can each have a child.”
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ n’adzekyeenyim no, abakan no see ekumaba no dɛ, Hwɛ, menye m’egya daa anafua yi: ma yɛmma ɔnnom nsa anafua yi so; na kɔ mu, na enye no nkɛda, ama yeenya hɛn egya no ho mba.

english_verse: That night they got their father drunk, and this time the younger sister slept with him. But once again he was too drunk even to know she was there.
translation_verse: Na wɔmaa hɔn egya noom nsa anafua no so; na ekumaba no soɛree, na ɔnye no kɛdae; na oennhu ne ndaa mu, eso no nsoɛree mu.

english_verse: That's how Lot's two daughters had children.
translation_verse: Dɛm na Lot ne mbabaa beenu no nyina wɔnye hɔn egya nyinsenii.

english_verse: The older daughter named her son Moab, and he is the ancestor of the Moabites.
translation_verse: Na abakan no woo babanyin, na ɔtoo no dzin Moab: ɔnoara nye Moabfo hɔn egya ɔdze bodu ndɛ da yi.

english_verse: The younger daughter named her son Benammi, and he is the ancestor of the Ammonites.
translation_verse: Na ekumaba no so woo babanyin, na ɔtoo no dzin Benammi: ɔnoara nye Ammonfo hɔn egya ɔdze bodu ndɛ da yi.

english_verse: he told everyone that his wife Sarah was his sister. So King Abimelech of Gerar had Sarah brought to him.
translation_verse: Na hɔ Abraham kaa no wɔ ne yer Sara ho dɛ, M’akyerɛbaa a: na Gerarhen Abimelek somae ma wɔkɛfaa Sara.

english_verse: But God came to Abimelech in a dream and said, “You have taken a married woman into your home, and for this you will die!”
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn baa Abimelek hɔ wɔ anafua adaaso mu, na ɔbɛsee no dɛ, Hwɛ, ɔbaa a akɛfa no ntsi ayɛ owufo ara nye no; na banyin no ne yer a.

english_verse: Abimelech said to the Lord, “Don't kill me! I haven't slept with Sarah. Didn't they say they were brother and sister? I am completely innocent.”
translation_verse: Ana ɔno noara annse me dɛ, M’akyerɛbaa a? Na noara, mekyerɛ ɔnoara ɔbaa no so see dɛ, Mo nua a: m’akoma mu tsewee, nye me nsa ho no krɔnkrɔnyɛ mu, na mayɛ yi.

english_verse: Then God continued: I know you are innocent. That's why I kept you from sleeping with Sarah and doing anything wrong.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn see no adaaso mu, dɛ, ɛhɛɛ, minyim dɛ w’akoma mu tsewee mu na ayɛ yi, na mo so musuoo wo mu amma aannyɛ bɔn enntsia me: dɛm ntsi na mamma annka no.

english_verse: Her husband is a prophet. Let her go back to him, and his prayers will save you from death. But if you don't return her, you and all your people will die.
translation_verse: Dɛm na yɛ afei, fa banyin no ne yer san kɛma no; na ɔyɛ nkɔnhyɛnyi, na ɔbɔsɔr ama wo, ma eenya nkwa: na sɛ ammfa no annsan amma no a, hu no ampaara dɛ ibowu, ɔwo, nye dza ɔyɛ wodze nyinara.

english_verse: Early the next morning Abimelech sent for his officials, and when he told them what had happened, they were frightened.
translation_verse: Na Abimelek soɛɛr anapaatu, na ɔfrɛɛ no nkowaa nyinara, na ɔkaa nsɛm yi nyinara wɔ hɔn aso mu: na nkorɔfo no hɔn akoma tuu kɛsenara.

english_verse: Abimelech then called in Abraham and said: Look what you've done to us! What have I ever done to you? Why did you make me and my nation guilty of such a terrible sin?
translation_verse: Nna Abimelek frɛɛ Abraham, na ɔsee no dɛ, Ebɛnadze na ayɛ hɛn yi? Na ebɛnadze mu na mayɛ bɔn etsia wo ma edze bɔn kokroko aba emi nye m’ahenman do yi? Adze a ɔnnsɛ dɛ wɔyɛ na edze ayɛ me yi.

english_verse: What were you thinking when you did this?
translation_verse: Na Abimelek bisaa Abraham dɛ, Ebɛnadze na ihunii, ma ayɛ dɛm adze yi?

english_verse: Abraham answered: I did it because I didn't think any of you respected God, and I was sure that someone would kill me to get my wife.
translation_verse: Na Abraham see dɛ, Osiandɛ modween dɛ, Ampaara Nyankopɔn no ho suro nnyi ha; na me yer ntsi woboku me.

english_verse: Besides, she is my half sister. We have the same father, but different mothers.
translation_verse: Na ɔyɛ m’akyerɛbaa so ampa, m’egya ba a, na nnyɛ me na ba bi a; na ɔbɛyɛɛ me yer.

english_verse: When God made us leave my father's home and start wandering, I told her, “If you really love me, then tell everyone that I am your brother.”
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ aber a Nyankopɔn maa mifii m’egya fie mikekyinii no, nna mesee ɔbaa no dɛ, Ayamuyie a fa yɛ me nye yi; beebiara mbrɛ yebodu no, ka no wɔ mo ho dɛ, Mo nua a.

english_verse: After Abimelech had given Abraham some sheep, cattle, and slaves, he sent Sarah back
translation_verse: Na Abimelek faa nguan, nye anantwi, nye nkowaa, nye mfonabaa, na ɔdze maa Abraham, na ɔdze ne yer Sara sanee maa no.

english_verse: and told Abraham he could settle anywhere in his country.
translation_verse: Na Abimelek see dɛ, Hwɛ, m’asaase na ɔwɔ w’enyim yi: tsena beebiara mbrɛ woara epɛ.

english_verse: Meanwhile, God had kept Abimelech's wife and slaves from having children. But Abraham prayed, and God let them start having children again.
translation_verse: Osiandɛ nna EWURADZE esiw Abimelek ne fie yafun nyinara mu kwan petsee wɔ Abraham ne yer Sara ntsi.

english_verse: The Lord was good to Sarah and kept his promise.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE bɛseraa Sara dɛ mbrɛ ɔsee no, na Ɔyɛɛ Sara dɛ mbrɛ ɔbɔɔ no anohoba no.

english_verse: Although Abraham was very old, Sarah had a son exactly at the time God had said.
translation_verse: Na Sara nyinsenii, na ɔwoo babanyin maa Abraham wɔ no nkɔdaaber mu, wɔ aber a EWURADZE kaa ho asɛm kyerɛɛ no no mu.

english_verse: and when the boy was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him, just as God had commanded.
translation_verse: Na Abraham ne ba Isaak dzii nda awɔtwe no, otwaa no twatsia, dɛ mbrɛ Nyankopɔn hyɛɛ no no.

english_verse: and Sarah said, “God has made me laugh. And now everyone will laugh with me.
translation_verse: Na Sara see dɛ, Nyankopɔn ama maserew; hɔn a wɔbɛtse nyinara nye me bɛserew.

english_verse: Who would have dared to tell Abraham that someday I would have a child? But in his old age, I have given him a son.”
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Woana na nkyɛ akyerɛ Abraham dɛ, Sara bɛma mba enumfo? Naaso mawo babanyin ama no wɔ no nkɔdaaber mu. Woyi Hagar Nye Ismael Edzi

english_verse: The time came when Sarah no longer had to nurse Isaac, and on that day Abraham gave a big feast. Hagar and Ishmael Are Sent Away
translation_verse: Na abofra no nyinii, na wotwaa no enumfo: na da a wotwaa Isaak enumfo no, Abraham too pon kɛsenara.

english_verse: One day, Sarah noticed Hagar's son Ishmael playing, and she said to Abraham, “Get rid of that Egyptian slave woman and her son! I don't want him to inherit anything. It should all go to my son.”
translation_verse: Dɛm ntsi ɔsee Abraham dɛ, Yi afonabaa yi nye ne ba no edzi: na afonabaa yi ne ba nnkɛnye me ba Isaak edzi adze.

english_verse: But God said, “Abraham, don't worry about your slave woman and the boy. Just do what Sarah tells you. Isaac will inherit your family name,
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn see Abraham dɛ, Mma mma ɔnnyɛ wo yaw, osian abofra no ntsi, na osian w’afonabaa no ntsi; dza Sara ka kyerɛ wo no nyinara mu, tsie ne ndze; na Isaak mu na wɔbɛfrɛ w’asefo.

english_verse: but the son of the slave woman is also your son, and I will make his descendants into a great nation.”
translation_verse: Na afonabaa no ne ba no so mebɛyɛ no ɔman, osiandɛ ɔyɛ w’asenyi.

english_verse: Early the next morning Abraham gave Hagar an animal skin full of water and some bread. Then he put the boy on her shoulder and sent them away. They wandered around in the desert near Beersheba,
translation_verse: Na Abraham soɛɛr anapaatu, na ɔfaa edziban nye nsu bɔdambɔ, na ɔdze maa Hagar, ɔdze sii no kɔn ho, na ogyaa ɔno nye abofra no kwan. Na Hagar sii mu, na okekyiin Beer-seba sar do.

english_verse: and after they had run out of water, Hagar put her son under a bush.
translation_verse: Na nsu a ɔwɔ bɔdambɔ no mu no no sae, na ɔdze abofra no too ndua no bi ase.

english_verse: Then she sat down a long way off, because she could not bear to watch him die. And she cried bitterly.
translation_verse: Na ɔkɔree, na ɔkɛtsenaa ne tsentsendo wɔ ekyir kakra, bɛyɛ dɛ mbrɛ wɔtow egyen a obodu: na ɔsee dɛ, Mma mma monnhwɛ abofra yi no wu. Na ɔtsenaa ne tsentsendo, na ɔmaa ne ndze do, na osui.

english_verse: When God heard the boy crying, the angel of God called out to Hagar from heaven and said, “Hagar, why are you worried? Don't be afraid. I have heard your son crying.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn tsee abofra no ne ndze; na Nyankopɔn no bɔfoe fi sor frɛɛ Hagar, na ɔsee no dɛ, Ebɛnadze yɛ wo, Hagar? Mma nnsuro; na Nyankopɔn atse abofra no ne ndze wɔ mbrɛ ɔwɔ no.

english_verse: Help him up and hold his hand, because I will make him the father of a great nation.”
translation_verse: Soɛr, kɛma abofra no do, na fa wo nsa suo no mu; na mebɛyɛ no ɔman kɛse.

english_verse: Then God let her see a well. So she went to the well and filled the skin with water, then gave some to her son.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn buee n’enyiwa, na ohun nsubura bi; na ɔkɔree, na ɔkɛhyɛɛ bɔdambɔ no nsu ma, na ɔmaa abofra no nsu ma ɔnomee.

english_verse: God blessed Ishmael, and as the boy grew older, he became an expert at hunting with his bow and arrows. He lived in the Paran Desert, and his mother chose an Egyptian woman for him to marry. A Peace Treaty
translation_verse: Na ɔtsenaa Paran sar do; na ne na kɔwaar yer wɔ Egypt asaase do brɛɛ no. Abraham Nye Abimelek Dzi Ahyɛmu

english_verse: About this time Abimelech and his army commander Phicol said to Abraham, “God blesses everything you do!
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ aber no no mu na Abimelek nye ne safohen tsitsir Fikol kasaa kyerɛɛ Abraham dɛ, Nyankopɔn dzi w’ekyir wɔ dza eyɛ nyinara mu:

english_verse: Now I want you to promise in the name of God that you will always be loyal to me and my descendants, just as I have always been loyal to you in this land where you have lived as a foreigner.”
translation_verse: dɛm ntsi afei, fa Nyankopɔn dzi nsew kyerɛ me wɔ ha dɛ ennkɛdaadaa emi, nye me ba, nye me mbanana: na dɛ mbrɛ ayamuyie a medze ayɛ wo no tse no ara, ebɛyɛ ama emi, nye asaase a aba do ahɔhoyɛ yi.

english_verse: And so, Abraham promised he would.
translation_verse: Na Abraham see dɛ, Mibedzi nsew.

english_verse: One day, Abraham told Abimelech, “Some of your servants have taken over one of my wells.”
translation_verse: Na Abraham twuuw Abimelek n’enyim, wɔ nsubura a Abimelek no nkowaa dze enyimpi gyee no ntsi.

english_verse: “This is the first I've heard about it,” Abimelech replied. “Why haven't you said something before? I don't have any idea who did it.”
translation_verse: Na Abimelek see dɛ, Minnyim nyia ɔyɛɛ iyi: na wo so annka annkyerɛ me, na nkyii so menntsee pɛn dɛ ndɛ.

english_verse: Abraham gave Abimelech some sheep and cattle, then the two men made a peace treaty.
translation_verse: Na Abraham faa nguan, nye anantwi, na ɔdze maa Abimelek; na hɔn beenu no dzii ahyɛmu.

english_verse: Abraham separated seven female lambs from his flock of sheep,
translation_verse: Na Abraham yii nguankuw mu nguambaa aber esuon dze hɔn sii nkyɛn.

english_verse: Abraham replied, “I want you to accept these seven lambs as proof that I dug this well.”
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Nguambaa aber esuon yi na ebɛgye no wɔ me nsamu ama ɔaayɛ dase ama me, dɛ mara na mutuu bura yi.

english_verse: So they called the place Beersheba, because they made a treaty there.
translation_verse: Dɛm ntsi ɔfrɛɛ hɔ Beer-seba; osiandɛ hɔ na hɔn beenu nyina wodzii nsew.

english_verse: When the treaty was completed, Abimelech and his army commander Phicol went back to the land of the Philistines.
translation_verse: Na wodzii ahyɛmu wɔ Beer-seba wiei no, Abimelek soɛree, nye ne safohen tsitsir Fikol, na wɔsanee ba Filistiafo asaase do.

english_verse: Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beersheba and worshiped the eternal Lord God.
translation_verse: Na Abraham tsɛɛw ɔsɛntsɛw dua wɔ Beer-seba, na ɔsɔɔr EWURADZE, ɔdomankoma Nyankopɔn no wɔ hɔ.

english_verse: Then Abraham lived a long time as a foreigner in the land of the Philistines.
translation_verse: Na Abraham yɛɛ hɔho wɔ Filistiafo asaase do nda pii.

english_verse: Some years later God decided to test Abraham, so he spoke to him. Abraham answered, “Here I am, Lord.”
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ iyinom n’ekyir no Nyankopɔn sɔɔ Abraham hwɛe, na ɔsee dɛ, Abraham; na ɔsee dɛ, Emi nye yi.

english_verse: The Lord said, “Go get Isaac, your only son, the one you dearly love! Take him to the land of Moriah, and I will show you a mountain where you must sacrifice him to me on the fires of an altar.”
translation_verse: Na, ɔsee dɛ, Afei fa wo ba, wo ba kortonoo a edɔ no, mekyerɛ Isaak no, na kɔ Moria asaase do; na fa no kɔbɔ ɔhyew afɔr wɔ hɔ, wɔ mbepɔw no mu kor a mebɛkyerɛ wo no do.

english_verse: So Abraham got up early the next morning and chopped wood for the fire. He put a saddle on his donkey and set out with Isaac and two servants for the place where God had told him to go.
translation_verse: Na Abraham soɛɛr anapaatu hyehyɛɛ n’asoasopɔnkɔ, na ɔfaa ne mberantsɛ no mu beenu kaa no ho, nye ne ba Isaak; na ɔpaa ɔhyew afɔr ne nyena no, na ɔsoɛree kɔr mbrɛ Nyankopɔn kyerɛɛ no no.

english_verse: Three days later Abraham looked off in the distance and saw the place.
translation_verse: Na ne ndaansa no Abraham maa n’enyiwa do, na ohun bea no wɔ ekyir noho ara.

english_verse: He told his servants, “Stay here with the donkey, while my son and I go over there to worship. We will come back.”
translation_verse: Na Abraham see ne mberantsɛ no dɛ, Hom nye asoasopɔnkɔ yi ntsena ha, na emi na abofra yi yɛbɔkɔ noho, na yɛakɔsɔr, na yɛasan aba hom nkyɛn.

english_verse: Abraham put the wood on Isaac's shoulder, but he carried the hot coals and the knife. As the two of them walked along,
translation_verse: Na Abraham faa ɔhyew afɔr no ne nyena no, na ɔdze soaa ne ba Isaak; na ɔfaa gya no nye sekan no kitsae; na hɔn beenu nyina bɔɔ mu kɔree.

english_verse: Isaac said, “Father, we have the coals and the wood, but where is the lamb for the sacrifice?” “My son,” Abraham answered, “God will provide the lamb.” The two of them walked on, and
translation_verse: Na Abraham see dɛ, Me ba, Nyankopɔn noara bɛkyerɛ eguambaa a wɔdze no bɔbɔ ɔhyew afɔr: na hɔn beenu nyina bɔɔ mu kɔree.

english_verse: when they reached the place that God had told him about, Abraham built an altar and placed the wood on it. Next, he tied up his son and put him on the wood.
translation_verse: Na wodur mbrɛ Nyankopɔn kyerɛɛ no no; na Abraham sii afɔrmbukyia wɔ hɔ, na osiesiee nyena no yie, na ɔkyekyeer ne ba Isaak dze no too afɔrmbukyia no do wɔ nyena no do.

english_verse: He then took the knife and got ready to kill his son.
translation_verse: Na Abraham tseen ne nsa, na ɔfaa sekan no dɛ ɔdze ruboku ne ba no.

english_verse: But the Lord's angel shouted from heaven, “Abraham! Abraham!” “Here I am!” he answered.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE no bɔfoe fi sor frɛɛ no, na ɔsee dɛ, Abraham, Abraham: na ɔsee dɛ, Emi nye yi.

english_verse: “Don't hurt the boy or harm him in any way!” the angel said. “Now I know that you truly obey God, because you were willing to offer him your only son.”
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Mma mmfa wo nsa nnka abofra no, nkyii so mma nnyɛ no biribiara: na afei na mehu dɛ isuro Nyankopɔn, susuampa dɛ ɔwo annkam me wo ba, wo ba kortonoo ntsi.

english_verse: Abraham looked up and saw a ram caught by its horns in the bushes. So he took the ram and sacrificed it instead of his son.
translation_verse: Na Abraham maa n’enyiwa do, na ɔhwɛe, na hwɛ, oguannyin a n’ambɛn ekosuo mbirikisi mu wɔ n’ekyir: na Abraham kɔree kɛfaa oguannyin no, na ɔdze no hyɛɛ ne ba n’ananmu bɔɔ hyew afɔr.

english_verse: Abraham named that place “The Lord Will Provide.” And even now people say, “On the mountain of the Lord it will be provided.”
translation_verse: Na Abraham frɛɛ bea no ne dzin Jehovah-jireh: dɛ mbrɛ ebodu ndɛ wɔda ho ara frɛ no no, dɛ, EWURADZE ne bepɔw do na wɔbɛkyerɛ.

english_verse: The Lord's angel called out from heaven a second time:
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE no bɔfoe fi sor frɛɛ Abraham bio dɛ,

english_verse: You were willing to offer your only son to the Lord, and so he makes you this solemn promise,
translation_verse: mbrɛ EWURADZE se nye iyi, osiandɛ ayɛ dɛm adze yi, dɛ ammfa wo ba, wo ba kortsee no, annkam me ntsi, maka moho ntam:

english_verse: “I will bless you and give you such a large family, that someday your descendants will be more numerous than the stars in the sky or the grains of sand along the seashore. They will defeat their enemies and take over the cities where their enemies live.
translation_verse: dɛ nhyira mu a, mibehyira wo, na ɔdɔr mu a, mebɛma w’asefo adɔr dɛ ɔsor nworaba, na dɛ mpoano anhwea; na w’asefo bɛfa hɔn atamfo hɔn abotsen;

english_verse: You have obeyed me, and so you and your descendants will be a blessing to all nations on earth.”
translation_verse: na w’asefo mu na wobehyira asaase amanaman nyinara; osiandɛ etsie me ndze.

english_verse: Abraham and Isaac went back to the servants who had come with him, and they returned to Abraham's home in Beersheba. The Children of Nahor
translation_verse: Nna Abraham sanee baa ne mberantsɛ no nkyɛn, na wɔsoɛree, na wɔbɔɔ mu kɔr Beer-seba; na Abraham tsenaa Beer-seba. Nahor Asefo

english_verse: Nahor also had another wife. Her name was Reumah, and she had four sons: Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
translation_verse: Na no nwewe a ne dzin dze Reuma no, ɔno so ɔwoo Teba, Gaham, nye Tahas, nye Maaka.

english_verse: he went to the Hittites and said,
translation_verse: Na Abraham soɛree fii no fun no enyim, na ɔkɛkasaa kyerɛɛ Hetmba no, dɛ,

english_verse: “I live as a foreigner in your land, and I don't own any property where I can bury my wife. Please let me buy a piece of land.”
translation_verse: Meyɛ ɔhɔho nye ɔmamforanyi wɔ hom nkyɛn: hom mma me efunsiebea bi wɔ hom nkyɛn, ma minsie mo fun mfi m’enyi do.

english_verse: “Sir,” they answered, “you are an important man. Choose the best place to bury your wife. None of us would refuse you a resting place for your dead.”
translation_verse: Mo wura, tsie hɛn: eyɛ okunyin katakyi wɔ hɛn mu: hɛn asamandan mu dza woara epɛ no, sie wo fun wɔ mu; hɛn mu biara nnkam wo n’asamandan, na sie wo fun.

english_verse: and replied, “If you are willing to let me bury my wife here, please ask Zohar's son Ephron
translation_verse: Na ɔnye hɔn kasae see dɛ, Sɛ hom adwen nye dɛ misie mo fun fi m’enyi do a, wontsie me, na wɔnserɛ Zohar ne ba Efron mma me,

english_verse: to sell me Machpelah Cave at the end of his field. I'll pay what it's worth, and all of you can be witnesses.”
translation_verse: ma ɔmfa Makepela ɔbo tu a ɔwɔ no a ɔwɔ ne haban ano no mma me; ɔngye bo a ɔfata na ɔmfa mma me ma minnya efunsiebea wɔ hom finimfin.

english_verse: Ephron was sitting there near the city gate, when Abraham made this request, and he answered,
translation_verse: Afei Efron tse Hetmba no hɔn finimfin: na Hetnyi Efron buaa Abraham wɔ Hetmba no asomu, mekyerɛ hɔn a wɔhɛn no kurow n’abotsen mu no nyinara asomu dɛ,

english_verse: “Sir, the whole field, including the cave, is yours. With my own people as witnesses, I freely give it to you as a burial place for your dead.”
translation_verse: Dabida, mo wura, tsie me: haban no medze ma wo, na ɔbo tu a ɔwɔ mu no medze ma wo; wɔ me man mba hɔn enyim medze ma wo: sie wo fun.

english_verse: and said to Ephron, “In front of these witnesses, I offer you the full price, so I can bury my wife. Please accept my offer.”
translation_verse: Na ɔkasaa kyerɛɛ Efron wɔ asaase no do nkorɔfo no hɔn asomu dɛ, Mbom sɛ ibetsie a, ɔno, meserɛ wo, tsie me: mebɛma haban no ne tsibo; gye no wɔ mo hɔ, ma minsie mo fun wɔ hɔ.

english_verse: “But sir,” the man replied, “the property is worth only
translation_verse: Mo wura, tsie me: dwetɛ dwetɛbona ahaanan asaase nye dɛn wɔ emi na ɔwo ntamu? Dɛm ntsi sie wo fun.

english_verse: So Abraham buried his wife Sarah in Machpelah Cave that was in the field
translation_verse: Na iyi n’ekyir no, Abraham siee ne yer Sara wɔ Makepela haban no mu ɔbo tu mu wɔ Mamre enyim , wɔ Keenan asaase do.

english_verse: Abraham was now a very old man. The Lord had made him rich, and he was successful in everything he did.
translation_verse: Na Abraham ayɛ akɔdaa, na oedzi mfe ara yie: na EWURADZE hyiraa Abraham wɔ adze nyinara mu.

english_verse: One day, Abraham called in his most trusted servant and said to him, “Solemnly promise me
translation_verse: Na Abraham see, ne fie somfo panyin a ɔhwɛ adze a ɔwɔ nyinara do no, dɛ, Meserɛ wo, fa wo nsa to m’aserɛ ase:

english_verse: in the name of the Lord, who rules heaven and earth, that you won't choose a wife for my son Isaac from the people here in the land of Canaan.
translation_verse: na mebɛma edze EWURADZE, sor Nyankopɔn nye asaase Nyankopɔn no, edzi nsew, dɛ ennkɛgye Keenanfo a metse hɔn mu yi, hɔn mbabaa mu bi yer ama me ba:

english_verse: Instead, go back to the land where I was born and find a wife for him from among my relatives.”
translation_verse: na ebɔkɔ mo kurow mu, na akɛgye yer wɔ m’ebusua mu abrɛ me ba Isaak.

english_verse: But the servant asked, “What if the young woman I choose refuses to leave home and come here with me? Should I send Isaac there to look for a wife?”
translation_verse: Na ɔsomfo no see dɛ, Bi a ɔbaa no nnkɛpɛ dɛ ɔbɛka mo do aba asaase yi do: ɔnodze memfa wo ba nsan nkɔ asaase a ifi do bae no do bio a?

english_verse: “No!” Abraham answered. “Don't ever do that, no matter what.
translation_verse: Na Abraham see no dɛ, Hwɛ woho yie mma aammfa me ba annkɔ hɔ bio.

english_verse: The Lord who rules heaven brought me here from the land where I was born and promised that he would give this land to my descendants forever. When you go back there, the Lord will send his angel ahead of you to help you find a wife for my son.
translation_verse: EWURADZE, ɔsor Nyankopɔn no a ɔfaa me fii m’egya fie, nye asaase a wɔwoo me do no, na ɔkasaa kyerɛɛ me, na odzii nsew kyerɛɛ me, dɛ, W’asefo na medze asaase yi mebɛma hɔn no; ɔbɔsoma no bɔfoe ma oeedzi w’enyim, na akɛgye yer wɔ hɔ abrɛ me ba.

english_verse: If the woman refuses to come along, you don't have to keep this promise. But don't ever take my son back there.”
translation_verse: Na sɛ ɔbaa no ammpɛ dɛ ɔbɛka wo do a, ɔno na wo ho efi me ntam yi mu: me ba no ara na mma mmfa no nnkɔ hɔ bio.

english_verse: So the servant gave Abraham his word that he would do everything he had been told to do.
translation_verse: Na akowaa no dze ne nsa too no wura Abraham ne serɛ ase, na odzii nsew kyerɛɛ no wɔ asɛm yi ho.

english_verse: Soon after that, the servant loaded ten of Abraham's camels with valuable gifts. Then he set out for the city in northern Syria, where Abraham's brother Nahor lived.
translation_verse: Na akowaa no faa no wura n’efumpɔnkɔ mu du, na no wura ne ndzɛmba papa nyinara awɔ ne nsamu: no bi na osii mu kɔr Mesopotemia, Nahor kurow mu.

english_verse: When he got there, he let the camels rest near the well outside the city. It was late afternoon, the time when the women came out for water.
translation_verse: Na ɔmaa efumpɔnkɔ no butubutuuw kurotsia nsubura no ho ewimbibaamu, aber a mbaa pue kɛsaw nsu no.

english_verse: The servant prayed: You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships. Please keep your promise to him and let me find a wife for Isaac today.
translation_verse: Na ɔbɔɔ mpaa dɛ, EWURADZE, mo wura Abraham ne Nyankopɔn, meserɛ wo, ma minhyia yie ndɛ, na yɛ mo wura Abraham ayamuyie.

english_verse: The young women of the city will soon come to this well for water,
translation_verse: Hwɛ, migyina nsubura yi ho; na kurow no mu mba mbabaa rupue abɛsaw nsu:

english_verse: and I'll ask one of them for a drink. If she gives me a drink and then offers to get some water for my camels, I'll know she is the one you have chosen and that you have kept your promise to my master.
translation_verse: na ma ɔmbra dɛ, akataasia a mebɛse no dɛ, Meserɛ wo, soɛ w’ehina, ma monnom bi; na ɔbɛse dɛ, Nom bi, na mebɛma w’efumpɔnkɔ no nsu ma wɔaanom no: ɔnoara na ma ɔnyɛ nyia eyi no ama w’akowaa Isaak; na ɔnam dɛm no do na mubohu dɛ ayɛ mo wura ayamuyie.

english_verse: While he was still praying, a beautiful unmarried young woman came by with a water jar on her shoulder. She was Rebekah, the daughter of Bethuel, the son of Abraham's brother Nahor and his wife Milcah. Rebekah walked past Abraham's servant, then went over to the well, and filled her water jar. When she started back,
translation_verse: Na akataasia no no ho yɛ fɛw dodow, na ɔyɛ ɔbaabun, osiandɛ ɔbanyin biara nnhun no pɛn: na osianee kɔr bura no mu, na ɔkɛhyɛɛ n’ehina ma, na ɔbaa sor.

english_verse: Abraham's servant ran to her and said, “Please let me have a drink of water.”
translation_verse: Na akowaa no tutuu mbirika kehyiaa no kwan, na ɔsee dɛ, Meserɛ wo, ma monnom w’ehina mu nsu kakra.

english_verse: “I'll be glad to,” she answered. Then she quickly took the jar from her shoulder and held it while he drank.
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Nom bi, mo wura: na ɔyɛɛ ntsɛm soɛɛ ehina no taa ne nsamu, na ɔmaa no nsu ma ɔnomee.

english_verse: After he had finished, she said, “Now I'll give your camels all the water they want.” She quickly poured out water for them, and she kept going back for more, until his camels had drunk all they wanted.
translation_verse: Na ɔyɛɛ ntsɛm, na ohuee n’ehina mu nsu guu ɔdaasee mu, na otutuu mbirika kɔr bura no mu bio kɛsawee, na ɔsawee maa n’efumpɔnkɔ no nyinara.

english_verse: Abraham's servant did not say a word, but he watched everything Rebekah did, because he wanted to know for certain if this was the woman the Lord had chosen.
translation_verse: Na banyin no hwɛɛ no haa dɛ obohu kyerɛ EWURADZE ama n’akwantu no ayɛ prɔmprɔm anaa ɔnnyɛe.

english_verse: The servant had brought along an expensive gold ring and two large gold bracelets. When Rebekah had finished bringing the water, he gave her the ring for her nose and the bracelets for her arms.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ, efumpɔnkɔ no wiee nsu nom no, nna banyin no faa sika mpɛtsea a n’ano tow dwetɛbona fa, nye ne nsakɔnadze ebien a n’ano tow sika dwetɛbona du dze hyɛɛ ne nsa;

english_verse: Then he said, “Please tell me who your father is. Does he have room in his house for me and my men to spend the night?”
translation_verse: na obisaa no dɛ, Woana babaa nye wo? Meserɛ wo, ka kyerɛ me. Ana ɔkwan wɔ w’egya fie ma hɛn dɛ yɛbɔsoɛ hɔ?

english_verse: She answered, “My father is Bethuel, the son of Nahor and Milcah.
translation_verse: Na Rebeka see no dɛ, Betuel a ɔyɛ Milka ne babanyin a ɔwoo no maa Nahor no, ne babaa nye me.

english_verse: We have a place where you and your men can stay, and we also have enough straw and feed for your camels.”
translation_verse: Nkyii so ɔsee no dɛ, Yɛwɔ efuw nye mbowa edziban pii, nye dan a wɔsoɛ mu.

english_verse: Then the servant bowed his head and prayed,
translation_verse: Na banyin no bɔɔ ne tsir adze, na ɔsɔɔr EWURADZE.

english_verse: “I thank you, Lord God of my master Abraham! You have led me to his relatives and kept your promise to him.”
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Nhyira nka EWURADZE, mo wura Abraham ne Nyankopɔn a ɔammpow n’ehumbɔbɔr nye no nokwar no wɔ mo wura ho: emi dze, EWURADZE egya me kwan aba mo wura no nuanom fie.

english_verse: Her brother Laban heard her tell what the servant had said, and he saw the ring and the bracelets she was wearing. So Laban ran out to Abraham's servant, who was standing by his camels at the well.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ, aber a ohun mpɛtsea nye nsakɔnadze wɔ n’akyerɛbaa Rebeka ne nsa, na ɔtsee n’akyerɛbaa ne nsɛm no, dɛ, Mbrɛ banyin no kaa kyerɛɛ no no, otutuu mbirika baa banyin no nkyɛn; na, hwɛ, ogyina efumpɔnkɔ no nkyɛn wɔ bura no do.

english_verse: Then Laban said, “The Lord has brought you safely here. Come home with me. There's no need for you to keep on standing outside. I have a room ready for you in our house, and there's also a place for your camels.”
translation_verse: Na Laban see no dɛ, Bra fie, ɔwo a EWURADZE ehyira wo; ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na igyina kurotsia yi? Mesiesie dan no, nye kwan ama efumpɔnkɔ no.

english_verse: Abraham's servant went home with Laban, where Laban's servants unloaded his camels and gave them straw and feed. Then they brought water into the house, so Abraham's servant and his men could wash their feet.
translation_verse: Na banyin no baa fie no mu, na Laban yiyii efumpɔnkɔ no hɔn do ndzɛmba; na ɔmaa hɔn edziban, na ɔmaa nsu dɛ banyin no nye hɔn a wɔka no ho no dze ahohor hɔn anan ho.

english_verse: After that, they brought in food. But the servant said, “Before I eat, I must tell you why I have come.” “Go ahead and tell us,” Laban answered.
translation_verse: Na wɔdze edziban besii n’enyim dɛ ondzi: na ɔsee dɛ, Minnkedzi, gyedɛ mabɔ m’amandzɛɛ ana. Na ɔsee dɛ, Bɔ ɛ.

english_verse: The servant explained: I am Abraham's servant.
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Meyɛ Abraham no somfo.

english_verse: The Lord has been good to my master and has made him very rich. He has given him many sheep, goats, cattle, camels, and donkeys, as well as a lot of silver and gold, and many slaves.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE ehyira mo wura kɛsenara; na ɔayɛ kɛse: na ɔama no nguan nye anantwi, na dwetɛ nye sika, na nkowaa nye mfonabaa, na efumpɔnkɔ nye nsoasopɔnkɔ.

english_verse: Sarah, my master's wife, didn't have any children until she was very old. Then she had a son, and my master has given him everything.
translation_verse: Na aber a mo wura ne yer yɛɛ aberwa no, nna ɔwoo babanyin maa mo wura: na ɔno na ɔdze dza ɔwɔ nyinara ama no.

english_verse: I solemnly promised my master that I would do what he said. And he told me, “Don't choose a wife for my son from the women in this land of Canaan.
translation_verse: Na mo wura maa midzii nsew dɛ, Mma nngye yer wɔ Kanaanfo a metse hɔn asaase do yi hɔn mbabaa mu mma me ba.

english_verse: Instead, go back to the land where I was born and find a wife for my son from among my relatives.”
translation_verse: Kɔ m’egya fie, nye m’ebusua mu, na kɛgye yer ma me ba.

english_verse: I asked my master, “What if the young woman refuses to come with me?”
translation_verse: Na mesee mo wura dɛ, Bi a ɔbaa no nnkɛka mo do.

english_verse: My master answered, “I have always obeyed the Lord, and he will send his angel to help you find my son a wife from among my own relatives.
translation_verse: Na ɔsee me dɛ, EWURADZE a menam n’enyim no, ɔbɔsoma no bɔfoe ma ɔaabɛka wo ho, na ɔama wo kwan do ayɛ dwudwoodwo; na ebɛgye yer wɔ m’ebusua mu, nye m’egya fie ama me ba.

english_verse: But if they refuse to let her come back with you, then you are freed from your promise.”
translation_verse: Sɛ ekɔ m’ebusua mu na sɛ wɔammfa no amma wo a, ɔnodze na wo ho efi me ntam mu.

english_verse: When I came to the well today, I silently prayed, “You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships, so please lead me to a wife for his son
translation_verse: Na mebaa bura no do ndɛ, na mesee dɛ, EWURADZE, mo wura Abraham ne Nyankopɔn, sɛ afei erema mo kwan a morokɔ yi ayɛ dwudwoodwo a:

english_verse: while I am here at the well. When a young woman comes out to get water, I'll ask her to give me a drink.
translation_verse: hwɛ migyina nsubura yi ho; na ma ɔmbra dɛ akataasia a obopue abɛsaw bi a mebɛse no dɛ, Meserɛ wo, ma me w’ehina mu nsu kakra ma monnom;

english_verse: If she gives me a drink and offers to get some water for my camels, I'll know she is the one you have chosen.”
translation_verse: na ɔbɛse me dɛ, ɔwoara nom bi, na mebɛsaw bi ama w’efumpɔnkɔ so no: ɔnoara na ma ɔnyɛ ɔbaa a EWURADZE eyi no ama mo wura ne ba.

english_verse: Even before I had finished praying, Rebekah came by with a water jar on her shoulder. When she had filled the jar, I asked her for a drink.
translation_verse: Na ansaana murubowie kasa wɔ m’akoma mu no, hwɛ, Rebeka puei na n’ehina si no kɔn ho; na osianee kɔr bura no mu, na ɔkɛsawee: na mesee no dɛ, Meserɛ wo, ma monnom bi.

english_verse: She quickly lowered the jar from her shoulder and said, “Have a drink. Then I'll get water for your camels.” So I drank, and after that she got some water for my camels.
translation_verse: Na ɔyɛɛ ntsɛm soɛɛ n’ehina fii no kɔn ho, na ɔsee dɛ, Nom bi, na mebɛma w’efumpɔnkɔ so bi ma wɔaanom: nna monomee, na ɔmaa efumpɔnkɔ no so bi ma wɔnomee.

english_verse: I asked her who her father was, and she answered, “My father is Bethuel the son of Nahor and Milcah.” At once I put the ring in her nose and the bracelets on her arms.
translation_verse: Na mibisaa no dɛ, Woana babaa nye wo? Na ɔsee dɛ, Nahor ba Betuel a Milka woo no maa no no, ne babaa nye me: na medze mpɛtsea no hyɛɛ no hwen, na medze nsakɔnadze no hyehyɛɛ ne nsa.

english_verse: Then I bowed my head and gave thanks to the God my master Abraham worships. The Lord had led me straight to my master's relatives, and I had found a wife for his son.
translation_verse: Na mobɔɔ me tsir adze, na mosɔɔr EWURADZE, na mihyiraa EWURADZE, mo wura Abraham ne Nyankopɔn no a ogyaa me wɔ ɔkwan pa mu dɛ membɛfa mo wura no nua ne babaa nkɛma ne ba no.

english_verse: Now please tell me if you are willing to do the right thing for my master. Will you treat him fairly, or do I have to look for another young woman?
translation_verse: Na afei sɛ hom bɛnye mo wura edzi no ayamuyie mu nye nokwar mu a, ɔno wɔnka nkyerɛ me: oho so a, wɔnka nkyerɛ me; ama maadan mafa enyimfa anaa abankum.

english_verse: Laban and Bethuel answered, “The Lord has done this. We have no choice in the matter.
translation_verse: Nna Laban nye Betuel buae na wɔsee dɛ, Asɛm no fi EWURADZE hɔ: yennkotum aka bɔn anaa papa akyerɛ wo.

english_verse: Take Rebekah with you; she can marry your master's son, just as the Lord has said.”
translation_verse: Hwɛ, Rebeka na ɔwɔ w’enyim yi, fa no kɔ, ma ɔnkɛyɛ wo wura ne ba ne yer, dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE aka no ara.

english_verse: Then he gave clothing, as well as silver and gold jewelry, to Rebekah. He also gave expensive gifts to her brother and her mother.
translation_verse: Na oyii dwetɛ egudze, nye sika egudze, nye atar, na ɔdze maa Rebeka: ɔmaa no nua nye ne na so ndzɛmba a ɔsom bo.

english_verse: Abraham's servant and the men with him ate and drank, then spent the night there. The next morning they got up, and the servant told Rebekah's mother and brother, “I would like to go back to my master now.”
translation_verse: Na wodzidzii na wɔnomee, ɔno nye mbanyin a wɔka no ho no, na wotsintsiim hɔ anafua no nyinara. Na wɔsoɛɛr anapa no, ɔsee dɛ, Hom ngya me kwan ma monkɔ mo wura hɔ.

english_verse: “Let Rebekah stay with us for a week or ten days,” they answered. “Then she may go.”
translation_verse: Na Rebeka no nua nye ne na see dɛ, Ma akataasia no nye hɛn ntsena nda kakra, bɛyɛ dɛ dadu; na n’ekyir no ɔaakɔ.

english_verse: But he said, “Don't make me stay any longer. The Lord has already helped me find a wife for my master's son. Now let us return.”
translation_verse: Na ɔsee hɔn dɛ, Mma wonnsiw me kwan, osiandɛ EWURADZE ama mo kwan ayɛ prɔmprɔm; hom ngya me kwan ma monkɔ mo wura hɔ.

english_verse: They answered, “Let's ask Rebekah what she wants to do.”
translation_verse: Na wɔsee dɛ, Yɛbɛfrɛ akataasia no, na yeebisa n’anomu.

english_verse: They called her and asked, “Are you willing to leave with this man at once?” “Yes,” she answered.
translation_verse: Na wɔfrɛɛ Rebeka, na wɔsee no dɛ, Ana enye ɔbarimba yi bɔkɔ? Na ɔsee dɛ, Mobɔkɔ.

english_verse: So they agreed to let Rebekah and an old family servant woman leave immediately with Abraham's servant and his men.
translation_verse: Na wogyaa hɔn akyerɛbaa Rebeka, nye no somfo, nye Abraham somfo no, nye no nkorɔfo no kwan.

english_verse: They gave Rebekah their blessing and said, “We pray that God will give you many children and grandchildren and that he will help them defeat their enemies.”
translation_verse: Na wohyiraa Rebeka, na wɔsee no dɛ, Hɛn akyerɛbaa, yɛ na ma ɔpedu horow mpempem, na w’asefo nnya hɔn a wokyir hɔn hɔn mbobow mfa.

english_verse: Afterwards, Rebekah and the young women who were to travel with her prepared to leave. Then they got on camels and left with Abraham's servant and his men.
translation_verse: Na Rebeka nye ne mbabaawa soɛree, na wɔtsenatsenaa efumpɔnkɔ no do, na wɔkaa banyin no do: na wosii mu kɔr hɔn kwan.

english_verse: At that time Isaac was living in the southern part of Canaan near a place called “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.”
translation_verse: Na Isaak fi Beer-lahai-roi kwan do aba; osiandɛ nna ɔtse Pomu Afamu Asaase no do.

english_verse: One evening he was walking out in the fields, when suddenly he saw a group of people approaching on camels. So he started toward them. Rebekah saw him coming; she got down from her camel, and asked, “Who is that man?” “He is my master Isaac,” the servant answered. Then Rebekah covered her face with her veil.
translation_verse: Na obisaa Abraham no somfo no dɛ, Ebɛn banyin na ɔnam haban mu ribehyia hɛn yi? Na akowaa no see dɛ, Mo wura a: na basia no faa ne nkataenyim dze kataa noho.

english_verse: The servant told Isaac everything that had happened.
translation_verse: Na ɔsomfo no kaa adze a ɔyɛe nyinara kyerɛɛ Isaak.

english_verse: Isaac took Rebekah into the tent where his mother had lived before she died, and Rebekah became his wife. He loved her and was comforted over the loss of his mother.
translation_verse: Na Isaak dze Rebeka baa asese a nna ne na Sara tse mu no; na ɔfaa Rebeka yɛɛ no ne yer; na ɔdɔɔ no: na Isaak no werɛ kyekyeree wɔ ne na no wu no ekyir.

english_verse: and they had six sons: Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah.
translation_verse: Na ɔwoo Zimran, Joksan, Medan, Midian, Isbak, nye Sua maa no.

english_verse: Later, Jokshan became the father of Sheba and Dedan, and when Dedan grew up, he had three sons: Asshurim, Letushim, and Leummim.
translation_verse: Na Joksan woo Seba, nye Dedan. Na Dedan ne mba nye Assurim, Letusim, nye Leumim.

english_verse: Midian also had five sons: Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah.
translation_verse: Na Midian ne mba nye Efa, Efer, Hanok, Abida, nye Eldaa. Woyinom nyinara yɛ Ketura ne mba.

english_verse: While Abraham was still alive, he gave gifts to the sons of Hagar and Keturah. He also sent their sons to live in the east far from his son Isaac, and when Abraham died, he left everything to Isaac. The Death of Abraham
translation_verse: Na mbasiafo nkaa no mba a Abraham wɔ no dze, Abraham kyɛɛ hɔn adze; na ogyaa hɔn kwan, boka afamu, fii ne ba Isaak ho, ber a ɔda ho tse ase no, ma wɔkɔr boka asaase no do. Abraham No Wu

english_verse: His sons Isaac and Ishmael buried him east of Hebron in Machpelah Cave that was part of the field Abraham had bought from Ephron son of Zohar the Hittite. Abraham was buried there beside his wife Sarah.
translation_verse: haban no a Abraham tɔɔ no wɔ Hetmba nsamu no mu: hɔ na wosiee Abraham nye ne yer Sara.

english_verse: God blessed Isaac after this, and Isaac moved to a place called “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.” Ishmael's Descendants
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ Abraham no wu ekyir no, Nyankopɔn hyiraa ne ba Isaak; na Isaak tsenaa Beer-lahai-roi ho. Ismael Asefo (

english_verse: Ishmael was the son of Abraham and Hagar, the slave woman of Sarah.
translation_verse: Afei iyinom nye Abraham ne ba Ismael a Egyptnyi Hagar, Sara n’afonabaa no, woo no maa Abraham no n’ebusuasantsen:

english_verse: Mishma, Dumah, Massa,
translation_verse: Misma, Duma, nye Massa

english_verse: Hadad, Tema, Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah.
translation_verse: Binom so nye Hadad, Tema, Jetur, Nafis, nye Kedema:

english_verse: Each of Ishmael's sons was a tribal chief, and a village was named after each of them.
translation_verse: iyinom nye Ismael ne mba, na iyinom nye hɔn edzin, dɛ mbrɛ hɔn nkuroba, nye hɔn nseraban no tsetsee; etsitsir duebien dɛ mbrɛ hɔn aman tsetsee.

english_verse: Ishmael had settled in the land east of his brothers, and his sons settled everywhere from Havilah to Shur, east of Egypt on the way to Asshur. Ishmael was
translation_verse: Na wɔtsenaa fi Havila kodur Sur a ɔwɔ Egypt enyim no, edze rokɔ Asiria afamu no: na ɔnye no nuanom no anntsena wɔ abodwee mu.

english_verse: Isaac was the son of Abraham,
translation_verse: Na iyinom nye Abraham ne ba Isaak n’ebusuasantsen: Abraham woo Isaak;

english_verse: Rebekah still had no children. So Isaac asked the Lord to let her have a child, and the Lord answered his prayer.
translation_verse: Na Isaak serɛɛ EWURADZE maa ne yer, osiandɛ ɔyɛ ɔbɔnyin: na EWURADZE tsiee no nkotoserɛ no, na ne yer Rebeka nyinsenii.

english_verse: Before Rebekah gave birth, she knew she was going to have twins, because she could feel them inside her, fighting each other. She thought, “Why is this happening to me?” Finally, she asked the Lord why her twins were fighting,
translation_verse: Na mbofra no dzii aper wɔ no mu; na ɔsee dɛ, Sɛ ɔtse dɛm a, ebɛnadze ntsi na metse ase? Na ɔkɔree kebisaa EWURADZE.

english_verse: and he told her: “Your two sons will become two separate nations. The younger of the two will be stronger, and the older son will be his servant.”
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see no dɛ, Aman ebien na wɔwɔ wo yamu, Na nkorɔfo ahorow ebien na wɔbɛpaa hɔnho mu efi wo yafun mu; Na ɔman no mu kor bɛyɛ dzen asen kor; Na ɔpanyin no bɔsom ekumaba no.

english_verse: the first baby was covered with red hair, so he was named Esau.
translation_verse: Na nyia odzi kan no ɔbae, no ho memennmen, no ho nyinara wɔ nhwi futuww dɛ nhwi atar; na wɔfrɛɛ ne dzin Esau.

english_verse: The second baby grabbed on to his brother's heel, so they named him Jacob. Isaac was
translation_verse: Na ɔno n’ekyir no no nua bae, na ne nsa dze Esau ne nantsir; na wɔfrɛɛ ne dzin Jakob na aber a wɔwoo hɔn no, nna Isaak edzi mfe eduosia.

english_verse: As Jacob and Esau grew older, Esau liked the outdoors and became a good hunter, while Jacob lived the quiet life of a shepherd.
translation_verse: Na mbofra no nyinii: na Esau yɛɛ ɔbɔfo a onyim ahayɛ papaapa, ɔhamunyi a; na Jakob dze ɔyɛɛ nyimpa a ɔyɛ ɔdwe na ɔtsetse asese mu. Esau Tɔn Ne Mpanyinyɛ

english_verse: Esau would take the meat of wild animals to his father Isaac, so Isaac loved him more, but Jacob was his mother's favorite son.
translation_verse: Afei Isaak dɔɔ Esau, osiandɛ odzii ne hanam no bi: na Rebeka dɔɔ Jakob.

english_verse: One day, when Jacob was cooking some stew, Esau came home hungry
translation_verse: Na Jakob yɛɛ edziban bi: na Esau fi hamu bae no, nna ɔatɔ bitsi.

english_verse: and said, “I'm starving to death! Here and now give me some of that red stew!” That's how Esau got the name “Edom.”
translation_verse: Na Esau see Jakob dɛ, Meserɛ wo, ma me edziban yi bi na mindzi; na matɔ bitsi: dɛm ntsi wɔdze too no dzin Edom.

english_verse: Jacob replied, “Sell me your rights as the first-born son.”
translation_verse: Na Jakob see dɛ, Tɔn wo mpanyinyɛ ma me ndɛ da yi.

english_verse: “I'm about to die,” Esau answered. “What good will those rights do me?”
translation_verse: Na Esau see dɛ, Hwɛ, migyina wuso: na ebɛn mfaso na panyin yi bɛyɛ ama me?

english_verse: But Jacob said, “Promise me your birthrights, here and now!” And that's what Esau did.
translation_verse: Na Jakob see dɛ, Dzi nsew kyerɛ me ndɛ da yi; na odzii nsew kyerɛɛ no: na ɔtɔɔn ne mpanyinyɛ maa Jakob.

english_verse: Jacob then gave Esau some bread and some of the bean stew, and when Esau had finished eating and drinking, he just got up and left, showing how little he thought of his rights as the first-born.
translation_verse: Na Jakob maa Esau boodoo nye eduwa noae, na odzii na ɔnomee, na ɔsoɛree ɔkɔr no kwan: na Esau emmbu ne mpanyinyɛ no hwee.

english_verse: Once during Abraham's lifetime, the fields had not produced enough grain, and now the same thing happened. So Isaac went to King Abimelech of the Philistines in the land of Gerar,
translation_verse: Na ɔkɔm sii asaase no do, ɔsor nkan no ɔkɔm a Abraham n’aber do no osii no. Na Isaak kɔr Filistiafo hɔn hen Abimelek nkyɛn wɔ Gerar.

english_verse: because the Lord had appeared to Isaac and said: Isaac, stay away from Egypt! I will show you where I want you to go.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE yii noho kyerɛɛ no, na ɔsee dɛ, Mma nnkɔ Egypt; tsena asaase a mebɛkyerɛ wo no do.

english_verse: You will live there as a foreigner, but I will be with you and bless you. I will keep my promise to your father Abraham by giving this land to you and your descendants.
translation_verse: Tsena asaase yi do dɛ ɔhɔho, na menye wo bɛtsena, na mehyira wo; na ɔwo, nye w’asefo, na medze asaase yi nyinara mebɛma hom, na mebɛma ntam a mekaa kyerɛɛ w’egya Abraham no etsim;

english_verse: I will give you as many descendants as there are stars in the sky, and I will give your descendants all of this land. They will be a blessing to every nation on earth,
translation_verse: na mebɛma w’asefo adɔr dɛ ɔsor nworaba, na medze asaase yi nyinara ama w’asefo; na w’asefo mu na wobehyira asaase do aman nyinara,

english_verse: because Abraham did everything I told him to do.
translation_verse: osiandɛ Abraham tsiee me ndze, na odzii me nkyerɛkyerɛ, me mbrasɛm, m’ahyɛdze, nye me mbra do.

english_verse: Isaac moved to Gerar
translation_verse: Na Isaak tsenaa Gerar.

english_verse: with his beautiful wife Rebekah. He was afraid that someone might kill him to get her, and so he told everyone that Rebekah was his sister.
translation_verse: Na hɔ mba no bisaa no wɔ ne yer ho; na ɔsee dɛ, M’akyerɛbaa a: osiandɛ osuroo dɛ ɔbɛse dɛ, Me yer a; na ɔsee dɛ, bɛyɛ na osian Rebeka ntsi hɔ mba no eku me: osiandɛ no ho yɛ fɛw.

english_verse: After Isaac had been there a long time, King Abimelech looked out a window and saw Isaac hugging and kissing Rebekah.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ, aber a ɔkyɛɛr hɔ no, Filistiafo hen Abimelek hwɛɛ tokura mu, na ohun dɛ Isaak nye ne yer Rebeka rowewe.

english_verse: Abimelech called him in and said, “Rebekah must be your wife! Why did you say she is your sister?” “Because I thought someone would kill me,” Isaac answered.
translation_verse: Na Abimelek frɛɛ Isaak, na ɔsee dɛ, Hwɛ, ampaara wo yer a: na ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na ekaa dɛ, ɔyɛ m’akyerɛbaa yi? Na Isaak see no dɛ, Osiandɛ mesee dɛ, Bɛyɛ na mewu wɔ ne ntsi.

english_verse: “Don't you know what you've done?” Abimelech exclaimed. “If someone had slept with her, you would have made our whole nation guilty!”
translation_verse: Na Abimelek see dɛ, Ebɛnadze na ayɛ hɛn yi? Nkyɛ erebɛyɛ ma nkorɔfo yi mu bi nye wo yer ada, ma edze afɔdzi aba hɛn do.

english_verse: Then Abimelech warned his people that anyone who even touched Isaac or Rebekah would be put to death.
translation_verse: Na Abimelek hyɛɛ nkorɔfo no nyinara, dɛ, Nyia ɔbɛtsen ne nsa aka banyin yi anaa ne yer no ampaara dɛ woboku no.

english_verse: Isaac planted grain and had a good harvest that same year. The Lord blessed him,
translation_verse: Na Isaak duaa aba wɔ asaase no do, no onyaa ɔha mbɔho wɔ afe no ara mu: osiandɛ EWURADZE hyiraa no.

english_verse: and Isaac was so successful that he became very rich.
translation_verse: Na banyin no nyaa no ho, na onyaa no ho araa kodur dɛ ɔyɛɛ kɛsenara:

english_verse: In fact, the Philistines were jealous of the large number of sheep, goats, and slaves that Isaac owned,
translation_verse: Onyaa nguankuw, anantwikuw, fifo kɛsenara: na Filistiafo hɔn enyi beer no.

english_verse: and they stopped up the wells that Abraham's servants had dug before his death.
translation_verse: Afei mbura a n’egya Abraham n’aber do no n’egya no nkowaa tutui no nyinara Filistiafo no sisiiw no, na wɔdze dɛtse hyehyɛɛ mu amaama.

english_verse: Finally, Abimelech said, “Isaac, I want you to leave our country. You have become too powerful to stay here.”
translation_verse: Na Abimelek see Isaak dɛ, Fi hɛn nkyɛn kɔ; na ɔwo ewɔ tum dodow sen hɛn.

english_verse: where he cleaned out those wells that the Philistines had stopped up. Isaac also gave each of the wells the same name that Abraham had given to them.
translation_verse: Na Isaak tutuu mbura a n’egya aber do no wotutui no fofor; osiandɛ Abraham wui n’ekyir no Filistiafo no sisiiw no: na ɔfrɛfrɛɛ hɔn edzin dɛ mbrɛ n’egya dze frɛfrɛɛ hɔn no ara.

english_verse: While his servants were digging in the valley, they found a spring-fed well.
translation_verse: Na Isaak no nkowaa tuu bɔnsa no mu, na wɔkɔtoo mbasu bura wɔ hɔ.

english_verse: But the shepherds of Gerar Valley quarreled with Isaac's shepherds and claimed the water belonged to them. So this well was named “Quarrel,” because they had quarreled with Isaac.
translation_verse: Na Gerar mbowahwɛfo wɔnye Isaak no mbowahwɛfo perperee, dɛ, Nsu no hɛndze a: na ɔfrɛɛ bura no ne dzin Esek; osiandɛ wɔnye no perperee.

english_verse: Isaac's servants dug another well, and the shepherds also quarreled about it. So that well was named “Jealous.”
translation_verse: Na wotuu bura fofor, na wɔperpeer ɔno so ho; na ɔfrɛɛ ne dzin Sitna.

english_verse: Finally, they dug one more well. There was no quarreling this time, and the well was named “Lots of Room,” because the Lord had given them room and would make them very successful.
translation_verse: Na ofii hɔ, na okotuu bura fofor; na ɔnodze wɔammperper ho: na ɔfrɛɛ ne dzin Rehobot, na ɔsee dɛ, na afei EWURADZE ama hɛn bea, na yɛbɔwowo wɔ asaase yi do.

english_verse: Isaac went on to Beersheba,
translation_verse: Na ofii hɔ kɔr Beer-seba.

english_verse: where the Lord appeared to him that night and told him, “Don't be afraid! I am the God who was worshiped by your father Abraham, my servant. I will be with you and bless you, and because of Abraham I will give you many descendants.”
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE yii noho kyerɛɛ no anafua no ara, na ɔsee dɛ, Emi nye w’egya Abraham ne Nyankopɔn: mma nnsuro, na menye wo nam, na mibehyira wo, na osian m’akowaa Abraham ntsi mebɛma w’asefo adɔr.

english_verse: Isaac built an altar there and worshiped the Lord. Then he set up camp, and his servants started digging a well.
translation_verse: Na osii afɔrmbukyia wɔ hɔ, na ɔsɔɔr EWURADZE, na ɔbɔɔ n’asesee wɔ hɔ: na hɔ na Isaak no nkowaa tuu bura. Isaak Nye Abimelek Dzi Ahyɛmu

english_verse: Meanwhile, Abimelech had left Gerar and was taking his advisor Ahuzzath and his army commander Phicol to see Isaac.
translation_verse: Na Abimelek nye ne nyɛnko Ahuz-zat, nye ne safohen tsitsir Fikol, fi Gerar kɔr ne nkyɛn.

english_verse: When they arrived, Isaac asked, “Why are you here? Didn't you send me away because you hated me?”
translation_verse: Na Isaak bisaa hɔn dɛ, ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na hom aba mo hɔ yi, hwɛ dɛ hom tan me, na hom eyi me edzi wɔ hom nkyɛn?

english_verse: They answered, “We now know for certain that the Lord is with you, and we have decided there needs to be a peace treaty between you and us. So let's make a solemn agreement
translation_verse: Na wɔsee dɛ, Yeehu no kotoo dɛ, EWURADZE nye wo nam: na yɛsee dɛ, Ma ntam nda hɛn ntamu, mekyerɛ hɛn nye ɔwo ntamu a, na ma yɛnye wo ndzi ahyɛmu;

english_verse: not to harm each other. Remember, we have never hurt you, and when we sent you away, we let you go in peace. The Lord has truly blessed you.”
translation_verse: dɛ ennkɛyɛ hɛn bɔn bi, osiandɛ yɛanntsen hɛn nsa annka wo, na yɛnnyɛ wo hwee dɛ papa nko, na yeegya wo kwan wɔ asomdwee mu: osiandɛ ɔwo nye nyia EWURADZE ehyira no.

english_verse: Isaac gave a big feast for them, and everyone ate and drank.
translation_verse: Na ɔtoo hɔn pon, ma wodzidzii na wɔnomee.

english_verse: Early the next morning Isaac and the others made a solemn agreement, then he let them go in peace.
translation_verse: Na wɔsoɛɛr anapahema, na wodzii nsew kyerɛkyerɛɛ hɔnho: na Isaak gyaa hɔn kwan, na wɔdze asomdwee fii ne nkyɛn kɔree.

english_verse: So Isaac named the well Shibah, and the town is still called Beersheba. Esau's Foreign Wives
translation_verse: Na ɔfrɛɛ no Siba: dɛm ntsi kurow no ne dzin dze Beer-seba ɔdze bodu ndɛ da yi.

english_verse: But these two women brought a lot of grief to Esau's parents Isaac and Rebekah.
translation_verse: na wɔyɛɛ ma Isaak nye Rebeka dzi awerɛhow.

english_verse: Isaac was old and almost blind, when he called in his first-born son Esau, who asked him, “Father, what can I do for you?”
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ aber a Isaak yɛɛ akɔdaa, na n’enyi do yɛɛ no kusukusu, na onnhu adze yie no, ɔsomaa frɛɛ ne ba panyin Esau, na ɔsee no dɛ, Me ba: na ɔgyee no do dɛ, Egya Emi nye yi.

english_verse: Isaac replied, “I am old and might die at any time.
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Afei hwɛ, mayɛ akɔdaa, minnyim mo wu da.

english_verse: So go hunting with your bow and arrows and kill a wild animal.
translation_verse: Dɛm ntsi afei, meserɛ wo, fa w’ahayɛdze, w’egyen kotoku nye w’egyen, na kɔ hamu kɛfa hanam brɛ me;

english_verse: Cook some of that tasty food that I love so much and bring it to me. I want to eat it once more and give you my blessing before I die.”
translation_verse: na yɛ me edziban a ɔyɛ huam, dɛ mbrɛ mepɛ, na fa brɛ me, ma mindzi; na me kra nhyira wo ana mewu.

english_verse: Rebekah had been listening, and as soon as Esau left to go hunting,
translation_verse: Na aber a Isaak rekasa kyerɛ ne ba Esau no, Rebeka tsee. Na Esau kɔr hamu dɛ orukoku abowa, na ɔdze aba no.

english_verse: she said to Jacob, “I heard your father tell Esau
translation_verse: Rebeka kaa kyerɛɛ ne ba Jakob dɛ, Hwɛ, metsee dɛ w’egya reka kyerɛ wo nua Esau dɛ,

english_verse: to kill a wild animal and cook some tasty food for him before he dies. Your father said this because he wants to bless your brother with the Lord as his witness.
translation_verse: Kɛfa hanam brɛ me, na yɛ me edziban a ɔyɛ huam, ma mindzi, na minhyira wo wɔ EWURADZE enyim ansaana mewu.

english_verse: Now, my son, listen carefully to what I want you to do.
translation_verse: Dɛm ntsi afei, me ba, tsie me ndze dɛ mbrɛ merehyɛ wo yi ara.

english_verse: Go and kill two of your best young goats and bring them to me. I'll cook the tasty food that your father loves so much.
translation_verse: Kɔ mpɔnkyebuw no mu, na kɛkyer mpɔnkyemba a woye ebien wɔ hɔ brɛ me: na medze bɛyɛ edziban a ɔyɛ huam ama w’egya, dɛ mbrɛ ɔpɛ:

english_verse: Then you can take it to him, so he can eat it and give you his blessing before he dies.”
translation_verse: na edze akɛma no, ama oedzi, na oeehyira wo ansaana oewu.

english_verse: “My brother Esau is a hairy man,” Jacob reminded her. “And I am not.
translation_verse: Na Jakob see ne na Rebeka dɛ, Hwɛ, mo nua Esau no ho wɔ nhwi, na emi mo ho yɛ monomonom.

english_verse: If my father touches me and realizes I am trying to trick him, he will put a curse on me instead of giving me a blessing.”
translation_verse: Bi a m’egya bɛkeka mo ho ahwɛ, na mebɛyɛ n’enyi do dɛ ɔdaadaafo; na medze mpaa mebɛba mo do, na nnyɛ nhyira a.

english_verse: Rebekah insisted, “Let his curse fall on me! Just do what I say and bring me the meat.”
translation_verse: Na ne na see no dɛ, Wo mpaama mbra mo do, me ba: ɔwo tsie me ndze ara, na kɔ kɛfa hɔn brɛ me.

english_verse: So Jacob brought the meat to his mother, and she cooked the tasty food that his father liked.
translation_verse: Na ɔkɔree kɛfae, na ɔdze brɛɛ ne na: na ne na ɔdze yɛɛ edziban a ɔyɛ huam, dɛ mbrɛ n’egya pɛ.

english_verse: She also covered the smooth part of his hands and neck with goatskins
translation_verse: na ɔdze mpɔnkyemba no hɔn nwoma duraduraa ne nsa ho nye no kɔn monomonom no ho:

english_verse: and gave him some bread and the tasty food she had cooked.
translation_verse: na ɔdze edziban a ɔyɛ huam no nye boodoo a woesiesie no dze hyɛɛ ne ba Jakob nsa.

english_verse: Jacob went to his father and said, “Father, here I am.” “Which one of my sons are you?” his father asked.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa n’egya hɔ, na ɔfrɛɛ no dɛ, M’egya: na ɔsee dɛ, Emi nye yi. Woana nye wo, me ba?

english_verse: Jacob replied, “I am Esau, your first-born, and I have done what you told me. Please sit up and eat the meat I have brought. Then you can give me your blessing.”
translation_verse: Na Jakob see n’egya dɛ, Emi nye w’abakan Esau; mayɛ dɛ mbrɛ ekyerɛɛ me no: soɛr meserɛ wo, tsena hɔ na dzi me hanam yi bi, ama wo kra eehyira me.

english_verse: Isaac asked, “My son, how did you find an animal so quickly?” “The Lord your God was kind to me,” Jacob answered.
translation_verse: Na Isaak bisaa ne ba no dɛ, ɔyɛ dɛn ma wo nsa kaa no ntsɛm dɛm, me ba? Na obuaa dɛ, Osiandɛ EWURADZE wo Nyankopɔn maa mihyiaa yie.

english_verse: “My son,” Isaac said, “come closer, where I can touch you and find out if you really are Esau.”
translation_verse: Na Isaak see Jakob dɛ, Pin ha, meserɛ wo, ma menkeka wo monhwɛ, me ba, kyerɛ dɛ me ba Esau noara nye wo anaa.

english_verse: Jacob went closer. His father touched him and said, “You sound like Jacob, but your hands feel hairy like Esau's.”
translation_verse: Na Jakob piin n’egya Isaak nkyɛn; na ɔkekaa no ho hwɛe, na ɔsee dɛ, Ndze no yɛ Jakob ne ndze, na nsa no na ɔyɛ Esau ne nsa.

english_verse: Isaac asked, “Are you really my son Esau?” “Yes, I am,” Jacob answered.
translation_verse: Na obisaa dɛ, Ana ɔwo nye me ba Esau ara? Na ɔsee dɛ, Emi nye no.

english_verse: So Isaac told him, “Serve me the wild meat, and I can give you my blessing.” Jacob gave him some meat, and he ate it. He also gave him some wine, and he drank it.
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Fa pin me nkyɛn, ma mindzi me ba ne hanam yi bi, ma me kra nhyira wo. Na ɔdze piin ne nkyɛn, ma odzii: na ɔdze nsa brɛɛ no, ma ɔnomee.

english_verse: Then Isaac said, “Son, come over here and kiss me.”
translation_verse: Na n’egya Isaak see no dɛ, Afei pin ha, na bɛfew m’ano, me ba.

english_verse: While Jacob was kissing him, Isaac caught the smell of his clothes and said: “The smell of my son is like a field the Lord has blessed.
translation_verse: Na ɔtwee pinii feew n’ano: na Isaak ohuaa n’atar no huam, na ohyiraa no, na ɔsee dɛ, Hwɛ, me ba ho hua Tse dɛ haban a EWURADZE ehyira do no mu hua:

english_verse: God will bless you, my son, with dew from heaven and with fertile fields, rich with grain and grapes.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn mma wo sor bɔsu bi, Nye asaase ne seradze bi, Nye eburow nye nsa pii:

english_verse: Nations will be your servants and bow down to you. You will rule over your brothers, and they will kneel at your feet. Anyone who curses you will be cursed; anyone who blesses you will be blessed.”
translation_verse: Aman nsom wo, Na amanaman nkotow wo: Yɛ wo nuanom do wura, Na wo na mba nkotow wo: Ndom nka hɔn a wɔdom wo nyinara, Na nhyira nka hɔn a wohyira wo nyinara. Esau Bisa Nhyira

english_verse: Right after Isaac had given Jacob his blessing and Jacob had gone, Esau came back from hunting.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ, Isaak wiee Jakob nhyira no, na Jakob fii n’egya nkyɛn firii ara pɛ nna no nua Esau fi n’ahayɛ baa fie.

english_verse: He cooked the tasty food, brought it to his father, and said, “Father, please sit up and eat the meat I have brought you, so you can give me your blessing.”
translation_verse: Na ɔno so yɛɛ edziban a ɔyɛ huam, na ɔdze brɛɛ n’egya; na ɔsee n’egya dɛ, Wɔmma m’egya nsoɛr, na ondzi ne ba ne hanam yi bi, ma ne kra nhyira me

english_verse: “Who are you?” Isaac asked. “I am Esau, your first-born son.”
translation_verse: Na n’egya Isaak bisaa no dɛ, Woana nye wo? Na ɔsee no dɛ, Mara wo ba, w’abakan Esau a.

english_verse: Isaac started trembling and said, “Then who brought me some wild meat right before you came in? I ate it and gave him a blessing that cannot be taken back.”
translation_verse: Na Isaak no ho popoo kɛsenara, na ɔsee dɛ, Woana mbɔ na ɔakɛfa hanam, na ɔdze abrɛ me, ma medzi ne nyinara bi ansaana ereba, na mehyira no yi? Ampa, mehyiraa no.

english_verse: Isaac answered, “Your brother tricked me and stole your blessing.”
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Wo nua dze ndaadaa aba abɛgye wo nhyira kɔ.

english_verse: Esau replied, “My brother deserves the name Jacob, because he has already cheated me twice. The first time he cheated me out of my rights as the first-born son, and now he has cheated me out of my blessing.” Then Esau asked his father, “Don't you have any blessing left for me?”
translation_verse: Na Esau see dɛ, Ana ɔno wɔmmfrɛ no Jakob bi? Iyi ka ho a oedzi me emim mpɛn ebien: ɔagye me panyin; na, hwɛ, afei ɔagye me nhyira. Na obisaa dɛ, Ana enngyaa me nhyira biara?

english_verse: “My son,” Isaac answered, “I have made Jacob the ruler over you and your brothers, and all of you will be his servants. I have also promised him all the grain and grapes that he needs. There's nothing left that I can do for you.”
translation_verse: Na Isaak buae na ɔsee Esau dɛ. Hwɛ mayɛ no wo wura, na medze no nuanom nyinara mayɛ no nkowaa; na eburow nye nsa nna medze awowaw no: na ɔwo dze, ebɛnadze na menyɛ mma wo, me ba?

english_verse: “Father,” Esau asked, “don't you have more than one blessing? You can surely give me a blessing too!” Then Esau started crying again.
translation_verse: Na Esau bisaa n’egya dɛ, Aso nhyira kor pɛ na ewɔ, m’egya? Hyira mo so, mo so bi, ao m’egya. Na Esau maa ne ndze do na osui.

english_verse: So his father said: “Your home will be far from that fertile land, where dew comes down from the heavens.
translation_verse: Na n’egya Isaak buae na ɔsee no dɛ, Hwɛ, mbrɛ asaase a seradze nnyi no na ɔbɛyɛ wo tsenabea, Na mbrɛ ɔsor ɔbɔsu yɛ hɔ na;

english_verse: You will live by the power of your sword and be your brother's slave. But when you decide to be free, you will break loose.”
translation_verse: Na edze w’afona bɛtsena ase, na ebɔsom wo nua; Na ɔbɛba dɛ sɛ egye woho a, ɔno ibeyi no kɔndua wɔ wo kɔn ho.

english_verse: Esau hated his brother Jacob because he had stolen the blessing that was supposed to be his. So he said to himself, “Just as soon as my father dies, I'll kill Jacob.”
translation_verse: Na Esau taan Jakob wɔ nhyira a n’egya dze hyiraa no ntsi: na Esau see n’akoma mu dɛ, Nda a wɔdze bosu m’egya no abɛn; afei muruboku mo nua Jakob.

english_verse: When Rebekah found out what Esau planned to do, she sent for Jacob and told him, “Son, your brother Esau is just waiting for a chance to kill you.
translation_verse: Na ne ba panyin Esua ne nsɛm no wɔkɛkaa kyerɛɛ Rebeka; na ɔsomae ma wɔkɛfrɛɛ ne ba kuma Jakob, na ɔsee no dɛ, Hwɛ, wo nua Esau kyekye no werɛ wɔ wo ho dɛ oruboku wo.

english_verse: Now listen carefully and do what I say. Go to the home of my brother Laban in Haran
translation_verse: Dɛm ntsi afei, me ba, tsie me ndze; na soɛr, guan kɔ mo nua Laban nkyɛn wɔ Haran;

english_verse: and stay with him for a while. When Esau stops being angry
translation_verse: na kɛtsena ne nkyɛn nda kakra, kɛpem dɛ wo nua n’ebufuw befi no mu;

english_verse: and forgets what you have done to him, I'll send for you to come home. Why should I lose both of my sons on the same day?”
translation_verse: na no werɛ befir dza edze ayɛ no no; na mobɔsoma ma wɔaabɛfa wo wɔ hɔ aba: bɛyɛ dɛn ntsi na mobɔhwer hom beenu nyina da kor?

english_verse: Rebekah later told Isaac, “Those Hittite wives of Esau are making my life miserable! If Jacob marries a Hittite woman, I'd be better off dead.”
translation_verse: Na Rebeka see Isaak dɛ, Mo nkwa afon me, osian Het mbabaa ntsi: sɛ Jakob war yer wɔ Het mbabaa mu, dɛ woyinom a, wɔ asaase yi do mbabaa yi mu a, nna membrɛe ngui bi? Nna metse ase reyɛ dɛn?

english_verse: Isaac called in Jacob, then gave him a blessing, and said: Don't marry any of those Canaanite women.
translation_verse: Na Isaak frɛɛ Jakob, na ohyiraa no, na ɔhyɛɛ no, na ɔsee no dɛ, Mma nnwar yer wɔ Keenan mbabaa mu.

english_verse: Go at once to your mother's father Bethuel in northern Syria and choose a wife from one of the daughters of Laban, your mother's brother.
translation_verse: Soɛr, kɔ Paddan-aram, wo na n’egya Betuel fie; na kɔwar yer wɔ wo na no nua Laban ne mbabaa no hɔn mu.

english_verse: I pray that God All-Powerful will bless you with many descendants and let you become a great nation.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn Otumfo nhyira wo, na ɔmma wo, na dɔr, ama aayɛ aman asafo;

english_verse: May he bless you with the land he promised Abraham, so that you will take over this land where we now live as foreigners.
translation_verse: na ɔmfa Abraham nhyira mma ɔwo nye w’asefo a wɔka wo ho; ama eenya asaase a eyɛ do hɔho a Nyankopɔn dze maa Abraham yi.

english_verse: Isaac then sent Jacob to stay with Rebekah's brother Laban, the son of Bethuel the Aramean. Esau Marries the Daughter of Ishmael
translation_verse: Na Isaak gyaa Jakob kwan: na ɔkɔr Sirianyi Betuel ne ba Laban a ɔyɛ Jakob nye Esau hɔn na Rebeka no nuabanyin no nkyɛn wɔ Paddan-aram.

english_verse: Esau found out that his father Isaac had blessed Jacob and had warned him not to marry any of the Canaanite women. He also learned that Jacob had been sent to find a wife in northern Syria
translation_verse: Afei Esau hun dɛ Isaak ehyira Jakob, na oegya no kwan dɛ ɔnkɔ Paddan-Aram nkɔwar yer wɔ hɔ; na orihyira no no ɔhyɛɛ no dɛ, Mma nnwar yer wɔ Keenan mbabaa hɔn mu;

english_verse: and that he had obeyed his father and mother.
translation_verse: ma Jakob etsie n’egya nye ne na, na ɔkɔ Paddan-aram.

english_verse: Esau already had several wives, but he now realized how much his father hated the Canaanite women.
translation_verse: Na Esau hun dɛ Keenan mbabaa no nnyɛ n’egya Isaak enyisɔ no;

english_verse: So he married Ishmael's daughter Mahalath, who was the sister of Nebaioth and the granddaughter of Abraham. Jacob's Dream at Bethel
translation_verse: osii mu kɔr Ismael nkyɛn, na ɔkɔwaar Abraham ne ba Ismael ne babaa Mahalat a ɔyɛ Nebaiot n’akyerɛbaa, kaa no yernom no ho. Jakob N’adaaso Wɔ Bɛtɛl

english_verse: Jacob left the town of Beersheba and started out for Haran.
translation_verse: Na Jakob fii Beer-seba, na osii mu kɔr Haran afamu.

english_verse: At sunset he stopped for the night and went to sleep, resting his head on a large rock.
translation_verse: Na ɔsoɛɛ bea bi do, na ɔtsenaa hɔ anafua no nyinara, osiandɛ iwi akɔtɔ, na ɔfaa hɔ abo no kor na ɔdze ne tsir sunii, na ɔdaa hɔ.

english_verse: In a dream he saw a ladder that reached from earth to heaven, and God's angels were going up and down on it.
translation_verse: Na ɔsoo daa, na hwɛ atwer si asaase do, na n’etsifi pem sor; Nyankopɔn n’abɔfoe ridzi do mfowsian.

english_verse: The Lord was standing beside the ladder and said: I am the Lord God who was worshiped by Abraham and Isaac. I will give to you and your family the land on which you are now sleeping.
translation_verse: Na, hwɛ, EWURADZE gyina n’etsifi, na ɔsee dɛ, Emi nye EWURADZE, w’egya Abraham ne Nyankopɔn, na Isaak ne Nyankopɔn: asaase a eda do no, medze mebɛma ɔwo, nye w’asefo.

english_verse: Your descendants will spread over the earth in all directions and will become as numerous as the specks of dust. Your family will be a blessing to all people.
translation_verse: Na w’asefo bɛyɛ dɛ asaase do huntuma, na ebɛtserɛw akɔ anee, boka, nye kwaamu, nye pomu: na ɔwo nye w’asefo mu na wobehyira asaase do ebusua ebusua nyinara.

english_verse: Wherever you go, I will watch over you, then later I will bring you back to this land. I won't leave you—I will do all I have promised.
translation_verse: Na, hwɛ, menye wo nam, na mobɔhwɛ wo do wɔ beebiara a ebɔkɔ, na medze wo maba asaase yi do bio; na minnkegya wo hɔ, ekodu dɛ mebɛma dza maka makyerɛ wo yi ayɛ hɔ.

english_verse: Jacob woke up suddenly and thought, “The Lord is in this place, and I didn't even know it.”
translation_verse: Na Jakob fii ne nda mu nyanee, na ɔsee dɛ, Ampaara, EWURADZE wɔ ha; naaso nna minnyim.

english_verse: Then Jacob became frightened and said, “What a frightening place! It must be the house of God and the gateway to heaven.”
translation_verse: Na n’akoma tui, na ɔsee dɛ, ha yi yɛ hu ampa ae! Ha nnyɛ beebiara dɛ Nyankopɔn ne fie a, na ha nye hɛben abotsen no.

english_verse: When Jacob got up early the next morning, he took the rock that he had used for a pillow and stood it up as a place of worship. Then he poured olive oil on the rock to dedicate it to God,
translation_verse: Na Jakob soɛɛr anapaatu, na ɔfaa ɔbo no a osunii no, na ɔdze yɛɛ idum sii hɔ, na ohuee ngo guu do.

english_verse: and he named the place Bethel. Before that it had been named Luz.
translation_verse: Na ɔfrɛɛ bea no ne dzin Bɛtɛl: na asɔpɔn no nkyɛ kurow no ne dzin dze Luz.

english_verse: Jacob solemnly promised God, “If you go with me and watch over me as I travel, and if you give me food and clothes
translation_verse: Na Jakob bɔɔ anohoba see dɛ, Sɛ Nyankopɔn dzi m’ekyir, na ɔhwɛ mo do wɔ ɔkwan yi a murutu yi mu, na ɔma me edziban ma midzi, nye atar ma mehyɛ,

english_verse: and bring me safely home again, you will be my God.
translation_verse: ma meba m’egya fie bio wɔ asomdwee mu a, ɔno EWURADZE bɛyɛ me Nyankopɔn,

english_verse: This rock will be your house, and I will give back to you a tenth of everything you give me.”
translation_verse: na ɔbo yi a medze mayɛ idum esi hɔ yi, bɛyɛ Nyankopɔn ne fi: na dza ebɛma me nyinara mebɛma wo mu ebupɛn du du.

english_verse: As Jacob continued on his way to the east,
translation_verse: Na Jakob sii no kwan do, na ɔbaa bokamba hɔn asaase do.

english_verse: he looked out in a field and saw a well where shepherds took their sheep for water. Three flocks of sheep were lying around the well, which was covered with a large rock.
translation_verse: Na ɔhwɛe no, nna ohun dɛ, bura bi wɔ haban no mu, na, hwɛ, nguankuw ebiasa gugu ho wɔ hɔ; osiandɛ bura no no mu nsu na wɔsaw ma nguankuw no ma wɔnom: na ɔbo a ɔda bura no n’ano no so.
