english_verse: Hirah asked the people of Enaim, “Where is the prostitute who sat along the road outside your town?” “There's never been one here,” they answered.
translation_verse: Nna obisaa no kuromfo no dɛ, edwaman a ɔtse Enaim kwan nkyɛn no wɔ hen? Na wɔsee dɛ, Edwaman biara mmbaa ha.

english_verse: Hirah went back and told Judah, “I couldn't find the woman, and the people of Enaim said no prostitute had ever been there.”
translation_verse: Na ɔsanee baa Juda hɔ, na ɔbɛsee dɛ, Mennhu no; na eso ekuromfo no see dɛ, Edwaman biara mmbaa ha.

english_verse: “If you couldn't find her, we'll just let her keep the things I gave her,” Judah answered. “And we'd better forget about the goat, or else we'll look like fools.”
translation_verse: Na Juda see dɛ, Ma ɔmfa, ɔno bohu na hɛn enyim egu ase: hwɛ, memaa wɔdze apɔnkyeba yi kɔree, na ennhu no.

english_verse: About three months later someone told Judah, “Your daughter-in-law Tamar has behaved like a prostitute, and now she's pregnant!” “Drag her out of town and burn her to death!” Judah shouted.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ odzii bɛyɛ abosoom ebiasa n’ekyir no, wɔkaa kyerɛɛ Juda dɛ, W’asew Tamar abɔ edwaman, na hwɛ, ɔnam n’edwaman mu enyinsen so. Na Juda see dɛ, Hom nkɛfa no mbra ma wɔnhyew no.

english_verse: As Tamar was being dragged off, she sent someone to tell her father-in-law, “The man who gave me this ring, this cord, and this walking stick is the one who got me pregnant.”
translation_verse: Na wɔkɛfaa no bae no, nna Tamar somae ma wɔkɛsee n’asew no dɛ, banyin a ndzɛmba yi yɛ nedze no, ɔno na menye no nyinsen: ntsi, Meserɛ wo, hwehwɛ nyia ndzɛmba yi yɛ nedze, nsɔwanokaba, nye ahoma, nye poma yi.

english_verse: “Those are mine!” Judah admitted. “She's a better person than I am, because I broke my promise to let her marry my son Shelah.” After this, Judah never slept with her again.
translation_verse: Na Juda hun dɛ noara nedze a, na ɔsee dɛ, ɔtsen sen me; susuampa dɛ mammfa no amma me ba Sela ntsi. Na ɔnye no annda bio.

english_verse: Tamar later gave birth to twins. But before either of them was born, one of them stuck a hand out of her womb. The woman who was helping tied a red thread around the baby's hand and explained, “This one came out first.”
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ aber a ɔrowo no, nna kor dze ne nsa puei; na awogyefo no faa ahoma memen dze kyekyeer ne nsa dɛ, Oyi na odzii kan puei.

english_verse: At once his hand went back in, and the other child was born first. The woman then said, “What an opening you've made for yourself!” So they named the baby Perez.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ, ɔtwee ne nsa kɔdɔɔ mu, na, hwɛ, no nua puei; na ɔwogyefo no see dɛ, ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na atsew mu aba yi? Dɛm ntsi wɔfrɛɛ ne dzin Perez.

english_verse: When the brother with the red thread was born, they named him Zerah.
translation_verse: Na n’ekyir no na nyia ahoma memen kyekyer ne nsa no bae: na wɔfrɛɛ no Zera.

english_verse: The Ishmaelites took Joseph to Egypt and sold him to Potiphar, the king's official in charge of the palace guard.
translation_verse: Na wɔdze Josef kɔr Egypt, na Potifar, Farao no somfo a ɔyɛ n’abrafohen no, tɔɔ no wɔ Ismaelfo a wɔdze no kɔr hɔ no hɔn nsamu.

english_verse: So Joseph lived in the home of Potiphar, his Egyptian owner. Soon Potiphar realized that the Lord was helping Joseph to be successful in whatever he did.
translation_verse: Na no wura hun dɛ EWURADZE nye no nam, na EWURADZE maa dza ɔyɛɛ no nyinara yɛɛ prɔmprɔm wɔ ne nsamu.

english_verse: Potiphar liked Joseph and made him his personal assistant, putting him in charge of his house and all of his property.
translation_verse: Na Josef nyaa n’enyim enyimnyam, na ɔsoom no: na ɔyɛɛ no panyin wɔ ne fie, na ɔdze dza ɔwɔ nyinara hyɛɛ ne nsa.

english_verse: Potiphar left everything up to Joseph, and with Joseph there, the only decision he had to make was what he wanted to eat. Joseph was well-built and handsome,
translation_verse: Na ɔdze dza ɔwɔ nyinara hyɛɛ Josef ne nsa; nna noara nnyim dza ɔwɔ no hɔ gyedɛ edziban a ɔnoara dzi. Nna Josef no ho yɛ fɛw, na n’enyim wɔ nyam.

english_verse: and Potiphar's wife soon noticed him. She asked him to make love to her,
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ iyinom ekyir no, no wura ne yer no dze n’enyi sii Josef do; na ɔsee dɛ, Enye me nda.

english_verse: but he refused and said, “My master isn't worried about anything in his house, because he has placed me in charge of everything he owns.
translation_verse: Na ɔpowee, na ɔsee no wura ne yer no dɛ, Hwɛ, mo wura nnyim dza ɔwɔ mo hɔ wɔ fie ha, na ɔdze dza ɔwɔ nyinara ahyɛ me nsa;

english_verse: No one in my master's house is more important than I am. The only thing he hasn't given me is you, and that's because you are his wife. I won't sin against God by doing such a terrible thing as this.”
translation_verse: obiara nnyɛ kɛse nnkyɛn me wɔ fie ha; nkyii so ɔnnkam me biribiara ka wo ho, osiandɛ eyɛ ne yer: na ɔbɛyɛ dɛn na mebɛyɛ emumuyɛ kɛse yi, na mayɛ bɔn etsia Nyankopɔn?

english_verse: She kept begging Joseph day after day, but he refused to do what she wanted or even to go near her.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ, daa daa nyinara ɔkaa kyerɛɛ Josef, naaso ɔanngye no pen, dɛ ɔnye no bɛda, anaa ɔnye no bɛyɛ biribi.

english_verse: One day, Joseph went to Potiphar's house to do his work, and none of the other servants were there.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ aber yi mu no ɔkɔr fie hɔ dɛ ɔrekɛyɛ n’edwuma; nna fie no mu nyimpa no mu biara nnyi hɔ

english_verse: Potiphar's wife grabbed hold of his coat and said, “Make love to me!” Joseph ran out of the house, leaving his coat there in her hands.
translation_verse: na basia no suoo n’atar mu, dɛ, Enye me nda: na Josef gyaa n’atar no wɔ ne nsamu, na oguanee puei.

english_verse: she called in her servants and said, “Look! This Hebrew has come just to make fools of us. He tried to rape me, but I screamed for help.
translation_verse: ɔfrɛfrɛɛ no nkorɔfo a wɔwɔ ne fie no, na ɔkaa kyerɛɛ hɔn dɛ, Wɔnhwɛ, ɔakɛfa Hebriwnyi aba ma oribesi hɛn atwetwe; ɔbaa me nkyɛn dɛ ɔnye me rebɛda, na medze ndze kɛse kekaa mu:

english_verse: And when he heard me scream, he ran out of the house, leaving his coat with me.”
translation_verse: na ɔbaa dɛ aber a ɔtsee dɛ memaa me ndze do na mekekaa mu no, nna ɔdaan n’atar gyaa me nkyɛn, na oguanee puei.

english_verse: Potiphar's wife kept Joseph's coat until her husband came home.
translation_verse: Na ɔdze n’atar no guu ne nkyɛn ara ma okodur dɛ no wura baa fie.

english_verse: Then she said, “That Hebrew slave of yours tried to rape me!
translation_verse: Na ɔkaa no dɛmara kyerɛɛ no dɛ, Akowaa Hebriwnyi a edze no brɛɛ hɛn no, ɔbaa me nkyɛn dɛ orubogu mo ho fi

english_verse: But when I screamed for help, he left his coat and ran out of the house.”
translation_verse: na ɔbaa dɛ, memaa me ndze do, na mekekaa mu no, nna ɔdaan n’atar gyaa me nkyɛn, na oguanee puei.

english_verse: and threw Joseph in the same prison where the king's prisoners were kept. While Joseph was in prison,
translation_verse: Na Josef no wura faa no dze no kɔtoo efiadze, beebi mbrɛ ɔhen n’adaduafo gu no: na ɔdaa efiadze hɔ.

english_verse: the Lord helped him and was good to him. He even made the jailer like Joseph so much that
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE dzii Josef n’ekyir, na ɔdoom no, na ɔmaa onyaa efiadzehwɛfo no n’enyim enyimnyam.

english_verse: he put him in charge of the other prisoners and of everything that was done in the jail.
translation_verse: Na efiadzehwɛfo no dze adaduafo a wɔwɔ efiadze hɔ no nyinara hyɛɛ Josef ne nsa; na biribiara a wɔyɛɛ no wɔ hɔ no, ɔnoara na ɔhwɛe.

english_verse: The jailer did not worry about anything, because the Lord was with Joseph and made him successful in all that he did.
translation_verse: Efiadzehwɛfo no annhaw no ho wɔ dza ɔdze hyɛɛ ne nsa mu no mu biara ho, osiandɛ EWURADZE nye no nam; na biribiara a ɔyɛe no, EWURADZE maa ɔyɛɛ prɔmprɔm.

english_verse: While Joseph was in prison, both the king's personal servant and his chief cook made the king angry. So he had them thrown into the same prison with Joseph.
translation_verse: Na ɔdze hɔn koguu efiadze wɔ abrafohen no ne fie, wɔ efiadze beebi mbrɛ Josef da no no mu.

english_verse: They spent a long time in prison, and the official in charge of the palace guard, made Joseph their servant.
translation_verse: Na abrafohen no dze hɔn hyɛɛ Josef ne nsa, na ɔsoom hɔn: na wɔkyɛɛr efiadze hɔ mber kakra.

english_verse: One night each of the two men had a dream, but their dreams had different meanings.
translation_verse: Na hɔn beenu nyina sosoo daa, anafua da kor a ɔsor obiara ne daa, nye ne nkyerɛasee.

english_verse: The next morning, when Joseph went to see the men, he could tell they were upset,
translation_verse: Na Josef baa hɔn nkyɛn anapa no, obohun hɔn dɛ wɔabombɔm.

english_verse: “We each had a dream last night,” they answered, “and there is no one to tell us what they mean.” Joseph replied, “Doesn't God know the meaning of dreams? Now tell me what you dreamed.”
translation_verse: Na wɔsee no dɛ, Yɛasoso daa, naaso yennya obi ma ɔnnkyerɛ hɛn ase. Na Josef see hɔn dɛ, Ana nnyɛ Nyankopɔn na ɔma nkyerɛase bi a? Meserɛ hom, hom nka nkyerɛ me.

english_verse: The king's personal servant told Joseph, “In my dream I saw a vine
translation_verse: Na nsahyɛfo panyin no kaa n’adaaso kyerɛɛ Josef, na ɔsee no dɛ, M’adaaso mu, hwɛ, wenyindua si m’enyim;

english_verse: with three branches. As soon as it budded, it blossomed, and its grapes became ripe.
translation_verse: na nkorbata ebiasa wɔ wenyindua no ho: na ɔyɛ dɛ ɔafefɛw, na ɔabɔ nhyiren; na no do nsiansiaw asow wenyinamba a ɔaber:

english_verse: I held the king's cup and squeezed the grapes into it, then I gave the cup to the king.”
translation_verse: na Farao no kɔɔpow kitsa me nsamu, na metsetseew wenyinamba no, na mikyii guu Farao no kɔɔpow mu, na medze kɔɔpow no hyɛɛ Farao ne nsa.

english_verse: Joseph said: This is the meaning of your dream. The three branches stand for three days,
translation_verse: Na Josef see no dɛ, Ne nkyerɛase nye yi; nkorbata ebiasa no ɔyɛ ndaansa.

english_verse: and in three days the king will pardon you. He will make you his personal servant again, and you will serve him his wine, just as you used to do.
translation_verse: Aka ndaansa ma Farao eyi wo efi ha na ɔama ekedzi wo dzibew bio: na edze Farao no kɔɔpow bɛhyɛ ne nsa, dɛ nkyɛ kan no a eyɛ ne nsahyɛfo no.

english_verse: But when these good things happen, please don't forget to tell the king about me, so I can get out of this place.
translation_verse: Na sɛ inya beebi si a, ɔno kaa me, na yɛ me ayamuyie, meserɛ wo, na bɔ me dzin kyerɛ Farao, na beyi me fi dan yi mu:

english_verse: I was kidnapped from the land of the Hebrews, and here in Egypt I haven't done anything to deserve being thrown in jail.
translation_verse: na owia na wowiaa me wɔ Hebriwfo asaase do: na ha so mennyɛɛ biribiara a ɔsɛ dɛ wɔdze me to efiadze.

english_verse: When the chief cook saw that Joseph had given a good meaning to the dream, he told Joseph, “I also had a dream. In it I was carrying three breadbaskets stacked on top of my head.
translation_verse: Na aber a paanootofo panyin no hun dɛ nkyerɛase no ye no, ɔsee Josef dɛ, Mo so m’adaaso mu no, muhun dɛ paanoo fufuw nkɛntsɛn ebiasa sisi m’etsifi:

english_verse: The top basket was full of all kinds of baked things for the king, but birds were eating them.”
translation_verse: na kɛntsɛn a odzi sor no adɔkɔdɔkɔdze a wɔto ma Farao nyinara bi wɔ mu; na nnoma ridzi no kɛntsɛn no mu wɔ m’etsifi.

english_verse: Joseph said: This is the meaning of your dream. The three baskets are three days,
translation_verse: Na Josef buae na ɔsee dɛ, Ne nkyerɛase nye yi: nkɛntsɛn ebiasa no yɛ ndaansa:

english_verse: and in three days the king will cut off your head. He will hang your body on a pole, and birds will come and peck at it.
translation_verse: na aka ndaansa ma Farao eyi wo tsir wɔ wo do, na wɔasɛn wo wɔ dua do ma nnoma edzi wo nam efi wo ho.

english_verse: Three days later, while the king was celebrating his birthday with a dinner for his officials, he sent for his personal servant and the chief cook.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ ne ndaansa no a ɔyɛ Farao n’awoda no, ɔtoo pon maa no nkowaa nyinara: na oyii nsahyɛfo panyin no nye paanootofo panyin no fii efiadze dze hɔn baa no nkowaa no enyim.

english_verse: He put the personal servant back in his old job
translation_verse: Na ɔmaa nsahyɛfo panyin no sanee yɛɛ ne nsahyɛdwuma no bio; na ɔdze kɔɔpow no hyɛɛ Farao ne nsa:

english_verse: and had the cook put to death. Everything happened just as Joseph had said it would,
translation_verse: na paanootofo panyin no dze, ɔsɛɛn no: dɛ mbrɛ Josef kyerɛɛ hɔn ase no ara.

english_verse: but the king's personal servant completely forgot about Joseph.
translation_verse: Naaso nsahyɛfo panyin no annkaa Josef, na no werɛ fiir no.

english_verse: Two years later the king of Egypt dreamed he was standing beside the Nile River.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ mfe ebien ekyir no, Farao soo daa: na ohun dɛ nna ogyina esutsen Nael no ho.

english_verse: Suddenly, seven fat, healthy cows came up from the river and started eating grass along the bank.
translation_verse: Na ohun dɛ anantwiber esuon a hɔn ho yɛ fɛw na wɔadɔ seradze, fi esutsen no mu puei; na wobedzidzii wɔ sar no do.

english_verse: The king went back to sleep and had another dream. This time seven full heads of grain were growing on a single stalk.
translation_verse: Na ɔdae na ɔsoo daa bio: na ohun dɛ, eburow mbɛtsem esuon efifir eburonan kor do, amaama na oye.

english_verse: Later, seven other heads of grain appeared, but they were thin and scorched by a wind from the desert.
translation_verse: Na hwɛ, mbɛtsem esuon a ɔyɛ nkɔkɔr na boka mframa enyinyam no fifirii dzii hɔn ekyir.

english_verse: The thin heads of grain swallowed the seven full heads. Again the king woke up, and it had only been a dream.
translation_verse: Na mbɛtsem nkɔkɔr no memeen mbɛtsem esuon a eburow wɔ mu na ɔyɛ amaama no. Na Farao nyanee, na, hwɛ, adaaso a.

english_verse: The next morning the king was upset. So he called in his magicians and wise men and told them what he had dreamed. None of them could tell him what the dreams meant.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ anapa no, n’ahom annka noho; na ɔsomae ma wɔkɛfrɛfrɛɛ Egypt nkongyaayifo nyinara, nye enyansafo a wɔwɔ hɔ nyinara ma wɔbae: na Farao kaa n’adaaso no kyerɛɛ hɔn; naaso obiara enntum annkyerɛ ase annkyerɛ no.

english_verse: The king's personal servant said: Now I remember what I was supposed to do.
translation_verse: Nna nsahyɛfo panyin no kaa kyerɛɛ Farao dɛ, Mekaa mo mfom ndɛ:

english_verse: When you were angry with me and your chief cook, you threw us both in jail in the house of the captain of the guard.
translation_verse: Farao no bo fuuw no nkowaa, emi nye paanootofo panyin no: na ɔdze hɛn koguu efiadze wɔ abrafohen no ne fie,

english_verse: One night we both had dreams, and each dream had a different meaning.
translation_verse: na yɛsosoo daa anafua da kor, emi nye paanotonyi no; a ɔsor obiara ne daa nye mbrɛ ne nkyerɛase tse.

english_verse: A young Hebrew, who was a servant of the captain of the guard, was there with us at the time. When we told him our dreams, he explained what each of them meant,
translation_verse: Na yɛkaa kyerɛɛ Hebriwnyi aberantsɛ bi, abrafohen no n’akowaa a nna ɔnye hɛn wɔ hɔ no, na ɔkyerɛɛ hɛn daa no ase; dɛ mbrɛ ɔbaako ne daa tse no ara na ɔkyerɛɛ no ase.

english_verse: and everything happened just as he said it would. I got my job back, and the cook was put to death.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ ɔyɛɛ hɔ dɛ mbrɛ ɔkyerɛɛ hɛn ase no ara; emi emaa mikedzii me dzibew, na ɔno esɛɛn no.

english_verse: The king sent for Joseph, who was quickly brought out of jail. He shaved, changed his clothes, and went to the king.
translation_verse: Na Farao somae ma wɔkɛfrɛɛ Josef, na wɔyɛɛ ntsɛm dze no fii efiadze no mu hɔ puei: na osusuae, na ɔsesaa n’atar, na ɔbaa Farao hɔ.

english_verse: The king said to him, “I had a dream, yet no one can explain what it means. I am told that you can interpret dreams.”
translation_verse: Na Farao see Josef dɛ, Maso daa, na obiara nnyi hɔ a otum kyerɛ ase: na ɔwo na matse wo nka dɛ, etse daa a, ekyerɛ ase.

english_verse: “Your Majesty,” Joseph answered, “I can't do it myself, but God can give a good meaning to your dreams.”
translation_verse: Na Josef buaa Farao dɛ, Onnyi mo mu: Nyankopɔn na ɔbɛma Farao asomdwee mbuae.

english_verse: The king told Joseph: I dreamed I was standing on the bank of the Nile River.
translation_verse: Na Farao kaa kyerɛɛ Josef dɛ, M’adaaso mu no, hwɛ, nna migyina esutsen Nael no kɔn do:

english_verse: I saw seven fat, healthy cows come up out of the river, and they began feeding on the grass.
translation_verse: na muhun dɛ anantwiber esuon a wɔadɔ seradze na hɔn ho yɛ fɛw fi nsu no mu bae; na wobedzidzii sar no do:

english_verse: Next, seven skinny, bony cows came up out of the river. I have never seen such terrible looking cows anywhere in Egypt.
translation_verse: na hwɛ, anantwiber esuon afofor so dzi hɔn ekyir bae a wɔyɛ mbɔbɔr na hɔn ho tantanntan nkamfenkamfe, a munnhun hɔn ho ne tantan ne saso wɔ Egypt asaase nyinara do da:

english_verse: But you couldn't tell it, because these skinny cows were just as skinny as they were before. At once, I woke up.
translation_verse: Na aber a wowiee hɔn memen no, obi ennhu dɛ woewie hɔn men: osiandɛ nna hɔn ho da ho ara yɛ tantan dɛ nkan no ara so bio. Nna minyanee.

english_verse: I also dreamed that I saw seven heads of grain growing on one stalk. The heads were full and ripe.
translation_verse: Na muhun wɔ m’adaaso mu bio, na hwɛ, mbɛtsem esuon efifir eburonan kor do, amaama na oye:

english_verse: Then seven other heads of grain came up. They were thin and scorched by a wind from the desert.
translation_verse: na muhun dɛ mbɛtsem esuon a enyinyam, na ɔyɛ nkɔkɔr, na boka mframa ama woetwintwam fifirii, dzii hɔn ekyir:

english_verse: These heads of grain swallowed the full ones. I told my dreams to the magicians, but none of them could tell me the meaning of the dreams.
translation_verse: na mbɛtsem esuon nkɔkɔr no memeen mbɛtsem esuon a oye no. Mekaa kyerɛɛ nkongyaayifo no; naaso obiara nnyi hɔ a otumii kyerɛɛ me ase.

english_verse: Joseph replied: Your Majesty, both of your dreams mean the same thing, and in them God has shown what he is going to do.
translation_verse: Na Josef see Farao dɛ, Farao n’adaaso ebien no yɛ kor: dza Nyankopɔn rebɛyɛ na oeyi akyerɛ Farao no.

english_verse: The seven good cows stand for seven years, and so do the seven good heads of grain.
translation_verse: Anantwiber pa esuon no yɛ mfe esuon; na mbɛtsem pa esuon no yɛ mfe esuon: daa no yɛ kor.

english_verse: The seven skinny, ugly cows that came up later also stand for seven years, as do the seven bad heads of grain that were scorched by the desert wind. The dreams mean there will be seven years when there won't be enough grain.
translation_verse: Na anantwiber esuon a wɔafomfɔn na hɔn ho yɛ tantan no a wodzii hɔn ekyir bae no, yɛ ɔkɔm mfe esuon, mbɛtsem esuon nkɔkɔr a boka mframa ama enyinyam no so dɛmara; ɔbɛyɛ ɔkɔm mfe esuon.

english_verse: It is just as I said—God has shown what he intends to do.
translation_verse: Ɔno nye asɛm a mekaa mekyerɛɛ Farao no; dɛ dza Nyankopɔn rebɛyɛ nna oeyi akyerɛ Farao no.

english_verse: For seven years Egypt will have more than enough grain,
translation_verse: Hwɛ, mfe esuon reba a edziban piinara bɛba Egypt asaase nyinara do:

english_verse: when the king made him governor, and he went everywhere for the king.
translation_verse: na ɔno n’ekyir no nna ɔkɔm mfe esuon bɔsoɛr; na werɛ befir edziban pii no nyinara wɔ Egypt asaase do; na ɔkɔm no bɛhyew asaase no;

english_verse: The famine will be so bad that no one will remember that once there had been plenty.
translation_verse: na osian ɔkɔm a odzi ekyir no ntsi wɔnnkɛkaa edziban pii no wɔ asaase no do; osiandɛ no mu bɛyɛ dzen dodow.

english_verse: God has given you two dreams to let you know that he has definitely decided to do this and that he will do it soon.
translation_verse: Na sɛ adaaso no yɛɛ mbɔho ebien maa Farao a, nna ne siantsir nye dɛ, Nyankopɔn esi no pi, na Nyankopɔn bɛma ayɛ hɔ ntsɛntsɛm.

english_verse: Your Majesty, you should find someone who is wise and will know what to do, so that you can put him in charge of all Egypt.
translation_verse: Dɛm ntsi afei wɔmma Farao nhwehwɛ nyimpa a ɔyɛ ɔbadwembanyi nye onyansafo, na ɔnyɛ no panyin wɔ Egypt asaase do.

english_verse: Then appoint some other officials to collect one fifth of every crop harvested in Egypt during the seven years when there is plenty.
translation_verse: Wɔmma Farao nyɛ iyi, na ɔnhyehyɛ ahwɛdofo wɔ asaase no do, na wɔngyegye Egypt asaase do edziban no mbumu enum enum wɔ mfe esuon a edziban pii wɔ mu no mu.

english_verse: Give them the power to collect the grain during those good years and to store it in your cities.
translation_verse: Na wɔmma wɔmboaboa mfe pa a ɔreba yi no mu edziban nyinara ano, na wɔmfa eburow nhyehyɛ asan do wɔ Farao ne nsa ase, ma ɔnyɛ edziban wɔ nkurow no mu, na wɔmfa nsie.

english_verse: It can be stored until it is needed during the seven years when there won't be enough grain in Egypt. This will keep the country from being destroyed because of the lack of food. Joseph Is Made Governor over Egypt
translation_verse: Na edziban no bɛyɛ dza wɔakora ama asaase no dze esie ɔkɔm mfe esuon a ɔbɛba Egypt asaase do no; amma asaase no aannsɛɛ wɔ ɔkɔm no mu. Wɔhyɛ Josef Enyimnyam

english_verse: The king and his officials liked this plan.
translation_verse: Na asɛm no yɛɛ Farao nye no nkowaa nyinara dɛw.

english_verse: So the king said to them, “Who could possibly handle this better than Joseph? After all, the Spirit of God is with him.”
translation_verse: Na Farao see no nkowaa dɛ, Ana yebenya nyimpa a ɔtse dɛ oyi, nyimpa a Nyankopɔn no sunsum wɔ no mu yi?

english_verse: The king told Joseph, “God is the one who has shown you these things. No one else is as wise as you are or knows as much as you do.
translation_verse: Na Farao see Josef dɛ, Susuampa dɛ Nyankopɔn dze iyi nyinara akyerɛ wo yi, obiara nnyi hɔ a ɔyɛ ɔbadwembanyi nye onyansafo dɛ ɔwo:

english_verse: I'm putting you in charge of my palace, and everybody will have to obey you. No one will be over you except me.
translation_verse: ɔwo na ebɔhwɛ me fi yi do, na dza ebɛka no, dza me man nyinara bedzi do nye no; gyedɛ ahengua no nkotsee na medze mebɛsen wo.

english_verse: You are now governor of all Egypt!”
translation_verse: Na Farao see Josef dɛ, Hwɛ, mayɛ wo Egypt asaase nyinara do panyin.

english_verse: Then the king took off his royal ring and put it on Joseph's finger. He gave him fine clothes to wear and placed a gold chain around his neck.
translation_verse: Na Farao worɔɔw no nsɔwanonkaba wɔ ne nsa, na ɔdze hyɛɛ Josef ne nsa, na ɔhyɛɛ no ndwera pa ntar, na ɔdze sika ntweaban too no kɔn mu;

english_verse: He also let him ride in the chariot next to his own, and people shouted, “Make way for Joseph!” So Joseph was governor of Egypt.
translation_verse: na ɔmaa ɔtsenaa ne tseaseanam a otsia ebien no mu; na wɔpaa mu wɔ n’enyim dɛ, Obiara mbu nkotodwe; na ɔyɛɛ no Egypt asaase nyinara do panyin.

english_verse: The king told Joseph, “Although I'm king, no one in Egypt is to do anything without your permission.”
translation_verse: Na Farao see Josef dɛ, Emi nye Farao, na obiara nnkokwetsir wo ayɛ biribiara wɔ Egypt asaase nyinara do.

english_verse: He gave Joseph the Egyptian name Zaphenath Paneah. And he let him marry Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera, a priest in the city of Heliopolis. Joseph traveled all over Egypt.
translation_verse: Na Farao frɛɛ Josef ne dzin Zafenat-panea; na ɔdze On ɔsɔfo Potifera ne babaa Asenat maa no awar.

english_verse: For seven years there were big harvests of grain.
translation_verse: Na bafuu mfe esuon no mu no, edziban baa asaase no do ma obuu do.

english_verse: Joseph collected and stored up the extra grain in the cities of Egypt near the fields where it was harvested.
translation_verse: Na ɔboaboaa mfe esuon edziban a ɔbaa Egypt asaase do no nyinara ano, na ɔdze siesiee nkurow no mu: nhaban a wɔabɔ nkurow no nyinara ho aprɔw no mu edziban na ɔdze siesiee nkurow no mu.

english_verse: In fact, there was so much grain that they stopped keeping record, because it was like counting the grains of sand along the beach.
translation_verse: Na Josef boaboaa eburow ano dɛ mpoano anhwea; ɔdɔɔ so dodow, ma nkyii ogyaa kan mpo; osiandɛ wobu ano a ɔnnyɛ yie.

english_verse: Their first son was named Manasseh, which means, “God has let me forget all my troubles and my family back home.”
translation_verse: Na Josef frɛɛ abakan no ne dzin Manaasɛ: Na ɔsee dɛ, Nyankopɔn ama mo werɛ efir me berɛ nyinara, nye m’egya fie nyinara.

english_verse: His second son was named Ephraim, which means “God has made me a success in the land where I suffered.”
translation_verse: Na nyia otsia ebien no ɔfrɛɛ ne dzin Efraim: osiandɛ ɔsee dɛ Nyankopɔn ama mawo wɔ amandzehu asaase no do.

english_verse: Egypt's seven years of plenty came to an end,
translation_verse: Na ndɔbaa pii a ɔbaa Egypt asaase do mfe esuon no bɔɔ adze.

english_verse: and the seven years of famine began, just as Joseph had said. There was not enough food in other countries, but all over Egypt there was plenty.
translation_verse: Na ɔkɔm mfe esuon hyɛɛ ase dɛ ɔreba, dɛ mbrɛ Josef see no ara; na ɔkɔm baa asaase nyinara do: nna Egypt asaase nyinara do dze, edziban wɔ hɔ.

english_verse: When the famine finally struck Egypt, the people asked the king for food, but he said, “Go to Joseph and do what he tells you to do.”
translation_verse: Na ɔkɔm kaa Egypt asaase nyinara no, ɔman no sui tɔɔ Farao do gyee edziban: na Farao see Egyptfo no nyinara dɛ, Hom nkɔ Josef hɔ; dza ɔbɛse hom no, hom nyɛ.

english_verse: The famine became bad everywhere in Egypt, so Joseph opened the storehouses and sold the grain to the Egyptians.
translation_verse: Na ɔkɔm no baa asaase nyinara do, na Josef buebuee mbrɛ edziban wɔ no nyinara, na ɔtɔnee maa Egyptfo no; na ɔkɔm no mu yɛɛ dzen wɔ Egypt asaase do.

english_verse: People from all over the world came to Egypt to buy grain, because the famine was so severe in their countries.
translation_verse: Na aman nyinara baa Josef hɔ wɔ Egypt bɔtɔɔ eburow; osiandɛ ɔkɔm no mu yɛɛ dzen wɔ asaase nyinara do.

english_verse: When Jacob found out there was grain in Egypt, he said to his sons, “Why are you just sitting here, staring at one another?
translation_verse: Afei Jakob tsee dɛ eburow wɔ Egypt, na ɔsee ne mbabanyin no dɛ, ɔyɛ dɛn na hom hwehwɛɛhwɛ hom enyim nkorkor yi?

english_verse: I have heard there is grain in Egypt. Go down and buy some, so we won't starve to death.”
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Hom nhwɛ, matse dɛ eburow wɔ Egypt: hom nkɔ hɔ na hom nkɔtɔ bi wɔ hɔ mbrɛ hɛn; ama yaatsena ase, na yeennwu.

english_verse: Ten of Joseph's brothers went to Egypt to buy grain.
translation_verse: Na Josef no nuanom du no kɔr Egypt dɛ wɔrokɔtɔ eburow;

english_verse: But Jacob did not send Joseph's younger brother Benjamin with them; he was afraid that something might happen to him.
translation_verse: na Josef no nua Benjamin dze, Jakob amma ɔannye no nuanom no annkɔ; na ɔsee dɛ, Bɛyɛ na mbusu bi ato no.

english_verse: So Jacob's sons joined others from Canaan who were going to Egypt because of the terrible famine.
translation_verse: Na Israel ne mba no foraforaa hɔn a wɔbae no hɔn mu baa atɔ; osiandɛ nna ɔkɔm no esi Keenan asaase do.

english_verse: Since Joseph was governor of Egypt and in charge of selling grain, his brothers came to him and bowed with their faces to the ground.
translation_verse: Nna Josef na ɔyɛ asaase no do ambrado; ɔno na ɔtɔn edziban ma asaase no do nkorɔfo nyinara: na Josef no nuanom no bae, na wɔbɔbɔɔ hɔn mu adze maa no dze hɔn enyim bumboom famu.

english_verse: Joseph remembered what he had dreamed about them and said, “You're spies! You've come here to find out where our country is weak.”
translation_verse: Na Josef kaa daa a ɔsosoo no hɔn ho no, na ɔsee hɔn dɛ, Hom yɛ akwanserafo; asaase yi n’adagyaw na hom bae bɔhwɛe.

english_verse: “No sir,” they replied. “We're your servants, and we have only come to buy grain.
translation_verse: Na wɔsee no dɛ, Dabida, mo wura, na wo nkowaa bae dɛ wɔrobɔtɔ edziban.

english_verse: We're honest men, and we come from the same family—we're not spies.”
translation_verse: Hɛn nyinara yɛ nyimpa kor mba; yɛyɛ anokwafo, wo nkowaa nnyɛ akwanserafo bi.

english_verse: “That isn't so!” Joseph insisted. “You've come here to find out where our country is weak.”
translation_verse: Na ɔsee hɔn dɛ, Dabida, na asaase yi n’adagyaw na hom bae bɔhwɛe.

english_verse: But they explained, “Sir, we come from a family of twelve brothers. The youngest is still with our father in Canaan, and one of our brothers is dead.”
translation_verse: Na wɔsee dɛ, Wo nkowaa yɛ enuanom duebien, nyimpa baako mba wɔ Keenan asaase do; na hwɛ, ɔkaekyir no mpo wɔ hɛn egya nkyɛn ndɛ ara, na ɔbaako na onnyi hɔ.

english_verse: Joseph replied: It's like I said. You're spies,
translation_verse: Na Josef see hɔn dɛ, Iyi ntsi na mekaa kyerɛɛ hom dɛ, Hom yɛ akwanserafo no.

english_verse: and I'm going to find out the truth. I swear by the life of the king that you won't leave this place until your youngest brother comes here.
translation_verse: Dza wɔdze bɔsɔ hom ahwɛ nye yi: dɛ Farao tse ase yi hom nnkefi ha akɔ, gyedɛ hom nua kakraba no aba ha ana.

english_verse: Choose one of you to go after your brother, while the rest of you stay here in jail. That will show whether you are telling the truth. But if you are lying, I swear by the life of the king that you are spies!
translation_verse: Hom nsoma hom mu baako ma ɔnkɛfa hom nua no mbra, na hom dze, wɔdze hom bogu efiadze ama wɔaasɔ hom nsɛm ahwɛ, dɛ nokwar wɔ hom mu anaa: annyɛ dɛm dze a, na ɔno dɛ Farao tse ase yi ampaara hom yɛ akwanserafo.

english_verse: Joseph kept them all under guard for three days,
translation_verse: Na ɔdze hɔn nyinara koguu efiadze ndaansa.

english_verse: before saying to them: Since I respect God, I'll give you a chance to save your lives.
translation_verse: Na ne ndaansa no Josef see hɔn dɛ, Hom nyɛ yi, na hom ntsena ase; osiandɛ musuro Nyankopɔn:

english_verse: If you are honest men, one of you must stay here in jail, and the rest of you can take the grain back to your starving families.
translation_verse: sɛ hom yɛ anokwafo a, ɔno wɔmma wɔnkyekyer hom nuanom mu baako wɔ efiadze mbrɛ hom da yi; na hom dze, hom nkɔ, hom nkɔsoa eburow nkɔ, wɔ hom efiefi kɔm ntsi:

english_verse: But you must bring your youngest brother to me. Then I'll know that you are telling the truth, and you won't be put to death. Joseph's brothers agreed
translation_verse: na wɔnkɛfa hom nua kakraba no mbrɛ me; nna mubohu dɛ hom nsɛm yɛ nokwar, na hom nnkowu. Na wɔyɛɛ dɛm.

english_verse: and said to one another, “We're being punished because of Joseph. We saw the trouble he was in, but we refused to help him when he begged us. That's why these terrible things are happening.”
translation_verse: Na wɔsesee hɔnho dɛ, Yedzi hɛn nua no ho fɔ ampaara, dɛ hɛnara yehun n’ahohiahia wɔ aber a ɔrototɔ serɛ hɛn no, na yeenntsie; dɛm ntsi na dɛm ahohiahia yi aba hɛn do yi.

english_verse: Reuben spoke up, “Didn't I tell you not to harm the boy? But you wouldn't listen, and now we have to pay the price for killing him.”
translation_verse: Na Reuben buaa hɔn dɛ, Mannka annkyerɛ hom dɛ, Mma hom nnyɛ bɔn nntsia abofra no; na hom enntsie? Dɛm ntsi hwɛ, no bɔgyaa na wɔrohwehwɛ yi.

english_verse: They did not know that Joseph could understand them, since he was speaking through an interpreter.
translation_verse: Nna wonnyim dɛ Josef tse kasa no ase; osiandɛ oyianofo gyina hɔn ntamu.

english_verse: Joseph turned away from them and cried, but soon he turned back and spoke to them again. Then he had Simeon tied up and taken away while they watched. Joseph's Brothers Return to Canaan
translation_verse: Na ɔdaan noho fii hɔn nkyɛn, na okosui; na ɔsanee baa hɔn nkyɛn, na ɔbɛkasaa kyerɛɛ hɔn, na oyii Simeon wɔ hɔn mu, na ɔkyekyeer no wɔ hɔn enyim hɔ. Josef No Nuanom San Kɔ Keenan

english_verse: Joseph gave orders for his brothers' grain sacks to be filled with grain and for their money to be put in their sacks. He also gave orders for them to be given food for their journey home. After this was done,
translation_verse: Na Josef hyɛɛ dɛ wɔmfa eburow nhyehyɛ hɔn nkotoku amaama, na wɔmfa hɔn nyinara hɔn sika nhyehyɛ hɔn nkotoku mu nkorkor, na wɔmma hɔn kwan mu edziban: na wɔyɛɛ hɔn dɛm.

english_verse: they each loaded the grain on their donkeys and left.
translation_verse: Na wɔdze hɔn eburow no soasoaa hɔn nsoasopɔnkɔ, na wosii mu fii hɔ.

english_verse: When they stopped for the night, one of them opened his sack to get some grain for his donkey, and at once he saw his moneybag.
translation_verse: Na hɔn mu baako saan no kotoku dɛ ɔrema n’asoasopɔnkɔ edziban wɔ asoɛe no, ohun dɛ ne sika no hyɛ no kotoku ano.

english_verse: “Here's my money!” he told his brothers. “Right here in my sack.” They were trembling with fear as they stared at one another and asked themselves, “What has God done to us?”
translation_verse: Na ɔsee no nuanom no dɛ, Wɔdze me sika asan ama me; hwɛ, ɔhyɛ mo kotoku mu: na hɔn akoma tutui, na wɔdze ahopopo sesee hɔnho dɛ, Ebɛnadze na Nyankopɔn ayɛ hɛn yi?

english_verse: When they returned to the land of Canaan, they told their father Jacob everything that had happened to them:
translation_verse: Na wɔbaa hɔn egya Jakob hɔ wɔ Keenan asaase do, na wɔbɛkaa dza ɔbaa hɔn do nyinara kyerɛɛ no dɛ,

english_verse: The governor of Egypt was rude and treated us like spies.
translation_verse: banyin no a ɔyɛ asaase no wura no nye hɛn kasaa ebufuw do, na ɔfaa hɛn dɛ yɛyɛ ɔman no ho akwanserafo:

english_verse: But we told him, “We're honest men, not spies.
translation_verse: na yɛsee no dɛ, Yɛyɛ anokwafo; na yɛnnyɛ akwanserafo.

english_verse: We come from a family of twelve brothers. The youngest is still with our father in Canaan, and the other is dead.”
translation_verse: Yɛyɛ enuanom duebien, egyamba; ɔbaako na onnyi hɔ, na ekumaba no wɔ hɛn egya nkyɛn wɔ Keenan asaase do ndɛ ara.

english_verse: Then the governor of Egypt told us, “I'll find out if you really are honest. Leave one of your brothers here with me, while you take the grain to your starving families.
translation_verse: Na banyin no, mekyerɛ asaase wura no, see hɛn dɛ, Dza medze bohu dɛ hom yɛ anokwafo nye yi; hom ndan hom nuanom mu baako ngya me nkyɛn, na hom mfa eburow nkɔ, wɔ hom efiefi kɔm ntsi, na hom nkɔ hom kwan:

english_verse: But bring your youngest brother to me, so I can be certain that you are honest men and not spies. After that, I'll let your other brother go free, and you can stay here and trade.”
translation_verse: na hom nkɛfa hom nua kakraba no mbrɛ me: nna mubohu dɛ hom nnyɛ akwanserafo, na dɛ hom yɛ anokwafo: nna mibeyi hom nua no ama hom, na hom edzi gua wɔ asaase yi do.

english_verse: When the brothers started emptying their sacks of grain, they found their moneybags in them. They were frightened, and so was their father Jacob,
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ wohuehuee hɔn nkotoku mu eburow no, nna nyimpa biara ne sikaboa wɔ no kotoku mu: na aber a hɔn nye hɔn egya hun hɔn sika mboaba no, hɔn akoma tui.

english_verse: who said, “You have already taken my sons Joseph and Simeon from me. And now you want to take away Benjamin! Everything is against me.”
translation_verse: Ma hɔn egya Jakob see hɔn dɛ, Emi na hom ama mahwer me mba: Josef nnyi hɔ, na Simeon nnyi hɔ, na hom rebɛfa Benjamin so akɔ: iyi nyinara aba mo do.

english_verse: Reuben spoke up, “Father, if I don't bring Benjamin back, you can kill both of my sons. Trust me with him, and I'll bring him back.”
translation_verse: Na Reuben kaa kyerɛɛ n’egya dɛ, Sɛ mammfa no ammbrɛ wo bio a, ku me mbabanyin beenu no: fa no hyɛ me nsa, na medze no bɛbrɛ wo bio.

english_verse: But Jacob said, “I won't let my son Benjamin go down to Egypt with the rest of you. His brother is already dead, and he is the only son I have left. I am an old man, and if anything happens to him on the way, I'll die from sorrow, and all of you will be to blame.”
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Me ba nnkɛnye hom akɔ; osiandɛ no nua ewu, na aka no nko: sɛ mbusu bi ba no do wɔ kwan a wɔnam mu rokɔ no mu a, ɔno hom dze mo gon nam awerɛhow mu bɔkɔ nda mu.

english_verse: The famine in Canaan got worse,
translation_verse: Na ɔkɔm no mu yɛɛ dzen wɔ asaase no do.

english_verse: until finally, Jacob's family had eaten all the grain they had bought in Egypt. So Jacob said to his sons, “Go back and buy some more grain.”
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ, aber a wowiee eburow a wɔdze fi Egypt bae no dzi no, hɔn egya see hɔn dɛ, Hom nkɔ bio, hom nkɔtɔ edziban kakra mbrɛ hɛn.

english_verse: Judah replied, “The governor strictly warned us that we would not be allowed to see him unless we brought our youngest brother with us. If you let us take Benjamin along, we will go and buy grain. But we won't go without him!”
translation_verse: na sɛ ennkɛma ɔaka hɛn ho dze a, ɔno yɛronnkɔ: osiandɛ banyin no see hɛn dɛ, Mma hom nnsi m’enyim, gyedɛ hom nua no ka hom ho.

english_verse: Jacob asked, “Why did you cause me so much trouble by telling the governor you had another brother?”
translation_verse: Na Israel see dɛ, ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na hom nye me dzi no bɔn dɛm, dɛ hom kaa kyerɛɛ banyin no dɛ hom wɔ nua bi so?

english_verse: They answered, “He asked a lot of questions about us and our family. He wanted to know if you were still alive and if we had any more brothers. All we could do was answer his questions. How could we know he would tell us to bring along our brother?”
translation_verse: Na wɔsee dɛ, ɔbarimba no bisaa hɛn ntsɛmara wɔ hɛnara hɛnho, nye hɛn ebusuafo ho, dɛ, Ana hom egya da ho tse ase? Hom wɔ nua bi so a? Na mbrɛ nsɛm yi tse no ara na hɛn so yeyii no ano. Ana hɛn so yenyim dɛ ɔreyɛ na ɔase dɛ, Wɔnkɛfa hom nua no mbra?

english_verse: Then Judah said to his father, “Let Benjamin go with me, and we will leave at once, so that none of us will starve to death.
translation_verse: Na Juda see n’egya Israel dɛ, Ma abofra no nye me nkɔ, na yɛbɔsoɛr na yɛakɔ; ama yeenya nkwa, na yeennwu, hɛn nye ɔwo nyina, nye hɛn mbofraba so.

english_verse: I promise to bring him back safely, and if I don't, you can blame me as long as I live.
translation_verse: Mibedzi no ho egyinamu; hwehwɛ no me nsamu: sɛ mammfa no ammbrɛ wo, na mammfa no emmbegyina w’enyim a, ɔno wɔmma asodzi no nka mo kɔn mu daa:

english_verse: If we had not wasted all this time, we could already have been there and back twice.”
translation_verse: sɛ yennkyinkyim a nkyɛ ebodu sisei, ɔyɛ dɛn ara a yɛakɔ aba mpɛn ebien.

english_verse: Their father said: If Benjamin must go with you, take the governor a gift of some of the best things from our own country, such as perfume, honey, spices, pistachio nuts, and almonds.
translation_verse: Na hɔn egya Israel see hɔn dɛ, Sɛ mbrɛ ɔtse nye yi a, ɔno hom nyɛ yi; hom mfa asaase yi do ndzepa a oye no bi ngu hom ndzɛmba mu, dɛ akyɛdze nkɛma banyin no, akyenkyendur kakra, ewo kakra, eduhuam mɛɛr, nkatse, nye eduadzewa almɔnd,

english_verse: Also take along twice the amount of money for the grain, because there must have been some mistake when the money was put back in your sacks.
translation_verse: na hom mfa sika fofor nkitsa; na sika a wɔdze sanee maa hom a wɔdze hyehyɛɛ hom nkotoku ano no, ɔno so hom mfa nkitsa bio; bi a enyi na oduaa do.

english_verse: Take Benjamin with you and leave at once.
translation_verse: Hom mfa hom nua no so, na wɔnsoɛr nkɔ banyin no hɔ bio:

english_verse: When you go in to see the governor, I pray that God All-Powerful will be good to you and that the governor will let your other brother and Benjamin come back home with you. But if I must lose my children, I suppose I must.
translation_verse: na Otumfo Nyankopɔn mma banyin no nhu hom mbɔbɔr, ama oeegyaa hom nua kor no nye Benjamin ama hom. Na emi dze, sɛ me mba fi me nsa a, nna woefi me nsa.

english_verse: The brothers took the gifts, twice the amount of money, and Benjamin. Then they hurried off to Egypt. When they stood in front of Joseph,
translation_verse: Nna nkorɔfo no faa akyɛdze no, na wɔfaa sika fofor siaan ho, nye Benjamin so; na wɔsoɛree kɔr Egypt, na wokegyinaa Josef n’enyim.

english_verse: he saw Benjamin and told the servant in charge of his house, “Take these men to my house. Slaughter an animal and cook it, so they can eat with me at noon.”
translation_verse: Na aber a Josef hun dɛ Benjamin ka hɔn ho no, ɔsee ne fie somfopanyin no dɛ, Fa mbanyin yi kɔ fie, na koku mbowa, na yɛ edziban; na mbanyin yi nye me bedzidzi ewiaber yi.

english_verse: The servant did as he was told and took the brothers to Joseph's house.
translation_verse: Na panyin no yɛɛ dɛ mbrɛ Josef see no ara; na ɔdze nkorɔfo no kɔr Josef fie.

english_verse: But on the way they got worried and started thinking, “We are being taken there because of the money that was put back in our sacks last time. He will arrest us, make us his slaves, and take our donkeys.”
translation_verse: Na nkorɔfo no hɔn akoma tui, osiandɛ wɔdze hɔn akɔ Josef ne fie ntsi; na wɔsee dɛ, Sika a wɔsanee dze hyehyɛɛ hɛn nkotoku mu asɔpɔn no ntsi na wɔdze hɛn aba fie yi; ama ɔaahwehwɛ biribi egyina do, na oeesuo hɛn mu, na ɔaafa hɛn ayɛ nkowaa, nye hɛn nsoasopɔnkɔ.

english_verse: So when they arrived at Joseph's house, they said to the servant in charge,
translation_verse: Na wopiin Josef ne fie somfopanyin no nkyɛn wɔ fi no n’abowano,

english_verse: “Sir, we came to Egypt once before to buy grain.
translation_verse: na wɔsee dɛ, Ao mo wura, yɛbaa ha bɔtɔɔ edziban asɔpɔn no ampa:

english_verse: But when we stopped for the night, we each found in our grain sacks the exact amount we had paid. We have brought that money back,
translation_verse: na ɔbaa dɛ, aber a yedur asoɛe, na yɛsansaan hɛn nkotoku ano no, nna, hwɛ, hɛn nyinara hɛn sika hyehyɛ hɛn nkotoku ano, hɛn sika mbrɛ ɔtow ara pɛ: na yɛdze asan aba.

english_verse: together with enough money to buy more grain. We don't know who put the money in our sacks.”
translation_verse: Na yɛdze sika fofor so aba a yɛdze robɔtɔ edziban: yennyim nyia ɔdze hɛn sika no hyehyɛɛ hɛn nkotoku mu.

english_verse: “It's all right,” the servant replied. “Don't worry. The God you and your father worship must have put the money there, because I received your payment in full.” Then he brought Simeon out to them.
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Asomdwee nka hom, mma hom nnsuro: hom Nyankopɔn, nye hom egya ne Nyankopɔn, na ɔama hom ehutudze wɔ hom nkotoku mu: me nsa kaa hom sika no. Na okeyii Simeon brɛɛ hɔn.

english_verse: The servant took them into Joseph's house and gave them water to wash their feet. He also tended their donkeys.
translation_verse: Na banyin no dze nkorɔfo no kɔr Josef fie, na ɔmaa hɔn nsu, na wɔhohoor hɔn anan ho; na ɔmaa hɔn nsoasopɔnkɔ no edziban.

english_verse: The brothers got their gifts ready to give to Joseph at noon, since they had heard they were going to eat there.
translation_verse: Na wɔyɛɛ akyɛdze no krado dze tweɔɔn Josef ne mbae a ɔbaa ewiaber no: osiandɛ wɔtsee dɛ woribedzidzi wɔ hɔ.

english_verse: When Joseph came home, they gave him the gifts they had brought, and they bowed down to him.
translation_verse: Na Josef baa fie no, wɔdze akyɛdze a okitsa hɔn nsamu no kɔr dan mu kɛmaa no, na wɔkɔbɔɔ hɔn mu adze wɔ famu maa no.

english_verse: After Joseph had asked how they were, he said, “What about your elderly father? Is he still alive?”
translation_verse: Na obisaa hɔn honam mu, na ɔsee dɛ, Ana hom egya akɔdaa a hom kaa no ho asɛm no, no ho ye? Ana ɔda ho tse ase?

english_verse: They answered, “Your servant our father is still alive and well.” And again they bowed down to Joseph.
translation_verse: Na wɔsee dɛ, W’akowaa hɛn egya no ho ye, ɔda ho tse ase. Na wɔbɔɔ hɔn tsir adze, na wɔkotoow no.

english_verse: When Joseph looked around and saw his brother Benjamin, he said, “This must be your youngest brother, the one you told me about. God bless you, my son.”
translation_verse: Na ɔmaa n’enyiwa do, na ohun no nua Benjamin, ne na ba no, na obisaa dɛ, Ana hom nua kuma a hom kaa no ho asɛm kyerɛɛ me no, ɔno nye yi a? Na ɔsee dɛ, Nyankopɔn nhyira wo, me ba.

english_verse: After washing his face and returning, he was able to control himself and said, “Serve the meal!”
translation_verse: Na ɔhohoor n’enyim, na opuei; na ɔhyɛɛ noho do, na ɔsee dɛ, Wɔmfa edziban mbesisi hɔ.

english_verse: Joseph was served at a table by himself, and his brothers were served at another. The Egyptians sat at yet another table, because Egyptians felt it was disgusting to eat with Hebrews.
translation_verse: Na wɔdze ɔnoara nedze sii fa, na wɔdze hɔndze sisii fa, na wɔdze Egyptfo a wɔnye no dzidzi no hɔndze sisii fa; osiandɛ Egyptfo nnye Hebriwfo nndzidzi; na ɔyɛ ekyiwadze ma Egyptfo.

english_verse: To the surprise of Joseph's brothers, they were seated in front of him according to their ages, from the oldest to the youngest.
translation_verse: Na wɔtsenatsenaa n’enyim, abakan no dɛ mbrɛ ne panyin tse, nye ekumaba no dɛ mbrɛ n’ekumaba tse: na ɔyɛɛ nkorɔfo no nwanwa wɔ hɔn mu.

english_verse: They were served food from Joseph's table, and Benjamin was given five times as much as each of the others. So Joseph's brothers ate and drank with him and had a good time.
translation_verse: Na oyiyii edziban ma wɔdze fii n’enyim maa hɔn: na Benjamin dze n’edziban no boor hɔn nyinara hɔndze do mpɛn enum. Na wɔnomee, na hɔn nyinara hɔn enyi gyee.

english_verse: Later, Joseph told the servant in charge of his house, “Fill the men's grain sacks with as much as they can hold and put their money in the sacks. Also put my silver cup in the sack of the youngest brother.” The servant did as he was told.
translation_verse: Na fa mo kɔɔpow no, mekyerɛ me dwetɛ kɔɔpow no, hyɛ hɔn mu kakraba no no kotoku mu, nye n’eburow sika no. Na ɔyɛɛ dɛ mbrɛ Josef kae no ara.

english_verse: Early the next morning, the men were sent on their way with their donkeys.
translation_verse: Enyim tsetsewee ara na wogyaa nkorɔfo no kwan, hɔn nye hɔn nsoasopɔnkɔ.

english_verse: But they had not gone far from the city when Joseph told the servant, “Go after those men! When you catch them, say, ‘My master has been good to you. So why have you stolen his silver cup?
translation_verse: Na wofii kurow no mu kɔree, na wonnyae nndur ekyirekyir no, Josef see ne somfo no dɛ, Soɛr, tsiw nkorɔfo no; na sɛ eto hɔn a, se hɔn dɛ, ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na hom dze bɔn etua papa kaw yi?

english_verse: Not only does he drink from his cup, but he also uses it to learn about the future. You have done a terrible thing.’ ”
translation_verse: Ana nnyɛ iyi na mo wura nom ano, na ɔdze hyɛ nkɔm ma ɔba mu no bi? Hom ayɛ bɔn dɛ hom ayɛ dɛm adze yi.

english_verse: When the servant caught up with them, he said exactly what Joseph had told him to say.
translation_verse: Na ɔtoo hɔn, na ɔkaa nsɛm yi kyerɛɛ hɔn.

english_verse: But they replied, “Sir, why do you say such things? We would never do anything like that!
translation_verse: Na wɔsee no dɛ, ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na mo wura ka nsɛm a ɔtse dɛm yi? Nyankopɔn mpa ngu dɛ wo nkowaa bɛyɛ dɛm adze yi.

english_verse: We even returned the money we found in our grain sacks when we got back to Canaan. So why would we want to steal any silver or gold from your master's house?
translation_verse: Hwɛ, sika a yehun no hɛn nkotoku mu no, yɛdze sanee fii Keenan asaase do brɛɛ wo bio: ɔbɛyɛ dɛn na yebowia dwetɛ anaa sika wɔ wo wura ne fie?

english_verse: If you find that one of us has the cup, then kill him, and the rest of us will become your slaves.”
translation_verse: Wo nkowaa hɛn mu biara a ibohu no wɔ no hɔ no, ma onwu, na hɛn so yɛbɛyɛ mo wura nkowaa.

english_verse: “Good!” the man replied, “I'll do what you have said. But only the one who has the cup will become my slave. The rest of you can go free.”
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Nna afei wɔmma ɔnyɛ dɛ mbrɛ hom aka yi ara: nyia wobohu no no hɔ no ɔbɛyɛ m’akowaa; na hom dze, hom nndzi so.

english_verse: Each of the brothers quickly put his sack on the ground and opened it.
translation_verse: Nna wɔyɛɛ no ntsɛm, na nyimpa biara soɛɛ no kotoku too famu, na nyimpa biara saan no kotoku ano.

english_verse: Joseph's servant started searching the sacks, beginning with the one that belonged to the oldest brother. When he came to Benjamin's sack, he found the cup.
translation_verse: Na ɔhwehwɛe, ofitsi panyin no do, na ɔdze kɛpeem kakraba no do: na wohun kɔɔpow no wɔ Benjamin no kotoku no mu.

english_verse: This upset the brothers so much that they began tearing their clothes in sorrow. Then they loaded their donkeys and returned to the city.
translation_verse: Nna wosunsuaan hɔn ntar mu, na obiara dze n’adze soaa n’asoasopɔnkɔ, na wɔsanee kɔr kurow no mu.

english_verse: When Judah and his brothers got there, Joseph was still at home. So they bowed down to Joseph,
translation_verse: Na Juda nye no nuanom no baa Josef fie; nna ɔda ho wɔ hɔ: na wɔhwehwee famu wɔ n’enyim.

english_verse: “Sir, what can we say?” Judah replied. “How can we say we are innocent, when God has shown we are guilty? And now all of us are your slaves, especially the one who had the cup.”
translation_verse: Na Juda see dɛ, Yɛnse mo wura dɛn? Yɛnka dɛn anaadɛ yerudua dɛn asan hɛnho? Nyankopɔn eyi wo nkowaa hɛn emumuyɛ edzi: hwɛ, yɛyɛ mo wura no nkowaa, hɛn nye nyia wohun kɔɔpow no wɔ ne nsamu nyinara.

english_verse: Joseph told them, “I would never punish all of you. Only the one who was caught with the cup will become my slave. The rest of you are free to go home to your father.” Judah Pleads for Benjamin
translation_verse: Na Josef see dɛ, Nyankopɔn mpa ngu dɛ mebɛyɛ dɛm: nyia woehu kɔɔpow no wɔ ne nsamu no, ɔnoara na ɔbɛyɛ m’akowaa; na hom dze, hom nkɔ hom egya nkyɛn wɔ asomdwee mu. Juda Serɛ Ma Benjamin

english_verse: Judah went over to Joseph and said: Sir, you have as much power as the king himself, and I am only your slave. Please don't get angry if I speak.
translation_verse: Nna Juda piin Josef nkyɛn, na ɔsee dɛ, Ao mo wura, meserɛ wo, ma w’akowaa nka asɛm kor wɔ mo wura n’asomu, na mma wo bo mmfuw w’akowaa: osiandɛ tserɛ woara nye Farao.

english_verse: You asked us if our father was still alive and if we had any more brothers.
translation_verse: Mo wura bisaa no nkowaa dɛ, Ana hom wɔ egya, anaa nua bi?

english_verse: So we told you, “Our father is a very old man. In fact, he was already old when Benjamin was born. Benjamin's brother is dead. Now Benjamin is the only one of the two brothers who is still alive, and our father loves him very much.”
translation_verse: Na yebuaa mo wura dɛ, Yɛwɔ egya, akɔdaa bi, na no nkɔdaa ba kakraba bi; na no nua ewu na ne ba a aka ara nye no nko, na n’egya dɔ no.

english_verse: You ordered us to bring him here, so you could see him for yourself.
translation_verse: Na esee wo nkowaa dɛ, Wɔnkɛfa no mbrɛ me, ma monhwɛ no.

english_verse: We told you that our father would die if Benjamin left him.
translation_verse: Na yɛsee mo wura dɛ, Abofra no fi n’egya nkyɛn a ɔnnkɛyɛ: na sɛ ofi n’egya nkyɛn a, n’egya bowu.

english_verse: But you warned us that we could never see you again, unless our youngest brother came with us.
translation_verse: Na esee wo nkowaa dɛ, Gyedɛ hom nua kakraba no nye hom ba, ɔno hom nnkohu m’enyim bio.

english_verse: So we returned to our father and reported what you had said.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ yɛkɔr w’akowaa, m’egya nkyɛn no, yɛkaa mo wura ne nsɛm no kyerɛɛ no.

english_verse: Later our father sent us back here to buy more grain.
translation_verse: Na hɛn egya see dɛ, Hom nkɔ bio nkɔtɔ edziban kakra mbrɛ hɛn.

english_verse: But we told him, “We can't go back to Egypt without our youngest brother. We will never be let in to see the governor, unless he is with us.”
translation_verse: Na yɛsee dɛ, Yɛkɔ a ɔnnkɛyɛ: gyedɛ hɛn nua kakraba no ka hɛn ho, ɔno yɛbɔkɔ: osiandɛ sɛ hɛn nua kakraba no nnka hɛn ho a, ɔno yennkohu banyin no n’enyim.

english_verse: Sir, our father then reminded us that his favorite wife had given birth to two sons.
translation_verse: Na w’akowaa, m’egya no see hɛn dɛ, Homara hom nyim dɛ me yer woo mba beenu maa me:

english_verse: One of them was already missing and had not been seen for a long time. My father thinks the boy was torn to pieces by some wild animal,
translation_verse: na baako fii me nkyɛn kɔree, na mesee dɛ, Ampaara wɔatsetsew no mu, na munnhun no ara na ɔdze ebodu ndɛ yi:

english_verse: and he said, “I am an old man. If you take Benjamin from me, and something happens to him, I will die of a broken heart.”
translation_verse: na sɛ hom yi oyi so wɔ me nkyɛn, na mbusu bi ba no do a, ɔno hom dze mo gon nam awerɛhow mu bɔkɔ nda mu.

english_verse: That's why Benjamin must be with us when I go back to my father. He loves him so much
translation_verse: Dɛm ntsi afei sɛ mokɔ w’akowaa, m’egya ne nkyɛn, na sɛ abofra no nnka hɛn ho a, ɔnam dɛ n’akoma da abofra no ho dodow ntsi,

english_verse: I promised my father that I would bring him safely home. If I don't, I told my father he could blame me the rest of my life.
translation_verse: Na w’akowaa midzii abofra no ho egyinamu maa m’egya dɛ, Sɛ mammfa no mammbrɛ wo a, ɔno mibedzi so wɔ m’egya n’enyim daa.

english_verse: Sir, I am your slave. Please let me stay here in place of Benjamin and let him return home with his brothers.
translation_verse: Dɛm ntsi afei, meserɛ wo, ma w’akowaa mindzi abofra no ananmu menyɛ mo wura ɔsomfo; na ma abofra no nye no nuanom nkɔ.

english_verse: How can I face my father if Benjamin isn't with me? I couldn't bear to see my father in such sorrow.
translation_verse: Na mibesi no dɛn na makɔ m’egya nkyɛn, ber a abofra no nnka mo ho? Bɛyɛ na mehu mbusu a ɔbɛba m’egya no do.

english_verse: Since Joseph could no longer control his feelings in front of his servants, he sent them out of the room. When he was alone with his brothers, he told them, “I am Joseph.”
translation_verse: Nna afei Josef hyɛ noho do a ɔnnyɛ yie, wɔ hɔn a wogyinagyina ne nkyɛn no nyinara hɔn enyim; na ɔkaa mu dɛ, Wɔmma nkorɔfo nyinara mfi me nkyɛn mfi. Na aber a Josef riyi noho edzi akyerɛ no nuanom no, nna obiara nngyina ne nkyɛn.

english_verse: Then he cried so loudly that the Egyptians heard him and told about it in the king's palace.
translation_verse: Na ɔmaa ne ndze do sui: na Egyptfo no tsee, na Farao fiefo tsee.

english_verse: Joseph asked his brothers if his father was still alive, but they were too frightened to answer.
translation_verse: Na Josef see no nuanom no dɛ, Emi nye Josef; ana m’egya da ho tse ase? Na no nuanom no enntum emmbua no; osiandɛ hɔn ho popoe wɔ n’enyim.

english_verse: Joseph told them to come closer to him, and when they did, he said: Yes, I am your brother Joseph, the one you sold into Egypt.
translation_verse: Na Josef see no nuanom no dɛ, Meserɛ hom, wompin me nkyɛn. Na wopinii. Na ɔsee dɛ, Emi nye hom nua Josef a hom tɔɔn no baa Egypt no.

english_verse: Don't worry or blame yourselves for what you did. God is the one who sent me ahead of you to save lives.
translation_verse: Na afei mma ɔnnyɛ hom yaw, na mma hom bo mmfufuw hom ho so, dɛ hom tɔɔn me baa ha: osiandɛ Nyankopɔn na ɔsomaa me ma midzii hom enyim kan, ama maabɛgye nkwa.

english_verse: There has already been a famine for two years, and for five more years no one will plow fields or harvest grain.
translation_verse: Osiandɛ ɔkɔm esi asaase yi do mfe ebien nye yi: na mfe enum so aka a adɔw nye buber so nnyi mu.

english_verse: But God sent me on ahead of you to keep your families alive and to save you in this wonderful way.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn somaa me ma midzii hom enyim kan ama maabɔkora edzinkaa bi ama hom wɔ asaase do, na medze ɔgye kɛsenara agye hom nkwa.

english_verse: After all, you weren't really the ones who sent me here—it was God. He made me the highest official in the king's court and placed me over all Egypt.
translation_verse: Dɛm ntsi afei, nnyɛ hom bi na wɔsomaa me wɔ ha, na Nyankopɔn a: na ɔayɛ me Farao n’egya, na ne fi nyinara wura na Egypt asaase nyinara do panyin.

english_verse: Now hurry back and tell my father that his son Joseph says, “God has made me ruler of Egypt. Come here as quickly as you can.
translation_verse: Hom nka hom ho nkɔ m’egya hɔ, na wɔnkɛse no dɛ, Mbrɛ wo ba Josef se nye yi, Nyankopɔn ayɛ me Egypt nyinara wura: bra me nkyɛn, mma nnkyinkyim:

english_verse: You will live near me in the region of Goshen with your children and grandchildren, as well as with your sheep, goats, cattle, and everything else you own.
translation_verse: Na bɛtsena Gosen asaase do, na bɛn me, ɔwo, nye wo mba, nye wo mbanananom, nye wo nguan, nye w’anantwi, nye dza ewɔ nyinara:

english_verse: I will take care of you there during the next five years of famine. But if you don't come, you and your family and your animals will starve to death.”
translation_verse: na mobɔhwɛ wo wɔ hɔ; na aka ɔkɔm mfe enum so; bɛyɛ na ehia, ɔwo, nye wo fidua, nye dza ewɔ nyinara.

english_verse: All of you, including my brother Benjamin, can tell by what I have said that I really am Joseph.
translation_verse: Na hwɛ, hom enyiwa tua, na mo nua Benjamin so n’enyiwa tua, dɛ m’ano na ɔrekasa kyerɛ hom yi.

english_verse: Tell my father about my great power here in Egypt and about everything you have seen. Hurry and bring him here.
translation_verse: Na hom nkɛka m’enyimnyam a mowɔ no wɔ Egypt nyinara no ho asɛm nkyerɛ m’egya, na dza hom ehu no nyinara; na hom nyɛ ntsɛm nkɛfa m’egya mbra ha.

english_verse: Joseph and Benjamin hugged each other and started crying.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaar no nua Benjamin no kɔn, na osui; na Benjamin so sui wɔ no kɔn ho.

english_verse: Joseph was still crying as he kissed each of his other brothers. After this, they started talking with Joseph.
translation_verse: Na ɔfefeew no nuanom nyinara hɔn ano, na ɔdzedze hɔn mu sui: na n’ekyir no no nuanom no nye no kasae.

english_verse: When it was told in the palace that Joseph's brothers had come, the king and his officials were happy.
translation_verse: Na wɔtsee nka wɔ Farao fie dɛ, Josef no nuanom aba: na Farao nye no nkowaa enyi gyee papa.

english_verse: So the king said to Joseph: Tell your brothers to load their donkeys and return to Canaan.
translation_verse: Na Farao see Josef dɛ, Se wo nuanom no dɛ, Iyi na hom nyɛ: hom mfa ndzɛmba nsoasoa hom mbowa no, na hom nkɔ, hom nsan nkɔ Keenan asaase do;

english_verse: Tell them to bring their father and their families here. I will give them the best land in Egypt, and they can eat and enjoy everything that grows there.
translation_verse: na hom nkɛfa hom egya nye hom fifo, na hom mbra me nkyɛn: na mebɛma hom Egypt asaase no mu dza oye, na hom bedzi asaase no do ndzepa.

english_verse: Also tell your brothers to take some wagons from Egypt for their wives and children to ride in. And be sure they bring their father.
translation_verse: Afei se hɔn dɛ, iyi na hom nyɛ; hom mfa ntseaseanam wɔ Egypt asaase do nkɛfa hom mbofraba, nye hom yernom na hom egya ma wɔmbra.

english_verse: They can leave their possessions behind, because they will be given the best of everything in Egypt.
translation_verse: Eso mma hom mma hom ndzɛmba ho nnhia hom; na dza oye wɔ Egypt asaase do nyinara yɛ homdze.

english_verse: Jacob's sons agreed to do what the king had said. And Joseph gave them wagons and food for their trip home, just as the king had ordered.
translation_verse: Na Israel ne mba no yɛɛ dɛm: na Josef maa hɔn ntseaseanam, dɛ mbrɛ Farao hyɛe no ara, na ɔmaa hɔn kwan mu edziban.

english_verse: Joseph gave some new clothes to each of his brothers, but to Benjamin he gave five new outfits and
translation_verse: Ɔmaa hɔn nyinara ntar nsakyerho nkorkor; na Benjamin dze, ɔmaa no ndwetɛbona ahaasa, nye ntar nsakyerho enum.

english_verse: To his father he sent ten donkeys loaded with the best things in Egypt, and ten other donkeys loaded with grain and bread and other food for the return trip.
translation_verse: Na dza ɔdze kɛmaa n’egya nye yi; nsoasopɔnkɔ du a wɔsoso Egypt ndzɛmba pa, nye nsoasopɔnkɔber du a wɔsoso eburow, nye boodoo, nye edzidzidze a n’egya bedzi no wɔ kwan mu.

english_verse: Then he sent his brothers off and told them, “Don't argue on the way home!”
translation_verse: Nna ogyaa no nuanom no kwan, na wosii mu na ɔsee hɔn dɛ, Hom nhwɛ mma hom aannka yaw wɔ kwan mu.

english_verse: Joseph's brothers left Egypt, and when they arrived in Canaan,
translation_verse: Na wofii Egypt kɔree, na wɔbaa hɔn egya Jakob nkyɛn wɔ Keenan asaase do.

english_verse: they told their father that Joseph was still alive and was the ruler of Egypt. But their father was so surprised that he could not believe them.
translation_verse: Na wɔkaa kyerɛɛ no dɛ, Josef da ho tse ase, na ɔno nye Egypt asaase nyinara do panyin. Na bobor boor no, osiandɛ ɔanngye hɔn enndzi.

english_verse: Then they told him everything Joseph had said. When he saw the wagons Joseph had sent, he felt much better
translation_verse: Na wɔkaa Josef ne nsɛm a ɔkaa kyerɛɛ hɔn no nyinara kyerɛɛ no: na aber a ohun ntseaseanam a Josef maa wɔdze kɔree dɛ wɔmfa nkɛfa no no, nna hɔn egya Jakob n’akoma tɔɔ ne yamu:

english_verse: and said, “Now I can believe you! My son Joseph must really be alive, and I will get to see him before I die.”
translation_verse: na Israel see dɛ, ɔdɔɔ so ma me; me ba Josef da ho tse ase: mobɔkɔ mekohu no ana mewu.

english_verse: Jacob packed up everything he owned and left for Egypt. On the way he stopped near the town of Beersheba and offered sacrifices to the God his father Isaac had worshiped.
translation_verse: Na Israel faa dza ɔwɔ nyinara sii mu, na ɔbaa Beer-seba, na ɔbɔɔ afɔr maa n’egya Isaak ne Nyankopɔn.

english_verse: That night, God spoke to him in a dream and said, “Jacob! Jacob!” “Here I am,” Jacob answered.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn kasaa kyerɛɛ Israel wɔ anafua nyikyerɛ mu, na ɔfrɛɛ no, Jakob, Jakob, na ɔsee dɛ, Emi nye yi.

english_verse: God said, “I am God, the same God your father worshiped. Don't be afraid to go to Egypt. I will give you so many descendants that one day they will become a nation.
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Emi nye Nyankopɔn, w’egya ne Nyankopɔn: mma nnsuro dɛ ebɔkɔ Egypt; na mebɛyɛ wo ɔman kɛse wɔ hɔ:

english_verse: I will go with you to Egypt, and later I will bring your descendants back here. Your son Joseph will be at your side when you die.”
translation_verse: menye wo bɔkɔ Egypt; na medze wo efi hɔ aba bio so ampaara: na Josef dze n’abaw mu na ibowu ada.

english_verse: When Jacob went to Egypt, his children who were born in northern Syria also went along with their families. Jacob and his wife Leah had a total of
translation_verse: Woyinom nye Leah ne mbabanyin a ɔwoo hɔn maa Jakob wɔ Paddan-aram nye ne babaa Dina: ne mbabanyin nye ne mbabaa nyinara yɛ akra eduasa ebiasa.

english_verse: The king will call you in and ask what you do for a living.
translation_verse: Na sɛ Farao frɛ hom, na obisa dɛ, Wɔyɛ ebɛn edwuma a?

english_verse: Jacob and Zilpah, the servant woman Laban had given his daughter Leah, had a total of
translation_verse: Woyinom nye Zilpa a Laban dze no maa ne babaa Leah no ne mbabanyin, na woyinom na ɔwoo hɔn maa Jakob; wɔyɛ akra duesia.

english_verse: Jacob and Bilhah, the servant woman Laban had given his daughter Rachel, had seven children and grandchildren. Their son Dan took his son Hushim. Their son Naphtali took his sons Jahzeel, Guni, Jezer, and Shillem.
translation_verse: Woyinom nye Bilha a Laban dze no maa ne babaa Rahel no ne mbabanyin na woyinom na ɔwoo hɔn maa Jakob: hɔn nyinara wɔyɛ akra esuon.

english_verse: Sixty-six members of Jacob's family went to Egypt with him, not counting his daughters-in-law.
translation_verse: Akra a wɔnye Jakob kɔr Egypt a wofi n’asen mu nyinara, sɛ Jakob ne mbabanyin hɔn yernom nnka ho a, akra no nyinara yɛ eduosia esia.

english_verse: Jacob had sent his son Judah ahead of him to ask Joseph to meet them in Goshen.
translation_verse: Na ɔsomaa Juda ma odzii n’enyim kan kɔr Josef hɔ, ama oeebedzi n’enyim akyerɛ no Gosen kwan; na wɔbaa Gosen asaase do.

english_verse: So Joseph got in his chariot and went to meet his father. When they met, Joseph hugged his father around the neck and cried for a long time.
translation_verse: Na Josef yɛɛ ne tseaseanam krado, na ɔkɔree kehyiaa n’egya Israel kwan wɔ Gosen; na ɔdze noho kyerɛɛ no, na ɔbaar no kɔn, na osui wɔ no kɔn ho ara ma ɔkyɛɛr kakra.

english_verse: Jacob said to Joseph, “Now that I have seen you and know you are still alive, I am ready to die.”
translation_verse: Na Israel see Josef dɛ, Menya mehu dɛ eda ho ara etse ase yi dze, afei mutum kɔ me nsamankyir.

english_verse: Then Joseph said to his brothers and to everyone who had come with them: I must go and tell the king that you have arrived from Canaan.
translation_verse: Na Josef see no nuanom, nye n’egya ne fifo dɛ, Morokɔ na makɛka akyerɛ Farao, na mase no dɛ, Mo nuanom, nye m’egya ne fifo a wɔwɔ Keenan asaase do no aba me nkyɛn:

english_verse: I will tell him that you are shepherds and that you have brought your sheep, goats, cattle, and everything else you own.
translation_verse: na nkorɔfo no yɛ nguanhwɛfo, na mbowa na wɔnyɛn; na wɔdze hɔn nguan, nye hɔn anantwi, nye dza wɔwɔ nyinara aba.

english_verse: When he does, be sure to say, “We are shepherds. Our families have always raised sheep.” If you tell him this, he will let you settle in the region of Goshen. Joseph wanted them to say this to the king, because the Egyptians did not like to be around anyone who raised sheep.
translation_verse: Ɔno hom nse dɛ, Ofitsi hɛn mbofra mu dze bɛpem ndɛ ara wo nkowaa mbowa ara na yɛnyɛn, hɛn, nye hɛn egyanom nyinara: ama hom aatsena Gosen asaase do; osiandɛ oguanhwɛfo biara yɛ ekyiwadze ma Egyptfo; wɔnye nguanhwɛfo nndzi afora.

english_verse: who asked them, “What do you do for a living?” “Sir, we are shepherds,” was their answer. “Our families have always raised sheep.
translation_verse: Na Farao bisaa no nuanom no dɛ, Wɔyɛ ebɛn edwuma? Na wɔsee Farao dɛ, Wo nkowaa yɛyɛ nguanhwɛfo, hɛn nye hɛn egyanom nyina.

english_verse: But in our country all the pastures are dried up, and our sheep have no grass to eat. So we, your servants, have come here. Please let us live in the region of Goshen.”
translation_verse: Na wɔsee Farao dɛ, Yɛaba dɛ yɛrebɛtsena asaase yi do; na wo nkowaa hɔn nguan nnya edzidzibea; osiandɛ ɔkɔm no mu ayɛ dzen wɔ Keenan asaase do: dɛm ntsi afei, yɛserɛ wo, ma wo nkowaa ntsena Gosen asaase do.

english_verse: The king said to Joseph, “It's good that your father and brothers have arrived.
translation_verse: Na Farao kaa kyerɛɛ Josef dɛ, W’egya nye wo nuanom aba wo nkyɛn:

english_verse: I will let them live anywhere they choose in the land of Egypt, but I suggest that they settle in Goshen, the best part of our land. I would also like for your finest shepherds to watch after my own sheep and goats.”
translation_verse: Egypt asaase da w’enyim; asaase no mu mbrɛ oye no na ma w’egya nye wo nuanom ntsena; ma wɔntsena Gosen asaase do: na sɛ inyim hɔn mu bi a wɔyɛ mbɔdzembɔfo a, ɔno yɛ hɔn mo mbowa do ahwɛfo.

english_verse: Then Joseph brought his father Jacob and introduced him to the king. Jacob gave the king his blessing,
translation_verse: Na Josef dze n’egya Jakob bae begyinaa Farao n’enyim: na Jakob hyiraa Farao.

english_verse: and the king asked him, “How old are you?”
translation_verse: Na Farao bisaa Jakob dɛ, Wo nkwa nda mu mfe yɛ ahen?

english_verse: Then Jacob gave the king his blessing once again and left.
translation_verse: Na Jakob hyiraa Farao, na ofii Farao n’enyim kɔree.

english_verse: Joseph obeyed the king's orders and gave his father and brothers some of the best land in Egypt near the city of Rameses.
translation_verse: Na Josef maa n’egya nye no nuanom no tsenabew, na ɔmaa hɔn beebi wɔ Egypt asaase do, wɔ asaase no do mbrɛ oye, wɔ Rameses asaase do, dɛ mbrɛ Farao hyɛe no ara.

english_verse: Joseph also provided food for their families. A Famine in Egypt
translation_verse: Na Josef bɔɔ n’egya, nye no nuanom, nye n’egya ne fifo nyinara akɔhoma, dɛ mbrɛ hɔn ebusua ebusua tsetsee.

english_verse: The famine was bad everywhere in Egypt and Canaan, and the people were suffering terribly.
translation_verse: Nna edziban nnyi asaase no do beebiara; na ɔkɔm no mu yɛɛ dzen dodoodow, ma Egypt asaase nye Keenan asaase tɔɔ bitsi wɔ ɔkɔm no ntsi.

english_verse: So Joseph sold them the grain that had been stored up, and he put the money in the king's treasury.
translation_verse: Na Josef boaboaa sika a wonyaa no Egypt asaase do, nye Keenan asaase do no, eburow a wɔtɔe no ne sika no nyinara ano: na Josef dze sika no baa Farao fie.

english_verse: But when everyone had run out of money, the Egyptians came to Joseph and demanded, “Give us more grain! If you don't, we'll soon be dead, because our money's all gone.”
translation_verse: Na aber a nkorɔfo ho sika nyinara sae wɔ Egypt nye Keenan asaase do no, Egyptfo no nyinara baa Josef hɔ, na wɔbɛsee dɛ, Ma hɛn edziban: na ɔbɛyɛ dɛn ntsi na yebowuwu wɔ w’enyim? Hɛn ho sika asa.

english_verse: “If you don't have any money,” Joseph answered, “give me your animals, and I'll let you have some grain.”
translation_verse: Na Josef see dɛ, sɛ hom ho sika asa a, hom mfa hom mbowa mma; na mebɛgye hom mbowa na mama hom bi.

english_verse: Then the people came to him and said: Sir, there's no way we can hide the truth from you. We are broke, and we don't have any more animals. We have nothing left except ourselves and our land.
translation_verse: Na afe no bɛsenee no, wɔbaa no hɔ afe a otsia ebien no, na wɔbɛsee no dɛ, Yɛnnkɛfa esuma mo wura dɛ, hɛn sika nyinara asa; na hɛn mbowa nyinara yɛ mo wura dze; biribiara nnyi hɔ a ɔaka wɔ mo wura n’enyim, ka hɛn honam nye hɛn asaase ho;

english_verse: Don't let us starve and our land be ruined. If you'll give us grain to eat and seed to plant, we'll sell ourselves and our land to the king. We'll become his slaves.
translation_verse: ebɛnadze ntsi na yebowu w’enyim, hɛn nye hɛn asaase nyina? Fa edziban tɔ hɛn nye hɛn asaase, na hɛn nye hɛn asaase bɛyɛ asomfo ama Farao: na ma hɛn eduaba, ama yaatsena ase, na yeennwuwu, na amma asaase yi aannda pan.

english_verse: The famine became so severe that Joseph finally bought every piece of land in Egypt for the king
translation_verse: Nna Josef tɔɔ Egypt asaase nyinara maa Farao; na Egyptfo nyinara tontɔɔn hɔn haban, osiandɛ ɔkɔm no mu yɛɛ dzen wɔ hɔn do: na asaase no bɛyɛɛ Farao dze.

english_verse: except the priests. The king gave the priests a regular food allowance, so they did not have to sell their land.
translation_verse: Asɔfo no hɔn asaase nko na ɔanntɔ: osiandɛ Farao maa asɔfo no kyɛfa bi, na wodzii hɔn kyɛfa a Farao dze maa hɔn no; dɛm ntsi wɔanntɔn hɔn asaase.

english_verse: Then Joseph said to the people, “You and your land now belong to the king. I'm giving you seed to plant,
translation_verse: Nna Josef see nkorɔfo no dɛ, Hwɛ matɔ hom nye hom asaase ndɛ ama Farao: hwɛ, hom eduaba nye yi, na hom nkoduadua no wɔ asaase no do.

english_verse: but one fifth of your crops must go to the king. You can keep the rest as seed or as food for your families.”
translation_verse: Na dza ɔbɛyɛ nye dɛ, hom dze ekuadze yi mu mbumu enum enum bɛma Farao, na anan no ayɛ homankasa homdze, ma wɔdze bi edua haban mu, na ɔayɛ hom, nye hom fifo nye hom mbofraba edziban.

english_verse: “Sir, you have saved our lives!” they answered. “We are glad to be slaves of the king.”
translation_verse: Na wɔsee dɛ, Agye hɛn nkwa: ma yennya mo wura n’enyim enyimnyam, na yɛbɛyɛ Farao n’asomfo.

english_verse: Then Joseph made a law that one fifth of the harvest would always belong to the king. Only the priests did not lose their land. Jacob Becomes an Old Man
translation_verse: Na Josef dze yɛɛ mbra too hɔ wɔ Egypt asaase do ɔdze bɛpem ndɛ ara, dɛ wɔmfa mbumu enum enum mma Farao; gyedɛ asɔfo no hɔn asaase nkotsee na annyɛ Farao dze.

english_verse: The people of Israel made their home in the land of Goshen, where they became prosperous and had large families.
translation_verse: Na Israel tsenaa Egypt wɔ Gosen asaase do; na wonyaa egyapadze wɔ hɔ, na wɔwowoe, na wɔdɔɔr pii. Jakob Ne Samansew

english_verse: When Jacob knew he did not have long to live, he called in Joseph and said, “If you really love me, you must make a solemn promise not to bury me in Egypt.
translation_verse: Na mber a Israel dze bowu no bɛnee no: ɔfrɛɛ ne ba Josef, na ɔsee no dɛ, Sɛ afei menya w’enyim enyimnyam a, meserɛ wo, fa wo nsa to m’aserɛ ase, na enye me ndzi no ayamuyie mu nye nokwar mu; meserɛ wo, mma nnsie me wɔ Egypt:

english_verse: Instead, bury me in the place where my ancestors are buried.” “I will do what you have asked,” Joseph answered.
translation_verse: na sɛ mokɔ me nsamankyir a, ɔno soa me fi Egypt, na kesie me wɔ m’egyanom hɔn esiei. Na ɔsee dɛ, Mebɛyɛ dɛ mbrɛ aka no ara.

english_verse: “Will you give me your word?” Jacob asked. “Yes, I will,” Joseph promised. After this, Jacob bowed down and prayed at the head of his bed.
translation_verse: Na Jakob see dɛ, Dzi nsew kyerɛ me: nna Josef dzii nsew kyerɛɛ no. Na Israel bɔɔ no mu adze wɔ mpa no n’etsifi.

english_verse: Joseph was told that his father Jacob had become very sick. So Joseph went to see him and took along his two sons, Manasseh and Ephraim.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ iyinom ekyir no, wɔkaa kyerɛɛ Josef dɛ, Hwɛ, w’egya yar: na ɔfaa ne mbabanyin beenu no, Manaasɛ nye Efraim kaa no ho.

english_verse: When Joseph arrived, someone told Jacob, “Your son Joseph has come to see you.” Jacob sat up in bed, but it took almost all his strength.
translation_verse: Na obi kaa kyerɛɛ Jakob dɛ, Hwɛ, wo ba Josef reba wo nkyɛn: na Israel miaa n’enyi, na ɔtsenaa mpa do.

english_verse: Jacob told Joseph: God All-Powerful appeared to me at Luz in the land of Canaan, where he gave me his blessing
translation_verse: Na Jakob see Josef dɛ, Otumfo Nyankopɔn yii noho kyerɛɛ me wɔ Luz wɔ Keenan asaase do, na ohyiraa me,

english_verse: and promised, “I will give you a large family with many descendants that will grow into a nation. And I am giving you this land that will belong to you and your family forever.”
translation_verse: na ɔsee me dɛ, Hwɛ, mebɛma awo, na adɔr, na mebɛyɛ wo aman asafo; na medze asaase yi ama w’asefo a wobedzi w’ekyir ma wɔdze ayɛ daa egyapadze.

english_verse: Then Jacob went on to say: Joseph, your two sons Ephraim and Manasseh were born in Egypt, but I accept them as my own, just as Reuben and Simeon are mine.
translation_verse: Na afei wo mba beenu a ewoo hɔn wɔ Egypt asaase do ansaana mereba wo nkyɛn wɔ Egypt no, wɔyɛ medze; Efraim nye Manaasɛ, bɛyɛ medze, dɛ Reuben nye Simeon ara so.

english_verse: Any children you have later will be considered yours, but their inheritance will come from Ephraim and Manasseh.
translation_verse: Na wo mba a hɔn ekyir ebɔwo hɔn no, na wɔbɛyɛ wodze; wɔdze hɔn nuanom dzin bɛfrɛ hɔn wɔ hɔn egyapadze mu.

english_verse: Unfortunately, your mother Rachel died in Canaan after we had left northern Syria and before we reached Bethlehem. And I had to bury her along the way.
translation_verse: Na emi dze, mifi Paddan mebae no, Rahel wuu m’enyim wɔ kwan mu wɔ Keenan asaase do, aber a aka ɔkwan kakraabi ma edu Efrat no: na misiee no Efrat kwan mu hɔ .

english_verse: Jacob turned to Joseph and told him, “For many years I thought you were dead and that I would never see you again. But now God has even let me live to see your children.”
translation_verse: Na Israel see Josef dɛ, Manndwen dɛ nkyɛ mubohu w’enyim: na hwɛ, Nyankopɔn ama mehu wo mba so.

english_verse: Then Joseph made his sons move away from Jacob's knees, and Joseph bowed down in front of him with his face to the ground.
translation_verse: Na Josef yii hɔn wɔ n’anan ntamu bae; na ɔbɔɔ no mu adze dze n’enyim boom famu.

english_verse: After Joseph got up, he brought his two sons over to Jacob again. He led his younger son Ephraim to the left side of Jacob and his older son Manasseh to the right.
translation_verse: Na Josef faa hɔn beenu nyina, ne nsa enyimfa dze Efraim mu wɔ Israel n’abankum, na ne nsa abankum dze Manaasɛ mu wɔ Israel n’enyimfa, na ɔdze hɔn piin ne nkyɛn.

english_verse: But before Jacob gave them his blessing, he crossed his arms, putting his right hand on the head of Ephraim and his left hand on the head of Manasseh.
translation_verse: Na Israel tseen ne nsa enyimfa, na ɔdze too Efraim a ɔyɛ kakraba no n’etsifi, na ɔdze ne nsa abankum too Manaasɛ n’etsifi, ɔboapa ara yɛɛ ne nsa dɛm; osiandɛ Manaasɛ nye abakan.

english_verse: Then he gave Joseph his blessing and said: My grandfather Abraham and my father Isaac worshiped the Lord God. He has been with me all my life,
translation_verse: Na ohyiraa Josef, na ɔsee dɛ, Nyankopɔn no a m’egyanom Abraham nye Isaak wɔnantseew n’enyim no, Nyankopɔn no a ɔahwɛ me mo nkwaber nyinara mu ma ɔdze abɛpem ndɛ no,

english_verse: and his angel has kept me safe. Now I pray that he will bless these boys and that my name and the names of Abraham and Isaac will live on because of them. I ask God to give them many children and many descendants as well.
translation_verse: sor abɔfo no a oeyi me efi mbusu nyinara mu no, onhyira mbofra yi: na wɔmma wɔmfa me dzin nye m’egyanom Abraham nye Isaak hɔn dzin mfrɛ hɔn; na wɔmma wonnyin wɔnyɛ ɔdodow wɔ asaase do.

english_verse: Joseph did not like it when he saw his father place his right hand on the head of the younger son. So he tried to move his father's right hand from Ephraim's head and place it on Manasseh.
translation_verse: Na aber a Josef hun dɛ n’egya dze ne nsa enyimfa ato Efraim n’etsifi no, ɔannyɛ no abodwe: na osuoo n’egya ne nsa mu dɛ oriyi wɔ Efraim n’etsifi ato Manaasɛ n’etsifi.

english_verse: Joseph said, “Father, you have made a mistake. This is the older boy. Put your right hand on him.”
translation_verse: Na Josef see n’egya dɛ, ɔnntse dɛm, m’egya: na abakan no nye yi; fa wo nsa enyimfa to n’etsifi.

english_verse: But his father said, “Son, I know what I am doing. It's true that Manasseh's family will someday become a great nation. But Ephraim will be even greater than Manasseh, because his descendants will become many great nations.”
translation_verse: Na n’egya ammpen, na ɔsee dɛ, Minyim, me ba, minyim: ɔno so bɛyɛ ɔman, na ɔno so bɛyɛ kɛse: naaso no nua kuma bɛyɛ kɛse asen no, na n’asefo bɛyɛ amanaman pii.

english_verse: Jacob told him that in the future the people of Israel would ask God's blessings on one another by saying, “I pray for God to bless you as much as he blessed Ephraim and Manasseh.” Jacob put Ephraim's name first to show that he would be greater than Manasseh.
translation_verse: Na ohyiraa hɔn da no see dɛ, ɔwo na Israel dze wo behyira dɛ, Nyankopɔn mma yɛ dɛ Efraim nye dɛ Manaasɛ: na ɔdze Efraim dzii Manaasɛ ne kan.

english_verse: After that, Jacob said, “Joseph, you can see that I won't live much longer. But God will be with you and will lead you back to the land he promised our family long ago.
translation_verse: Na Israel see Josef dɛ, Hwɛ, muruwu: na Nyankopɔn bedzi hom ekyir, na ɔdze hom akɔ hom egyanom hɔn asaase do bio.

english_verse: Jacob called his sons together and said: My sons, I am Jacob, your father Israel.
translation_verse: Na Jakob frɛɛ ne mbabanyin, na ɔsee dɛ: Hom mboa hom ano, na menka dza ndaamba ɔbɛba hom do nkyerɛ hom.

english_verse: Come, gather around, as I tell your future.
translation_verse: Hom nhyia, na wontsie, hom Jakob mba; Na wontsie hom egya Israel.

english_verse: Reuben, you are my oldest, born at the peak of my powers; you were an honored leader.
