translation_verse: Reuben, ɔwo nye m’abakan, mo tum, nye m’ahoɔdzen ahyɛse; Nyia ɔkyɛn wɔ enyidzi mu na nyia ɔkyɛn wɔ tum mu.

english_verse: Uncontrollable as a flood, you slept with my wife and disgraced my bed. And so you no longer deserve the place of honor.
translation_verse: Ihihim dɛ nsu, ennkɛkyɛn; Osiandɛ efoow w’egya ne mpa: Nna iguu ho fi: efoow m’adae.

english_verse: Simeon and Levi, you are brothers, each a gruesome sword.
translation_verse: Simeon nye Levi wɔyɛ enuanom; Hɔn mfona yɛ enyiber akodze.

english_verse: I never want to take part in your plans or deeds. You slaughtered people in your anger, and you crippled cattle for no reason.
translation_verse: Ao me kra, mma mmba hɔn apam mu; M’enyimnyam, mma mmfora hɔn mboano mu; Osiandɛ hɔn ebufuw mu na wokum nyimpa, Na hɔn ahopɔw mu na wotwitwaa nantwi anantsir.

english_verse: Now I place a curse on you because of your fierce anger. Your descendants will be scattered among the tribes of Israel.
translation_verse: Ndom nka hɔn ebufuw, osiandɛ ɔyɛ hyew; Na hɔn enyiber, osiandɛ no mu yɛ dzen: Mebɛpaapaa hɔn mu wɔ Jakob mu, Na mabɔ hɔn apetse wɔ Israel mu.

english_verse: Judah, you will be praised by your brothers; they will bow down to you, as you defeat your enemies.
translation_verse: Juda, ɔwo na wo nuanom beyi wo ayɛw: Wo nsa bɛhyɛ w’atamfo hɔn kɔn do; W’egya mba bɔbɔ hɔn mu adze wɔ w’enyim.

english_verse: My son, you are a lion ready to eat your victim! You are terribly fierce; no one will bother you.
translation_verse: Juda yɛ ogyata ba; Abɔgyanam ara na edze enyin, me ba: ɔkotowee, obutuuw hɔ dɛ ogyata, Na dɛ ogyataber; woana na obekenyan no?

english_verse: You will have power and rule until nations obey you and come bringing gifts.
translation_verse: Ahempoma nnkefi Juda nsa, Nna mbrahyɛfo poma nnkefi n’anan ntamu, Kɛpem dɛ Siloh bɛba; Na ɔno na aman bɛyɛ setsie ama no.

english_verse: You will tie your donkey to a choice grapevine and wash your clothes in wine from those grapes.
translation_verse: Ɔdze n’asoasopɔnkɔba amontan wenyindua, Na n’asoasopɔnkɔber ba wɔ wenyindua pa ho; ɔahor ne ntar wɔ wenyin mu, Na n’akataho wɔ wenyinamba bɔgya mu:

english_verse: Your eyes are darker than wine, your teeth whiter than milk.
translation_verse: N’enyiwa yɛ dɛnn sen nsa memen, Na numfonsu ama ne se ho ahoa fitaa.

english_verse: Zebulun, you will settle along the seashore and provide safe harbors as far north as Sidon.
translation_verse: Zebulun bɛtsena po berekyir: Na ɔbɛyɛ mbrɛ ahɛn gyinagyina; Na ɔnye Zidon bɔbɔ ɔhe.

english_verse: Issachar, you are a strong donkey resting in the meadows.
translation_verse: Isakar yɛ asoaso a no ho yɛ dzen, Obutuuw nguamban ntamu:

english_verse: You found them so pleasant that you worked too hard and became a slave.
translation_verse: Na ohun ahomgye bea dɛ oye, Na asaase no dɛ ɔyɛ fɛw; Na ɔbrɛɛ n’abatsir adze dze soae, Na ɔbɛyɛɛ ɔsomfo wɔ edwumadzen mu.

english_verse: Dan, you are the tribe that will bring justice to Israel.
translation_verse: Dan bobua no nkorɔfo atsɛn, Dɛ Israel mbusuakuw mu kor.

english_verse: You are a snake that bites the heel of a horse, making its rider fall.
translation_verse: Dan bɛyɛ ɔwɔ a ɔbew kwan mu, Ehurutsia a ɔda akwantsen, ɔsɔw ɔpɔnkɔ nantsir, na no do atsefo no obu dua n’ekyir.

english_verse: Gad, you will be attacked, then attack your attackers.
translation_verse: Gad, dɔm bɛhyɛ no do: Na ɔno so bɛhyɛ hɔn etsia hɔn anantsir.

english_verse: Asher, you will eat food fancy enough for a king.
translation_verse: Aser n’edziban fi no mu bɔtɔ angoa, Na ɔbɛma ahemfo adɔkɔdɔkɔdze.

english_verse: Naphtali, you are a wild deer with lovely fawns.
translation_verse: Naftali yɛ ɔtrɔmber a woegyaa no: ɔka ahomkasɛm.

english_verse: Joseph, you are a fruitful vine growing near a stream and climbing a wall.
translation_verse: Josef yɛ dua a ɔsow, Abaw a ɔsow wɔ nsunyiwa ho; No nkorbata tratra ban.

english_verse: Enemies attacked with arrows, refusing to show mercy.
translation_verse: Egyentofo no eyi no awerɛhow kɛsenara, Na wɔatotow no, na wɔayɛ no enyienyi:

english_verse: But you stood your ground, swiftly shooting back with the help of Jacob's God, the All-Powerful One— his name is the Shepherd, Israel's mighty rock.
translation_verse: Na n’egyen dze ɔwɔ hɔ pintsinn, Na ne nsa n’abasa no mu yɛ dzen, ɔnam Jakob no Tumfo Kor no ne nsa do, Hɔ na oguanhwɛfo no, Israel no bo no ofi.

english_verse: Your help came from the God your father worshiped, from God All-Powerful. God will bless you with rain and streams from the earth; he will bless you with many descendants.
translation_verse: Mekyerɛ w’egya ne Nyankopɔn a ɔbɔboa wo no, Na Otumfo a ɔdze ɔsor nhyira, Bun a ɔda daadzetsirmu ne nhyira, Enumfo mu, nye yafun mu nhyira, Behyira wo no,

english_verse: My son, the blessings I give are better than the promise of ancient mountains or eternal hills. Joseph, I pray these blessings will come to you, because you are the leader of your brothers.
translation_verse: W’egya ne nhyira Akyɛn daa mbepɔw ne nhyira ɔdze kodu tsetse mbepɔw no n’ano no ho ara: ɔbɛtsena Josef n’etsifi, Nyia wɔtseew no fii no nuanom ho no n’etsifi pampamu.

english_verse: Benjamin, you are a fierce wolf, destroying your enemies morning and evening.
translation_verse: Benjamin yɛ pataku a ɔtsetsew mbowa mu: Obedzi abɔgyanam anapa, Na ewimbir no ɔakyɛ asadze. Jakob No Wu

english_verse: These are the twelve tribes of Israel, and this is how Jacob gave each of them their proper blessings. Jacob's Death
translation_verse: Woyinom nyinara nye Israel mbusuakuw duebien no: na iyi na hɔn egya kaa kyerɛɛ hɔn na ɔdze hyiraa hɔn no; hɔn nyinara mbrɛ hɔn nhyira tsetsee nkorkor, na ohyiraa hɔn.

english_verse: Both the cave and the land that goes with it were bought from the Hittites.
translation_verse: haban no nye ɔbo tu a ɔwɔ mu no a wɔtɔɔ no wɔ Hetmba hɔ no a.

english_verse: When Jacob had finished giving these instructions to his sons, he lay down on his bed and died.
translation_verse: Na Jakob wiee ne nsamansew a ɔhyɛɛ ne mba no ara, nna ɔkaa n’anan boaa ano dze guu mpa no do, na ogyaa no sunsum mu, na otsiiw n’egyanom wɔ samanadze.

english_verse: Joseph started crying, then leaned over to hug and kiss his father.
translation_verse: Na Josef twae hwee n’egya n’enyim, na osui guu no do, na ɔfeew n’ano.

english_verse: Joseph gave orders for Jacob's body to be embalmed,
translation_verse: Na Josef hyɛɛ no nkowaa a wɔhyɛ fun edur no dɛ wɔnhyɛ n’egya akyenkyendur: na wɔhyɛɛ Israel akyenkyendur.

english_verse: When the time of mourning was over, Joseph said to the Egyptian leaders, “If you consider me your friend, please speak to the king for me.
translation_verse: Na nda a wɔdze suu no no twaa mu no, Josef kaa kyerɛɛ Farao ne fifo, dɛ, Sɛ afei menya hom enyim enyimnyam a, meserɛ hom, wɔnka no wɔ Farao n’asomu, dɛ,

english_verse: Just before my father died, he made me promise to bury him in his burial cave in Canaan. If the king will give me permission to go, I will come back here.”
translation_verse: M’egya maa midzii nsew dɛ, Hwɛ, muruwu: na nda a mabɔ ama moho wɔ Keenan asaase do no mu hɔ na kesie me. Dɛm ntsi, afei meserɛ wo, ma monkɔ, na minkesie m’egya, na membra bio.

english_verse: The king answered, “Go to Canaan and keep your promise to your father.”
translation_verse: Na Farao see dɛ, Kɔ, na kesie w’egya, dɛ mbrɛ ɔmaa idzii nsew no ara.

english_verse: After crossing the Jordan River, Joseph stopped at Atad's threshing place, where they all mourned and wept seven days for Jacob.
translation_verse: Na wɔbaa Atad ewuporoweebea a ɔwɔ Jordan egya no, na hɔ na wotwaa kwadwom kɛse a no mu yɛ dzen papanara: na Josef suu n’egya nda esuon.

english_verse: The Canaanites saw this and said, “The Egyptians are in great sorrow.” Then they named the place “Egypt in Sorrow.”
translation_verse: Na hɔn a wɔtse asaase no do no, Keenanfo no, hun osu a wosuu no wɔ Atad ewuporoweebea no, wɔsee dɛ, Iyi yɛ eyi kɛse ma Egyptfo: dɛm ntsi wɔfrɛɛ ne dzin Abel-mizraim a ɔwɔ Jordan egya.

english_verse: So Jacob's sons did just as their father had instructed.
translation_verse: Na ne mba yɛɛ no dɛ mbrɛ ɔhyɛɛ hɔn no ara:

english_verse: They took him to Mamre in Canaan and buried him in Machpelah Cave, the burial place Abraham had bought from Ephron the Hittite.
translation_verse: na ne mba soaa no kɔr Keenan asaase do, na wokesiee no wɔ Makepela haban mu ɔbo tu no mu, dza Abraham dze kaa haban no ho tɔɔ no wɔ Hetnyi Efron hɔ, dze yɛɛ efunsiebea wɔ Mamre enyim no a.

english_verse: After the funeral, Joseph, his brothers, and everyone else returned to Egypt. Joseph's Promise to His Brothers
translation_verse: Na Josef siee n’egya wiei ekyir no, ɔnoara nye no nuanom no, nye hɔn a wɔnye no kɔree kesiee n’egya no nyinara sanee kɔr Egypt. Josef Gye No Nuanom Anoyie

english_verse: After Jacob died, Joseph's brothers said to each other, “What if Joseph still hates us and wants to get even with us for all the cruel things we did to him?”
translation_verse: Na Josef no nuanom no hun dɛ hɔn egya ewu no, wɔsee dɛ, Bɛyɛ na Josef atan hɛn, na bɔn a yɛdze yɛɛ no no nyinara oesusu mbrɛ ɔtse no ara atɔ hɛn do wer.

english_verse: So they sent this message to Joseph: Before our father died,
translation_verse: Na wɔsomae ma wɔkɛsee Josef dɛ, Ansaana w’egya ruwu no ɔhyɛɛ dɛ,

english_verse: he told us, “You did some cruel and terrible things to Joseph, but you must ask him to forgive you.” Now we ask you to please forgive the terrible things we did. After all, we serve the same God that your father worshiped. When Joseph heard this, he started crying.
translation_verse: Dɛm na hom nka nkyerɛ Josef, Afei meserɛ wo, fa wo nuanom hɔn mbrato, nye hɔn bɔn kyɛ hɔn, dɛ wɔyɛɛ wo bɔn: na afei yɛserɛ wo, fa w’egya ne Nyankopɔn no nkowaa hɔn bɔn kyɛ hɔn. Na Josef sui wɔ aber a wɔkaa dɛm kyerɛɛ no no.

english_verse: Right then, Joseph's brothers came and bowed down to the ground in front of him and said, “We are your slaves.”
translation_verse: Na no nuanom no so kɔree, na wokotwitwae hwehwee n’enyim; na wɔsee dɛ, Hwɛ, yɛyɛ wo nkowaa.

english_verse: But Joseph told them, “Don't be afraid! I have no right to change what God has decided.
translation_verse: Na Josef see hɔn dɛ, Mma hom nnsuro: ana migyina Nyankopɔn ananmu a?

english_verse: You tried to harm me, but God made it turn out for the best, so that he could save all these people, as he is now doing.
translation_verse: Hom dze, hom dween dɛ wɔreyɛ me bɔn; na Nyankopɔn dze ɔdween dɛ oye, ma ɔaayɛ dɛ mbrɛ ndɛ ɔayɛ yi, ma wɔaagye nkorɔfo piinara nkwa.

english_verse: Don't be afraid! I will take care of you and your children.” After Joseph said this, his brothers felt much better. Joseph's Death
translation_verse: Dɛm ntsi afei, mma hom nnsuro: mobɔhwɛ hom, nye hom mbofraba. Na ɔkaa hɔn akoma too hɔn yamu, na ɔkasaa ayamuyie mu kyerɛɛ hɔn. Josef No Wu

english_verse: Joseph lived in Egypt with his brothers until he died at the age of
translation_verse: Na Josef tsenaa Egypt, ɔnoara nye n’egya ne fifo: na Josef dzii mfe ɔha nye du.

english_verse: Joseph lived long enough to see Ephraim's children and grandchildren. He also lived to see the children of Manasseh's son Machir, and he welcomed them into his family.
translation_verse: Na Josef hun Efraim ne mbanana a otsia ebiasa: Manaasɛ ne babanyin Makir ne mba so wɔwoo hɔn guu Josef n’aserɛ do.

english_verse: Before Joseph died, he told his brothers, “I won't live much longer. But God will take care of you and lead you out of Egypt to the land he promised Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
translation_verse: Na Josef see no nuanom no dɛ, Morobɔkɔ me nsamankyir: na ampaara Nyankopɔn bɛba abɛsera hom, na oebeyi hom efi asaase yi do akɔ asaase a ɔkaa ho ntam kyerɛɛ Abraham, Isaak nye Jakob no no do.

english_verse: Now promise me that you will take my body with you when God leads you to that land.”
translation_verse: Na Josef maa Israelmba dzii nsew dɛ, Ampaara, Nyankopɔn bɛba abɛsera hom, na hom nsoa me mbiew mfi ha nkɔ.

english_verse: After Joseph, his brothers, and everyone else in that generation had died,
translation_verse: Na Josef, nye no nuanom nyinara, nye ebusuasantsen no nyinara, wuwui.

english_verse: the people of Israel became so numerous that the whole region of Goshen was full of them.
translation_verse: Na Israelmba no wowoe, na wɔdɔɔr menyamenya, na hɔn dodow dɔɔ so, na hɔn ho yɛɛ dzen papaara; na wɔhyɛɛ asaase no do ma.

english_verse: Many years later a new king came to power. He did not know what Joseph had done for Egypt,
translation_verse: Na ɔhen fofor bi soɛree wɔ Egypt a nna onnyim Josef.

english_verse: and he told the Egyptians: There are too many of those Israelites in our country, and they are becoming more powerful than we are.
translation_verse: Na ɔkaa kyerɛɛ ne man dɛ, Hwɛ, Israelmba no dɔɔ so, na hɔn ho yɛ dzen sen hɛn:

english_verse: If we don't outsmart them, their families will keep growing larger. And if our country goes to war, they could easily fight on the side of our enemies and escape from Egypt.
translation_verse: hom mbra ma yɛnye hɔn ndzi no nyansa mu, amma wɔaanndɔɔ so, na aammba dɛ sɛ ɔsa bi ba a, wɔaannkɛka hɛn atamfo ho nye hɛn ammbɔko, na woeenntu emmfi asaase yi do annkɔ.

english_verse: The Egyptians put slave bosses in charge of the people of Israel and tried to wear them down with hard work. Those bosses forced them to build the cities of Pithom and Rameses, where the king could store his supplies.
translation_verse: Dɛm ntsi wɔhyehyɛɛ abɔmbaafo wɔ hɔn do dɛ wɔmma hɔn ndzesoadze nhyɛ hɔn do. Na wɔkyekyeer adzekora nkurow, Pitom nye Raamses, maa Farao.

english_verse: But even though the Israelites were mistreated, their families grew larger, and they took over more land. Because of this, the Egyptians feared them worse than before
translation_verse: Na mber dodow a wɔtseetsee hɔn kɛse no mbom na wɔdɔɔr kɛse na wɔtrɛe. Na Israelmba ho nketsenketse kyeer hɔn.

english_verse: and made them work so hard
translation_verse: Na Egyptfo yɛɛ Israelmba ma wodzii suhia:

english_verse: that their lives were miserable. The Egyptians were cruel to the people of Israel and forced them to make bricks and to mix mortar and to work in the fields.
translation_verse: na wɔmaa hɔn asetsena foon hɔn wɔ ndwumadzen mu, wɔ dɛtse nye ntayaa, nye hamudwuma ahorow nyinara mu. Na wɔmaa wodzii suhia wɔ hɔn som nyinara mu.

english_verse: Finally, the king called in Shiphrah and Puah, the two women who helped the Hebrew mothers when they gave birth.
translation_verse: Na Egypthen no kasaa kyerɛɛ Hebriwfo awogyefo a hɔn mu kor ne dzin dze Sifra, na ɔbaako so ne dzin dze Pua no:

english_verse: He told them, “If a Hebrew woman gives birth to a girl, let the child live. If the baby is a boy, kill him!”
translation_verse: na ɔsee dɛ, Sɛ hom regye Hebriwfo mbaa no awoo, na hom hu wɔ awoo egua no do dɛ ɔyɛ ɔbanyin a, ɔno hom nku no; na sɛ ɔyɛ ɔbabaa a, ɔno ɔntsena ase.

english_verse: But the two women were faithful to God and did not kill the boys, even though the king had told them to.
translation_verse: Na awogyefo no suroo Nyankopɔn, na wɔannyɛ dɛ mbrɛ Egypthen no hyɛɛ hɔn no, na mbom wɔgyee mbanyimba no nkwa.

english_verse: The king called them in again and asked, “Why are you letting those baby boys live?”
translation_verse: Na Egypthen no frɛɛ awogyefo no, na obisaa hɔn dɛ, Ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na hom ayɛ dɛm adze yi, na hom agye mba mbanyimba no nkwa yi?

english_verse: They answered, “Hebrew women have their babies much quicker than Egyptian women. By the time we arrive, their babies are already born.”
translation_verse: Na awogyefo no see Farao dɛ, Osiandɛ Hebriwfo mbaa no wɔnntse dɛ Egyptfo mbaa; na hɔn dze, wɔyɛ pirim pirim, na awogyefo nnyae nndur hɔn nkyɛn mpo nna wɔawo dada.

english_verse: until finally, the king gave a command to everyone in the nation, “As soon as a Hebrew boy is born, throw him into the Nile River! But you can let the girls live.”
translation_verse: Na Farao hyɛɛ no nkorɔfo nyinara dɛ, Ɔbabanyin biara a wɔbɔwo no no, hom nkɔtow no nkyen esutsen no mu, na ɔbabaa biara dze, hom mma ɔntsena ase.

english_verse: A man from the Levi tribe married a woman from the same tribe,
translation_verse: Na ɔbanyin bi fi Levi fie kɔwaar Levi babaa bi.

english_verse: and she later had a baby boy. He was a beautiful child, and she kept him inside for three months.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa no nyinsenii, na ɔwoo ɔbabanyin: na ohun dɛ ɔyɛ abofra a no ho yɛ fɛw no, ɔdze no sumaa abosoom ebiasa.

english_verse: But when she could no longer keep him hidden, she made a basket out of reeds and covered it with tar. She put him in the basket and placed it in the tall grass along the edge of the Nile River.
translation_verse: Na ɔdze no suma bio a ɔnnyɛ yie no, ɔfaa mbɛw adakaba, na ɔdze hua na aman seraam ho: na ɔdze abofra no too mu, na ɔdze kesii atsen no mu wɔ esutsen no no nkɔkɔnoa.

english_verse: The baby's older sister stood off at a distance to see what would happen to him.
translation_verse: Na n’akyerɛbaa gyinaa ekyirekyir, dɛ ɔrohwɛ dza ɔbɛyɛ no.

english_verse: About that time one of the king's daughters came down to take a bath in the river, while her servant women walked along the river bank. She saw the basket in the tall grass and sent one of them to pull it out of the water.
translation_verse: Na Farao ne babaa sianee baa esutsen no ho dɛ oruboguar; na ne mbaawa pasaar esutsen no nkyɛn. Ber a ohun adaka no wɔ atsen no mu no, ɔsomaa n’abaawa dɛ ɔnkɛfa mbra.

english_verse: When the king's daughter opened the basket, she saw the baby crying and felt sorry for him. She said, “This must be one of the Hebrew babies.”
translation_verse: Na obuee do, na ohun abofra no: na, hwɛ, akɔkowaba no sui. Na ohun no mbɔbɔr, na ɔsee dɛ, Hebriwfo mba no kor nye yi.

english_verse: At once the baby's older sister came up and asked, “Do you want me to get a Hebrew woman to take care of the baby for you?”
translation_verse: Nna abofra no n’akyerɛbaa no bisaa Farao babaa no dɛ, Ana monkɔ nkɛfrɛ Hebriwfo mbaa no mu onufomafo mbrɛ wo, ma ɔmma abofra no enumfo mma wo anaa?

english_verse: “Yes,” the king's daughter answered. So the girl brought the baby's mother,
translation_verse: Na Farao ne babaa no see no dɛ, Kɔ. Na akataasia no kɔree kɛfrɛɛ abofra no ne na.

english_verse: and the king's daughter told her, “Take care of this child, and I will pay you.” The baby's mother carried him home and took care of him.
translation_verse: Na Farao ne babaa no see no dɛ, Fa abofra yi kɔ, na kɛma no enumfo ma me, na medze w’akatua bɛma wo. Na ɔbaa no faa abofra no, na ɔkɛmaa no enumfo.

english_verse: And when he was old enough, she took him to the king's daughter, who adopted him. She named him Moses because she said, “I pulled him out of the water.” Moses Escapes from Egypt
translation_verse: Na abofra no nyinii, na ɔdze no brɛɛ Farao ne babaa no, na ɔbɛyɛɛ ne babanyin. Na ɔtoo no dzin Moses, na ɔsee dɛ, Osiandɛ mefaa no nsu mu. Moses Guan Kɔ Midian

english_verse: After Moses had grown up, he went out to where his own people were hard at work, and he saw an Egyptian beating one of them.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ nda no mu no, aber a Moses nyinii no, ofiir edzi kɔr no nuanom nkyɛn, na ɔkɔhwɛɛ hɔn ndzesoadze. Ohun dɛ Egyptnyi bi robor Hebriwnyi bi, no nuanom mu baako.

english_verse: Moses looked around to see if anyone was watching, then he killed the Egyptian and hid his body in the sand.
translation_verse: Na ɔhwɛɛ ha hwɛɛ ha, na ohun dɛ nyimpa bi nnyi hɔ no, ɔbɔɔ Egyptnyi no kum no, na ɔdze no hyɛɛ anhwea mu.

english_verse: When Moses went out the next day, he saw two Hebrews fighting. So he went to the man who had started the fight and asked, “Why are you beating up one of your own people?”
translation_verse: Na n’adzekyee no ofiir edzi, na, hwɛ, Hebriwfo beenu roko: na obisaa nyia odzi fɔ no dɛ, Ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na ebɔ wo nyɛnko yi?

english_verse: The man answered, “Who put you in charge of us and made you our judge? Are you planning to kill me, just like you killed that Egyptian?” This frightened Moses because he was sure that people must have found out what had happened.
translation_verse: Na obuaa no dɛ, Woana yɛɛ wo ɔpanyin nye ɔsɛndzifo wɔ hɛn do? Edwen dɛ iruboku me dɛ mbrɛ ikum Egyptnyi no anaa? Na Moses suroi, na ɔsee dɛ, Ampaara asɛm no epue.

english_verse: When the king heard what Moses had done, he wanted to kill him. But Moses escaped and went to the land of Midian. One day, when Moses was sitting by a well,
translation_verse: Ber a Farao tsee asɛm yi no, ɔhwehwɛɛ dɛ nkyɛ oboku Moses. Na Moses guanee fii Farao enyim, na ɔkɛtsenaa Midian asaase do: na ɔkɔkoo nsubura bi ho.

english_verse: the seven daughters of Jethro, the priest of Midian, came up to water their father's sheep and goats.
translation_verse: Nna Midian ɔsɔfo no wɔ mbabaa beesuon; na wɔbae bɔtoow nsu, na wɔdze hyehyɛɛ ndaasee no amaama dɛ wɔrema hɔn egya no nguan anom.

english_verse: Some shepherds tried to chase them away, but Moses came to their rescue and watered their animals.
translation_verse: Na nguanhwɛfo bi bae beyii hɔn edzi: na Moses soɛree boaa hɔn, na ɔmaa hɔn nguan no nomee.

english_verse: When Jethro's daughters returned home, their father asked, “Why have you come back so early today?”
translation_verse: Na wɔbaa hɔn egya Reuel nkyɛn no, obisaa hɔn dɛ, Ɔyɛ dɛn na ndɛ hom aba ntsɛm dɛm yi?

english_verse: They answered, “An Egyptian rescued us from the shepherds, and he even watered our sheep and goats.”
translation_verse: Na wɔsee dɛ, Egyptnyi bi na oyii hɛn fii nguanhwɛfo no nsamu, na ɔtoow nsu mpo maa hɛn, na ɔmaa nguan no nomee.

english_verse: “Where is he?” Jethro asked. “Why did you leave him out there? Invite him to eat with us.”
translation_verse: Na obisaa ne mbabaa no dɛ, Na ɔwɔ hen? Ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na hom adan ɔbanyin no egya yi? Wɔnkɛfrɛ no ma ombedzidzi.

english_verse: Moses agreed to stay on with Jethro, who later let his daughter Zipporah marry Moses.
translation_verse: Na Moses peen dɛ ɔnye ɔbanyin no bɛtsena: na ɔpanyin no dze ne babaa Zipora maa Moses awar.

english_verse: And when she had a son, Moses said, “I will name him Gershom, since I am a foreigner in this country.”
translation_verse: Na ɔwoo ɔbabanyin, na ɔtoo no dzin Gersom: na ɔsee dɛ, Osiandɛ mayɛ ɔmamforanyi wɔ ananaben asaase do.

english_verse: After the death of the king of Egypt, the Israelites still complained because they were forced to be slaves. They cried out for help,
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ nda pii guu mu no, Egypthen no wui: na nkowaasom no ntsi Israelmba pempenee, na wosui, na hɔn su fowee kɔr Nyankopɔn nkyɛn.

english_verse: and God heard their loud cries. He did not forget the promise he had made to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob,
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn tsee hɔn nkrumsi, na Nyankopɔn kaa n’ahyɛmu a ɔnye Abraham, Isaak, na Jakob dzii no.

english_verse: and because he knew what was happening to his people, he felt sorry for them.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn hwɛɛ Israelmba no, na ohun hɔn haw.

english_verse: One day, Moses was taking care of the sheep and goats of his father-in-law Jethro, the priest of Midian, and Moses decided to lead them across the desert to Sinai, the holy mountain.
translation_verse: Dabi a nna Moses rohwɛ n’asew Jetro, Midian ɔsɔfo no, no nguan no, ɔkaa hɔn kɔr sar no ekyir noho, na odur Nyankopɔn bepɔw Horeb ho.

english_verse: There an angel of the Lord appeared to him from a burning bush. Moses saw that the bush was on fire, but it was not burning up.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE bɔfoe beyii noho edzi kyerɛɛ no wɔ haban bi finimfin gyaframee mu: na ɔhwɛe, na, hwɛ, haban no redzɛw ogya, naaso haban no nnhyew.

english_verse: “This is strange!” he said to himself. “I'll go over and see why the bush isn't burning up.”
translation_verse: Na Moses see dɛ, Iyi dze, mebɛman akɔhwɛ adzehu kɛse yi, siantsir a haban no nnhyew.

english_verse: When the Lord saw Moses coming near, he called him by name from the bush, and Moses answered, “Here I am.”
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE hun dɛ ɔaman rohwɛ no, Nyankopɔn fii haban no mu frɛɛ no, na ɔsee dɛ, Moses, Moses. Na ɔgyee do dɛ, Emi nye yi.

english_verse: God replied, “Don't come any closer. Take off your sandals—the ground where you are standing is holy.
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Mma nntwe mmpin ha: yiyi w’ananadze mpaboa no, na bea a igyina do no yɛ asaase krɔnkrɔn.

english_verse: I am the God who was worshiped by your ancestors Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” Moses was afraid to look at God, and so he hid his face.
translation_verse: Nkyii so ɔsee dɛ, Emi nye w’egyanom, Abraham, Isaak, nye Jakob Nyankopɔn no. Na Moses dze n’enyim sumae; osiandɛ osuroo dɛ ɔbɔhwɛ Nyankopɔn.

english_verse: The Lord said: I have seen how my people are suffering as slaves in Egypt, and I have heard them beg for my help because of the way they are being mistreated. I feel sorry for them,
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see dɛ, Ampaara mehu me man a wɔwɔ Egypt hɔn amandzehu, na matse hɔn su a hɔn abɔmbaafo no ntsi wosu no; na minyim hɔn awerɛhow ntsi;

english_verse: and I have come down to rescue them from the Egyptians. I will bring my people out of Egypt into a country where there is a lot of good land, rich with milk and honey. I will give them the land where the Canaanites, Hittites, Amorites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites now live.
translation_verse: na mesian dɛ miribeyi hɔn wɔ Egyptfo nsamu, na metutu hɔn mefi asaase no do makɔ asaase pa a ɔso do, asaase a numfonsu nye ewo pem wɔ do; Keenanfo, Hitifo, Amorifo, Perizfo, Hivifo, nye Jebusfo hɔn tsenabew no.

english_verse: My people have begged for my help, and I have seen how cruel the Egyptians are to them.
translation_verse: Na afei, hwɛ, Israel hɔn su edu m’enyim: nkyii so mehu ɔhaw a Egyptfo dze haw hɔn no.

english_verse: Now go to the king! I am sending you to lead my people out of his country.
translation_verse: Dɛm ntsi afei bra, na morosoma wo Farao hɔ, ma eekeyi me man Israelmba no efi Egypt efir.

english_verse: But Moses said, “Who am I to go to the king and lead your people out of Egypt?”
translation_verse: Na Moses see Nyankopɔn dɛ, Woana nye emi, na maakɔ Farao nkyɛn ekeyi Israelmba efi Egypt efir?

english_verse: God replied, “I will be with you. And you will know that I am the one who sent you, when you worship me on this mountain after you have led my people out of Egypt.”
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Ampaara mibedzi w’ekyir; na iyi bɛyɛ wo asɛnkyerɛdze dɛ mara na masoma wo. Na sɛ inya yi ɔman no fi Egypt ba a, hom bɔsom Nyankopɔn wɔ bepɔw yi do.

english_verse: Moses answered, “I will tell the people of Israel that the God their ancestors worshiped has sent me to them. But what should I say, if they ask me your name?”
translation_verse: Na Moses see Nyankopɔn dɛ, Hwɛ, sɛ mudu Israelmba nkyɛn, na mese hɔn dɛ, Hom egyanom Nyankopɔn na ɔasoma me hom hɔ; na wobisa me dɛ, Ne dzin dze dɛn a, menka dɛn menkyerɛ hɔn?

english_verse: God said to Moses: I am the eternal God. So tell them that the Lord, whose name is “I Am,” has sent you. This is my name forever, and it is the name that people must use from now on.
translation_verse: Na nkyii so Nyankopɔn see Moses dɛ, Dɛm na ka kyerɛ Israelmba, EWURADZE, hom egyanom Nyankopɔn no, Abraham Nyankopɔn, Isaak Nyankopɔn, nye Jakob Nyankopɔn no, na ɔasoma me hom hɔ: iyi nye me dzin daa, na iyi nye me nkaadze kɔ ebusuasantsen nyinara mu.

english_verse: Call together the leaders of Israel and tell them that the God who was worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob has appeared to you. Tell them I have seen how terribly they are being treated in Egypt,
translation_verse: Kɔ, na kɔboa Israel mpanyimfo ano, na ka kyerɛ hɔn dɛ, EWURADZE, hom egyanom Nyankopɔn no, Abraham, Isaak, nye Jakob Nyankopɔn no, eyi noho akyerɛ me, dɛ, Ɔsera na mabɛsera hom, na mehu dza wɔdze reyɛ hom wɔ Egypt no:

english_verse: and I promise to lead them out of their troubles. I will give them a land rich with milk and honey, where the Canaanites, Hittites, Amorites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites now live.
translation_verse: na mase dɛ, Mibeyi hom efi Egypt amandzehu no mu akɔ Keenanfo, Hitifo, Amorifo, Perizfo, Hivifo, nye Jebusfo asaase do, asaase a numfonsu nye ewo pem wɔ do.

english_verse: The leaders of Israel will listen to you. Then you must take them to the king of Egypt and say, “The Lord God of the Hebrews has appeared to us. Let us walk three days into the desert, where we can offer a sacrifice to him.”
translation_verse: Na wobetsie wo ndze: na ɔwo, nye Israel mpanyimfo nkɔ Egypthen no nkyɛn, na hom nse no dɛ, EWURADZE, Hebriwfo Nyankopɔn no, nye hɛn ehyia mu: na afei yɛserɛ wo, ma yɛnkɔ sar do ndaansa kwan nkɔbɔ afɔr mma EWURADZE, hɛn Nyankopɔn.

english_verse: But I know that the king of Egypt won't let you go unless something forces him to.
translation_verse: Na minyim dɛ Egypthen remma hom kwan ma wɔnnkɔ, mpo, nnyɛ nsa a ɔyɛ dzen do a.

english_verse: So I will use my mighty power to perform all kinds of miracles and strike down the Egyptians. Then the king will send you away.
translation_verse: Na mebɛtsen me nsa, na medze anwanwadze a mebɛyɛ wɔ no mu hɔ no mabɔ Egypt: na ɔno n’ekyir na ɔbɛma hom akɔ.

english_verse: After I punish the Egyptians, they will be so afraid of you that they will give you anything you want. You are my people, and I will let you take many things with you when you leave the land of Egypt.
translation_verse: Na mebɛma ɔman yi enyim aba nyam wɔ Egyptfo enyim: na ɔbɛba dɛ, sɛ hom rokɔ a, hom nnkɔkɔ no nsapan:

english_verse: Every Israelite woman will go to her Egyptian neighbors or to any Egyptian woman living with them and ask them for gold and silver jewelry and for their finest clothes. The Egyptians will give them to you, and you will put these fine things on your sons and daughters. Carry all this away when you leave Egypt.
translation_verse: na ɔbaa biara bebisa ne fisamenanyi, na ne fie ɔmamforanyi dwetɛ edwindze, nye sika edwindze, nye atam: na hom dze ahyehyɛ hom mbabanyin nye hom mbabaa; na hom bɛfa Egyptfo no afo.

english_verse: Moses asked the Lord, “Suppose everyone refuses to listen to my message, and no one believes that you really appeared to me?”
translation_verse: Na Moses buae see dɛ, hwɛ, wɔnnkɛgye me edzi, eso wonketsie me ndze: na wɔbɛka dɛ, EWURADZE nnyii noho nnkyerɛɛ wo.

english_verse: The Lord answered, “What's that in your hand?” “A walking stick,” Moses replied.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE bisaa no dɛ, Ebɛnadze na ɔdze wo nsamu no? Na ɔsee dɛ, Poma a.

english_verse: “Throw it down!” the Lord commanded. So Moses threw the stick on the ground. It immediately turned into a snake, and Moses jumped back.
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Tow kyen famu. Na ɔtowee kyeen famu, na ɔdaan abowatsena; na Moses fii n’enyim guanee.

english_verse: “Pick it up by the tail!” the Lord told him. And when Moses did this, the snake turned back into a walking stick.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Tsen wo nsa, na suo no dua mu:

english_verse: “Do this,” the Lord said, “and the Israelites will believe that you have seen me, the God who was worshiped by their ancestors Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.”
translation_verse: ama wɔaagye edzi dɛ EWURADZE, hɔn egyanom Nyankopɔn no, Abraham, Isaak nye Jakob Nyankopɔn no, eyi noho akyerɛ wo.

english_verse: Next, the Lord commanded Moses, “Put your hand inside your shirt.” Moses obeyed, and when he took it out, his hand had turned white as snow—like someone with leprosy.
translation_verse: Na nkyii so EWURADZE see no dɛ, Afei fa wo nsa hyɛ wo bo mu. Na ɔdze ne nsa hyɛɛ no bo mu: na oyii puei no, hwɛ, nna ne nsa ho egu kwata fitaa dɛ esukwankyɛba.

english_verse: Then the Lord said, “If no one believes either of these miracles, take some water from the Nile River and pour it on the ground. The water will immediately turn into blood.”
translation_verse: Na ɔbɛba dɛ, sɛ wɔanngye nsɛnkyerɛdze ebien yi mpo enndzi, nkyii so woenntsie wo ndze a, ɔno kɛsaw esutsen no mu nsu bi, na hue gu asaase kɛsɛɛ do: na nsu a ebɛsaw no wɔ esutsen no mu no ɔbɛdan bɔgya wɔ asaase kɛsɛɛ no do.

english_verse: Moses replied, “I have never been a good speaker. I wasn't one before you spoke to me, and I'm not one now. I am slow at speaking, and I can never think of what to say.”
translation_verse: Na Moses see EWURADZE dɛ, Ao, EWURADZE, m’ano nntsew, nkan no o, ofitsi aber a ɔwo enye w’akowaa kasae yi o: osiandɛ m’ano yɛ dur, na me tɛkyerɛma yɛ pembɔɔ.

english_verse: But the Lord answered, “Who makes people able to speak or makes them deaf or unable to speak? Who gives them sight or makes them blind? Don't you know that I am the one who does these things?
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see no dɛ, Woana na ɔyɛɛ nyimpa ano? Anaa woana na ɔyɛ nyimpa mmum, anaa otsitsifo, anaa ɔdzehufo, anaa onyifurafo? Ana nnyɛ emi EWURADZE bi a?

english_verse: Now go! When you speak, I will be with you and give you the words to say.”
translation_verse: Dɛm ntsi afei kɔ, na mibedzi w’ano ekyir, na makyerɛ wo dza ebɛka.

english_verse: Moses begged, “Lord, please send someone else to do it.”
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Ao EWURADZE, mepa wo kyɛw, dua nyia woara epɛ dɛ esoma no, ne nsa do soma.

english_verse: The Lord became angry with Moses and said: What about your brother Aaron, the Levite? I know he is a good speaker. He is already on his way here to visit you, and he will be happy to see you again.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE n’ebufuw dzɛɛw wɔ Moses do, na ɔsee dɛ, Ana wo nua Aaron Levinyi no nnyi hɔ bi a? Minyim dɛ obotum akasa yie. Na eso, hwɛ, orifi edzi nye wo ebehyia: na sɛ ohu wo a, n’akoma bɔtɔ ne yamu.

english_verse: Aaron will speak to the people for you, and you will be like me, telling Aaron what to say. I will be with both of you as you speak, and I will tell each of you what to do.
translation_verse: Na ɔbɛyɛ wo kyeame ama ɔman no: na ɔbɛba dɛ, ɔno na ɔbɛyɛ ano ama wo, na ɔwo aayɛ dɛ Nyankopɔn ama no.

english_verse: Now take this walking stick and use it to perform miracles. Moses Returns to Egypt
translation_verse: Na fa poma yi a edze bɛyɛ nsɛnkyerɛdze no kitsa wo nsamu. Moses San Kɔ Egypt

english_verse: Moses went to his father-in-law Jethro and asked, “Please let me return to Egypt to see if any of my people are still alive.” “All right,” Jethro replied. “I hope all goes well.”
translation_verse: Na Moses sanee kɔr n’asew Jetro nkyɛn, na ɔkɛsee no dɛ, Mepa wo kyɛw, ma monkɔ, na mensan nkɔ mo nuanom a wɔwɔ Egypt no nkyɛn, na monkɔhwɛ kyerɛ wɔda ho tse ase anaa. Na Jetro see Moses dɛ, Kɔ no asomdwee mu.

english_verse: But even before this, the Lord had told Moses, “Leave the land of Midian and return to Egypt. Everyone who wanted to kill you is now dead.”
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses wɔ Midian dɛ, Kɔ, san kɔ Egypt: na nyimpa a wɔhwehwɛ wo nkwa no nyinara ewuwu.

english_verse: So Moses put his wife and sons on donkeys and headed for Egypt, holding the walking stick that had the power of God.
translation_verse: Na Moses faa ne yer nye ne mbabanyin no, na ɔdze hɔn tsenatsenaa asoaso do, na ɔsanee dɛ ɔrokɔ Egypt asaase do: na Moses faa Nyankopɔn no poma no kitsaa ne nsamu.

english_verse: On the way the Lord said to Moses: When you get to Egypt, go to the king and work the miracles I have shown you. But I will make him so stubborn that he will refuse to let my people go.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Sɛ esan kɔ Egypt a, hwɛ na anwanwadze a medze mahyɛ wo nsa no nyinara yɛ no wɔ Farao enyim: na mibepirim n’akoma, na ɔnnkɛma ɔman no akɔ.

english_verse: Then tell him that I have said, “Israel is my first-born son,
translation_verse: Na se Farao dɛ, Mbrɛ EWURADZE se nye yi, Israel yɛ me babanyin, m’abakan a:

english_verse: and I commanded you to release him, so he could worship me. But you refused, and now I will kill your first-born son.” Zipporah's Son Is Circumcised
translation_verse: na mase wo dɛ, Ma me ba nkɔ nkɔsom me; na ɔwo ammpen ma ɔannkɔ: hwɛ, muboku wo babanyin, w’abakan no.

english_verse: One night while Moses was in camp, the Lord was about to kill him.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ ɔkwan do asoɛe no, EWURADZE hyiaa no, na ɔpɛɛ dɛ oboku no.

english_verse: But Zipporah circumcised her son with a flint knife. She touched his legs with the skin she had cut off and said, “My dear son, this blood will protect you.”
translation_verse: Nna Zipora faa ɔtwerɛbo, na ɔdze twaa ne babanyin no twatsia, na ɔtowee kyeen Moses n’ananadze; na ɔsee dɛ, Ampaara ɔwo yɛ bɔgya ho kun fofor dze ma me.

english_verse: So the Lord did not harm Moses. Then Zipporah said, “Yes, my dear, you are safe because of this circumcision.” Aaron Is Sent To Meet Moses
translation_verse: Na Ewuradze gyaa Moses. Na osian twatsiatwa no ntsi Zipora see dɛ, Bɔgya ho kun fofor nye wo no. Moses Hyia Aaron

english_verse: The Lord sent Aaron to meet Moses in the desert. So Aaron met Moses at Mount Sinai and greeted him with a kiss.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Aaron dɛ, Kɔ sar no do kehyia Moses. Na ɔkɔree, kehyiaa no wɔ Nyankopɔn ne bepɔw no ho, na ɔfeew n’ano.

english_verse: Moses told Aaron what God had sent him to say; he also told him about the miracles God had given him the power to perform.
translation_verse: Na Moses kaa nsɛm a EWURADZE dze somaa no no nyinara, nye nsɛnkyerɛdze a ɔdze hyɛɛ no no nyinara kyerɛɛ Aaron.

english_verse: Later they brought together the leaders of Israel,
translation_verse: Na Moses nye Aaron kɔree na wɔkɔboaboaa Israelmba mpanyimfo nyinara ano.

english_verse: and Aaron told them what the Lord had sent Moses to say. Then Moses worked the miracles for the people,
translation_verse: Na Aaron kaa nsɛm a EWURADZE kaa kyerɛɛ Moses no nyinara, na ɔyɛɛ nsɛnkyerɛdze no wɔ ɔman no enyim.

english_verse: and everyone believed. They bowed down and worshiped the Lord because they knew that he had seen their suffering and was going to help them.
translation_verse: Na ɔman no gyee dzii: na aber a wɔtsee dɛ EWURADZE abɛsera Israelmba, na ɔahwɛ hɔn amandzehu no wɔbobɔɔ hɔn tsir adze na wɔsɔree.

english_verse: Moses and Aaron went to the king of Egypt and told him, “The Lord God says, ‘Let my people go into the desert, so they can honor me with a celebration there.’ ”
translation_verse: Na n’ekyir no Moses nye Aaron bae, na wɔbɛsee Farao dɛ, Mbrɛ EWURADZE, Israel Nyankopɔn no se nye yi, Ma me man nkɔ, na wɔnkɛhyɛ fa mma me wɔ sar no do.

english_verse: “Who is this Lord and why should I obey him?” the king replied. “I refuse to let you and your people go!”
translation_verse: Na Farao see dɛ, Woana nye EWURADZE a mibetsie ne ndze na mama Israel akɔ? Minnyim EWURADZE, na nkyii so meremma Israel nnkɔ.

english_verse: They answered, “The Lord God of the Hebrews has appeared to us. Please let us walk three days into the desert where we can offer sacrifices to him. If you don't, he may strike us down with terrible troubles or with war.”
translation_verse: Na wɔsee dɛ, Hebriwfo Nyankopɔn nye hɛn ehyia: yɛpa wo kyɛw, ma yɛnkɔ sar no do ndaansa kwan, na yɛnkɔbɔ afɔr mma EWURADZE hɛn Nyankopɔn; mma ɔaammfa ɔyardɔm, anaa afona emmbotua hɛn.

english_verse: The king said, “Moses and Aaron, why are you keeping these people from working? Look how many you are keeping from doing their work. Now everyone get back to work!”
translation_verse: Na Farao see dɛ, Hwɛ, afei asaase no do nkorɔfo no adɔɔ so, na hom ma wɔhom fi hɔn ndzesoadze ho yi.

english_verse: That same day the king gave orders to his Egyptian slave bosses and to the Israelite men directly in charge of the Israelite slaves. He told them:
translation_verse: Na da no ara Farao hyɛɛ ɔman no hɔn abɔmbaafo, nye hɔn ahwɛdofo no dɛ,

english_verse: Don't give the slaves any more straw to put in their bricks. Force them to find their own straw wherever they can,
translation_verse: Mma hom mma ɔman no sar a wɔdze bɛyɛ ntayaa no bio dɛ nkan no: wɔmma hɔnara wɔnkɔ na wɔnkɔboaboa sar ano mma hɔnho.

english_verse: but they must make the same number of bricks as before. They are lazy, or else they would not beg me to let them go and sacrifice to their God.
translation_verse: Na ntayaa dodow a wɔyɛ no dada no, hom ngye hɔn; mma hom nnyi do bi: na wɔyɛ enyihaw; dɛm ntsi na wosu dɛ, Wɔmma yɛnkɔ nkɔbɔ afɔr mma hɛn Nyankopɔn no.

english_verse: Make them work so hard that they won't have time to listen to these lies.
translation_verse: Hom mma edwuma durdur nda nkorɔfo no do, ama wɔaayɛ mu edwuma aberɛ; na mma wonntsie nsɛnhun.

english_verse: The slave bosses and the men in charge of the slaves went out and told them, “The king says he will not give you any more straw.
translation_verse: Na ɔman no hɔn abɔmbaafo, nye hɔn ahwɛdofo no fiir edzi, na wɔbɛkaa kyerɛɛ ɔman no dɛ, Mbrɛ Farao se nye yi, Meremma hom sar bio.

english_verse: Go and find your own straw wherever you can, but you must still make as many bricks as before.”
translation_verse: Homara nkɔ nkɛfa sar wɔ mbrɛ hom benya bi: na worinnyi hom edwuma do biribiara.

english_verse: The slaves went all over Egypt, looking for straw.
translation_verse: Nna ɔman no petsee Egypt asaase nyina do kɔboaboaa mburonan ano dze yɛɛ sar.

english_verse: But the slave bosses were hard on them and kept saying, “Each day you have to make as many bricks as you did when you were given straw.”
translation_verse: Na abɔmbaafo no piapiaa hɔn dɛ, Hom nwie hom da kor edwuma wɔ ne da mu, dɛ aber a sar wɔ hɔ no ara.

english_verse: The bosses beat the men in charge of the slaves and said, “Why didn't you force the slaves to make as many bricks yesterday and today as they did before?”
translation_verse: Na wɔhwee Israelmba ahwɛdofo no a Farao n’abɔmbaafo no dze hɔn esisi hɔn do no, na wobisaa dɛ, Ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na hom ennwie ntayaa a wɔahyɛ hom no yɛ ndeda nye ndɛ dɛ nkan no yi?

english_verse: Finally, the men in charge of the slaves went to the king and said, “Why are you treating us like this?
translation_verse: Nna Israelmba ahwɛdofo no bae bosui tɔɔ Farao do dɛ, Ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na enye wo nkowaa dzi no dɛm yi?

english_verse: No one brings us any straw, but we are still ordered to make the same number of bricks. We are beaten with whips, and your own people are to blame.”
translation_verse: Wɔmmaa wo nkowaa sar, na wɔse hɛn dɛ, Hom nyɛ ntayaa: na, hwɛ, wɔahwe wo nkowaa dze; na wankasa wo man na wɔayɛ bɔn.

english_verse: The king replied, “You are lazy—nothing but lazy! That's why you keep asking me to let you go and sacrifice to your Lord.
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Enyiahafo nye hom, enyiahafo nye hom: dɛm ntsi na hom se dɛ, Ma yɛnkɔ nkɔbɔ afɔr mma EWURADZE a.

english_verse: Get back to work! You won't be given straw, but you must still make the same number of bricks.”
translation_verse: Dɛm ntsi afei hom nkɔ nkɛyɛ edwuma; na sar dze wɔremma hom, naaso ntayaa no dodow dze hom mma.

english_verse: The men knew they were in deep trouble when they were ordered to make the same number of bricks each day.
translation_verse: Na Israelmba ahwɛdofo no hun dɛ wɔwɔ amandzenya mu wɔ aber a wɔkaa dɛ, Mma hom nnyi hom da kor edwuma mu ntayaa no do dabiarada no.

english_verse: After they left the king, they went to see Moses and Aaron, who had been waiting for them.
translation_verse: Na wofi Farao nkyɛn reba no, wɔbɔtoo Moses nye Aaron dɛ wogyina ɔkwan no mu hɔ:

english_verse: Then the men said, “We hope the Lord will punish both of you for making the king and his officials hate us. Now they even have an excuse to kill us.” The Lord's Promise to Moses
translation_verse: na wɔsee hɔn dɛ, EWURADZE nhwɛ hom, na ombua; dɛ hom ama hɛn ho abɔn wɔ Farao, nye no nkowaa enyim, na hom dze afona ahyɛ hɔn nsa dɛ wɔmfa nku hɛn ntsi. Moses Kosu Kyerɛ EWURADZE

english_verse: Moses left them and prayed, “Our Lord, why have you brought so much trouble on your people? Is that why you sent me here?
translation_verse: Na Moses sanee kɔr EWURADZE nkyɛn kɛsee dɛ, EWURADZE, ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na enye ɔman yi edzi no bɔn dɛm yi? Ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na esomaa me yi?

english_verse: Ever since you told me to speak to the king, he has caused nothing but trouble for these people. And you haven't done a thing to help.”
translation_verse: Na ofitsi aber ara a mebaa Farao nkyɛn dɛ medze wo dzin nye no kasae no, ɔayɛ ɔman yi bɔn; nkyii so enngyee wo man koraa.

english_verse: The Lord God told Moses: Soon you will see what I will do to the king. Because of my mighty power, he will let my people go, and he will even chase them out of his country.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Afei na irubohu dza medze merebɛyɛ Farao: na nsa a ɔyɛ dzen do na ɔbɛma wɔakɔ, na nsa a ɔyɛ dzen do na ɔbɛpam hɔn efi n’asaase do. Nyankopɔn Kasa Kyerɛ Moses

english_verse: But when I appeared to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, I came as God All-Powerful and did not use my name.
translation_verse: na miyii moho mekyerɛɛ Abraham, Isaak, nye Jakob, dɛ Nyankopɔn Otumfo, na me dzin YHWH no dze mamma wɔammfa ennhu me.

english_verse: I made an agreement and promised them the land of Canaan, where they were living as foreigners.
translation_verse: Na eso mama m’ahyɛmu a menye hɔn dzii no etsim, dɛ medze Keenan asaase a ɔyɛ hɔn ahɔhobea asaase no a wɔyɛ do ahɔho no mebɛma hɔn.

english_verse: Now I have seen how the people of Israel are suffering because of the Egyptians, and I will keep my promise.
translation_verse: Na nkyii so matse Israelmba a Egyptfo dzii hɔn nya no, hɔn apempen; na makaa m’ahyɛmu no.

english_verse: Here is my message for Israel: “I am the Lord! And with my mighty power I will punish the Egyptians and free you from slavery.
translation_verse: Dɛm ntsi se Israelmba dɛ, Emi nye Jehova, na mibeyi hom mefi Egyptfo ndzesoadze no ase, na meyi hom efi hɔn nkowaasom no mu, na medze abasa a wɔatsɛw mu, nye ntsɛmbu akɛse agye hom:

english_verse: I will accept you as my people, and I will be your God. Then you will know that I was the one who rescued you from the Egyptians.
translation_verse: na mafa hom ayɛ me man, na mayɛ hom Nyankopɔn: na hom ehu dɛ emi nye Jehova hom Nyankopɔn a oyii hom fii Egyptfo ndzesoadze ase no.

english_verse: I will bring you into the land that I solemnly promised to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, and it will be yours. I am the Lord!”
translation_verse: Na medze hom bɔkɔ asaase a memaa me nsa do wɔ ho dɛ medze mebɛma Abraham, Isaak, nye Jakob no do, na medze bɛma hom dɛ egyapadze: emi nye Jehova.

english_verse: When Moses told this to the Israelites, they were too discouraged and mistreated to believe him.
translation_verse: Na Moses kaa dɛm kyerɛɛ Israelmba no: na ahomtsew nye nkowaasom dzendzen ntsi woenntsie Moses.

english_verse: Then the Lord told Moses
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE kaa kyerɛɛ Moses dɛ,

english_verse: to demand that the king of Egypt let the Israelites leave.
translation_verse: Kɔ, kɛka kyerɛ Egypthen Farao, dɛ ɔmma Israelmba mfi asaase no do nkɔ.

english_verse: But Moses replied, “I'm not a powerful speaker. If the Israelites won't listen to me, why should the king of Egypt?”
translation_verse: Na Moses kasae wɔ EWURADZE enyim see dɛ, Hwɛ, Israelmba enntsie me; na ɔbɛyɛ dɛn na Farao betsie emi a wonntwaa m’anofamfa twatsia yi?

english_verse: But the Lord sent Aaron and Moses with a message for the Israelites and for the king. He also ordered Aaron and Moses to free the people from Egypt. Family Record of Aaron and Moses
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE kasaa kyerɛɛ Moses nye Aaron, na ɔdze hyɛɛ hɔn nsa dɛ wɔnkɔ Israelmba, nye Egypthen Farao nkyɛn, nkeyi Israelmba no mfi Egypt asaase do. Moses Na Aaron Hɔn Ebusua

english_verse: The following men were the heads of their ancestral clans: The sons of Reuben, Jacob's oldest son, were Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi.
translation_verse: Iyinom nye hɔn egyanom efi mu etsitsir: Israel n’abakan Reuben ne mbabanyin; Hanok, Pallu, Hezron, nye Karmi: iyinom nye Reuben n’ebusuafo.

english_verse: The sons of Simeon were Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar, and Shaul, the son of a Canaanite woman.
translation_verse: Na Simeon ne mbabanyin; Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jakin, Zohar, nye Saul, Keenannyi ɔbaa bi ne babanyin no: iyinom nye Simeon n’ebusuafo.

english_verse: Gershon's sons were Libni and Shimei.
translation_verse: Gerson ne mbabanyin nye Libni na Simei, dɛ mbrɛ hɔn ebusuafo tsetsee.

english_verse: Merari's sons were Mahli and Mushi. All of the above were from the Levi tribe.
translation_verse: Merari ne mbabanyin nye Mahili nye Musi. Iyinom nye Levifo ebusuafo dɛ mbrɛ hɔn ebusuasantsen tsetsee.

english_verse: Izhar's sons were Korah, Nepheg, and Zichri.
translation_verse: Izhar ne mbabanyin nye Kora, Nefeg, nye Zikri.

english_verse: Uzziel's sons were Mishael, Elzaphan, and Sithri.
translation_verse: Uziel ne mbabanyin nye Misael, Elzafan, nye Sitri.

english_verse: Aaron married Elisheba. She was the daughter of Amminadab and the sister of Nahshon; they had four sons, Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
translation_verse: Na Aaron faa Eliseba, Aminadab ne babaa no, Nahson n’akyerɛbaa no waar no; na ɔnye no woo Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar nye Itamar.

english_verse: Korah's sons were Assir, Elkanah, and Abiasaph.
translation_verse: Na Kora ne mbabanyin nye Assir, Elkana, na Abiasaf; iyinom nye Korafo ebusuafo.

english_verse: Aaron's son Eleazar married one of Putiel's daughters, and their son was Phinehas. This ends the list of those who were the heads of clans in the Levi tribe.
translation_verse: Na Aaron ne babanyin Eleazar faa Putiel ne mbabaa mu baako waar no; na ɔnye no woo Finehas. Iyinom nye Levifo egyanom efi mu etsitsir dɛ mbrɛ hɔn ebusuafo tsetsee.

english_verse: The Lord had commanded Aaron and Moses to lead every family and tribe of Israel out of Egypt,
translation_verse: Iyinom nye Aaron nye Moses no a EWURADZE see hɔn dɛ, wonnyi Israelmba mfi Egypt asaase do dɛ mbrɛ hɔn dɔm tsetsee no.

english_verse: and so they told the king of Egypt to set the people of Israel free. The Lord Commands Moses and Aaron To Speak to the King
translation_verse: Moses na Aaron nye hɔn a wɔkasaa kyerɛɛ Egypthen Farao dɛ wobeyi Israelmba efi Egypt akɔ no. EWURADZE N’asɛm Ba Moses Nye Aaron Hɔ

english_verse: When the Lord spoke to Moses in the land of Egypt,
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ da no a EWURADZE kasaa kyerɛɛ Moses wɔ Egypt asaase do no,

english_verse: he said, “I am the Lord. Tell the king of Egypt everything I say to you.”
translation_verse: EWURADZE kaa kyerɛɛ Moses dɛ, Emi nye EWURADZE: ka dza meka kyerɛ wo nyinara kyerɛ Egypthen Farao.

english_verse: But Moses answered, “You know I am a very poor speaker, and the king will never listen to me.”
translation_verse: Na Moses kae wɔ EWURADZE enyim dɛ, Hwɛ, emi wonntwaa m’anofamfa twatsia, na Farao bɛyɛ dɛn etsie me?

english_verse: The Lord said: I am going to let your brother Aaron speak for you. He will tell your message to the king, just as a prophet speaks my message to the people.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Hwɛ, mebɛma ayɛ dɛ Nyame wɔ Farao enyim; na wo nua Aaron bɛyɛ wo nkɔnhyɛnyi.

english_verse: Tell Aaron everything I say to you, and he will order the king to let my people leave his country.
translation_verse: Ɔwo bɛka dza mehyɛ wo nyinara: na wo nua Aaron bɛka akyerɛ Farao ma ɔaama Israelmba efi n’asaase do akɔ.

english_verse: But I will make the king so stubborn that he won't listen to you. He won't listen even when I do many terrible things to him and his nation. Then I will bring a final punishment on Egypt, and the king will let Israel's families and tribes go.
translation_verse: Na Farao rinntsie hom, na medze me nsa bɔto Egypt do, na meyi mo dɔm, me man Israelmba no, mefi Egypt asaase do wɔ atsɛmbu akɛse mu.

english_verse: When this happens, the Egyptians will know that I am the Lord.
translation_verse: Na sɛ metsen me nsa wɔ Egypt do na miyi Israelmba fi hɔn mu fir a, nna Egyptfo bohu dɛ emi nye EWURADZE.

english_verse: Moses and Aaron obeyed the Lord
translation_verse: Na Moses nye Aaron yɛɛ dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛɛ hɔn no ara.

english_verse: The Lord said, “Moses, when the king asks you and Aaron to perform a miracle, command Aaron to throw his walking stick down in front of the king, and it will turn into a snake.”
translation_verse: Sɛ Farao ka kyerɛ hom dɛ, Hom nyi hom anwanwadze bi nkyerɛ a, ɔnodze ka kyerɛ Aaron dɛ, Yi wo poma no, na tow kyen famu wɔ Farao enyim, ma ɔndan abowatsena.

english_verse: Moses and Aaron went to the king and his officials and did exactly as the Lord had commanded—Aaron threw the stick down, and it turned into a snake.
translation_verse: Na Moses nye Aaron kɔr Farao nkyɛn, na wɔyɛɛ dɛm, dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛe no: na Aaron toow no poma kyeen famu wɔ Farao enyim nye no nkowaa enyim, na ɔdaan abowatsena.

english_verse: Then the king called in the wise men and the magicians, who used their secret powers to do the same thing—
translation_verse: Nna Farao so frɛfrɛɛ enyansafo no, na abayifo no: na hɔn so, Egypt akɔmfo no, dze hɔn nkaber yɛɛ dɛmara.

english_verse: they threw down sticks that turned into snakes. But Aaron's snake swallowed theirs.
translation_verse: Na hɔn nyinara totoow hɔn mpoma guu famu, na ɔdandaan mbowatsena: na Aaron no poma no memeen hɔn mpoma no.

english_verse: The king behaved just as the Lord had said and stubbornly refused to listen. The Nile River Turns into Blood
translation_verse: Na Farao n’akoma pirimii, na oenntsie hɔn; dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE kae no. Ɔhaw Ahorow Ba Egypt: Mbɔgya

english_verse: The Lord said to Moses: The Egyptian king stubbornly refuses to change his mind and let the people go.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Farao n’akoma epirim, ɔmmpen dɛ ɔman no kɔ.

english_verse: Tomorrow morning take the stick that turned into a snake, then wait beside the Nile River for the king.
translation_verse: Kɔ Farao nkyɛn ampa: hwɛ, orupue akɔ nsu mu: na ɔwo kegyina esutsen no no nkɔnkɔnoa hɔ hyia no; na poma no a ɔdaan abowatsena no fa kitsa wo nsamu.

english_verse: Tell him, “The Lord God of the Hebrews sent me to order you to release his people, so they can worship him in the desert. But until now, you have paid no attention.
translation_verse: Na se no dɛ, EWURADZE, Hebriwfo Nyankopɔn no, na ɔasoma me wo hɔ dɛ, Ma me man nkɔ nkɔsom me wɔ sar no do: na, hwɛ, enntsie ara dze ebodu sesei.

english_verse: “The Lord is going to do something to show you that he really is the Lord. I will strike the Nile with this stick, and the water will turn into blood.
translation_verse: Mbrɛ EWURADZE se nye yi, Iyi mu na ibohu dɛ emi nye EWURADZE: Hwɛ, medze poma a ɔdze me nsamu yi mobɔbɔ esutsen yi mu nsu, na ɔbɛdan bɔgya.

english_verse: The fish will die, the river will stink, and none of you Egyptians will be able to drink the water.”
translation_verse: Na nam a wɔwɔ esutsen no mu bowuwu, na esutsen no bɔbɔn; na Egyptfo bekyir dɛ wɔbɔnom esutsen no mu nsu.

english_verse: Moses, then command Aaron to hold his stick over the water. And when he does, every drop of water in Egypt will turn into blood, including rivers, canals, ponds, and even the water in buckets and jars.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Se Aaron dɛ, Fa wo poma, na tsen wo nsa wɔ Egypt esuesu do, hɔn esutsen do, hɔn nsubansuba do, nye hɔn mbakansu nyina do, ma ɔndandan bɔgya; na bɔgya bɛba Egypt asaase nyinara do, ndua esupi nye abo esupi nyina mu.

english_verse: Moses and Aaron obeyed the Lord. Aaron held out his stick, then struck the Nile, as the king and his officials watched. The river turned into blood,
translation_verse: Na Moses nye Aaron yɛɛ dɛm, dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛe no; na ɔmaa poma no do, na ɔdze bɔɔ nsu a ɔwɔ esutsen no mu no wɔ Farao enyim, nye no nkowaa enyim; na nsu a ɔwɔ esutsen no mu nyinara daan bɔgya.

english_verse: the fish died, and the water smelled so bad that none of the Egyptians could drink it. Blood was everywhere in Egypt.
translation_verse: Na nam a wɔwɔ esutsen no mu wuwui, na esutsen no bɔnee, na Egyptfo enntum annom esutsen no mu nsu; na bɔgya no baa Egypt asaase nyinara do.

english_verse: But the Egyptian magicians used their secret powers to do the same thing. The king did just as the Lord had said—he stubbornly refused to listen.
translation_verse: Na Egypt akɔmfo no dze hɔn nkaber no yɛɛ dɛmara: na Farao n’akoma pirimii, na oenntsie hɔn; dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE kae no.

english_verse: Then he went back to his palace and never gave it a second thought.
translation_verse: Na Farao daan noho na ɔkɔr ne fie, na ɔammfa iyi so mpo annto n’akoma mu.

english_verse: The Egyptians had to dig holes along the banks of the Nile for drinking water, because water from the river was unfit to drink. Frogs
translation_verse: Na Egyptfo nyina tutuu amona wɔ esutsen no ho dɛ wobenya nsu anom; osiandɛ wonntum nnom esutsen no mu nsu.

english_verse: Seven days after the Lord had struck the Nile,
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE bɔɔ esutsen no ekyir no, nda esuon botwaa mu.

english_verse: he said to Moses: Go to the palace and tell the king of Egypt that I order him to let my people go, so they can worship me.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE kaa kyerɛɛ Moses dɛ, Kɔ Farao nkyɛn, na se no dɛ, Mbrɛ EWURADZE se nye yi, Ma me man nkɔ, na wɔnkɔsom me.

english_verse: If he refuses, I will cover his entire country with frogs.
translation_verse: Na sɛ ammpen ma wɔaannkɔ a, hwɛ, medze ntwɛr bofura wo ntoaado nyinara:

english_verse: Warn the king that the Nile will be full of frogs, and from there they will spread into the royal palace, including the king's bedroom and even his bed. Frogs will enter the homes of his officials and will find their way into ovens and into the bowls of bread dough.
translation_verse: na esutsen no bɔdɔ ntwɛr menyamenya, na wɔbɔfofow aba wo fie, nye w’adankyir, nye wo mpa do, nye wo nkowaa fie, nye wo man do, nye wo mfononoo mu, nye wo mbɔrnkutu mu:

english_verse: Frogs will be crawling on everyone—the king, his officials, and every citizen of Egypt.
translation_verse: na ntwɛr no bɔfow aba ɔwo, nye wo mamfo, nye wo nkowaa nyinara do.

english_verse: Moses, now command Aaron to hold his stick over the water. Then frogs will come from all rivers, canals, and ponds in Egypt, and they will cover the land.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Se Aaron dɛ, Fa wo poma tsen wo nsa wɔ esutsen, nye nsubansuba, nye nsutaae do, na ma ntwɛr mbra Egypt asaase do.

english_verse: But the magicians used their secret powers to do the same thing.
translation_verse: Na akɔmfo no dze hɔn nkaber no yɛɛ dɛmara, na wɔmaa ntwɛr fowee baa Egypt asaase do.

english_verse: The king sent for Moses and Aaron and told them, “If you ask the Lord to take these frogs away from me and my people, I will let your people go and offer sacrifices to him.”
translation_verse: Nna Farao frɛɛ Moses nye Aaron, na ɔsee dɛ, Hom mpa EWURADZE kyɛw ma onyi ntwɛr no mfi emi, nye me man do; na mebɛma ɔman no akɔ, ma wɔaakɔbɔ afɔr ama EWURADZE.

english_verse: “All right,” Moses answered. “You choose the time when I am to pray for the frogs to stop bothering you, your officials, and your people, and for them to leave your houses and be found only in the river.”
translation_verse: Na Moses see Farao dɛ, W’adaworom: ebɛn aber na mempa kyɛw memma ɔwo, nye wo nkowaa, nye wo man, ma wɔnsɛɛ ntwɛr no mfi ɔwo nye w’efi do, na wɔnka esutsen no nko mu?

english_verse: “Do it tomorrow!” the king replied. “As you wish,” Moses agreed. “Then everyone will discover that there is no god like the Lord,
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Ɔkyena a. Na ɔsee dɛ, Ɔnyɛ dɛ dza aka no ara: ama eehu dɛ obiara nnyi hɔ a ɔtse dɛ EWURADZE hɛn Nyankopɔn.

english_verse: and frogs will no longer be found anywhere, except in the Nile.”
translation_verse: Na ntwɛr no befi ɔwo, nye w’efi, nye wo nkowaa, nye wo man do; esutsen no mu nko na wɔbɛka.

english_verse: After Moses and Aaron left the palace, Moses begged the Lord to do something about the frogs he had sent as punishment for the king.
translation_verse: Na Moses nye Aaron fii Farao nkyɛn kɔree: na Moses sui frɛɛ EWURADZE wɔ ntwɛr no a ɔdze baa Farao do no ho.

english_verse: The Lord listened to Moses, and the frogs died everywhere—in houses, yards, and fields.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE yɛɛ dɛ mbrɛ Moses kae no: na ntwɛr no wuwui wɔ efi mu, nye abangua do, nye haban mu.

english_verse: The dead frogs were placed in piles, and the whole country began to stink.
translation_verse: Na wɔboaboaa hɔn ano ekuwekuw: na asaase no do bɔnee.

english_verse: But when the king saw that things were now better, he again did just as the Lord had said he would and stubbornly refused to listen to Moses and Aaron. Gnats
translation_verse: Na Farao hun dɛ ahotɔ aba mu no, opiriim n’akoma, na oenntsie hɔn: dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE kae no. Dwuw

english_verse: The Lord said to Moses, “Command Aaron to strike the ground with his walking stick, and everywhere in Egypt the dust will turn into gnats.”
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Se Aaron dɛ, Tsen wo poma no, na fa bɔ asaase do huntuma, ma ɔndan dwuw wɔ Egypt asaase nyinara do.

english_verse: They obeyed, and when Aaron struck the ground with the stick, gnats started swarming on people and animals. In fact, every speck of dust in Egypt turned into a gnat.
translation_verse: Na wɔyɛɛ dɛm; na Aaron dze no poma no tseen ne nsa, na ɔdze bɔɔ asaase do huntuma, ma dwuw fɔɔr nyimpa nye mbowa: asaase no do huntuma nyinara daan dwuw wɔ Egypt asaase nyinara do.

english_verse: When the magicians tried to use their secret powers to do this, they failed, and gnats stayed on people and animals.
translation_verse: Na akɔmfo no dze hɔn nkaber yɛɛ dɛmara dɛ wɔrema dwuw aba na woenntum: na dwuw no wɔ nyimpa nye mbowa ho.

english_verse: The magicians told the king, “God has done this.” But, as the Lord had said, the king was too stubborn to listen. Flies
translation_verse: Nna akɔmfo no see Farao dɛ, Nyankopɔn nsatseaba nye yi: na Farao n’akoma pirimii, na oenntsie hɔn; dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE kae no. Atsɛn

english_verse: The Lord said to Moses: Early tomorrow morning, while the king is on his way to the river, go and say to him, “The Lord commands you to let his people go, so they can worship him.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Soɛr anapaatu, na kegyina Farao enyim; hwɛ, ɔreba nsu no ho; na se no dɛ, Mbrɛ EWURADZE se nye yi, Ma me man nkɔ, ma wɔnkɔsom me.

english_verse: If you don't, he will send swarms of flies to attack you, your officials, and every citizen of your country. Your houses will be full of flies, and the ground will crawl with them.
translation_verse: Na sɛ ennkɛma me man akɔ a, hwɛ, mebɛma atsɛn menyamenya aba ɔwo, nye wo nkowaa, nye wo man, nye w’efi do: na Egyptfo efiefi mu, na asaase a wɔtse do no so atsɛn menyamenya bɛyɛ no amaama.

english_verse: The Lord kept his promise—the palace and the homes of the royal officials swarmed with flies, and the rest of the country was infested with them as well.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE yɛɛ dɛm; na atsɛn menyamenya a hɔn ano yɛ yaw baa Farao fi mu, nye no nkowaa efi mu: na osian atsɛn menyamenya no ntsi Egypt asaase no nyinara do sɛɛe.

english_verse: Then the king sent for Moses and Aaron and told them, “Go ahead and sacrifice to your God, but stay here in Egypt.”
translation_verse: Na Farao frɛɛ Moses nye Aaron, na ɔsee dɛ, Hom nkɔ nkɔbɔ afɔr mma hom Nyankopɔn wɔ asaase no do.

english_verse: “That's impossible!” Moses replied. “Any sacrifices we offer to the Lord our God would disgust the Egyptians, and they would stone us to death.
translation_verse: Na Moses dɛ, Ɔnnsɛ dɛ wɔyɛ no dɛm; osiandɛ Egyptfo ekyiwadze na yɛdze bɔbɔ afɔr ama EWURADZE hɛn Nyankopɔn no: hwɛ, ana yɛmfa Egyptfo ekyiwadze mbɔ afɔr wɔ hɔn enyiwa mu, na wonnkesiw hɛn abo bi a?

english_verse: No indeed! The Lord has ordered us to walk three days into the desert before offering sacrifices to him, and that's what we have to do.”
translation_verse: Yebotu ndaansa kwan akɔ sar no do, na yɛakɔbɔ afɔr ama EWURADZE hɛn Nyankopɔn, dɛ mbrɛ ɔbɛhyɛ hɛn no.

english_verse: Then the king told him, “I'll let you go into the desert to offer sacrifices, if you don't go very far. But in the meantime, pray for me.”
translation_verse: Na Farao see dɛ, mebɛma hom akɔ, ama hom aakɔbɔ afɔr ama EWURADZE hom Nyankopɔn no wɔ sar no do; na ekyir nko na hom nnkɔ: hom mpa kyɛw mma me.

english_verse: “Your Majesty,” Moses replied, “I'll pray for you as soon as I leave, and by tomorrow the flies will stop bothering you, your officials, and the citizens of your country. Only make sure that you're telling the truth this time and that you really intend to let our people offer sacrifices to the Lord.”
translation_verse: Na Moses see dɛ, Hwɛ, mirifi wo nkyɛn akɔ, na makɛpa EWURADZE kyɛw ma atsɛn menyamenya no eefi Farao, nye no nkowaa, nye ne man do ɔkyena: na abrasɛm nko na mma Farao nndzi bio, dɛ ɔnnkɛma ɔman no akɔ akɔbɔ afɔr ama EWURADZE.

english_verse: After leaving the palace, Moses prayed,
translation_verse: Na Moses fii Farao nkyɛn kɔree, na ɔkɛpaa EWURADZE kyɛw.

english_verse: and the Lord answered his prayer. Not a fly was left to pester the king, his officials, or anyone else in Egypt.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE yɛɛ dɛ mbrɛ Moses kae no; na oyii atsɛn menyamenya no fii Farao, nye no nkowaa, nye ne man do; kor mpo annka.

english_verse: But the king turned stubborn again and would not let the people go.
translation_verse: Na Farao piriim n’akoma aber yi so, na ɔamma ɔman no annkɔ.

english_verse: The Lord sent Moses with this message for the king of Egypt: The Lord God of the Hebrews commands you to let his people go, so they can worship him.
translation_verse: Nna EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Kɔ Farao nkyɛn, na ka kyerɛ no dɛ, Mbrɛ EWURADZE Hebriwfo Nyankopɔn se nye yi, Ma me man nkɔ, ma wɔnkɔsom me.

english_verse: he will bring a terrible disease on your horses and donkeys, your camels and cattle, and your sheep and goats.
translation_verse: hwɛ, EWURADZE nsa wɔ wo mbowa a wɔwɔ haban mu do, na ɔdze mbowawu yayaayaw bɛba mpɔnkɔ, nsoasopɔnkɔ, efumpɔnkɔ, anantwikuw, nye nguankuw do.

english_verse: But the Lord will protect the animals that belong to the people of Israel, and none of theirs will die.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE dze sorbi bɔto Israel mbowa nye Egypt mbowa ntamu na dza ɔyɛ Israelmba dze no nyinara mu biara nnkowu.

english_verse: Tomorrow is the day the Lord has set to do this.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE hyɛɛ aber, dɛ, Ɔkyena na EWURADZE bɛyɛ adze yi wɔ asaase no do.

english_verse: It happened the next day—all of the animals belonging to the Egyptians died, but the Israelites did not lose even one.
translation_verse: Na adze kyee no EWURADZE yɛɛ adze no, na Egypt mbowa nyinara wuwui: na Israelmba mbowa dze no mu kor mpo ennwu.

english_verse: When the king found out, he was still too stubborn to let the people go. Sores
translation_verse: Na Farao somae, na hwɛ, nna Israelfo mbowa mu kor mpo nnwui. Na Farao n’akoma sorowee, na ɔamma ɔman no annkɔ. Mpɔmpɔ

english_verse: The Lord said to Moses and Aaron: Take a few handfuls of ashes from a stove and you, Moses, throw them into the air. Be sure the king is watching.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses nye Aaron dɛ, Hom mfa fononoo mu nso nsama nsama, na Moses ntow mpetse wimu wɔ Farao enyim.

english_verse: The ashes will blow across the land of Egypt, causing sores to break out on people and animals.
translation_verse: Na ɔbɛdan tutuw wɔ Egypt asaase nyinara do, na ɔayɛ mpɔmpɔ a ɔpaapaa tutu ekur wɔ nyimpa nye mbowa ho, wɔ Egypt asaase nyinara do.

english_verse: So they took a few handfuls of ashes and went to the king. Moses threw them into the air, and sores immediately broke out on the Egyptians and their animals.
translation_verse: Na wɔfaa fononoo mu nso, na wokegyinaa Farao enyim; na Moses towee petsee wimu; na ɔdaan pɔmpɔ a ɔpaapaa tutu ekur wɔ nyimpa nye mbowa ho.

english_verse: The magicians were suffering so much from the sores, that they could not even come to Moses.
translation_verse: Na osian mpɔmpɔ no ntsi akɔmfo no enntum enngyina Moses enyim; osiandɛ nna mpɔmpɔ no abobɔ akɔmfo no nye Egyptfo nyinara.

english_verse: Everything happened just as the Lord had told Moses—he made the king too stubborn to listen to Moses and Aaron. Hailstones
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE piriim Farao n’akoma, na oenntsie hɔn; dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE kaa kyerɛɛ Moses no. Ɔsorbo

english_verse: The Lord told Moses to get up early the next morning and say to the king: The Lord God of the Hebrews commands you to let his people go, so they can worship him!
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Soɛr anapaatu, na kegyina Farao enyim, na se no dɛ, Mbrɛ EWURADZE, Hebriwfo Nyankopɔn no se nye yi, Ma me man nkɔ, ma wɔnkɔsom me.

english_verse: If you don't, he will send his worst plagues to strike you, your officials, and everyone else in your country. Then you will find out that no one can oppose the Lord.
translation_verse: Na afei dze merema me yardɔm nyinara aba w’akoma do, nye wo nkowaa do, nye wo man do; ama eehu dɛ obiara nnyi hɔ a ɔtse dɛ emi wɔ asaase nyinara do.

english_verse: In fact, he could already have sent a terrible disease and wiped you from the face of the earth.
translation_verse: Na afei sɛ matsen me nsa, na medze owuyar abɔ ɔwo nye wo man a, nkyɛ woetwa wo efi asaase do:

english_verse: But he has kept you alive, just to show you his power and to bring honor to himself everywhere in the world.
translation_verse: na ampaara iyi ntsi na mama egyina hɔ, dɛ mebɛkyerɛ wo mo tum, na wɔaabɔ me dzin akyerɛ wɔ asaase nyinara do.

english_verse: You are still determined not to let the Lord's people go.
translation_verse: Eda ho ara rema woho do wɔ me man do, dɛ eremma wɔnnkɔ anaa?

english_verse: All right. At this time tomorrow, he will bring on Egypt the worst hailstorm in its history.
translation_verse: Hwɛ, ɔkyena siseber yi mebɛma ɔsorbo a n’ano yɛ dur dodoodow a ne saso mmbaa Egypt pɛn ofi da a wɔkyekyeree dze bodu ndɛ yi atɔ.

english_verse: You had better give orders for every person and every animal in Egypt to take shelter. If they don't, they will die.
translation_verse: Dɛm ntsi afei soma na wɔnkɛfa wo mbowa nye biribiara a ewɔ wɔ haban mu nyina mbra ntsɛm; na nyimpa biara nye abowa biara a wobohu hɔn wɔ haban mu, na wɔammfa hɔn ammba fie no, ɔsorbo no bɔhwe hɔn, na wobowuwu.

english_verse: Some of the king's officials were frightened by what the Lord had said, and they hurried off to make sure their slaves and animals were safe.
translation_verse: Farao nkowaa mu nyia osuroo EWURADZE n’asɛm no maa no nkowaa nye ne fiembowa guanee baa fie.

english_verse: But others paid no attention to his threats and left their slaves and animals out in the open.
translation_verse: Na nyia oemmbu EWURADZE n’asɛm bi no gyaa no nkowaa nye ne fiembowa wɔ haban mu.

english_verse: Then the Lord told Moses, “Stretch your arm toward the sky, so that hailstones will fall on people, animals, and crops in the land of Egypt.”
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Tsen wo nsa wɔ sor, ma ɔsorbo mbra Egypt asaase nyinara do, nyimpa, nye abowa, nye haban mu efuw biara do, wɔ Egypt asaase nyinara do.

english_verse: Moses pointed his walking stick toward the sky, and hailstones started falling everywhere. Thunder roared, and lightning flashed back and forth, striking the ground. This was the worst storm in the history of Egypt.
translation_verse: Nna ɔsorbo no bae, na ogya a ɔdzɛw brambram toatoa mu a ɔfora ɔsorbo no mu a n’ano yɛ dzen dodoodow a ne saso mmbaa Egypt asaase nyinara do pɛn ofi da a ɔbɛyɛɛ ɔman no.

english_verse: Only Goshen, where the Israelites lived, was safe from the storm.
translation_verse: Gosen asaase do mbrɛ Israelmba wɔ no nko na ɔsorbo no bi ammba.

english_verse: The king sent for Moses and Aaron and told them, “Now I have really sinned! My people and I are guilty, and the Lord is right.
translation_verse: Na Farao somae ma wɔkɛfrɛɛ Moses nye Aaron, na ɔsee hɔn dɛ, Afei dze mayɛ bɔn: EWURADZE dzi bem, na emi nye me man yɛyɛ afɔdzifo.

english_verse: We can't stand any more of this thunder and hail. Please ask the Lord to make it stop. Your people can go—you don't have to stay in Egypt any longer.”
translation_verse: Hom mpa EWURADZE kyɛw; na asenaman dzendzen yi nye ɔsorbo no adɔɔ so; na mebɛma hom akɔ, na hom renntsena ha bio.

english_verse: Moses answered, “As soon as I leave the city, I will lift my arms in prayer. When the thunder and hail stop, you will know that the earth belongs to the Lord.
translation_verse: Na Moses see no dɛ, Mefi kurow mu akɔ ara a, nkyɛ matserɛw me nsamu akyerɛ EWURADZE; asenaman no begyaa, nkyii so ɔsorbo biara nnkɛba bio; ama eehu dɛ asaase yɛ EWURADZE dze.

english_verse: But I am certain that neither you nor your officials really fear the Lord God.”
translation_verse: Na ɔwo nye wo nkowaa dze, minyim dɛ hom nnyae nnsuroo EWURADZE Nyankopɔn bi.

english_verse: Meanwhile, the flax and barley crops had been destroyed by the storm because they were ready to ripen.
translation_verse: Na ɔhwee asaaba nye atoko: osiandɛ atoko no ayɛ mbɛtsem, na asaaba no so abɔ nkukuduuduw.

english_verse: But the wheat crops ripen later, and they were not damaged.
translation_verse: Na ewifua nye ewu dze wɔannhwe hɔn: osiandɛ nna wonntuu mpon biara.

english_verse: After Moses left the royal palace and the city, he lifted his arms in prayer to the Lord, and the thunder, hail, and drenching rain stopped.
translation_verse: Na Moses fi Farao nkyɛn fii kurow no mu, na ɔkɛtserɛɛw ne nsamu kyerɛɛ EWURADZE, na nsenaman nye ɔsorbo no gyaae, na nyankom no woennhue enngu asaase no do.

english_verse: When the king realized that the storm was over, he disobeyed once more. He and his officials were so stubborn
translation_verse: Na Farao hun dɛ nyankom, nye ɔsorbo, nye asenaman no egyae no, ɔyɛɛ bɔn bio, na opiriim n’akoma, ɔno nye no nkowaa.

english_verse: that he refused to let the Israelites go. This was exactly what the Lord had said would happen.
translation_verse: Na Farao n’akoma pirimii, na ɔamma Israelmba annkɔ; dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE nam Moses do kae no.

english_verse: The Lord said to Moses: Go back to the king. I have made him and his officials stubborn, so that I could work these miracles.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Kɔ Farao nkyɛn: na mepirim n’akoma, nye no nkowaa akoma, ama meeyi me nsɛnkyerɛdze yi akyerɛ no wɔ hɔn mu:

english_verse: I did this because I want you to tell your children and your grandchildren about my miracles and about my harsh treatment of the Egyptians. Then all of you will know that I am the Lord.
translation_verse: na aaka no wɔ wo ba, nye wo ba ne ba asomu, ndzɛmba a mayɛ no wɔ Egypt do, na me nsɛnkyerɛdze a mayɛ no wɔ hɔn mu; ama hom eehu dɛ emi nye EWURADZE.

english_verse: Moses and Aaron went to the king and told him that the Lord God of the Hebrews had said: How long will you stubbornly refuse to obey? Release my people so they can worship me.
translation_verse: Na Moses nye Aaron kɔr Farao nkyɛn, na wɔkɛsee no dɛ, Mbrɛ EWURADZE, Hebriwfo Nyankopɔn no se nye yi, Eropow dɛ ebrɛ woho adze wɔ m’enyim ma ekodu dabɛn? Ma me man nkɔ, na wɔnkɔsom me.

english_verse: Do this by tomorrow, or I will cover your country with so many locusts
translation_verse: Na sɛ ammpen ma me man aannkɔ a, hwɛ, ɔkyena medze mbɛbɛw bɛba wo ntoaado:

english_verse: that you won't be able to see the ground. Most of your crops were ruined by the hailstones, but these locusts will destroy what little is left, including the trees.
translation_verse: na wɔbɛkata asaase enyi, dɛ obi nnkotum ehu asaase: na wobedzi edzinkaa a ɔsorbo no gyaae ma ɔkae maa hom no, na wobedzi dua biara a ɔrefefɛw wɔ haban mu ama hom no:

english_verse: Your palace, the homes of your officials, and all the other houses in Egypt will overflow with more locusts than have ever been seen in this country. After Moses left the palace,
translation_verse: na wɔbɛhyehyɛ w’efi mu, nye wo nkowaa nyinara efi mu, nye Egyptfo nyinara efi mu amaama; dɛ mbrɛ w’egyanom, nye w’egyanom hɔn egyanom nnhun bi pɛn ofi da a wɔdze baa asaase do dze bodu ndɛ da yi. Na ɔdaan noho, na ofii Farao nkyɛn puei.

english_verse: the king's officials asked, “Your Majesty, how much longer is this man going to be a troublemaker? Why don't you let the people leave, so they can worship the Lord their God? Don't you know that Egypt is a disaster?”
translation_verse: Na Farao no nkowaa see no dɛ, Nyimpa yi bɛtseetsee hɛn araa ma ekodu dabɛn? Ma nkorɔfo no nkɔ, ma wɔnkɔsom EWURADZE hɔn Nyankopɔn no. Ana Egypt asɛɛ yi innhu bi a?

english_verse: The king had Moses and Aaron brought back, and he said, “All right, you may go and worship the Lord your God. But first tell me who will be going.”
translation_verse: Na wɔdze Moses nye Aaron baa Farao nkyɛn bio: na ɔsee hɔn dɛ, Hom nkɔ nkɔsom EWURADZE hom Nyankopɔn: na woananom ara na wɔbɔkɔ?

english_verse: “Everyone, young and old,” Moses answered. “We will even take our sheep, goats, and cattle, because we want to hold a celebration in honor of the Lord.”
translation_verse: Na Moses see dɛ, Yɛnye hɛn mbofra nye hɛn mpanyin bɔkɔ, yɛnye hɛn mbabanyin na ɔnye hɛn mbabaa, yɛnye hɛn nguan na ɔnye hɛn anantwi bɔkɔ; osiandɛ otwar dɛ yɛhyɛ fa ma EWURADZE.

english_verse: The king replied, “The Lord had better watch over you on the day I let you leave with your families! You're up to no good.
translation_verse: Na ɔsee hɔn dɛ, Hm, EWURADZE ndzi hom ekyir ɛ, ber a merema hom, nye hom mbofraba no akɔ yi: mutum hu dɛ bɔn na ɔwɔ hom adwen mu.

english_verse: Do you want to worship the Lord? All right, take only the men and go.” Then Moses and Aaron were chased out of the palace.
translation_verse: Dabida: hom a hom yɛ mbanyin no ara nkɔ, na wɔnkɔsom EWURADZE; na ɔnoara nye dza hom rohwehwɛ. Na woyii hɔn edzi fii Farao enyim.

english_verse: The Lord told Moses, “Stretch your arm toward Egypt. Swarms of locusts will come and eat everything left by the hail.”
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Tsen wo nsa wɔ Egypt asaase do, wɔ mbɛbɛw no ntsi, ma wɔmbra Egypt asaase do, na wɔmbɔwe asaase no do efuw nyinara, mekyerɛ dza ɔsorbo no egyaa no nyinara.

english_verse: Moses held out his walking stick, and the Lord sent an east wind that blew across Egypt the rest of the day and all that night. By morning, locusts
translation_verse: Na Moses tseen no poma no wɔ Egypt asaase do, na EWURADZE dze boka mframa bɔɔ asaase no do da no nyinara, na anadwe no nyinara; na anapa no, boka mframa dze mbɛbɛw no bae.

english_verse: were swarming everywhere. Never before had there been so many locusts in Egypt, and never again will there be so many.
translation_verse: Na mbɛbɛw no kɔr Egypt asaase nyinara do, na wɔkɛgyegyee Egypt ntoaado nyinara; wɔdɔɔ so dodow; mbɛbɛw biara a ɔtse dɛm mbaa pɛn, na hɔn ekyir so bi nnkɛba dɛm da.

english_verse: The ground was black with locusts, and they ate everything left on the trees and in the fields. Nothing green remained in Egypt—not a tree or a plant.
translation_verse: Na wɔkataa asaase no nyinara enyi, ma isum gyee asaase no do; na wɔwee asaase no do efuw nyinara, nye ndua a ɔsorbo no gyaae no do aba nyinara: na adze momon biara annka a ɔyɛ dua anaa haban mu efuw, wɔ Egypt asaase nyinara do.

english_verse: At once the king sent for Moses and Aaron. He told them, “I have sinned against the Lord your God and against you.
translation_verse: Nna Farao maa wɔkɛfrɛɛ Moses nye Aaron ntsɛmara; na ɔsee dɛ, Mayɛ bɔn etsia EWURADZE hom Nyankopɔn, nye hom.

english_verse: Forgive me one more time and ask the Lord to stop these insects from killing every living plant.”
translation_verse: Dɛm ntsi afei, mepa wo kyɛw, fa mo bɔn fir me prɛko yi ara, na hom mpa EWURADZE hom Nyankopɔn kyɛw, ma onyi owu yi ara mfi mo do.

english_verse: Moses left the palace and prayed.
translation_verse: Na ofii Farao nkyɛn kɔree, na ɔkɛpaa EWURADZE kyɛw.

english_verse: Then the Lord sent a strong west wind that swept the locusts into the Red Sea. Not one locust was left anywhere in Egypt,
