translation_verse: Na EWURADZE dze daan anee mframa dzendzen mbordo a ɔbɛfaa mbɛbɛw no, na ɔdze hɔn koguu Mbɛw Po no mu; abɛbɛw kor mpo annka wɔ Egypt ntoaado nyinara.

english_verse: but the Lord made the king so stubborn that he still refused to let the Israelites go. Darkness
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE piriim Farao n’akoma, na ɔamma Israelmba no annkɔ. Sum

english_verse: The Lord said to Moses, “Stretch your arm toward the sky, and everything will be covered with darkness thick enough to touch.”
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Tsen wo nsa wɔ sor, ma sum mbra Egypt asaase do, mekyerɛ sum a wosuo mu a ɔbɛyɛ a yie.

english_verse: Moses stretched his arm toward the sky, and Egypt was covered with darkness for three days.
translation_verse: Na Moses tseen ne nsa wɔ sor; na sum kebii hyɛɛ Egypt asaase nyinara dze, do nda ebiasa;

english_verse: During that time, the Egyptians could not see each other or leave their homes, but there was light where the Israelites lived.
translation_verse: obiara ennhu ne nyɛnko, nkyii so obiara annsoɛr emmfi ne tsenabew nda ebiasa: na Israelmba nyinara wonyaa kan wɔ hɔn efiefi.

english_verse: The king sent for Moses and told him, “Go worship the Lord! And take your families with you. Just leave your sheep, goats, and cattle.”
translation_verse: Na Farao frɛɛ Moses, na ɔsee dɛ, Hom nkɔ nkɔsom EWURADZE; hom nguan nye hom anantwi nko na wɔmma wɔnka: wɔmma hom mbofraba no so nye hom nkɔ.

english_verse: “No!” Moses replied. “You must let us offer sacrifices to the Lord our God,
translation_verse: Na Moses see dɛ, Ohia dɛ edze okum afɔrbɔdze nye ɔhyew afɔrbɔdze so hyɛ hɛn nsa ama yaabɔ afɔr ama EWURADZE hɛn Nyankopɔn.

english_verse: and we won't know which animals we will need until we get there. That's why we can't leave even one of them here.”
translation_verse: Hɛn mbowa so nye hɛn bɔkɔ; ɔnnkɛka abowaanamɔn mpo wɔ ekyir; na ɔno mu bi na otwar dɛ yɛkɛfa som EWURADZE hɛn Nyankopɔn; na yennyim dza yɛdze bɔsom EWURADZE, gyedɛ yedu hɔ ana.

english_verse: This time the Lord made the king so stubborn
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE piriim Farao n’akoma, na ɔammpɛ dɛ ɔma wɔkɔ.

english_verse: that he said to Moses, “Get out and stay out! If you ever come back, you're dead!”
translation_verse: Na Farao see no dɛ, Fi mo do kɔ, hwɛ woho yie, mma nnhu m’enyim bio; na da a ibohu m’enyim no ibowu.

english_verse: “Have it your way,” Moses answered. “You won't see me again.”
translation_verse: Na Moses see dɛ, Aka no yie; munnkohu w’enyim bio.

english_verse: The Lord said to Moses: I am going to punish the king of Egypt and his people one more time. Then the king will gladly let you leave his land. In fact, he will even chase you out.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Mebɛma ɔyardɔm kor so aba Farao, nye Egypt do bio; na n’ekyir no ɔbɛma hom efi ha akɔ: aber a ɔbɛma hom akɔ no, ampaara, ɔbɛpam hom efi ha koraa.

english_verse: Now go and tell my people to ask their Egyptian neighbors for gold and silver jewelry.
translation_verse: Afei kasa wɔ ɔman no asomu, na ma ɔbanyin biara mbisa wɔ ne nyɛnko hɔ, na ɔbaa mbisa wɔ ne nyɛnko hɔ, dwetɛ ndwindze, nye sika ndwindze.

english_verse: So the Lord made the Egyptians greatly respect the Israelites, and everyone, including the king's officials, considered Moses an important leader.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE maa ɔman no enyimpa wɔ Egyptfo enyim. Nkyii so nna ɔyɛɛ Moses otsitsir paara wɔ Egypt asaase do, wɔ Farao nkowaa enyim, nye ɔman no enyim.

english_verse: Moses went to the king and said: I have come to let you know what the Lord is going to do. About midnight he will go through the land of Egypt,
translation_verse: Na Moses see dɛ, Mbrɛ EWURADZE se nye yi, Bɛyɛ esuom na mibefir edzi mafa Egypt mu:

english_verse: and wherever he goes, the first-born son in every family will die. Your own son will die, and so will the son of the lowest slave woman. Even the first-born males of your cattle will die.
translation_verse: na Egypt asaase do mbakan nyinara bowuwu, ofi Farao n’abakan a ɔtsena n’ahengua do do, dze kodu afonabaa mpo a ɔwɔ ewuyambo ekyir n’abakan do; nye ofiembowa mbakan nyinara.

english_verse: Everywhere in Egypt there will be loud crying. Nothing like this has ever happened before or will ever happen again.
translation_verse: Na su kɛse bɛba Egypt asaase nyinara do a ne saso bi mbaa da, eso ne saso bi nnkɛba bio.

english_verse: But there won't be any need for the Israelites to cry. Things will be so quiet that not even a dog will be heard barking. Then you Egyptians will know that the Lord is good to the Israelites, even while he punishes you.
translation_verse: Na Israelmba nyinara dze, ɔkraman mpo nnkɔtwe ne tɛkyerɛma ahye hɔn, ɔnnkɛhye nyimpa anaa abowa: ama hom eehu mbrɛ EWURADZE si dze ɔhe to Egyptfo nye Israel ntamu.

english_verse: Your leaders will come and bow down, begging me to take my people and leave your country. Then we will leave. Moses was very angry; he turned and left the king.
translation_verse: Na wo nkowaa yi nyinara besian aba mo hɔ, na wɔabɔbɔ hɔn mu adze ama me dɛ, Fir edzi kɔ, ɔwo nye ɔman a wɔka wo do nyinara: na ɔno ekyir no na mibefir edzi makɔ. Na ɔdze ebufuhyew fii Farao nkyɛn kɔree.

english_verse: What the Lord had earlier said to Moses came true. He had said, “The king of Egypt won't listen. Then I will perform even more miracles.”
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Farao nnketsie hom; ama m’anwanwadze aadɔɔ so wɔ Egypt asaase do.

english_verse: So the king of Egypt saw Moses and Aaron work miracles, but the Lord made him stubbornly refuse to let the Israelites leave his country.
translation_verse: Na Moses nye Aaron yɛɛ anwanwadze yi nyinara wɔ Farao enyim; na EWURADZE piriim Farao n’akoma, na ɔamma Israelmba emmfi n’asaase do annkɔ.

english_verse: Some time later the Lord said to Moses and Aaron:
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE kasaa kyerɛɛ Moses nye Aaron wɔ Egypt asaase do dɛ,

english_verse: This month is to be the first month of the year for you.
translation_verse: Bosoom yi bɛyɛ abosoom ahyɛse ama hom: ɔbɛyɛ afrenhyia mu bosoom a odzi kan ama hom.

english_verse: Tell the people of Israel that on the tenth day of this month the head of each family must choose a lamb or a young goat for his family to eat.
translation_verse: Hom nkasa nkyerɛ Israel mboanofo no nyinara dɛ, Bosoom yi ne da a otsia du no mu, wɔmfefa nguambaa nkorkor, dɛ mbrɛ hom egyanom efiefi tsetsee, fie kor eguambaa kor:

english_verse: Each family must take care of its animal until the evening of the fourteenth day of the month, when the animals are to be killed.
translation_verse: na hom mfa no nsie ma onkodu bosoom kor no ara ne da a otsia duanan no: na Israel mboanofo kuw no nyina nkuku hɔn ewimbir.

english_verse: Some of the blood must be put on the two doorposts and above the door of each house where the animals are to be eaten.
translation_verse: Na wɔmfa bɔgya no bi, na wɔmfa nsera efi a wobedzi no wɔ mu no n’apondua ebien no nye mpondua etsifi no ho.

english_verse: That night the animals are to be roasted and eaten, together with bitter herbs and thin bread made without yeast.
translation_verse: Na wondzi nam no anadwe no ara, wɔmfa gya ntoto, na wɔmfa epitsi boodoo, nye fan wenwen ndzi.

english_verse: Don't eat the meat raw or boiled. The entire animal, including its head, legs, and insides, must be roasted.
translation_verse: Mma wonndzi no amon, nkyii so mma wɔmmfa nsu nnoa, mbom wɔmfa gya ntoto; ne tsir nye no ntwɛr na ɔnye n’ayamudze no.

english_verse: Eat what you want that night, and the next morning burn whatever is left.
translation_verse: Na mma hom mma no ho biribiara nnka ma adze nnkye do; na dza ɔbɛka ma adze akye do no hom mfa gya nhyew.

english_verse: When you eat the meal, be dressed and ready to travel. Have your sandals on, carry your walking stick in your hand, and eat quickly. This is the Passover Festival in honor of me, your Lord.
translation_verse: Na mbrɛ hom ndzi no nye yi; hom mbobɔ hom asen do, hom mpaboa nhyehyɛ hom anan, na hom mpoma nkitsakitsa hom nsamu: na hom ndzi no ɔhar do: ɔyɛ EWURADZE apaho.

english_verse: That same night I will pass through Egypt and kill the first-born son in every family and the first-born male of all animals. I am the Lord, and I will punish the gods of Egypt.
translation_verse: Osiandɛ mubotwa mu wɔ Egypt asaase do anadwe no, na muboku Egypt asaase do mbakan nyinara, nyimpa nye mbowa nyina; na mubobu Egypt enyame nyinara ntsɛn: emi nye EWURADZE.

english_verse: The blood on the houses will show me where you live, and when I see the blood, I will pass over you. Then you won't be bothered by the terrible disasters I will bring on Egypt.
translation_verse: Na bɔgya no bɛyɛ asɛnkyerɛdze ama hom wɔ efi a hom wɔ mu no ho: na sɛ muhu mbɔgya no a, mebɛpa hom ho, na ɔyardɔm bi nnkɛba hom do abɛsɛɛ hom, sɛ morobɔbɔ Egypt asaase no a.

english_verse: Remember this day and celebrate it each year as a festival in my honor.
translation_verse: Na da yi nyɛ nkaa mma hom, na hom nhyɛ no fa mma EWURADZE: hom mfa mbra do nyɛ no afahyɛ wɔ hom ebusuasantsen mu daa.

english_verse: For seven days you must eat bread made without yeast. And on the first of these seven days, you must remove all yeast from your homes. If you eat anything made with yeast during this festival, you will no longer be part of Israel.
translation_verse: Nda esuon na hom ndzi epitsi boodoo; da a odzi kan no ara na hom nyi mbɔrkaw mfi hom efiefi: na obiara a obedzi mbɔrkaw boodoo ofitsi da a odzi kan no do dze kodu da a otsia esuon no do no, ɔkra no wobotwa no efi Israel.

english_verse: Meet together for worship on the first and seventh days of the festival. The only work you are allowed to do on either of these two days is that of preparing the bread.
translation_verse: Na da a odzi kan no hom nyɛ no nhyiamu krɔnkrɔn, na da a otsia esuon no so nhyiamu krɔnkrɔn; mma wɔnnyɛ edwuma biara wɔ mu, gyedɛ dza nyimpa biara bedzi nko na hom nyɛ.

english_verse: Celebrate this Festival of Thin Bread as a way of remembering the day that I brought your families and tribes out of Egypt. And do this each year.
translation_verse: Na hom ndwen epitsi afahyɛ no do; na dɛm da yi ara so na miyii hom dɔm no mifii Egypt asaase do: dɛm ntsi hom ndwen da yi do wɔ hom ebusuasantsen mu nyɛ no mbra daa.

english_verse: Begin on the evening of the fourteenth day of the first month by eating bread made without yeast. Then continue this celebration until the evening of the twenty-first day.
translation_verse: Bosoom a odzi kan no mu, bosoom no mu da a otsia duanan ewimbir no, na hom ndzi epitsi ma onkodu bosoom no mu da a otsia eduonu biako ewimbir.

english_verse: During these seven days no yeast is allowed in anyone's home, whether they are native Israelites or not. If you are caught eating anything made with yeast, you will no longer be part of Israel.
translation_verse: Nda esuon na mma mma wonnhu mbɔrkaw wɔ hom efi mu: na obiara a obedzi dza ɔaka no, ɔkra no wobotwa no efi Israel mboanofo mu, sɛ ɔyɛ ɔmamforanyi o, obi a wɔwoo no wɔ asaase no do o.

english_verse: Stay away from yeast, no matter where you live. No one is allowed to eat anything made with yeast!
translation_verse: Mma hom nndzi biribiara a akaw; hom ndzi epitsi wɔ hom tsenabew nyinara.

english_verse: Moses called the leaders of Israel together and said: Each family is to pick out a sheep and kill it for Passover.
translation_verse: Nna Moses frɛɛ Israel mpanyimfo nyinara, na ɔsee hɔn dɛ, Hom nkɔ nkeyiyi nguambaa dɛ mbrɛ hom ebusua tsetsee, na hom nku apaho no.

english_verse: Make a brush from a few small branches of a hyssop plant and dip the brush in the bowl that has the blood of the animal in it. Then brush some of the blood above the door and on the posts at each side of the door of your house. After this, everyone is to stay inside until morning.
translation_verse: Na hom mfa odwonhataw siaw, na hom mfa mbɔ bɔgya a ɔwɔ bɛɛsen no mu no mu, na hom mfa bɔgya a ɔwɔ bɛɛsen no mu no bi mpetse mpondua etsifi nye apondua ebien no ho; na mma hom mu obiara mmfir edzi mmfi ne fie abowano ma onkodu anapa.

english_verse: During that night the Lord will go through the country of Egypt and kill the first-born son in every Egyptian family. He will see where you have put the blood, and he will not come into your house. His angel that brings death will pass over and not kill your first-born sons.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE botwa mu abɔbɔ Egyptfo; na sɛ ohu bɔgya no wɔ mpondua etsifi, nye apondua ebien no ho a, EWURADZE bɛpa ɔpon no ho, na ɔnnkɛma ɔsɛɛfo no kwan ma ɔaaba hom efi mu abɔbobɔ hom.

english_verse: After you have entered the country promised to you by the Lord, you and your children must continue to celebrate Passover each year.
translation_verse: Na ɔbɛba dɛ, sɛ hom du asaase a EWURADZE dze bɛma hom no do, dɛ mbrɛ ɔabɔ anohoba no a, ɔno hom ndzi ɔsom yi do.

english_verse: Your children will ask you, “What are we celebrating?”
translation_verse: Na ɔbɛba dɛ, sɛ hom mba bisa hom dɛ, Hom som yi ase nye dɛn?

english_verse: And you will answer, “The Passover animal is killed to honor the Lord. We do these things because on that night long ago the Lord passed over the homes of our people in Egypt. He killed the first-born sons of the Egyptians, but he spared our children from death.” After Moses finished speaking, the people of Israel knelt down and worshiped the Lord.
translation_verse: Ɔno hom nse dɛ, Ɔyɛ EWURADZE n’apaho afɔrbɔdze, nyia ɔpaa Israelmba hɔn efi ho wɔ Egypt, wɔ aber a ɔbɔɔ Egyptfo, na oyii hɛn efi fii mu no. Na ɔman no bɔɔ hɔn tsir adze na wɔsɔree.

english_verse: Then they left and did what Moses and Aaron had told them to do. Death for the First-Born Sons
translation_verse: Na Israelmba kɔree kɛyɛɛ dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛɛ Moses nye Aaron no. Mbakamba Hɔn Wu

english_verse: At midnight the Lord killed the first-born son of every Egyptian family, from the son of the king to the son of every prisoner in jail. He also killed the first-born male of every animal that belonged to the Egyptians.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ esuom no, EWURADZE bɔbɔɔ Egypt asaase do mbakan nyinara, ofi Farao n’abakan a ɔtsena n’ahengua do no do dze kodu adaduadan mu ndɔmmum n’abakan do; nye ofiembowa mbakan nyinara.

english_verse: That night the king, his officials, and everyone else in Egypt got up and started crying bitterly. In every Egyptian home, someone was dead. The People of Israel Escape from Egypt
translation_verse: Na Farao soɛɛr anadwe no ara, ɔno, nye no nkowaa nyinara, nye Egyptfo nyinara: na su kɛse baa Egypt; osiandɛ fie biara nnyi hɔ a obi ennwu wɔ mu.

english_verse: During the night the king sent for Moses and Aaron and told them, “Get your people out of my country and leave us alone! Go and worship the Lord, as you have asked.
translation_verse: Na ɔfrɛɛ Moses nye Aaron anadwe, na ɔsee dɛ, Hom nsoɛr, hom mfi me man mu mfir, hom nye Israelmba no nyina; na hom nkɔ nkɔsom EWURADZE, dɛ mbrɛ hom aka no.

english_verse: Take your sheep, goats, and cattle, and get out. But ask your God to be kind to me.”
translation_verse: Hom mfa hom nguan nye hom anantwi so, dɛ mbrɛ hom aka no, na hom nkɔ; na hom nhyira mo so.

english_verse: The Egyptians did everything they could to get the Israelites to leave their country as quickly as possible. They said, “Please hurry and leave. If you don't, we will all be dead.”
translation_verse: Na Egyptfo no piapiaa ɔman no dɛ wɔrepam hɔn ntsɛm efi asaase no do; na wɔsee dɛ, Hɛn nyinara ayɛ ewufo ara nye no.

english_verse: So the Israelites quickly made some bread dough and put it in pans. But they did not mix any yeast in the dough to make it rise. They wrapped cloth around the pans and carried them on their shoulders.
translation_verse: Na ɔman no fefaa hɔn mbɔr ansaana ɔrekaw, hɔn mbɔrakorow no wɔakyekyer no wɔ hɔn atam mu sisi hɔn kɔn ho.

english_verse: The Israelites had already done what Moses had told them to do. They had gone to their Egyptian neighbors and asked for gold and silver and for clothes.
translation_verse: Na Israelmba no yɛɛ dɛ mbrɛ Moses kae no; na wobisabisaa dwetɛ ndwindze, nye sika ndwindze, nye atam wɔ Egyptfo hɔ:

english_verse: The Lord had made the Egyptians friendly toward the people of Israel, and they gave them whatever they asked for. In this way they carried away the wealth of the Egyptians when they left Egypt.
translation_verse: na EWURADZE maa ɔman no enyimpa wɔ Egyptfo no enyim, ma wɔmaa hɔn nsa kaa dza wobisae no. Na wɔfoom Egyptfo egyapadze kɔree. Israelmba No Fi Egypt

english_verse: The Israelites walked from the city of Rameses to the city of Succoth. There were about
translation_verse: Na Israelmba no sii mu fii Rameses kɔr Sukɔt, mbanyin ara a wɔnam famu bɛyɛ mpem ahaesia, a mbofraba nnka ho.

english_verse: Many other people went with them as well, and there were also a lot of sheep, goats, and cattle.
translation_verse: Na aforafora dɔm so nye hɔn kɔree; na nguan, na anantwi, mekyerɛ ofiembowa piigyan.

english_verse: They left Egypt in such a hurry that they did not have time to prepare any food except the bread dough made without yeast. So they baked it and made thin bread.
translation_verse: Na wɔdze mbɔr a wɔdze fi Egypt bae no totoo epitsi tatar, nna ɔnnkawee; osiandɛ wɔpaam hɔn fii Egypt ntsi, na woenntum enntsinntsim, nkyii so woennsiesie edzidzidze biara amma hɔnho.

english_verse: On that night the Lord kept watch for them, and on this same night each year Israel will always keep watch in honor of the Lord. Instructions for Passover
translation_verse: Ɔyɛ anadwe a ɔwɔ dɛ wɔhyɛ no nsew ma EWURADZE dɛ oyii hɔn fii Egypt asaase do: iyi nye EWURADZE n’anadwe no a wɔhyɛ no nsew wɔ Israelmba nyinara mu wɔ hɔn ebusuasantsen nyina mu no. Apaho No Ho Nhyehyɛɛ

english_verse: The Lord gave Moses and Aaron the following instructions for celebrating Passover: Only Israelites may eat the Passover meal.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses nye Aaron dɛ, Iyi nye apaho no ho mbra no: ɔnanaben biara nndzi bi:

english_verse: Your slaves may eat the meal if they have been circumcised,
translation_verse: na nyimpa biara n’akowaa a ɔdze sika atɔ no no dze, sɛ etwa no twatsia a, ɔno ondzi bi.

english_verse: but no foreigners who work for you are allowed to have any.
translation_verse: Mma ɔmamforanyi nye akowaa ɔpaanyi nndzi bi.

english_verse: The entire meal must be eaten inside, and no one may leave the house during the celebration. No bones of the Passover lamb may be broken.
translation_verse: Ofi kor mu na wondzi; mma mmfa nam no bi mmfi fie no mu mmpue; nkyii so mma hom mmbu ne biew bi.

english_verse: And all Israelites must take part in the meal.
translation_verse: Israel mboanofo nyinara ndzi do.

english_verse: If anyone who isn't an Israelite wants to celebrate Passover with you, every man and boy in that family must first be circumcised. Then they may join in the meal, just like native Israelites. No uncircumcised man or boy may eat the Passover meal!
translation_verse: Na sɛ ɔhɔho bi bɛnye wo tsena, na ɔpɛ dɛ odzi apaho no ma EWURADZE a, ma wontwa ne mbarimba nyinara twatsia, na nkyii ɔntwe mbɛn na ombedzi; na ɔnyɛ dɛ nyia wɔawo no asaase no do: monomonomnyi biara dze mma onndzi bi.

english_verse: This law applies both to native Israelites and to those foreigners who live among you.
translation_verse: Mbra kor na ɔnda hɔ mma nyia ɔyɛ okuromnyi, nye ɔhɔho a ɔtsena hom mu.

english_verse: The Israelites obeyed everything the Lord had commanded Moses and Aaron to tell them.
translation_verse: Na Israelmba nyinara yɛɛ dɛm; dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛɛ Moses nye Aaron no, dɛmara so na wɔyɛe.

english_verse: And on that same day the Lord brought Israel's families and tribes out of Egypt.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ da kor no ara, EWURADZE yii Israelmba no fii Egypt asaase do dɛ mbrɛ hɔn dɔm tsetsee.

english_verse: The Lord said to Moses,
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE kaa kyerɛɛ Moses dɛ,

english_verse: “Dedicate to me the first-born son of every family and the first-born males of your flocks and herds. These belong to me.” The Festival of Thin Bread
translation_verse: Tu mbakan nyinara si nkyɛn ma me, adze biara a obue yafun mu, wɔ Israelmba mu, wɔ onyimpa nye abowa mu nyinara; yɛ medze. Epitsi Afahyɛ

english_verse: The Lord promised your ancestors that he would bring you into the land of the Canaanites, Hittites, Amorites, Hivites, and Jebusites. It is a land rich with milk and honey. Each year during the month of Abib, celebrate these events in the following way:
translation_verse: Na ɔbɛba dɛ sɛ EWURADZE nya dze wo du Keenanfo, Hitifo, Amorifo, Hivifo, nye Jebusfo asaase a ɔkaa ho ntam kyerɛɛ w’egyanom dɛ ɔdze bɛma wo no, asaase a numfonsu nye ewo pem wɔ do no do a, ɔno som ɔsom yi wɔ bosoom yi mu.

english_verse: For seven days you are to eat bread made without yeast, and on the seventh day you are to celebrate a festival in honor of the Lord.
translation_verse: Nda esuon na dzi epitsi boodoo, na da a otsia esuon no bɛyɛ afahyɛ ama EWURADZE.

english_verse: During those seven days, you must not eat anything made with yeast or even have yeast anywhere near your homes.
translation_verse: Wondzi epitsi boodoo nda esuon no mu nyina; na mma wonnhu mbɔrkaw boodoo wɔ wo nkyɛn, nkyii so mma wonnhu mbɔrkaw wɔ wo nkyɛn, wɔ wo ntoaado nyinara mu.

english_verse: Then on the seventh day you must explain to your children that you do this because the Lord brought you out of Egypt.
translation_verse: Na da no kyerɛ mu kyerɛ wo ba dɛ, Osian dza EWURADZE yɛe maa me wɔ ber a mirifi Egypt aba no ntsi a.

english_verse: This celebration will be like wearing a sign on your hand or on your forehead, because then you will pass on to others the teaching of the Lord, whose mighty power brought you out of Egypt.
translation_verse: Na ɔbɛyɛ wo wo nsa do asɛnkyerɛdze, na w’enyiwa ntamu nkaadze, ama EWURADZE mbra no aako w’anomu: na nsa a ɔyɛ dzen na EWURADZE dze eyi wo efi Egypt.

english_verse: Celebrate this festival each year at the same time.
translation_verse: Dɛm ntsi dzi mbra yi do wɔ ne ber mu afrenhyia daa.

english_verse: The Lord will give you the land of the Canaanites, just as he promised you and your ancestors.
translation_verse: Na ɔbɛba dɛ sɛ EWURADZE dze wo du Keenanfo asaase do, dɛ mbrɛ ɔkaa ntam kyerɛɛ ɔwo nye w’egyanom no, na ɔdze ma wo a,

english_verse: From then on, you must give him every first-born son from your families and every first-born male from your animals, because these belong to him.
translation_verse: ɔno yi dza obue yafun mu nyinara si nkyɛn ma EWURADZE, na abakan biara a ewɔ a ofi abowa mu: enyin no nyɛ EWURADZE dze.

english_verse: You can spare the life of a first-born donkey by sacrificing a lamb; if you don't, you must break the donkey's neck. You must spare every first-born son.
translation_verse: Na asoasopɔnkɔ abakan biara fa eguambaa sesa; na sɛ annsesa a, ɔno bu no kɔn mu; na nyimpa mbakan a wɔwɔ wo mbabanyin mu nyinara dze sesa gye hɔn.

english_verse: In the future your children will ask what this ceremony means. Explain it to them by saying, “The Lord used his mighty power to rescue us from slavery in Egypt.
translation_verse: Na ɔbɛba dɛ sɛ ndaamba bi wo ba bisa wo dɛ, Ebɛnadze nye yi a, ase no dɛ, Nsa a ɔyɛ dzen na EWURADZE dze yii hɛn fii Egypt, fii nkowaasom fi no mu.

english_verse: The king stubbornly refused to set us free, so the Lord killed the first-born male of every animal and the first-born son of every Egyptian family. This is why we sacrifice to the Lord every first-born male of every animal and save every first-born son.”
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ, aber a nna Farao mmpen dɛ ɔbɛma yɛakɔ no, EWURADZE kunkum Egypt asaase do mbakan nyinara, nyimpa abakan, nye mbowa abakan nyina: dɛm ntsi na mo so medze enyin nyinara a wobue yafun mu no mobɔ EWURADZE afɔr; na me mbabanyin mu mbakan nyinara dze medze mbowa sesa gye hɔn.

english_verse: This ceremony will serve the same purpose as a sign on your hand or on your forehead to tell how the Lord's mighty power rescued us from Egypt. The Lord Leads His People
translation_verse: Na ɔbɛyɛ wo nsa do asɛnkyerɛdze, nye w’enyiwa ntamu nkaadze: na nsa a ɔyɛ dzen na EWURADZE dze yii hɛn fii Egypt. Ogya Na Mununkum Fadum

english_verse: After the king had finally let the people go, the Lord did not lead them through Philistine territory, though that was the shortest way. God had said, “If they are attacked, they may decide to return to Egypt.”
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ, Farao maa ɔman no kwan no, Nyankopɔn ammfa hɔn annkɛfa Filistiafo asaase do kwan do, kaansa ɔno ɔbɛn mpo a; na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Bi a bɛyɛ na ɔman no ekehyia ɔsa, na woenu hɔnho, na wɔasan akɔ Egypt:

english_verse: So he led them around through the desert and toward the Red Sea. The Israelites left Egypt, prepared for battle.
translation_verse: mbom Nyankopɔn dze ɔman no danee faa Mbɛw Po no ho sar do ɔkwan do; na Israelmba no miae fii Egypt asaase do kɔree.

english_verse: Moses had them take the bones of Joseph, whose dying words had been, “God will come to your rescue, and when he does, be sure to take my bones with you.”
translation_verse: Na Moses faa Josef mbiew no kaa no ho: osiandɛ ɔaka Israelmba ntam dɛ, Ampaara Nyankopɔn bɛba abɛsera hom; na hom mfa me mbiew nka hom ho mfi ha nkɔ.

english_verse: The people of Israel left Succoth and camped at Etham at the border of Egypt near the desert.
translation_verse: Na wotuu hɔn kwan fii Sukɔt, na wɔkɛkyeer nsera wɔ Etam, wɔ sar no ano.

english_verse: At Etham the Lord said to Moses:
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE kaa kyerɛɛ Moses dɛ,

english_verse: Tell the people of Israel to turn back and camp across from Pi-Hahiroth near Baal-Zephon, between Migdol and the Red Sea.
translation_verse: Ka kyerɛ Israelmba dɛ wɔnsan hɔn ekyir na wɔnkɛkyer nsera wɔ Pi-hahirot a wɔ Migidɔl nye Mbɛw po no ntamu na ɔbɛn Baal-zefon no enyim: ne tsentsendo na hom nkyer nsera no wɔ po no ne nkyɛn.

english_verse: The king will think you were afraid to cross the desert and that you are wandering around, trying to find another way to leave the country.
translation_verse: Na Farao bɛse wɔ Israelmba ho dɛ, Wɔatoto wɔ asaase no do, sar no atow hɔn mu.

english_verse: I will make the king stubborn again, and he will try to catch you. Then I will destroy him and his army. People everywhere will praise me for my victory, and the Egyptians will know that I really am the Lord. The Israelites obeyed the Lord and camped where he told them.
translation_verse: Na mibepirim Farao n’akoma, na obetsiw hom; na mebɛhyɛ m’enyim nyam wɔ Farao nye no dɔm nyinara do; na Egyptfo bohu dɛ emi nye EWURADZE. Na wɔyɛɛ dɛm.

english_verse: When the king of Egypt heard that the Israelites had finally left, he and his officials changed their minds and said, “Look what we have done! We let them get away, and they will no longer be our slaves.”
translation_verse: Na wɔkaa kyerɛɛ Egypthen no dɛ ɔman no eguan kɔ: na Farao nye no nkowaa no hɔn akoma sakyeree wɔ ɔman no ho, na wɔsee dɛ, Ebɛnadze na yɛayɛ yi, dɛ yɛama Israel egyaa hɛn som kɔ yi?

english_verse: The king got his war chariot and army ready.
translation_verse: Na ɔhyehyɛɛ ne tseaseanam, na ɔfaa ne man kaa no ho:

english_verse: The Lord made the king so stubborn that he went after them, while the Israelites proudly went on their way.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE piriim Egypthen Farao n’akoma, na otsiiw Israelmba no: na Israelmba no dze ahosɛpɛw kɛse fiir edzi kɔree.

english_verse: But the king's horses and chariots and soldiers caught up with them while they were camping by the Red Sea near Pi-Hahiroth and Baal-Zephon.
translation_verse: Na Egyptfo tsiiw hɔn, Farao no mpɔnkɔ nye ne ntseaseanam nyinara, nye no mpɔnkɔdofo, nye no dɔm no, na wɔbɔtoo hɔn dɛ wɔakyer nsera wɔ po no ano, Pi-hahirot nkyɛn, wɔ Baal-zefon enyim.

english_verse: When the Israelites saw the king coming with his army, they were frightened and begged the Lord for help.
translation_verse: Na aber a Farao bɛnee no, Israel memaa hɔn enyi do, na, hwɛ, Egyptfo etu dzi hɔn ekyir reba; na wosuroo dodow; na Israelmba no sui frɛɛ EWURADZE.

english_verse: They also complained to Moses, “Wasn't there enough room in Egypt to bury us? Is that why you brought us out here to die in the desert? Why did you bring us out of Egypt anyway?
translation_verse: Na wɔsee Moses dɛ, Osiandɛ ndakamona nnyi Egypt ntsi, na eyi hɛn hɔ ma yerubowuwu wɔ sar do yi? Ebɛnadze ntsi na enye hɛn edzi no dɛm yi, dɛ iyii hɛn fii Egypt bae yi?

english_verse: While we were there, didn't we tell you to leave us alone? We'd rather be slaves in Egypt than die in this desert!”
translation_verse: Ana nnyɛ yi nye asɛm a yɛkaa kyerɛɛ wo wɔ Egypt no, dɛ, Gyaa hɛn ma yɛnsom Egyptfo no? Osiandɛ nkyɛ obeye ama hɛn dɛ yɛbɔsom Egyptfo mbom sen dɛ yebowuwu wɔ sar do.

english_verse: But Moses answered, “Don't be afraid! Be brave, and you will see the Lord save you today. These Egyptians will never bother you again.
translation_verse: Na Moses see ɔman no dɛ, Mma hom nnsuro, hom ngyina dzinn, na hom nhwɛ EWURADZE no nkwagye a ɔrebɛyɛ akyerɛ hom ndɛ da yi: na Egyptfo a hom ehu hɔn ndɛ da yi, hom nnkohu hɔn bio dabiarada.

english_verse: The Lord will fight for you, and you won't have to do a thing.”
translation_verse: EWURADZE bɔko ama hom, na hom nka hom ano nto mu.

english_verse: The Lord said to Moses, “Why do you keep calling out to me for help? Tell the Israelites to move forward.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE bisaa Moses dɛ, Ebɛnadze ntsi na isu frɛ me yi? Kasa kyerɛ Israelmba, ma wɔnkɔ kan.

english_verse: Then hold your walking stick over the sea. The water will open up and make a road where they can walk through on dry ground.
translation_verse: Na ma wo poma no do, na tsen wo nsa wɔ po no do, na paa mu: na Israelmba nnantsew mfa po no ne finimfin wɔ daadze kɛsɛɛ do.

english_verse: I will make the Egyptians so stubborn that they will go after you. Then I will be praised because of what happens to the king and his chariots and cavalry.
translation_verse: Na emi, hwɛ, mibepirim Egyptfo akoma, na wobedzi hom ekyir: na mebɛhyɛ m’enyim nyam wɔ Farao do, nye no mpɔnkɔdofo do.

english_verse: All this time God's angel had gone ahead of Israel's army, but now he moved behind them. A large cloud had also gone ahead of them,
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn no bɔfoe a odzi Israel nseraban enyim no, ɔtwee noho na okedzii hɔn ekyir; na mununkum dum no twee fii hɔn enyim, na okegyinaa hɔn ekyir:

english_verse: but now it moved between the Egyptians and the Israelites. The cloud gave light to the Israelites, but made it dark for the Egyptians, and during the night they could not come any closer.
translation_verse: na ɔbaa Egypt nseraban no nye Israel nseraban no ntamu; na ɔyɛɛ mununkum nye sum, naaso ɔmaa kan anadwe: na kor ammba kor nkyɛn anadwe ne nyinara.

english_verse: Moses stretched his arm over the sea, and the Lord sent a strong east wind that blew all night until there was dry land where the water had been. The sea opened up,
translation_verse: Na Moses tseen ne nsa wɔ po no do; na EWURADZE dze boka mframa dzendzen maa po no kɔr ekyir anadwe no nyinara, na ɔyɛɛ po no asaase kɛsɛɛ, na nsu no mu paae.

english_verse: and the Israelites walked through on dry land with a wall of water on each side.
translation_verse: Na Israelmba no nantsewee faa po no ne finimfin wɔ daadze kɛsɛɛ no do: na nsu no yɛɛ hɔn fadum wɔ hɔn nsa enyimfa, nye hɔn abankum.

english_verse: But before daylight the Lord looked down at the Egyptian army from the fiery cloud and made them panic.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ anapa dasu mu no, EWURADZE nam ogya nye mununkum dum no mu hwɛɛ Egyptfo dɔm no, na ɔkaa Egyptfo dɔm no gui.

english_verse: Their chariot wheels got stuck, and it was hard for them to move. So the Egyptians said to one another, “Let's leave these people alone! The Lord is on their side and is fighting against us.”
translation_verse: Na ohuhuaan hɔn ntseaseanam n’anan hwiir ma no ntwee mu yɛɛ dzen: ma dɛm ntsi Egyptfo no see dɛ, Hom mma yemfi Israel enyim nguan; osiandɛ EWURADZE roko tsia Egyptfo dze ma hɔn.

english_verse: The Lord told Moses, “Stretch your arm toward the sea—the water will cover the Egyptians and their cavalry and chariots.”
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Tsen wo nsa wɔ po no do, ma nsu no mbra bio wɔ Egyptfo do, hɔn ntseaseanam do, nye hɔn mpɔnkɔdofo do.

english_verse: Moses stretched out his arm, and at daybreak the water rushed toward the Egyptians. They tried to run away, but the Lord drowned them in the sea.
translation_verse: Na Moses tseen ne nsa wɔ po no do, na adze kyee anapa no po no saan n’ekyir peem dɛ mbrɛ ɔtse no ara, na Egyptfo guanee behyiaa no; na EWURADZE toow Egyptfo guu po no ne finimfin.

english_verse: The water came and covered the chariots, the cavalry, and the whole Egyptian army that had followed the Israelites into the sea. Not one of them was left alive.
translation_verse: Na nsu no saan ekyir, na ɔbɛkataa ntseaseanam, nye mpɔnkɔdofo no, mekyerɛ Farao no dɔm a wodzii hɔn ekyir kɔr po no ne finimfin no nyinara do; hɔn mu kor mpo annka.

english_verse: But the sea had made a wall of water on each side of the Israelites, so they walked through on dry land.
translation_verse: Na Israelmba no dze, wɔnantseew asaase kɛsɛɛ do wɔ po no ne finimfin; na nsu no yɛɛ fadum maa hɔn wɔ hɔn nsa enyimfa, nye hɔn abankum.

english_verse: On that day, when the Israelites saw the bodies of the Egyptians washed up on the shore, they knew that the Lord had saved them.
translation_verse: Mbrɛ EWURADZE sii gyee Israel nkwa da no fii Egyptfo nsamu nye no: na Israel hun Egyptfo no dɛ woewuwu gu po no ano.

english_verse: Because of the mighty power he had used against the Egyptians, the Israelites worshiped him and trusted him and his servant Moses.
translation_verse: Na Israel hun dwuma kɛse a EWURADZE dzii no wɔ Egyptfo do, na ɔman no suroo EWURADZE: na wɔgyee EWURADZE, nye n’akowaa Moses dzii.

english_verse: Moses and the Israelites sang this song in praise of the Lord: I sing praises to the Lord for his great victory! He has thrown the horses and their riders into the sea.
translation_verse: Na Moses nye Israelmba no toow ndwom yi maa EWURADZE, na wɔkaa dɛ, Mobɔtow ndwom ama EWURADZE, osiandɛ ɔama noho do enyimnyam mu: Ɔpɔnkɔ nye no do tsefo no ɔatow hɔn egu po mu.

english_verse: The Lord is my strength, the reason for my song, because he has saved me. I praise and honor the Lord— he is my God and the God of my ancestors.
translation_verse: EWURADZE nye m’ahoɔdzen nye mo ndwom, Na ɔabɛyɛ mo nkwagye: Oyi nye me Nyankopɔn, na mibeyi no ayɛw; M’egya ne Nyankopɔn a, na mubohuhor no.

english_verse: The Lord is his name, and he is a warrior!
translation_verse: EWURADZE yɛ ɔsabarimba: EWURADZE nye ne dzin.

english_verse: He threw the chariots and army of Egypt's king into the Red Sea, and he drowned the best of the king's officers.
translation_verse: Farao ne ntseaseanam nye no dɔm no ɔatow hɔn egu po mu: Na n’asafohemfo a wɔasan mu eyi no wɔamemem Mbɛw Po no mu.

english_verse: They sank to the bottom just like stones.
translation_verse: Ebun akata hɔn do: Wɔamemem kɔ ase too dɛ ɔbo.

english_verse: With the tremendous force of your right arm, our Lord, you crushed your enemies.
translation_verse: EWURADZE, wo nsa enyimfa yɛ tum mu onyimnyamfo, EWURADZE, wo nsa enyimfa dwerew ɔtamfo no.

english_verse: What a great victory was yours, when you defeated everyone who opposed you. Your fiery anger wiped them out, as though they were straw.
translation_verse: Na w’abɔso akɛseyɛ no mu ara na edze butuw hɔn a wɔsoɛr tsia wo no: Ɔwo soma w’ebufuhyew ba, ɔhyew hɔn dɛ eburonan.

english_verse: You were so furious that the sea piled up like a wall, and the ocean depths curdled like cheese.
translation_verse: Na wo hwen mu ehuw mu na nsu no ano boae, Nsuyirii no gyinaa bantsenn dɛ kuw: Ebun no kyekyeer wɔ po akoma mu.

english_verse: Your enemies boasted that they would pursue and capture us, divide up our possessions, treat us as they wished, then take out their swords and kill us right there.
translation_verse: Ɔtamfo no see dɛ, Mibetsiw hɔn, mobɔto hɔn, mebɛkyɛ asadze no mu: Wɔbɛmee me kra; Mobɔtwe m’afona, me nsa bɛsɛɛ hɔn.

english_verse: But when you got furious, they sank like lead, swallowed by ocean waves.
translation_verse: Ɔwo dze wo mframa huwii, epo no kataa hɔn do: Wɔmeem dɛ sumbui wɔ nsu kɛse no mu.

english_verse: Our Lord, no other gods compare with you— Majestic and holy! Fearsome and glorious! Miracle worker!
translation_verse: EWURADZE, woana na ɔtse dɛ ɔwo, wɔ enyame mu? Woana na ɔtse dɛ ɔwo, krɔnkrɔnyɛ mu onyimnyamfo, Ayɛyi mu ɔhoyifo, anwanwadzeyɛfo?

english_verse: When you signaled with your right hand, your enemies were swallowed deep into the earth.
translation_verse: Ɔwo tseen wo nsa enyimfa, Asaase meen hɔn.

english_verse: The people you rescued were led by your powerful love to your holy place.
translation_verse: Ɔwo wo mbɔbɔrhu mu na egya ɔman a apon hɔn no kwan: Ɔwo w’ahoɔdzen mu na egya hɔn akɔ wo tsenabew krɔnkrɔn no.

english_verse: Nations learned of this and trembled— Philistines shook with horror.
translation_verse: Aman atsetse, hɔn ho rihim: Ahopopo aka hɔn a wɔtse Filistia.

english_verse: The leaders of Edom and of Moab were terrified. Everyone in Canaan fainted,
translation_verse: Nna Edom asafohen mpanyimfo bɔɔ hu; Moab akatakyi no, ahohim aka hɔn: Hɔn a wɔtse Keenan nyinara anan anan.

english_verse: struck down by fear. Our Lord, your powerful arm kept them still as a rock until the people you rescued to be your very own had marched by.
translation_verse: Nketsenketse nye suro ekitsa hɔn; W’abasa ne kɛseyɛ ntsi wɔayeyɛ dzindzinn dɛ ɔbo; Kodu dɛ wo man botwa mu, EWURADZE, Kodu dɛ wo man a enya hɔn no botwa mu.

english_verse: You will let your people settle on your own mountain, where you chose to live and to be worshiped.
translation_verse: Ɔwo dze hɔn bɛba, na abɛtsɛw hɔn wɔ w’egyapadze bepɔw no do, Bea a, EWURADZE, edze ayɛ wo tsenabew no a, Krɔnkrɔnbea a, EWURADZE, edze wo nsa dze etsim hɔ no a.

english_verse: Our Lord, you will rule forever! The Song of Miriam
translation_verse: EWURADZE bedzi hen daa. Miriam No Ndwom

english_verse: The Lord covered the royal Egyptian cavalry and chariots with the sea, after the Israelites had walked safely through on dry ground.
translation_verse: Na Farao no mpɔnkɔ nye ne ntseaseanam na ɔnye no mpɔnkɔdofo na wɔkɔr po no mu, na EWURADZE maa po no mu nsu no sanee bɛkataa hɔn do; na Israelmba no dze wɔnantseew asaase kɛsɛɛ do wɔ po ne finimfin.

english_verse: Miriam the sister of Aaron was a prophet. So she took her tambourine and led the other women out to play their tambourines and to dance.
translation_verse: Na nkɔnhyɛnyi Miriam, Aaron n’akyerɛbaa no, faa mfuaba kitsae: na mbaa no nyinara dze mfuaba na ɔnye asaw puei dzii n’ekyir.

english_verse: Then she sang to them: “Sing praises to the Lord for his great victory! He has thrown the horses and their riders into the sea.” Bitter Water at Marah
translation_verse: Na Miriam gyee hɔn do dɛ, Hom ntow ndwom mma EWURADZE, na oedzi konyim wɔ enyimnyam mu; Ɔpɔnkɔ nye no do tsefo no ɔatow hɔn egu po mu.

english_verse: After the Israelites left the Red Sea, Moses led them through the Shur Desert for three days, before finding water.
translation_verse: Na Moses gyaa Israel fii Mbɛw Po no ho kɔr kan, na wɔkɔr Sur sar do, na wɔnantseew sar no do ndaansa, na woennhu nsu.

english_verse: They did find water at Marah, but it was bitter, which is how that place got its name.
translation_verse: Na wɔbaa Mara no, woenntum annom Mara nsu no bi, osiandɛ ɔyɛ wen: dɛm ntsi wɔfrɛɛ ne dzin Mara.

english_verse: The people complained and said, “Moses, what are we going to drink?”
translation_verse: Na ɔman no hwinhwiin Moses ho dɛ, Ebɛnadze na yɛnnom?

english_verse: Moses asked the Lord for help, and the Lord told him to throw a certain piece of wood into the water. Moses did so, and the water became fit to drink. At Marah the Lord tested his people and also gave them some laws and teachings.
translation_verse: Na osui frɛɛ EWURADZE; na EWURADZE kyerɛɛ no dua bi, na ɔtowee kyeen nsu no mu, na nsu no yɛɛ dɛw. Hɔ na ɔhyɛɛ hɔn ahyɛdze nye mbra, na hɔ so na ɔsɔɔ hɔn hwɛe;

english_verse: Then he said, “I am the Lord your God, and I cure your diseases. If you obey me by doing right and by following my laws and teachings, I won't punish you with the diseases I sent on the Egyptians.”
translation_verse: na ɔsee dɛ, Sɛ ibetsie EWURADZE wo Nyankopɔn ne ndze yie, na ayɛ dza ɔtsen wɔ n’enyiwa do, na ayɛ aso wɔ ne mbransɛm do, na edzi n’ahyɛdze nyinara do a, ɔnodze mennkɛfa yarba biara a medze megu Egyptfo do no bi megu wo do: na emi nye EWURADZE a ɔsa wo yarba no.

english_verse: Later the Israelites came to Elim, where there were twelve springs and
translation_verse: Na wɔbaa Elim, mbrɛ nsunyiwa duebien, nye mbɛdua eduosuon wɔ: na wɔkyeer nsera wɔ hɔ wɔ nsu no ho.

english_verse: On the fifteenth day of the second month after the Israelites had escaped from Egypt, they left Elim and started through the western edge of the Sinai Desert in the direction of Mount Sinai.
translation_verse: Na wotui fii Elim, na Israel mboanofo no nyinara baa Sin sar a ɔda Elim nye Sinai ntamu no do, nna wofii Egypt asaase do no, no bosoom a otsia ebien no mu da a otsia duenum nye no.

english_verse: There in the desert they started complaining to Moses and Aaron,
translation_verse: Na Israelmba mboanofo man mu nyinara hwinhwiin Moses nye Aaron ho wɔ sar no do:

english_verse: “We wish the Lord had killed us in Egypt. When we lived there, we could at least sit down and eat all the bread and meat we wanted. But you have brought us out here into this desert, where we are going to starve.”
translation_verse: na Israelmba no see hɔn dɛ, Mbom nkyɛ EWURADZE mma yenwuwu ne nsaano wɔ Egypt asaase do, aber a yɛtsetse nam nkutu ho no, aber a yedzi edziban mee no; osiandɛ hom dze hɛn efir edzi aba sar yi do, dɛ hom dze akɔmfun rubokuku mboanofo man mu yi nyinara.

english_verse: The Lord said to Moses, “I will send bread down from heaven like rain. Tell the people to go out each day and gather only enough for that day. That's how I will see if they obey me.
translation_verse: Nna EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Hwɛ, mebɛma edziban efi sor atɔ ama hom; na ɔman no befir edzi akɛtase dza ɔbɔso hɔn ano da kor wɔ ne da mu, ma maasɔ hɔn mahwɛ kyerɛ dɛ wobedzi me mbra do anaa wonnkedzi do.

english_verse: But on the sixth day of each week they must gather and cook twice as much.”
translation_verse: Na ɔbɛba dɛ da a otsia esia no, wobesiesie dza wɔdze ba no, na ɔbɛyɛ dza wɔtase no daa no no mbɔho ebien.

english_verse: Moses and Aaron told the people, “This evening you will know that the Lord was the one who rescued you from Egypt.
translation_verse: Na Moses nye Aaron see Israelmba no nyinara dɛ, Ewimbir nna hom bohu dɛ EWURADZE eyi hom efi Egypt asaase do:

english_verse: And in the morning you will see his glorious power, because he has heard your complaints against him. Why should you grumble to us? Who are we?”
translation_verse: na anapa nna hom bohu EWURADZE n’enyimnyam; osiandɛ ɔtse hom ehwinhwin a hom hwinhwin EWURADZE ho no: na hɛn dze, ebɛnadze nye hɛn, na hom hwinhwin hɛn ho?

english_verse: Then Moses continued, “You will know it is the Lord when he gives you meat each evening and more than enough bread each morning. He is really the one you are complaining about, not us—we are nobodies—but the Lord has heard your complaints.”
translation_verse: Na Moses see dɛ, Iyi bɛba, EWURADZE bɛma hom nam ewimbir ma woeedzi, na edziban anapa ma wɔaamee, osiandɛ EWURADZE tse hom ehwinhwin a hom dze hwinhwin no ho: na ebɛnadze nye hɛn? Nnyɛ hɛn ho na hom hwinhwin, mbom EWURADZE ho a.

english_verse: Moses turned to Aaron and said, “Bring the people together, because the Lord has heard their complaints.”
translation_verse: Na Moses see Aaron dɛ, Se Israelmba mboanofo no nyinara dɛ, Hom ntwe mpin EWURADZE enyim: na ɔatse hom ehwinhwin no.

english_verse: Aaron was speaking to them, when everyone looked out toward the desert and saw the bright glory of the Lord in a cloud.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ Aaron rekasa kyerɛ Israelmba mboanofo nyinara no, wɔdandaan hɔn enyi kɔr sar no do, na hwɛ, EWURADZE enyimnyam puei wɔ mununkum no mu.

english_verse: The Lord said to Moses,
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE kaa kyerɛɛ Moses dɛ,

english_verse: “I have heard my people complain. Now tell them that each evening they will have meat and each morning they will have more than enough bread. Then they will know that I am the Lord their God.”
translation_verse: Matse Israelmba ehwinhwin no: ka kyerɛ hɔn dɛ, Ewimbir hom bedzi nam, na anapa hom edzi edziban amee; na hom ehu dɛ emi nye EWURADZE hom Nyankopɔn.

english_verse: That evening a lot of quails came and landed everywhere in the camp, and the next morning dew covered the ground.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ ewimbir no Nkokɔhwerɛw bae, na wɔbɛkataa nseraban no do: na anapa no ɔbɔw tɔr nseraban no ho hyiae.

english_verse: After the dew had gone, the desert was covered with thin flakes that looked like frost.
translation_verse: Na ɔbɔw a ɔtɔree no kɔr sor no, hwɛ, adze bi nkorkorba nketsenketse deda sar no enyi, nketsenketse dɛ famu ɔbɔsu kyenkyenee.

english_verse: The people had never seen anything like this, and they started asking each other, “What is it?” Moses answered, “This is the bread that the Lord has given you to eat.
translation_verse: Na aber a Israelmba hunii no, wɔsesee hɔnho nkorkor dɛ, Ebɛnadze nye yi? Osiandɛ wonnyim adze kor no. Na Moses see hɔn dɛ, Iyi nye edziban a EWURADZE dze ama hom dɛ hom ndzi.

english_verse: And he orders you to gather about two liters for each person in your family—that should be more than enough.”
translation_verse: Iyi nye adze a EWURADZE ahyɛ, Hom ntase bi dɛ mbrɛ nyimpa biara n’edzidzi tse; olonka nkorkor, dɛ mbrɛ hom nyimpa dodow tse, nna hom mfefa, nyimpa biara mfa mma hɔn a wɔwɔ n’asesee mu no.

english_verse: They did as they were told. Some gathered more and some gathered less.
translation_verse: Na Israelmba no yɛɛ dɛm, na wɔtasee, binom dze so, binom dze suar.

english_verse: Moses told them not to keep any overnight.
translation_verse: Na Moses see hɔn dɛ, Mma obi nngya bi ma adze nnkye do.

english_verse: Some of them disobeyed, but the next morning what they kept was stinking and full of worms, and Moses was angry.
translation_verse: Naaso woenntsie Moses; mbom hɔn mu binom gyaa bi ma adze kyee do, na ɔdɔɔ mbowaba, na ɔbɔnee; na Moses no bo fuuw hɔn.

english_verse: Each morning everyone gathered as much as they needed, and in the heat of the day the rest melted.
translation_verse: Na wɔtasee no anapa anapa, nyimpa biara dɛ mbrɛ n’edzidzi tse: na ewia ano yɛɛ dzen no, ɔnanee.

english_verse: However, on the sixth day of the week, everyone gathered enough to have four liters, instead of two. When the leaders reported this to Moses,
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ, da a otsia esia no wɔtasee edziban no mbɔho, ɔbaako dze yɛ olonka ebien: na mboanofo no mu mpanyimfo nyinara baa bɛkaa kyerɛɛ Moses.

english_verse: he told them that the Lord had said, “Tomorrow is the Sabbath, a sacred day of rest in honor of me. So gather all you want to bake or boil, and make sure you save enough for tomorrow.”
translation_verse: Na ɔsee hɔn dɛ, Iyi nye dza EWURADZE aka no, Ɔkyena yɛ awɔse ahomgye, ahomgyeda krɔnkrɔn ma EWURADZE: hom nto dza hom bɔto, na hom nnoa dza hom bɔnoa; na dza ɔka no nyinara hom mfa nsie ma adze nkye do.

english_verse: The people obeyed, and the next morning the food smelled fine and had no worms.
translation_verse: Na wɔdze siee ma okodur anapa, dɛ mbrɛ Moses hyɛe no: na ɔammbɔn, nkyii so abowaba bi nnyi mu.

english_verse: “You may eat the food,” Moses said. “Today is the Sabbath in honor of the Lord, and there won't be any of this food on the ground today.
translation_verse: Na Moses see dɛ, Hom ndzi ɔno ndɛ; na ndɛ ɔyɛ ahomgyeda ma EWURADZE: ndɛ hom nnkohu bi wɔ haban mu.

english_verse: You will find it there for the first six days of the week, but not on the Sabbath.”
translation_verse: Nda esia na hom ntase; na da a otsia esuon no dze ɔyɛ ahomgyeda, ɔno mu bi nnkɛba hɔ.

english_verse: A few of the Israelites did go out to look for some, but there was none.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ da a otsia esuon no, ɔman no mu bi fiir edzi dɛ wɔrekɛtase, na woennhu bi.

english_verse: Then the Lord said, “Moses, how long will you people keep disobeying my laws and teachings?
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Hom ropow dɛ wodzi me mbransɛm nye me mbra do ma ekodu dabɛn?

english_verse: Remember that I was the one who gave you the Sabbath. That's why on the sixth day I provide enough bread for two days. Everyone is to stay home and rest on the Sabbath.”
translation_verse: Hom nhwɛ, osiandɛ EWURADZE ama hom ahomgyeda ntsi, na da a otsia esia no ɔma hom nda ebien edziban: hom mu nyimpa biara ntsena ne tsenabew, mma obiara mmfir edzi mmfi ne tsenabew wɔ da a otsia esuon no mu.

english_verse: And so they rested on the Sabbath.
translation_verse: Nna ɔman no hoom da a otsia esuon no.

english_verse: The Israelites called the bread manna. It was white like coriander seed and delicious as wafers made with honey.
translation_verse: Na Israel fi frɛɛ ne dzin Manna: nna ɔtse dɛ wusa aba, fitaa, na ne dɛw tse dɛ tatar ntrɛtraba a wɔdze ewo ayɛ.

english_verse: Moses told the people that the Lord had said, “Store up two liters of this manna, because I want future generations to see the food I gave you during the time you were in the desert after I rescued you from Egypt.”
translation_verse: Na Moses see dɛ, Iyi nye adze a EWURADZE ahyɛ, Wɔmfa bi nhyɛ esusudze olonka ma mfa nsie hom ebusuasantsen; ama woeehu edziban a medze menyɛɛn hom wɔ sar no do, aber a miyii hom fii Egypt asaase do no.

english_verse: Then Moses told Aaron, “Put some manna in a jar and store it in the place of worship for future generations to see.”
translation_verse: Na Moses see Aaron dɛ, Fa kutu bi, na fa manna esusudze olonka kor gu mu, na fa si EWURADZE enyim, ma wɔmfa nsie hom ebusuasantsen.

english_verse: Aaron followed the Lord's instructions and put the manna in front of the sacred chest for safekeeping.
translation_verse: Dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛɛ Moses no, dɛmara so na Aaron dze sii Adasehyɛdze no enyim, ma wɔdze eesie.

english_verse: The Israelites ate manna for
translation_verse: Afei esusudze olonka yɛ efa mu ebupɛn du mu kor.

english_verse: The Israelites left the desert and moved from one place to another each time the Lord ordered them to. Once they camped at Rephidim, but there was no water for them to drink.
translation_verse: Na Israelmba mboanofo no nyinara tui fii Sin sar no do, wɔ hɔn akwantu mu, dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE mbrasɛm tse, na wɔkɛkyekyeer nsera wɔ Refidim: na nsu nnyi hɔ mma ɔman no dɛ wɔbɔnom.

english_verse: The people started complaining to Moses, “Give us some water!” Moses replied, “Why are you complaining to me and trying to put the Lord to the test?”
translation_verse: Dɛm ntsi ɔman no nye Moses hamee, na wɔsee dɛ, Ma hɛn nsu ma yɛnnom. Na Moses see hɔn dɛ, Ebɛnadze ntsi na hom nye me reham? Ebɛnadze ntsi na hom rosɔ EWURADZE ahwɛ yi?

english_verse: But the people were thirsty and kept on complaining, “Moses, did you bring us out of Egypt just to let us and our families and our animals die of thirst?”
translation_verse: Na nsukɔm dzee ɔman no wɔ hɔ; na ɔman no hwinhwiin Moses ho, na wɔsee dɛ, Ebɛnadze ntsi na iyii hɛn fii Egypt bae, dɛ edze nsukɔm ruboku hɛn nye hɛn mba nye hɛn mbowa yi?

english_verse: Then Moses prayed to the Lord, “What am I going to do with these people? They are about to stone me to death!”
translation_verse: Na Moses sui frɛɛ EWURADZE see dɛ, Ebɛnadze na menyɛ memma ɔman yi? Aka kakra ma woeesiw me abo.

english_verse: The Lord answered, “Take some of the leaders with you and go ahead of the rest of the people. Also take along the walking stick with which you struck the Nile River.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Pa ho kedzi ɔman no enyim, na fa Israel mpanyin no bi ka woho; na wo poma a edze bɔɔ esutsen no, fa kitsa wo nsamu, na kɔ.

english_verse: When you get to the rock at Mount Sinai, I will be there with you. Strike the rock with the stick, and water will pour out for the people to drink.” Moses did this while the leaders watched.
translation_verse: Hwɛ, mibegyina w’enyim wɔ hɔ wɔ Horeb ɔbotan no do; na bɔ ɔbotan no, na nsu befi mu aba, ma ɔman no aanom. Na Moses yɛɛ dɛm wɔ Israel mpanyin no enyim.

english_verse: The people had complained and tested the Lord by asking, “Is the Lord really with us?” So Moses named that place Massah, which means “testing” and Meribah, which means “complaining.” Israel Defeats the Amalekites
translation_verse: Na ɔfrɛɛ bea no ne dzin Massa, nye Meriba, osian Israelmba hɔn ham no ntsi, na EWURADZE a wɔsɔɔ no hwɛe see dɛ, Ana EWURADZE wɔ hɛn mu, anaa onnyi mu ntsi a? Israel nye Amalek Ko

english_verse: When the Israelites were at Rephidim, they were attacked by the Amalekites.
translation_verse: Nna Amalek bae, na ɔnye Israel bɔkoe wɔ Refidim.

english_verse: So Moses told Joshua, “Have some men ready to attack the Amalekites tomorrow. I will stand on a hilltop, holding this walking stick that has the power of God.”
translation_verse: Na Moses see Josua dɛ, San mu yiyi mbanyin ma hɛn, na fir edzi kɔ, nye Amalek nkɔko: ɔkyena medze Nyankopɔn poma no bekitsa me nsamu mekegyina koko no etsifi.

english_verse: Joshua led the attack as Moses had commanded, while Moses, Aaron, and Hur stood on the hilltop.
translation_verse: Nna Josua yɛɛ dɛ mbrɛ Moses see no no, na ɔnye Amalek kɔkoe: na Moses, Aaron, nye Hur fowee kɔr koko no etsifi.

english_verse: The Israelites out-fought the Amalekites as long as Moses held up his arms, but they started losing whenever he lowered them.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ, sɛ Moses ma ne nsa do a, nna Israel edzi nyim: na sɛ ɔto ne nsa famu a, nna Amalek edzi nyim.

english_verse: After a while, his arms were so tired that Aaron and Hur got a rock for him to sit on. Then they stood beside him and supported his arms in the same position until sunset.
translation_verse: Na Moses ne nsa gyenee; na wɔfaa ɔbo, na wɔdze too n’ase, na ɔtsenaa do; na Aaron nye Hur susuoo ne nsamu gyinae, ɔbaako dze afa, na ɔbaako so dze afa so; na ne nsa gyinaa dzinn kodur witɔree.

english_verse: That's how Joshua defeated the Amalekites.
translation_verse: Na Josua dwereew Amalek nye ne man afona ano.

english_verse: Afterwards, the Lord said to Moses, “Write an account of this victory and read it to Joshua. I want the Amalekites to be forgotten forever.”
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Kyerɛw iyi wɔ nwoma mu fa yɛ nkaa, na ka hyɛ Josua n’asomu: dɛ pepa ara na mebɛpepa Amalek ho nkaa mefi ɔsor ase.

english_verse: Moses built an altar and named it “The Lord Gives Me Victory.”
translation_verse: Na Moses sii afɔrmbukyia, na ɔfrɛɛ ne dzin Yahweh-nissi, ,

english_verse: Then Moses explained, “This is because I depended on the Lord. But in future generations, the Lord will fight the Amalekites again and again.”
translation_verse: na ɔsee dɛ, EWURADZE esua: EWURADZE nye Amalek bedzi ako fi ebusuasantsen do ekodu ebusuasantsen do.

english_verse: While Israel was camped in the desert near Mount Sinai, Jethro sent Moses this message: “I am coming to visit you, and I am bringing your wife and two sons.”
translation_verse: na ɔsee Moses dɛ, Emi w’asew Jetro, nye wo yer, nye wo mbabanyin beenu no ka no ho, aba wo nkyɛn.

english_verse: When they arrived, Moses went out and bowed down in front of Jethro, then kissed him. After they had greeted each other, they went into the tent,
translation_verse: Na Moses fiir edzi kehyiaa n’asew, na ɔkotowee, na ɔfeew n’ano; na wobisabisaa hɔn apɔwmu; na wɔbaa asese mu.

english_verse: where Moses told him everything the Lord had done to protect Israel against the Egyptians and their king. He also told him how the Lord had helped them in all of their troubles.
translation_verse: Na Moses kaa dza osian Israel ntsi EWURADZE dze ayɛ Farao nye Egyptfo nyinara kyerɛɛ n’asew, awoko a ɔbaa hɔn do wɔ ɔkwan no mu nyinara, na mbrɛ EWURADZE sii yii hɔn no.

english_verse: Now I know that the Lord is the greatest God, because he has rescued Israel from their arrogant enemies.”
translation_verse: Afei na mehu dɛ EWURADZE yɛ kɛse kyɛn enyame nyinara: nyew, adze a wɔdze dzi hɔn do nkontompɔsɛm no ara mu a.

english_verse: Jethro offered sacrifices to God. Then Aaron and Israel's leaders came to eat with Jethro there at the place of worship. Judges Are Appointed (Deuteronomy
translation_verse: Na Jetro, Moses n’asew no, faa ɔhyew afɔr nye okum afɔr kɛmaa Nyankopɔn: na Aaron nye Israel mpanyin nyinara baa dɛ wɔnye Moses n’asew no ribedzidzi wɔ Nyankopɔn enyim. Moses Yi Atsɛmbuafo (Deuteronomi

english_verse: You and the people who come to you will soon be worn out. The job is too much for one person; you can't do it alone.
translation_verse: Ampa ebɛberɛ, ɔwo nye ɔman a wɔka wo ho yi nyina, osiandɛ adze no yɛ dur dodow ma wo; ɔwo woara nko irunntum nnyɛ.

english_verse: The next morning Moses sat down at the place where he decided legal cases for the people, and everyone crowded around him until evening.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ adze kyee no, Moses tsenaa famu dɛ orubu ɔman no atsɛn: na ɔman no gyinaa Moses do tsentsen fi anapa kodur ewimbir.

english_verse: Jethro saw how much Moses had to do for the people, and he asked, “Why are you the only judge? Why do you let these people crowd around you from morning till evening?”
translation_verse: Na aber a Moses n’asew hun dza ɔyɛe maa ɔman no nyinara no, ɔsee dɛ, Ebɛnadze na ereyɛ ma ɔman yi? Ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na woara nko etse hɔ, na ɔman no nyinara gyinagyina wo do tsentsen fi anapa ebodu ewimbir yi?

english_verse: Moses answered, “Because they come here to find out what God wants them to do.
translation_verse: Na Moses see n’asew no dɛ, Osiandɛ ɔman no ba me nkyɛn Nyankopɔn hɔ ebisa:

english_verse: They bring their complaints to me, and I make decisions on the basis of God's laws.”
translation_verse: sɛ wonya asɛm bi a, wɔba me nkyɛn; na mubu nyimpa nye ne nyɛnko ntamu atsɛn, na mema wohu Nyankopɔn n’ahyɛdze, nye ne mbra.

english_verse: Jethro replied: That isn't the best way to do it.
translation_verse: Na Moses n’asew no see no dɛ, Adze a eyɛ yi onnye.

english_verse: God will help you if you follow my advice. You should be the one to speak to God for the people,
translation_verse: Afei tsie me ndze, mebɛma wo apam, na Nyankopɔn ndzi w’ekyir; ɔwo gyina Nyankopɔn afamu ma ɔman no, na fa nsɛm no brɛ Nyankopɔn:

english_verse: and you should teach them God's laws and show them what they must do to live right.
translation_verse: na kyerɛkyerɛ hɔn ahyɛdze no nye mbra no, na ma wonhu ɔkwan kor a ɔwɔ dɛ wɔnantsew mu, na edwuma a ɔwɔ dɛ wɔyɛ.

english_verse: You will need to appoint some competent leaders who respect God and are trustworthy and honest. Then put them over groups of
translation_verse: Afei so yiyi ɔman no nyinara mu mbanyin a wotum a wosuro Nyankopɔn a wɔyɛ anokwafo a wokyir enyikaber, na fa hɔn sisi hɔn do, ma wɔnyɛ apem apem do mpanyin, ɔha ɔha do mpanyin, eduonum eduonum do mpanyin, nye du du do mpanyin;

english_verse: These judges can handle the ordinary cases and bring the more difficult ones to you. Having them to share the load will make your work easier.
translation_verse: na ma wombu ɔman no ntsɛn aber nyinara mu: na ɔmbra dɛ asɛnkɛse biara dze wɔmfa mbrɛ wo, na nsɛnketseaba nyinara dze, hɔnara wondzi: ama aayɛ wo do har, na wɔnye wo aasoa adzesoa no.

english_verse: This is the way God wants it done. You won't be under nearly as much stress, and everyone else will return home feeling satisfied.
translation_verse: Sɛ eyɛ dɛm adze yi, na Nyankopɔn hyɛ wo dɛm a, ɔno na ibotum asɔw, na ɔman yi nyinara so bɔkɔ hɔn tsenabew dwudwoodwo.

english_verse: They served as judges, deciding the easy cases themselves, but bringing the more difficult ones to Moses.
translation_verse: Na wobuu ɔman no ntsɛn aber nyinara mu: nsɛm a ɔyɛ dzen no wɔdze brɛɛ Moses, na nsɛnketseaba dze hɔnara wodzii.

english_verse: After Moses and his father-in-law Jethro had said goodbye to each other, Jethro returned home.
translation_verse: Na Moses gyaa n’asew no kwan; na ɔfaa no kwan kɔr nankasa n’asaase do.

english_verse: The Israelites left Rephidim and arrived at the desert. Then two months after leaving Egypt, they arrived at the desert near Mount Sinai, where they set up camp at the foot of the mountain. This was two months after they had left Egypt.
translation_verse: Na aber a wotui fii Refidim, na wɔbaa Sinai sar do no, wɔkyeer nsera wɔ sar no do; na hɔ na Israel kyeer nsera wɔ bepɔw no enyim.

english_verse: Moses went up the mountain to meet with the Lord God, who told him to say to the people:
translation_verse: Na Moses fowee kɔr Nyankopɔn nkyɛn, na EWURADZE fii bepɔw no do frɛɛ no see dɛ, Dɛm na se Jakob fi, na ka kyerɛ Israelmba;

english_verse: You saw what I did in Egypt, and you know how I brought you here to me, just as a mighty eagle carries its young.
translation_verse: Hom ehu dza medze mayɛ Egyptfo, na mbrɛ mosoaa hom nkɔr ntsaban do, na medze hom baa mara me nkyɛn.

english_verse: Now if you will faithfully obey me, you will be my very own people. The whole world is mine,
translation_verse: Dɛm ntsi afei, sɛ hom betsie me ndze ara ampa, na woedzi m’ahyɛmu do a, ɔnodze, hom bɛyɛ egyapadze a ɔsom bo efi aman nyinara mu ama me: osiandɛ asaase nyinara yɛ medze:

english_verse: but you will be my holy nation and serve me as priests. Moses, that is what you must tell the Israelites.
translation_verse: na hom bɛyɛ asɔfo ahendzi, nye ɔman krɔnkrɔn ama me. Iyinom nye nsɛm a fa ka kyerɛ Israelmba no.

english_verse: After Moses went back, he reported to the leaders what the Lord had said,
translation_verse: Na Moses bae na ɔbɛfrɛɛ ɔman mpanyimfo no, na ɔdze nsɛm yi a EWURADZE dze hyɛɛ no nyinara too hɔn enyim.

english_verse: and they all promised, “We will do everything the Lord has commanded.” So Moses told the Lord about this.
translation_verse: Na ɔman no nyinara bɔɔ mu buae, na wɔsee dɛ, Dza EWURADZE aka nyinara, yɛbɛyɛ. Na Moses kɛkaa ɔman no hɔn nsɛm no kyerɛɛ EWURADZE.

english_verse: The Lord said to Moses, “I will come to you in a thick cloud and let the people hear me speak to you. Then they will always trust you.” Again Moses reported to the Lord what the people had said.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Hwɛ, mereba wo nkyɛn wɔ mununkum kebii mu, ama sɛ menye wo kasa a ɔman no aatse, na eso wɔaagye wo edzi daa. Na Moses kaa ɔman no hɔn nsɛm no kyerɛɛ EWURADZE.

english_verse: Once more the Lord spoke to Moses: Go back and tell the people that today and tomorrow they must get themselves ready to meet me. They must wash their clothes
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Kɔ ɔman no nkyɛn, na tsew hɔn ho ndɛ nye ɔkyena, na ma wɔnhohor hɔn ntar,

english_verse: and be ready by the day after tomorrow, when I will come down to Mount Sinai, where all of them can see me.
translation_verse: na wonsiesie hɔnho nsie da a otsia ebiasa no: na da a otsia ebiasa no EWURADZE besian wɔ ɔman no nyinara enyim aba bepɔw Sinai do.

english_verse: Warn the people that they are forbidden to touch any part of the mountain. Anyone who does will be put to death,
translation_verse: Na bɔ ɔman no ho ɔheban hyia, se dɛ, Hom nhwɛ homho yie, ma hom aammfow bepɔw no, anaa hom annkɛka ne he: obiara a ɔbɛka bepɔw no oku na woboku no:

english_verse: either with stones or arrows, and no one must touch the body of the person being put to death in this way. Even an animal that touches this mountain must be put to death. You may go up the mountain only after a signal is given on the trumpet.
translation_verse: nsa biara nnka no, mbom abo na wonsiw no, anaa tow na wɔntow no ma onhwira mu; sɛ abowa o, nyimpa o, orinnya nkwa: sɛ abɛn no hɛn kyɛr a, wɔmfow bepɔw no.

english_verse: After Moses went down the mountain, he gave orders for the people to wash their clothes and make themselves acceptable to worship God.
translation_verse: Na Moses sianee fi bepɔw no do kɔr ɔman no nkyɛn, na ɔtseew ɔman no ho; na wɔhohoor hɔn ntar.

english_verse: He told them to be ready in three days and not to have sex in the meantime. The Lord Comes to Mount Sinai
translation_verse: Na ɔsee ɔman no dɛ, Hom nsiesie homho nsie da a otsia ebiasa no: mma wɔnnkɔ ɔbaa biara ho.

english_verse: On the morning of the third day there was thunder and lightning. A thick cloud covered the mountain, a loud trumpet blast was heard, and everyone in camp trembled with fear.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ da a otsia ebiasa no, anapa no, asenaman nye enyinam, nye mununkum kebii baa bepɔw no do, na abɛn ndze dzendzen mbordo; na ɔman a wɔwɔ nseraban no mu nyinara ho popoe.

english_verse: Moses led them out of the camp to meet God, and they stood at the foot of the mountain.
translation_verse: Na Moses dze ɔman no fir edzi fii nseraban no mu dɛ worikehyia Nyankopɔn; na wokegyinagyinaa bepɔw no ase afamu.

english_verse: Mount Sinai was covered with smoke because the Lord had come down in a flaming fire. Smoke poured out of the mountain just like a furnace, and the whole mountain shook.
translation_verse: Na bepɔw Sinai no nyinara rupun wusiw, osiandɛ EWURADZE nam gya mu sianee baa do: na no wusiw no tui kɔr sor dɛ fononoo mu wusiw, na bepɔw no nyinara wosoow kɛsenara.

english_verse: The trumpet blew louder and louder. Moses spoke, and God answered him with thunder.
translation_verse: Na abɛn no ne ndze yɛɛ dzendzenndzen dodow no, Moses kasae, na Nyankopɔn dze ndze gyee no do.

english_verse: The Lord came down to the top of Mount Sinai and told Moses to meet him there.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE sianee baa bepɔw Sinai do, wɔ bepɔw no etsifi: na EWURADZE frɛɛ Moses dɛ ɔnkɔ bepɔw no etsifi: na Moses fowee kɔree.

english_verse: Then he said, “Moses, go and warn the people not to cross the boundary that you set at the foot of the mountain. They must not cross it to come and look at me, because if they do, many of them will die.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Sian famu behyia ɔman no, amma wɔaanntsetsew mu ammba EWURADZE nkyɛn ammbɔhwɛ, ma hom mu pii aanntotɔ.

english_verse: Only the priests may come near me, and they must obey strict rules before I let them. If they don't, they will be punished.”
translation_verse: Na ma asɔfo no so a wɔba EWURADZE nkyɛn no ntsew hɔnho, amma EWURADZE eenntu annhyɛ hɔn do.

english_verse: Moses replied, “The people cannot come up the mountain. You warned us to stay away because it is holy.”
translation_verse: Na Moses see EWURADZE dɛ, Ɔman no runntum mmfow mmba bepɔw Sinai do: osiandɛ woara hyɛɛ hɛn dɛ, Bɔ bepɔw no ho ɔheban hyia, na tsew ho.

english_verse: Then the Lord told Moses, “Go down and bring Aaron back here with you. But the priests and people must not try to push their way through, or I will rush at them like a flood!”
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see no dɛ, Kɔ, sian famu; na fow bra, ɔwo, nye Aaron ka wo ho: na mma asɔfo no nye ɔman no nntsetsew mu mmfow mmba EWURADZE nkyɛn, amma oeenntu annhyɛ hɔn do.

english_verse: After Moses had gone back down, he told the people what the Lord had said.
translation_verse: Nna Moses sianee kɔr ɔman no nkyɛn, na ɔkɛkaa kyerɛɛ hɔn.

english_verse: God said to the people of Israel:
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn kaa nsɛm yi nyinara, see dɛ,

english_verse: But when Moses went near the thick cloud where God was, the people stayed a long way off. Idols and Altars
translation_verse: Na ɔman no gyinaa ekyirekyir, na Moses twee bɛɛn sum kadoo no mbrɛ Nyankopɔn wɔ no. Afɔrmbukyia Ho Mbra

english_verse: I am the Lord your God, the one who brought you out of Egypt where you were slaves.
translation_verse: Emi nye EWURADZE wo Nyankopɔn a miyii wo wɔ Egypt asaase do, wɔ nkowaasom fi mu no.

english_verse: Do not worship any god except me.
translation_verse: Mma nnya enyame fofor nnka mo ho.

english_verse: Do not make idols that look like anything in the sky or on earth or in the ocean under the earth.
translation_verse: Mma nnyɛ ohonyi bi, anaa adze a ɔwɔ wimu wɔ sor, anaa dza ɔwɔ asaase do wɔ famu, anaa dza ɔwɔ nsu mu wɔ asaase ase, ne saso mma woho:

english_verse: But if you love me and obey my laws, I will be kind to your families for thousands of generations.
translation_verse: na muhu mpempem a wɔdɔ me na wodzi me mbransɛm do mbɔbɔr.

english_verse: Do not misuse my name. I am the Lord your God, and I will punish anyone who misuses my name.
translation_verse: Mma mmbɔ EWURADZE wo Nyankopɔn dzin gyangyan, na EWURADZE nnkɛma nyia no a ɔbɔ ne dzin gyangyan afa noho tɔtrɔtɔ.

english_verse: Remember that the Sabbath Day belongs to me.
translation_verse: Kaa ahomgyeda no na sie no krɔnkrɔn.

english_verse: You have six days when you can do your work,
translation_verse: Nda esia na yɛ edwuma, na dzi wo dwuma nyinara:

english_verse: but the seventh day of each week belongs to me, your God. No one is to work on that day—not you, your children, your slaves, your animals, or the foreigners who live in your towns.
translation_verse: na da a otsia esuon no yɛ EWURADZE wo Nyankopɔn ahomgyeda: mma nnyɛ edwuma biara wɔ mu, ɔwo o, wo babanyin o, wo babaa o, w’akowaa o, w’afonabaa o, wo mbowa o, wo hɔho a ɔwɔ wo kuropon mu o:

english_verse: In six days I made the sky, the earth, the oceans, and everything in them, but on the seventh day I rested. That's why I made the Sabbath a special day that belongs to me.
translation_verse: na nda esia na EWURADZE bɔɔ sor nye asaase, po, nye dza ɔwɔ mu nyinara, na ɔhoom da a otsia esuon no: dɛm ntsi na EWURADZE hyiraa ahomgyeda no, na ɔyɛɛ no krɔnkrɔn.

english_verse: Respect your father and your mother, and you will live a long time in the land I am giving you.
translation_verse: Dzi w’egya nye wo na nyi: ama wo nda aawar wɔ asaase a EWURADZE wo Nyankopɔn dze rema wo no do.

english_verse: Do not murder.
translation_verse: Mma nndzi ewu.

english_verse: Be faithful in marriage.
translation_verse: Mma nnto awar.

english_verse: Do not steal.
translation_verse: Mma nnwia adze.

english_verse: Do not tell lies about others.
translation_verse: Mma nngye wo nyɛnko ho dasewia.

english_verse: Do not desire to possess anything that belongs to another person—not a house, a wife, a husband, a slave, an ox, a donkey, or anything else. The People Are Afraid (Deuteronomy
translation_verse: Mma w’enyi mmber wo nyɛnko fi, mma w’enyi mmber ne yer, anaa n’akowaa, anaa n’afonabaa, anaa ne nantwi, anaa n’asoaso, anaa biribiara a ɔyɛ wo nyɛnko dze. Suro Ka Ɔman No (Deuteronomi

english_verse: So you must never make idols of silver or gold to worship in place of me.
translation_verse: Mma hom nnyɛ me saso enyame bi mma hom ho; dwetɛ enyame, anaa sika enyame o, mma hom nnyɛ.

english_verse: The people trembled with fear when they heard the thunder and the trumpet and saw the lightning and the smoke coming from the mountain. They stood a long way off
translation_verse: Na aber a ɔman no nyinara hun asenaman no, nye enyinam no, nye bepɔw a orupun wusiw no, na wɔtsee abɛn ndze no, hɔn ho popoe, na wokegyinaa ekyirekyir.

english_verse: and said to Moses, “If you speak to us, we will listen. But don't let God speak to us, or we will die!”
translation_verse: Na wɔsee Moses dɛ, Woara nye hɛn nkasa, na yebetsie: na mma Nyankopɔn nnye hɛn nnkasa, ɔno yebowuwu.

english_verse: “Don't be afraid!” Moses replied. “God has come only to test you, so that by obeying him you won't sin.”
translation_verse: Na Moses see ɔman no dɛ, Mma hom nnsuro: na Nyankopɔn aba dɛ ɔrobɔsɔ hom ahwɛ, ama no ho suro aaba hom enyi do, ma hom aannyɛ bɔn.

english_verse: The Lord told Moses to say to the people of Israel: With your own eyes you saw me speak to you from heaven.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Mbrɛ se Israelmba nye yi, Homara hom ehu dɛ mifi ɔsor na menye hom akasa.

english_verse: Build an altar out of earth, and offer on it your sacrifices of sheep, goats, and cattle. Wherever I choose to be worshiped, I will come down to bless you.
translation_verse: Dɛtse afɔrmbukyia na yɛ ma me, na dze wo hyew afɔr, nye w’asomdwee afɔr, wo nguan, nye w’anantwi no bɔ do afɔr: beebiara a mebɛma wɔakaa me dzin no, mebɛba wo nkyɛn na mebehyira wo.

english_verse: If you ever build an altar for me out of stones, do not use any tools to chisel the stones, because that would make the altar unfit for use in worship.
translation_verse: Na sɛ ereyɛ abo afɔrmbukyia ama me: mma mmfa abo a woetwa nnsia osiandɛ sɛ ema w’edwindadze do bɔ do a, egu ho fi.

english_verse: And don't build an altar that requires steps; you might expose yourself when you climb up.
translation_verse: Nkyii so mma mmfow atwer nnkɔ afɔrmbukyia do, amma w’adagyaw aannda edzi wɔ do.

english_verse: Death is the punishment for attacking your father or mother.
translation_verse: Na nyia ɔbɔ n’egya, anaa ne na no, oku na wonku no.

english_verse: Death is the punishment for murder.
translation_verse: Nyia ɔbɔ nyimpa ma owu no, oku na wonku no.

english_verse: Suppose two of you are arguing, and you hit the other with either a rock or your fist, without causing a fatal injury. If the victim has to stay in bed,
translation_verse: Na sɛ nyimpa bi woridzi aper, na kor bɔ kor no ɔbo, anaa kutuku, na oennwu, na ɔka no to ne mpa do a:

english_verse: The Lord gave Moses the following laws for his people:
translation_verse: Na iyinom nye ntsɛmbusɛm a fa to hɔn enyim.

english_verse: If you buy a Hebrew slave, he must remain your slave for six years. But in the seventh year you must set him free, without cost to him.
translation_verse: Sɛ etɔ akowaa a ɔyɛ Hebriwnyi a, mfe esia na ɔnsom: na dza otsia esuon no mu ɔmfa no ho ndzi nkɔ kwa.

english_verse: If he was single at the time you bought him, he alone must be set free. But if he was married at the time, both he and his wife must be given their freedom.
translation_verse: Sɛ noara nko na ɔbae a, noara nko nkɔ: sɛ ɔawar a, ɔno ne yer nye no nkɔ.

english_verse: If you give him a wife, and they have children, only the man himself must be set free; his wife and children remain the property of his owner.
translation_verse: Sɛ no wura ma no yer, na ɔwo mbabanyin anaa mbabaa ma no a, yer no nye ne mba nyɛ ɔbaa no no wura dze, na noara nko nkɔ.

english_verse: But suppose the slave loves his wife and children and his owner so much that he won't leave them.
translation_verse: Na sɛ akowaa no ka no petee dɛ, Modɔ mo wura, me yer, nye me mba; mennkɛfa moho edzi akɔ a:

english_verse: Then he must stand beside either the door or the doorpost at the place of worship, while his owner punches a small hole through one of his ears with a sharp metal rod. This makes him a slave for life.
translation_verse: ɔno no wura no mfa no mbra Nyankopɔn enyim, na ɔmfa no mbra ɔpon ho, anaa ɔpon dua ho; na no wura no mfa fitsii mfitsi n’asomu; na ɔnsom no daa.

english_verse: A young woman who was sold by her father doesn't gain her freedom in the same way that a man does.
translation_verse: Na sɛ ɔbanyin bi tɔn ne babaa ma ɔkɛyɛ afonabaa a, ɔnodze mma ɔmmfa noho nndzi nnkɔ dɛ mbrɛ akowaa no yɛ.

english_verse: If she doesn't please the man who bought her to be his wife, he must let her be bought back. He cannot sell her to foreigners; this would break the contract he made with her.
translation_verse: Sɛ ɔnnsɔ no wura a noara ɔagye no ayewar no enyi a, ɔnodze wɔmma wɔmbɔpon no: onnyi tum dɛ ɔtɔn no ma ɔnanaben bi, osiandɛ ɔadaadaa no.

english_verse: If he selects her as a wife for his son, he must treat her as his own daughter.
translation_verse: Na sɛ ɔgye no ayewar ma ne babanyin a, ɔnyɛ no dɛ mbrɛ wɔdze yɛ mbabaa.

english_verse: If the man later marries another woman, he must continue to provide food and clothing for the one he bought and to treat her as a wife.
translation_verse: Sɛ ɔgye yer fofor ma no a; mma onnkuw n’edziban, n’akataho, nye n’awar edwuma do.

english_verse: If he fails to do any of these things, she must be given her freedom without paying for it. Murder and Other Violent Crimes The Lord said:
translation_verse: Na sɛ ɔannyɛ iyinom ebiasa yi amma no a, ɔnodze ɔmfa noho ndzi nkɔ kwa, sika bi nnyi mu. Basabasayɛ a Ɔdze Pira Anaa Owu Ba

english_verse: But if you did not intend to kill someone, and I, the Lord, let it happen anyway, you may run for safety to a place that I have set aside.
translation_verse: Na sɛ nyimpa annkɔ atsɛwee, na Nyankopɔn na ɔama ne nsa apa dze a; ɔno mibeyi beebi mama wo na oeeguan akɔ hɔ.

english_verse: If you plan in advance to murder someone, there's no escape, not even by holding on to my altar. You will be dragged off and killed.
translation_verse: Na sɛ nyimpa bi ba ne nyɛnko do enyidaho mu dze ndaadaa ku no a; yi no fi m’afɔrmbukyia ho, ma onwu.

english_verse: Death is the punishment for kidnapping. If you sell the person you kidnapped, or if you are caught with that person, the penalty is death.
translation_verse: Na nyia owia nyimpa, na ɔtɔn no no, anaa sɛ wohu no wɔ ne nsamu a, oku na wonku no.

english_verse: Death is the punishment for cursing your father or mother.
translation_verse: Na nyia ɔdom n’egya, anaa ne na no, oku na wonku no.

english_verse: and later has to use a stick when walking outside, you must pay for the loss of time and do what you can to help until the injury is completely healed. That's your only responsibility.
translation_verse: sɛ ɔsoɛr bio, na osuo no poma mu nantsew fir edzi a, ɔnodze nyia ɔbɔɔ no no no ho nyɛ no tɔtrɔtɔ: ne mber a asɛɛ no nko na ontua kaw, na ɔmma wɔnsa no yarba koraa.

english_verse: Death is the punishment for beating to death any of your slaves.
translation_verse: Na sɛ nyimpa bobɔ n’akowaa, anaa n’afonabaa abaa, ma owu ne nsaano a; ɔno wondzi no wer.

english_verse: However, if the slave lives a few days after the beating, you are not to be punished. After all, you have already lost the services of that slave who was your property.
translation_verse: Naaso sɛ ɔtsena hɔ da kor anaa nda ebien a, mma wonndzi no wer: osiandɛ ɔyɛ ne sika.

english_verse: Suppose a pregnant woman suffers a miscarriage as the result of an injury caused by someone who is fighting. If she isn't badly hurt, the one who injured her must pay whatever fine her husband demands and the judges approve.
translation_verse: Na sɛ mbanyin roko, na wopira onyinsemfo, ma ɔpon ɔba, na mbusu bi ammba ekyir dze a: brabo na wɔngye no, dɛ mbrɛ ɔbaa no no kun dze bɔto no do no; na ontua no dɛ mbrɛ ntsɛmbuafo no bɛkyerɛ no.

english_verse: But if she is seriously injured, the payment will be life for life,
translation_verse: Na sɛ mbusu bi ba ekyir a, ɔnodze fa nkwa hyɛ nkwa ananmu,

english_verse: eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,
translation_verse: enyiwa hyɛ enyiwa ananmu, se hyɛ se ananmu, nsa hyɛ nsa ananmu, anan hyɛ anan ananmu,

english_verse: burn for burn, cut for cut, and bruise for bruise.
translation_verse: hyehyew hyɛ hyehyew ananmu, ɔkam hyɛ ɔkam ananmu, mfemfem hyɛ mfemfem ananmu.

english_verse: If you hit one of your slaves and cause the loss of an eye, the slave must be set free.
translation_verse: Na sɛ obi bɔ n’akowaa enyiwa, anaa n’afonabaa enyiwa na ɔsɛɛ no a, n’enyiwa no ntsi ɔmma ɔmfa noho ndzi nkɔ.

english_verse: The same law applies if you knock out a slave's tooth—the slave goes free.
translation_verse: Na sɛ ɔbɔ n’akowaa ne se tu no, anaa n’afonabaa ne se a; ne se no ntsi ɔmma ɔmfa noho ndzi nkɔ. Ewuranom Hɔn Asodzii

english_verse: A bull that kills someone with its horns must be killed and its meat destroyed, but the owner of the bull isn't responsible for the death.
