translation_verse: Na sɛ ɔnantwi sɛ ɔbanyin anaa ɔbaa, ma owu a, ɔno ɔnantwi no wonsiw no abo, na mma wonndzi ne nam; na ɔnantwi no wura dze wongyaa no.

english_verse: Suppose you own a bull that has been in the habit of attacking people, but you have refused to keep it fenced in. If that bull kills someone, both you and the bull must be put to death by stoning.
translation_verse: Na sɛ ɔnantwi no ɔtɔ sesɛ dada na wɔama no wura atse, na oennsie no fie, na oekoku ɔbanyin anaa ɔbaa dze a; ɔnantwi no wonsiw no abo, na no wura no so wonku no.

english_verse: pieces of silver for the loss of the slave, and the bull must be killed by stoning.
translation_verse: Sɛ wɔbɛgye no mpata a, ɔno ɔmma adze biara a wɔbɛgye no no mfa ntɔ no nkwa.

english_verse: This same law applies if the bull gores someone's son or daughter.
translation_verse: Sɛ ɔbabanyin na ɔasɛ no o, ɔbabaa na ɔasɛ no o, atsɛmbu yi ara na wɔmfa mbu no.

english_verse: If the bull kills a slave, you must pay the slave owner
translation_verse: Sɛ ɔnantwi no sɛ akowaa anaa afonabaa a; ɔno ɔmma hɔn wura dwetɛ dwetɛbona eduasa, na wonsiw ɔnantwi no abo.

english_verse: Suppose someone's ox or donkey is killed by falling into an open pit that you dug or left uncovered on your property.
translation_verse: Na sɛ nyimpa bi kobue amona do, anaa sɛ nyimpa bi kotu amona na ɔannkata do, na ɔnantwi anaa asoaso kɔhwe mu a,

english_verse: You must pay for the dead animal, and it becomes yours.
translation_verse: ma no wura no nhyɛ ananmu; ɔmfa sika mma hɔn wura no, na mbowafun no nyɛ nedze.

english_verse: If your bull kills someone else's, yours must be sold. Then the money from your bull and the meat from the dead bull must be divided equally between you and the other owner.
translation_verse: Na sɛ nyimpa kor bi ne nantwi pira kor nedze ma owu a; ɔno wɔntɔn nantwi nyikan no, na wɔnkyɛ ne tsiadze no; na fun no so wɔnkyɛ.

english_verse: If you refuse to fence in a bull that is known to attack others, you must replace any animal it kills, but the dead animal will belong to you.
translation_verse: Mbom sɛ wonyim dɛ ɔnantwi no ɔtɔ sesɛ dada, na no wura no ammfa no ennsie fie a; ɔno ɔmfa ɔnantwi nhyɛ ɔnantwi ananmu, na abowafun no nyɛ noara nedze.

english_verse: If you steal an ox and slaughter or sell it, you must replace it with five oxen; if you steal a sheep and slaughter it or sell it, you must replace it with four sheep.
translation_verse: Sɛ nyimpa bi kowia nantwi, anaa guan, na oku no, anaa ɔtɔn no a; ɔmfa anantwi enum nhyɛ nantwi kor ananmu, na nguan anan nhyɛ guan kor ananmu ntua kaw.

english_verse: If you allow any of your animals to stray from your property and graze in someone else's field or vineyard, you must repay the damage from the best part of your own harvest of grapes and grain.
translation_verse: Sɛ nyimpa bi ma wodzi haban anaa wenyin ture mu adze, na ogyaa n’abowa ma okedzi nyimpa kor so ne haban mu adze a, ɔmfa nankasa ne haban mu ndzepa, nye nankasa no wenyin ture mu ndzepa bi nhyɛ ananmu.

english_verse: If you carelessly let a fire spread from your property to someone else's, you must pay the owner for any crops or fields destroyed by the fire.
translation_verse: Sɛ ogya pue, na ɔkɔsɔ nsoɛ mu ma eburow ekuwekuw, anaa eburow a ogyinagyina hɔ, anaa haban no hyew a; ɔhyɛ na nyia ɔsɔɔ gya no nhyɛ ananmu.

english_verse: Suppose a neighbor asks you to keep some silver or other valuables, and they are stolen from your house. If the thief is caught, the thief must repay double.
translation_verse: Sɛ nyimpa bi dze sika anaa efidze ma ne nyɛnko esie, na wowia no wɔ nyimpa no ne fie a; sɛ wohu owifo no a, ontua kaw mbɔho.

english_verse: But if the thief isn't caught, some judges will decide if you are the guilty one.
translation_verse: Sɛ woennhu owifo no a, ɔno fiwura no ntwe mbɛn Nyankopɔn nhwɛ kyerɛ dɛ ɔnntsen ne nsa nnka ne nyɛnko n’adze no a.

english_verse: Suppose two people claim to own the same ox or donkey or sheep or piece of clothing. Then the judges must decide the case, and the guilty person will pay the owner double.
translation_verse: Na mbrato ho asɛm biara, sɛ ɔyɛ ɔnantwi dze o, asoaso dze o, oguan dze o, anaa adze a ayew biara a obi bɛse wɔ ho dɛ, Ɔno nye yi o, wɔmfa hɔn beenu nyina hɔn asɛm mbra Nyankopɔn enyim; nyia Nyankopɔn bobu no fɔ no, ontua kaw mbɔho mma ne nyɛnko no.

english_verse: Suppose a neighbor who is going to be away asks you to keep a donkey or an ox or a sheep or some other animal, and it dies or gets injured or is stolen while no one is looking.
translation_verse: Sɛ nyimpa bi dze asoaso, anaa nantwi, anaa guan, anaa abowa biara ma ne nyɛnko esie; na owu anaa opira, anaa wɔka no to hamu, na nyimpa bi ennhu a:

english_verse: If you swear with me as your witness that you did not harm the animal, you do not have to replace it. Your word is enough.
translation_verse: EWURADZE ntam mbra hɔn beenu no ntamu, kyerɛ dɛ ɔnntsen ne nsa nnka ne nyɛnko no n’adze no a: na adze no wura no ɔmpen do, na mma ɔnnhyɛ ananmu.

english_verse: But if the animal was stolen while in your care, you must replace it.
translation_verse: Sɛ wowia no wɔ ne nkyɛn dze a, ɔnhyɛ adze no wura ananmu.

english_verse: If the animal was attacked and killed by a wild animal, and you can show the remains of the dead animal to its owner, you do not have to replace it.
translation_verse: Sɛ wɔatsetsew no esinesin a, ɔmfa mbra mbedzi dase; dza wɔatsetsew no no mma ɔnnhyɛ ananmu.

english_verse: Suppose you borrow an animal from a neighbor, and it gets injured or dies while the neighbor isn't around. Then you must replace it.
translation_verse: Na sɛ nyimpa bi kɛfɛm biribi wɔ ne nyɛnko hɔ, na ɔsɛɛ, anaa owu, dɛ adze no wura no no ho nnyi mu a, ɔhyɛ na ɔnhyɛ ananmu.

english_verse: But if something happens to the animal while the owner is present, you do not have to replace it. If you had leased the animal, the money you paid the owner will cover any harm done to it. Laws for Everyday Life The Lord said:
translation_verse: Sɛ adze no wura no no ho wɔ mu a, mma ɔnnhyɛ ananmu: sɛ ɔyɛ apaabɔ adze a, na ogyina n’apaabɔdze do. Abrabɔ Na Nyamesom Ho Mbra

english_verse: Suppose a young woman has never had sex and isn't engaged. If a man talks her into having sex, he must pay the bride price and marry her.
translation_verse: Na sɛ nyimpa bi kɛdaadaa ɔbaabun a wɔnngyee no yer, na ɔnye no da a, ɔno ɔmma ne tsiadze ma ɔnyɛ ne yer.

english_verse: But if her father refuses to let her marry the man, the bride price must still be paid.
translation_verse: Sɛ n’egya pow koraa dɛ ɔdze no ma no a, onker sika mma, dɛ mbrɛ mbaabun tsiadze tse.

english_verse: Death is the punishment for witchcraft.
translation_verse: Mma mma ɔbaa anyɛn kwan ma ɔntsena ase.

english_verse: Death is the punishment for having sex with an animal.
translation_verse: Obiara a ɔnye abowa bɛda no, oku na wonku no.

english_verse: Death is the punishment for offering sacrifices to any god except me.
translation_verse: Nyia ɔbɔbɔ afɔr ama onyame biara, na nnyɛ EWURADZE nko no, wɔnsɛɛ no koraa.

english_verse: Do not mistreat or abuse foreigners who live among you. Remember, you were foreigners in Egypt.
translation_verse: Na ɔhɔho dze mma nnyɛ no bɔn, nkyii so mma nnhaw no: osiandɛ hom yɛɛ ahɔho wɔ Egypt asaase do.

english_verse: Do not mistreat widows or orphans.
translation_verse: Mma hom nntseetsee ɔbaa kunafo biara, anaa egyankaba.

english_verse: If you do, they will beg for my help, and I will come to their rescue.
translation_verse: Sɛ etseetsee hɔn ɔkwan biara mu, na wosu frɛ me dze a, otsie ara na mibetsie hɔn sufrɛ no;

english_verse: In fact, I will get so angry that I will kill your men and make widows of their wives and orphans of their children.
translation_verse: na m’ebufuw ano bɛyɛ dzen, na medze afona boku hom; na hom yernom bɛyɛ ekunafo na hom mba ayɛ ngyanka.

english_verse: Don't charge interest when you lend money to any of my people who are in need.
translation_verse: Sɛ efɛm me man mu ohianyi biara a ɔnye wo wɔ hɔ sika a, mma nnyɛ dɛ ɔpempensinyi; nkyii so mma hom nngye no nsiho.

english_verse: Before sunset you must return any coat taken as security for a loan,
translation_verse: Sɛ egye wo nyɛnko tam ahoba a, wi rotɔ no fa san ma no:

english_verse: because that is the only cover the poor have when they sleep at night. I am a merciful God, and when they call out to me, I will come to help them.
translation_verse: osiandɛ ɔnoara nko nye n’akataho, no honam ho tam a: ebɛnadze na ɔmfa nda? Na ɔbɛba dɛ, sɛ osu frɛ me a, mibetsie: osiandɛ mbɔbɔrhufo nye me.

english_verse: Don't speak evil of me or of the ruler of your people.
translation_verse: Mma nnyeyaw Nyankopɔn, anaa nndom wo man mu ɔpanyin.

english_verse: Don't fail to give me the offerings of grain and wine that belong to me. Dedicate to me your first-born sons
translation_verse: Mma nnkyinkyim dɛ edze wo ndɔbaa anaa wo nsa mu bi bɔbɔ afɔr. Wo mbabanyin mu abakan no fa ma me.

english_verse: and the first-born of your cattle and sheep. Let the animals stay with their mothers for seven days, then on the eighth day give them to me, your God.
translation_verse: Dɛmara na yɛ w’anantwi, na ɔnye wo nguan: nda esuon na ɔnye ne na ntsena; da a otsia awɔtwe no fa no brɛ me.

english_verse: You are my chosen people, so don't eat the meat of any of your livestock that was killed by a wild animal. Instead, feed the meat to dogs.
translation_verse: Na hom bɛyɛ nyimpa krɔnkrɔn ama me: dɛm ntsi mma hom nndzi bɔnam bi a abowa atsetsew mu wɔ haban mu; hom ntow nkyen mbɔdɔm.

english_verse: Always tell the truth in court, even if everyone else is dishonest and stands in the way of justice.
translation_verse: Mma nndzi ɔdodow ekyir nnkɛyɛ bɔn; nkyii so mma nnkasa asɛm bi mu nnkedzi ɔdodow ekyir nndan atsɛmbu enyi:

english_verse: And don't favor the poor, simply because they are poor.
translation_verse: nkyii so mma nndzi nnkɔ ohianyi afa wɔ n’asɛm mu.

english_verse: If you find an ox or a donkey that has wandered off, take it back where it belongs, even if the owner is your enemy.
translation_verse: Sɛ ekɔto wo tamfo ne nantwi anaa n’asoaso dɛ ɔatɔ gon a, fa no san ekyir brɛ no bio.

english_verse: If a donkey is overloaded and falls down, you must do what you can to help, even if it belongs to someone who doesn't like you.
translation_verse: Sɛ ihu nyia okyir wo n’asoaso dɛ ɔhyɛ n’adzesoa ase, na nkyɛ ennkɛpɛ dɛ eboa no a, boa na nye no mboa no.

english_verse: Make sure that the poor are given equal justice in court.
translation_verse: Mma nndan wo hianyi n’atsɛmbu enyi wɔ n’asɛm mu.

english_verse: Don't bring false charges against anyone or sentence an innocent person to death. I won't forgive you if you do.
translation_verse: Twe woho fi atorsɛm ho kɔ ekyirekyir; na mma nnku nyia odzi bem na ɔtsen: osiandɛ murummbu omumuyɛfo bem.

english_verse: Don't accept bribes. Judges are blinded and justice is twisted by bribes.
translation_verse: Na mma nngye akyɛdze: osiandɛ akyɛdze ma hɔn a wɔwɔ enyiwa fura, na ɔkyeakyea atseneneefo nsɛm.

english_verse: Don't mistreat foreigners. You were foreigners in Egypt, and you know what it is like. Laws for the Sabbath The Lord said:
translation_verse: Na mma nnhaw ɔhɔho: osiandɛ hom nyim ɔhɔho n’akoma, ɔnam dɛ hom yɛɛ ahɔho wɔ Egypt asaase do ntsi. Afe Esuon No Na Nda Esuon No

english_verse: Plant and harvest your crops for six years,
translation_verse: Na mfe esia na pe w’asaase do aba, na boaboa no ndɔbaa ano sie:

english_verse: but let the land rest during the seventh year. The poor are to eat what they want from your fields, vineyards, and olive trees during that year, and when they have all they want from your fields, leave the rest for wild animals.
translation_verse: na afe a otsia esuon no dze ma ɔngye n’ahom na ɔnda hɔ kwa; ma wo man mu ehiafo ndzi: na dza wobegya no, hamu abowa ebedzi. Dɛmara na yɛ wo wenyin ture, nye wo ngodua ture.

english_verse: Work the first six days of the week, but rest and relax on the seventh day. This law is not only for you, but for your oxen, donkeys, and slaves, as well as for any foreigners among you.
translation_verse: Nda esia na fa yɛ w’edwuma, na da a otsia esuon no fa hom: na wo nantwi nye w’asoasopɔnkɔ eenya ahomgye: na w’afonabaa ba, nye ɔhɔho no so eenya ahomgye.

english_verse: Make certain that you obey everything I have said. Don't pray to other gods or even mention their names. Three Annual Festivals (Exodus
translation_verse: Na hom nhwɛ yie wɔ ndzɛmba a maka akyerɛ hom nyinara ho: na mma hom mmbobɔ enyame afofor bi dzin, nkyii so mma mma wɔnntse no wɔ w’anomu. Afahyɛ Akɛse Ebiasa No (Eksodɔs

english_verse: Do not offer bread made with yeast when you sacrifice an animal to me. And make sure that the fat of the animal is burned that same day.
translation_verse: Mma mmfa mbɔrkaw nnka m’afɔrbɔ bɔgya no ho mmbɔ afɔr; nkyii so mma m’afahyɛ mu seradze no ɔnnko hɔ anadwe nyinara ma onnkodu adzekyee.

english_verse: Your women will give birth to healthy children, and everyone will live a long life.
translation_verse: Obiara nnkɔpon ɔba, na ɔnnkɛyɛ ɔbɔnyin so, wɔ w’asaase do: mebɛma wo nda no dodow ano edu.

english_verse: Celebrate the Harvest Festival each spring when you start harvesting your wheat, and celebrate the Festival of Shelters each autumn when you pick your fruit.
translation_verse: na otwaber afahyɛ no, w’edwumadze a eyɛ no wɔ haban mu no mu nduadzewakan no: na anoboaboa afahyɛ no wɔ afe no ewiei, aber a eboaboa wo ndwumadze ano fi haban mu no.

english_verse: Your men must come to these three festivals each year to worship me.
translation_verse: Wo mbanyin nyinara mbefir EWURADZE Nyankopɔn enyim mpɛn ebiasa wɔ afrenhyia mu.

english_verse: Celebrate three festivals each year in my honor.
translation_verse: Mpɛn ebiasa na hyɛ fa ma me wɔ afrenhyia mu.

english_verse: Celebrate the Festival of Thin Bread by eating bread made without yeast, just as I have commanded. Do this at the proper time during the month of Abib, because it is the month when you left Egypt. And make certain that everyone brings the proper offerings.
translation_verse: Sie epitsi afahyɛ no: nda esuon na dzi epitsi boodoo, dɛ mbrɛ mehyɛɛ wo no, wɔ mber a wɔahyɛ no wɔ bosoom Abib mu no ; na mma obiara mmfa nsapan mmbefir m’enyim:

english_verse: Each year bring the best part of your first harvest to the place of worship. Don't boil a young goat in its mother's milk. A Promise and a Warning The Lord said:
translation_verse: Wo daadze mu nduadzewakan mu kan dze no fa bra EWURADZE wo Nyankopɔn fie. Mma nnoa apɔnkyeba wɔ ne na no numfonsu mu. EWURADZE N’anohoba Na Mboa

english_verse: I am sending an angel to protect you and to lead you into the land I have ready for you.
translation_verse: Hwɛ, morosoma ɔbɔfo ma oeedzi w’enyim akɔhwɛ wo wɔ ɔkwan no mu, na ɔdze wo ekodu bea a mesiesie no do.

english_verse: Carefully obey everything the angel says, because I am giving him complete authority, and he won't tolerate rebellion.
translation_verse: Hom nhwɛ yie wɔ no ho, na wontsie ne ndze; mma wonnyi no ebufuw: na ɔnnkɛfa hom mbrato akyɛ hom; osiandɛ me dzin wɔ no mu.

english_verse: If you faithfully obey him, I will be a fierce enemy of your enemies.
translation_verse: Na sɛ itsie ne ndze ara ampa, na eyɛ dza meka nyinara a; ɔnodze, mebɛyɛ ɔtamfo ama w’atamfo, nye ɔhohiahianyi mama w’ahohiahiafo.

english_verse: My angel will lead you into the land of the Amorites, Hittites, Perizzites, Canaanites, Hivites, and Jebusites, and I will wipe them out.
translation_verse: Na mo bɔfoe no bedzi w’enyim akɔ, na ɔdze wo ekodu Amorifo, Hitifo, Perizfo, Keenanfo, Hivifo, nye Jebusfo nkyɛn: na mubotwa hɔn egu.

english_verse: Don't worship their gods or follow their customs. Instead, destroy their idols and shatter their stone images.
translation_verse: Mma mmbɔ wo mu adze mma hɔn enyame, na nnsom hɔn, na nnyɛ hɔn ndzeyɛɛ so: na butuw na butuw hɔn, na bubu hɔn edum mu esinesin.

english_verse: Worship only me, the Lord your God! I will bless you with plenty of food and water and keep you strong.
translation_verse: Na hom nsom EWURADZE hom Nyankopɔn, na obehyira w’edziban, nye wo nsu do; na mibeyi yarba mefi wo mu.

english_verse: I will terrify those nations and make your enemies so confused that they will run from you.
translation_verse: Mebɛma mo ho hu edzi w’enyim, na mebɛka aman a ebɛba hɔn do nyinara megu, na mama w’atamfo nyinara adan hɔn ekyir ama wo.

english_verse: I will make the Hivites, Canaanites, and Hittites panic as you approach.
translation_verse: Na mobɔsoma nkotokrodo ma woeedzi w’enyim, apam Hivifo, Keenanfo, nye Hitifo no wɔ w’enyim.

english_verse: But I won't do all this in the first year, because the land would become poor, and wild animals would be everywhere.
translation_verse: Mennkɛpam hɔn wɔ w’enyim wɔ afe kor mu; ɔno bɛyɛ na asaase no adan amamfo, na hamu mbowa adɔɔ so wɔ wo do.

english_verse: Instead, I will force out your enemies little by little and give your nation time to grow strong enough to take over the land.
translation_verse: Nkakrankakra na mebɛpam hɔn wɔ w’enyim, ekodu dɛ ebɔdɔr, na enya asaase no.

english_verse: I will see that your borders reach from the Red Sea to the Euphrates River and from the Mediterranean Sea to the desert. I will let you defeat the people who live there, and you will force them out of the land.
translation_verse: Na mubotwa wo he efi Mbɛw Po no ho araa ekodu Filistiafo po no ho, na medze mefi sar no ano ekodu Esutsen no ho: na mibeyi asaase no do mba mahyɛ wo nsa; na ebɛpam hɔn wɔ w’enyim.

english_verse: But you must not make any agreements with them or with their gods.
translation_verse: Mma nnye hɔn nye hɔn enyame so nndzi ahyɛmu.

english_verse: Don't let them stay in your land. They will trap you into sinning against me and worshiping their gods.
translation_verse: Mma wɔnntsena w’asaase do, ɔno bɛyɛ na wɔama ayɛ bɔn etsia me: na sɛ esom hɔn enyame a, ampaara ɔbɛyɛ efir ama wo.

english_verse: The Lord said to Moses, “Come up to me on this mountain. Bring along Aaron, as well as his two sons Nadab and Abihu, and
translation_verse: Na ɔsee Moses dɛ, Fow bra EWURADZE nkyɛn, ɔwo, nye Aaron, Nadab, nye Abihu, nye Israel mpanyimfo mu eduosuon; na hom nkɔsɔr wɔ ekyirekyir:

english_verse: but you are to come near. Don't let anyone else come up.”
translation_verse: na Moses nko ntwe mbɛn EWURADZE; na hɔn dze, mma wɔnntwe mmbɛn; nkyii so mma ɔman no nnye no mmfow nnkɔ.

english_verse: Moses gave the Lord's instructions to the people, and they all promised, “We will do everything the Lord has commanded!”
translation_verse: Na Moses bae bɛkaa EWURADZE ne nsɛm no nyinara kyerɛɛ ɔman no, na atsɛmbu no nyinara: na ɔman no nyinara dze ndze kor buae, na wɔsee dɛ, Nsɛm a EWURADZE aka no nyinara yɛbɛyɛ.

english_verse: Then Moses wrote down what the Lord had said. The next morning Moses got up early. He built an altar at the foot of the mountain and set up a large stone for each of the twelve tribes of Israel.
translation_verse: Na Moses kyerɛɛw EWURADZE ne nsɛm no nyinara, na ɔsoɛɛr anapaatu, na osii afɔrmbukyia wɔ bepɔw no ase, nye edum duebien, dɛ mbrɛ Israel ebusuakuw duebien no ano tse.

english_verse: He also sent some young men to burn offerings and to sacrifice bulls as special offerings to the Lord.
translation_verse: Na ɔsomaa Israelmba no mu mberantsɛ a wɔdze anantwi kɔbɔɔ ɔhyew afɔr, nye asomdwee afɔr maa EWURADZE.

english_verse: Moses put half of the blood from the animals into bowls and sprinkled the rest on the altar.
translation_verse: Na Moses faa bɔgya no ne fa guguu mbɛɛsen mu, na ɔdze bɔgya fa no petsee afɔrmbukyia no do.

english_verse: Then he read aloud the Lord's commands and promises, and the people shouted, “We will obey the Lord and do everything he has commanded!”
translation_verse: Na ɔfaa ahyɛmu nwoma no, na ɔkenkaan no wɔ ɔman no asomu: na wɔsee dɛ, Dza EWURADZE aka no nyinara yɛbɛyɛ, na yɛayɛ setsie.

english_verse: Moses took the blood from the bowls and sprinkled it on the people. Next, he told them, “With this blood the Lord makes his agreement with you.”
translation_verse: Na Moses faa bɔgya no, na ɔdze petsee ɔman no do, na ɔsee dɛ, Hwɛ ahyɛmu a EWURADZE nye hom edzi wɔ nsɛm yi nyinara ho no no bɔgyaa nye yi.

english_verse: Moses and Aaron, together with Nadab and Abihu and the
translation_verse: Nna Moses, nye Aaron, Nadab, nye Abihu, nye Israel mpanyimfo mu eduosuon no fowee kɔree:

english_verse: and saw the God of Israel. Under his feet was something that looked like a pavement made out of sapphire, and it was as bright as the sky.
translation_verse: na wohun Israel Nyankopɔn no; n’anan ase tse dɛ wɔdze hoabo ataa hɔ, na n’enyi tsew dɛ ɔsor noara.

english_verse: Even though these leaders of Israel saw God, he did not punish them. So they ate and drank. Moses on Mount Sinai
translation_verse: Na ɔammfa ne nsa annto Israelmba mu ekunyin no do: na wɔhwɛɛ Nyankopɔn, na wodzidzii na wɔnomee. Moses Kɔ Bepɔw Sinai Do

english_verse: The Lord said to Moses, “Come up on the mountain and stay here for a while. I will give you the two flat stones on which I have written the laws that my people must obey.”
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Fow bra me nkyɛn wɔ bepɔw no do, na tsena hɔ: na medze abopon, nye mbrasɛm a makyerɛw no mebɛma wo, ma aakyerɛkyerɛ hɔn.

english_verse: Moses and Joshua his assistant got ready, then Moses started up the mountain to meet with God.
translation_verse: Na Moses soɛree, ɔnye no somfo Josua: na Moses fowee kɔr Nyankopɔn ne bepɔw no do.

english_verse: Moses had told the leaders, “Wait here until we come back. Aaron and Hur will be with you, and they can settle any arguments while we are away.”
translation_verse: Na ɔsee mpanyimfo no dɛ, Hom ntsintsim ha ntweɔn hɛn ma onkodu dɛ yɛbɛba hom nkyɛn bio: na hwɛ, Aaron nye Hur nye hom wɔ ha: obiara a ɔwɔ asɛm bi no, wɔmma ɔntwe mbɛn hɔn.

english_verse: When Moses went up on Mount Sinai, a cloud covered it,
translation_verse: Na Moses fowee kɔr bepɔw no do, na mununkum no bɛkataa bepɔw no do.

english_verse: and the bright glory of the Lord came down and stayed there. The cloud covered the mountain for six days, and on the seventh day the Lord told Moses to come into the cloud.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE n’enyimnyam bɛtsenaa bepɔw Sinai do, na mununkum no kataa do nda esia: na da a otsia esuon no, ofi mununkum no mu frɛɛ Moses.

english_verse: blue, purple, and red wool; fine linen; goat hair;
translation_verse: nye bibir, nye ɔberdum, nye serdaa, nye ndwera fɛɛfɛw, nye mpɔnkyenhwi;

english_verse: Tell everyone in Israel who wants to give gifts that they must bring them to you.
translation_verse: Ka kyerɛ Israelmba ma wɔmfa ayɛyɛdze mbrɛ me: nyimpa biara a n’akoma pɛ no no hɔ na hom ngye m’ayɛyɛdze.

english_verse: Here is a list of what you are to collect: Gold, silver, and bronze;
translation_verse: Na iyi nye ayɛyɛdze a hom ngye no wɔ hɔn hɔ; sika, nye dwetɛ, nye boran;

english_verse: tanned ram skins; fine leather; acacia wood;
translation_verse: nye nguannyin nwoma a wɔahyɛ no memen, nye nsukraman nwoma; nye akeesia dua;

english_verse: olive oil for the lamp; sweet-smelling spices to mix with the incense and with the oil for dedicating the tent and ordaining the priests;
translation_verse: kandzea ho ngo, ɔserango ho nduhuam, nye nduhuam a wɔhyew ho edur;

english_verse: and onyx stones and other gems for the sacred vest and the breastpiece.
translation_verse: abo a no su tse dɛ epopobibir, nye abo a wɔdze besisi egugudo no, nye akatabo no mu no.

english_verse: I also want them to build a special place where I can live among my people.
translation_verse: Na ma wɔnyɛ krɔnkrɔnbea mma me; ma maatsena hɔn mu.

english_verse: The lamp on the top and those at the end of each of its six branches must be made so as to shine toward the front of the lampstand.
translation_verse: Na yɛ ne nkandzea no esuon: na wɔnsosɔ ne nkandzea no, ma ɔngyegye wɔ n’enyim.

english_verse: Cover it inside and out with pure gold and put a gold edging around the lid.
translation_verse: Na fa sika mapa dura ho, no mu nye n’ekyir na fa dura, na yɛ ho sika hankra twa ho hyia.

english_verse: Make four gold rings and attach one of them to each of the four legs of the chest.
translation_verse: Na gu no ho sika nkaba anan, na fa tuatua n’anan anan no ho; na nkaba ebien ndzi n’afa fakor, na nkaba ebien ndzi n’afa no ho.

english_verse: Make two poles of acacia wood. Cover them with gold
translation_verse: Na fa dua sian mpoma, na fa sika dura ho.

english_verse: and put them through the rings, so the chest can be carried by the poles.
translation_verse: Na fa mpoma no hyehyɛ nkaba no mu wɔ adaka no ne nkyɛn mu, ma wɔmfa nsoa adaka no.

english_verse: Don't ever remove the poles from the rings.
translation_verse: Mpoma no nhyehyɛ adaka no ne nkaba no mu: mma wonnyiyi mmfi mu.

english_verse: Then hammer out two winged creatures of pure gold and fasten them to the lid at the ends of the chest.
translation_verse: Na yɛ kerubim kor wɔ ne tsir kor, nye kerubim kor wɔ ne tsir kor so: mbɔbɔrhu egua no mu na hom mma kerubim ebien no mfi wɔ ne tsir ebien no do.

english_verse: Inside it place the two flat stones with the Ten Commandments on them and put the gold lid on top of the chest.
translation_verse: Na fa mbɔbɔrhu egua no si adaka no do sor; na adaka no mu na fa dase a medze mebɛma wo no to.

english_verse: I will meet you there between the two creatures and tell you what my people must do and what they must not do. The Table for the Sacred Bread (Exodus
translation_verse: Na hɔ na menye wo behyia, na mefi mbɔbɔrhu egua no do wɔ kerubim ebien a wɔwɔ dasedaka no do no ntamu maka wo ndzɛmba a mebɛhyɛ wo dɛ fa kyerɛ Israelmba no nyinara ho nsɛm. Pon No Ho Nhyehyɛɛ (Eksodɔs

english_verse: Cover it with pure gold and put a gold edging around it with a border
translation_verse: Na yɛ ho ntotoano a ɔbɛyɛ nsatseama hyia, na yɛ ntotoano no ho sika hankra hyia.

english_verse: Make four gold rings and attach one to each of the legs
translation_verse: Na yɛ ho sika nkaba anan, na fa nkaba no sisi nkutuw anan a ɔwowɔ n’anan anan no ho no do.

english_verse: near the edging. The poles for carrying the table are to be placed through these rings and are to be made of acacia wood covered with gold.
translation_verse: Na fa akeesia dua sinsian mpoma no, na fa sika duradura ho, ama wɔdze aasoa pon no.

english_verse: Make a lampstand of pure gold. The whole lampstand, including its decorative flowers, must be made from a single piece of hammered gold
translation_verse: Na fa sika mapa yɛ kandzeadua: ɔbor edwuma na fa yɛ kandzeadua no, n’anan, nye no dua; no ho mpokuwa, no nkukuduuduw, nye ne nhyiren, mfi sin kor no ara mu:

english_verse: The lampstand, including its branches and decorative flowers, must be made from a single piece of hammered pure gold.
translation_verse: No ho nkukuduuduw nye no ho abaw no mfi mu ara, ne nyinara sika mapa na wɔmfa nyɛ no ɔbor edwuma kor.

english_verse: The tongs and trays for taking care of the lamps are to be made of pure gold.
translation_verse: Na no ho adabaa, nye no ho nsompokuwa nyɛ sika mapa.

english_verse: The lampstand and its equipment will require
translation_verse: Sika mapa kyɛpɛn kor na wɔmfa nyɛ, ɔnye no ho ndzɛmba yi nyinara.

english_verse: and they must be made according to the pattern I showed you on the mountain.
translation_verse: Na hwɛ na yɛ no dɛ mbrɛ no nsusudo a wɔdze akyerɛ wo wɔ bepɔw no do no tse.

english_verse: For the entrance to the tent, use a piece of fine linen woven with blue, purple, and red wool and embroidered with fancy needlework.
translation_verse: Na fa bibir, nye ɔberdum, nye serdaa, nye ndwera fɛɛfɛw a woefira, wen nkyermu fa yɛ ntamadan no n’abowano nkataano.

english_verse: As the material for protecting the tent, use goat hair to weave eleven sections of cloth
translation_verse: Nsensɛnho tam kor ne tsentsen nyɛ basafa duenum, na nsensɛnho tam kor ne tɛtɛr nyɛ basafa ebien: nsensɛnho atam dubiako no no nsusui ngyina kor.

english_verse: The top of the sacred tent must be made from ten pieces of the finest linen, woven with blue, purple, and red wool and embroidered with figures of winged creatures.
translation_verse: Nkyii so fa nsensɛnho atam du yɛ ntamadan no; ndwera fɛɛfɛw a woefira, nye bibir, nye ɔberdum, nye serdaa na fa yɛ me kerubim a ɔyɛ odwimfo pa edwinsa.

english_verse: Sew five of the sections together to make one panel. Then sew the other six together to make a second panel, and fold the sixth section double over the front of the tent.
translation_verse: Na fa nsensɛnho atam enum toatoa yɛ no kor, na yɛ nsensɛnho atam esia so kor, na boka nsensɛnho tam a otsia esia no ma ɔnkata buw no do wɔ n’enyim.

english_verse: Make two more coverings—one with tanned ram skins and the other with fine leather. The Framework for the Sacred Tent (Exodus
translation_verse: Na fa nguannyin nwoma a wɔahyɛ no memen yɛ buw no ne nkatado; na fa nsukraman nwoma yɛ no sor akatado.

english_verse: Inside the most holy place, put the sacred chest that has the place of mercy on its lid.
translation_verse: Na fa mbɔbɔrhu egua no si dasedaka no do wɔ krɔnkrɔn mu krɔnkrɔnbea no mu.

english_verse: Build a framework of acacia wood for the walls of the sacred tent.
translation_verse: Na fa akeesia dua yɛ ntamadan no ne ntaabow a osisi hɔ.

english_verse: Place two silver stands under each frame with sockets for the pegs, so the frames can be joined together. Put
translation_verse: na no dwetɛ ngyinado eduanan no; ngyinado ebien nhyehyɛ taabow ase, na ngyinado ebien nhyehyɛ taabow kor so ase.

english_verse: For the back wall along the west side use six frames
translation_verse: Na ntamadan no n’ekyir a odzi anee afamu no yɛ hɔ ntaabow esia.

english_verse: with two more at the southwest and northwest corners. Make certain that these corner frames are joined from top to bottom.
translation_verse: Na ɔmpam ho wɔ ase, na ɔnyɛ mu dɛmara nkodu no sor nkaba kor ho: dɛm na n’ebien nyina nyɛ; wondzi kutuw ebien no do.

english_verse: Altogether, this back wall will have eight frames with two silver stands under each one.
translation_verse: Na ɔnyɛ ntaabow awɔtwe, nye no dwetɛ ngyinado, ngyinado duesia; ngyinado ebien nhyehyɛ taabow kor ase, na ngyinado ebien nhyehyɛ taabow kor so ase.

english_verse: with the center crossbar running the full length of the wall.
translation_verse: Na finimfin mbeamu a ɔwɔ ntaabow no ne finimfin no ntwa mu mfi tsir nkodu tsir.

english_verse: Cover the frames and the crossbars with gold and attach gold rings to the frames to run the crossbars through.
translation_verse: Na fa sika duradura ntaabow no ho, na fa sika yɛ no ho nkaba no ma ɔnyɛ mbeabeamu no hyehyɛbea: na fa sika duradura mbeabeamu no ho.

english_verse: Then set up the tent in the way I showed you on the mountain. The Curtain inside the Sacred Tent (Exodus
translation_verse: Na si ntamadan no dɛ mbrɛ ne nyɛbea a woeyi akyerɛ wo wɔ bepɔw no do no tse.

english_verse: Outside the most holy place, as you face the curtain, put the table for the sacred bread on the right side and the gold lampstand on the left.
translation_verse: Na fa ɔpon no si ntwamutam no ekyir, na kandzeadua no ndzi ɔpon no tsentsendo wɔ ntamadan no nkyɛn mu wɔ pomu afamu: na fa ɔpon no si kwaamu afamu.

english_verse: Make a curtain to separate the holy place from the most holy place. Use fine linen woven with blue, purple, and red wool, and embroidered with figures of winged creatures. Cover four acacia wood posts with gold and set them each on a silver stand. Then fasten gold hooks to the posts and hang the curtain there.
translation_verse: Na fa ntwamutam no sensɛn nkɔtɔkorba no ase, na fa dasedaka no bra ntwamutam no mu: na ntwamutam no ntwa bea krɔnkrɔn no nye krɔnkrɔn mu krɔnkrɔn no mu mma wo.

english_verse: Cover five acacia wood posts with gold and set them each on a bronze stand. Then put gold hooks on the posts and hang the curtain there.
translation_verse: Na yɛ akeesia edum enum fa ma nkataano no, na fa sika duradura ho; no nkwaba nyɛ sika: na gu boran ngyinado enum wɔ ase.

english_verse: Cover two acacia wood poles with bronze and put them through the rings for carrying the altar.
translation_verse: Na wɔmfa no mpoma no nwurawura nkaba no mu, na mpoma no nhyehyɛ afɔrmbukyia no ne nkyɛn nkyɛn ebien no, na wɔdze aasoa.

english_verse: All the equipment for the altar must also be made of bronze—the pans for the hot ashes, the shovels, the sprinkling bowls, the meat forks, and the fire pans.
translation_verse: Na yɛ n’asɛn a wɔdze no nso bogu mu, nye no nsofi, nye no nkɔɔpow, nye n’edzinam, nye ne gyansenama kukuwa: no ho ndzɛmba nyinara boran na fa yɛ.

english_verse: Halfway up the altar build a ledge around it, and cover the bottom half of the altar with a decorative bronze grating. Then attach a bronze ring beneath the ledge at the four corners of the altar.
translation_verse: Na fa si afɔrmbukyia no ne bamba no ho wɔ ase, ma atena no ndu afɔrmbukyia no ne finimfin.

english_verse: Construct the altar in the shape of an open box, just as you were shown on the mountain. The Courtyard around the Sacred Tent (Exodus
translation_verse: Fa ntaabow yɛ, na no mu nda kwan: dɛ mbrɛ wɔakyerɛ wo wɔ bepɔw no do no, dɛmara na wɔnyɛ no. Asɛndzibea No Ho Nhyehyɛɛ (Eksodɔs

english_verse: Command the people of Israel to supply you with the purest olive oil. Do this so the lamp will keep burning
translation_verse: Na hyɛ Israelmba no ma wɔmbrɛ wo olive ngo gyenengyenen a woesiw a wɔdze sɔ gya, ma ɔmma kandzea nsi do daa.

english_verse: for the west. Then hang a curtain of fine linen on the posts along each of these three sides by using silver hooks and rods. Place three bronze posts on each side of the entrance at the east and hang a curtain
translation_verse: na n’edum nyɛ eduonu, na ne ngyinado eduonu a wɔdze boran ayɛ: edum no no nkwaba nye no mbɔtsir nyɛ dwetɛ.

english_verse: Use four more of these posts for the entrance way, then hang on them an embroidered curtain of fine linen nine meters long and woven with blue, purple, and red wool.
translation_verse: Na dampan no ano ponkɛse no nyɛ basafa du, wɔmfa bibir, ɔberdum, serdaa, nye ndwera fɛɛfɛw a woefira ayɛ nkyermu, nyɛ ne nkataano: n’edum anan, nye ne ngyinado anan.

english_verse: Make the rest of the equipment for the sacred tent of bronze, including the pegs for the tent and for the curtain surrounding the courtyard. The Oil for the Lamp in the Holy Place (Leviticus
translation_verse: Ntamadan no ho som nyinara mu ndzɛmba nyinara, nye no mbɔfondua nyinara, nye dampan no ho mbɔfondua nyinara nyɛ boran. Kandzea No Ho Mbra (Levitikɔs

english_verse: in front of the curtain that separates the holy place from the most holy place, where the sacred chest is kept. Aaron and his sons are responsible for keeping the lamp burning every night in the sacred tent. The Israelites must always obey this command.
translation_verse: Ehyiadzibuw no mu, ntwamutam a ɔwɔ dase no enyim no n’ekyir, na Aaron nye ne mbabanyin no nsiesie mfi ewimbir nkodu anapa wɔ EWURADZE enyim: ɔnyɛ daa ahyɛdze wɔ Israelmba afamu wɔ hɔn ebusuasantsen nyina mu.

english_verse: Send for your brother Aaron and his sons Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar. They are the ones I have chosen from Israel to serve as my priests.
translation_verse: Na ma wo nua Aaron, nye ne mbabanyin no nka no ho, mfi Israelmba no mu mbra wo nkyɛn, ama wɔaabɛyɛ m’asɔfoe, mekyerɛ Aaron, Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar nye Itamar, Aaron ne mbabanyin no.

english_verse: Aaron is to be dedicated as my high priest, and his clothes must be made only by persons who possess skills that I have given them.
translation_verse: Na kasa kyerɛ hɔn a wɔyɛ abadwembafo a medze nyansa sunsum mahyɛ hɔn ma nyinara, ma wɔmpam Aaron ntar no mfa ntsew no ho, ma ondzi mo sɔfoe.

english_verse: Here are the items that need to be made: a breastpiece, a priestly vest, a robe, an embroidered shirt, a turban, and a sash. These sacred clothes are to be made for your brother Aaron and his sons who will be my priests.
translation_verse: Na ntar a wɔmpam nye yi; akatabo, nye egugudo, nye bataker, nye sortar a wɔawen no dam dam, asɔfokyɛw, nye abɔdo: na wɔmpam ntar krɔnkrɔn mma wo nua Aaron, nye ne mbabanyin, ma woeedzi mo sɔfoe.

english_verse: Use only gold and fine linen, woven with blue, purple, and red wool, for making these clothes. The Vest for the High Priest (Exodus
translation_verse: Na wɔmfa sika no, nye bibir no, nye ɔberdum no, nye serdaa no, nye ndwera fɛɛfɛw no nyɛ.

english_verse: Attach two gold settings to the shoulder straps and fasten them with two braided chains of pure gold. The Breastpiece for the High Priest (Exodus
translation_verse: nye sika mapa ntweaban ebien; yɛ no dɛ ahoma, mbɛsawɔw edwuma: na fa ntweaban a wɔawɔw no mbɛsa no hyehyɛ mbraho no do.

english_verse: and engrave on each of them the name of one of the twelve tribes of Israel.
translation_verse: Na abo no nyɛ dɛ mbrɛ Israelmba hɔn edzin tsetsee, duebien, dɛ mbrɛ hɔn edzin tsetsee; dɛ nsɔwanokaba nkyerɛwee, dɛ mbrɛ baako biara ne dzin tse, wɔmfa mma ebusuakuw duebien no.

english_verse: From the same costly material make a breastpiece for the high priest to use in finding out what I want my people to do.
translation_verse: Na yɛ atsɛmbu akatabo, odwimfo pa edwinsa; dɛ mbrɛ egugudo edwuma no tse na yɛ no; sika, bibir, ɔberdum, serdaa, nye ndwera fɛɛfɛw a woefira na fa yɛ.

english_verse: with four rows of three precious stones: In the first row put a carnelian, a chrysolite, and an emerald;
translation_verse: Na fa nsisimu abo sisi mu, abo akyerɛpɛn anan: kyerɛpɛn a odzi kan no nyɛ bɔgyanambo, kratsebo, nye ahatawmomonbo kyerɛpɛn;

english_verse: in the second row a turquoise, a sapphire, and a diamond;
translation_verse: na kyerɛpɛn a otsia ebien no nyɛ nserɛmɛmbo, hoabo, nye dɛnkyɛmbo;

english_verse: in the third row a jacinth, an agate, and an amethyst;
translation_verse: na kyerɛpɛn a otsia ebiasa no nyɛ ekutuhonbo, mfrafrabo nye ɔberdumbo;

english_verse: and in the fourth row a beryl, an onyx, and a jasper. Mount the stones in delicate gold settings
translation_verse: na kyerɛpɛn a otsia anan no nyɛ sikabereebo, epopobibirbo nye ahwehwɛbo: na ne nsisimu no, sika ntwa ho nhyia.

english_verse: Attach two gold rings to the upper front corners of the breastpiece and fasten them with two braided gold chains to gold settings on the shoulder straps.
translation_verse: Na ntweaban a wɔawɔw no mbɛsa ebien no n’ano ebien a aka no fa toatoa mbraho ebien no mu, na fa hyehyɛ egugudo no n’abatsir ahoma no do wɔ n’enyim afamu.

english_verse: Then take a blue cord and tie the two lower rings on the breastpiece to those on the vest. This will keep the breastpiece in place.
translation_verse: Na wɔmfa bibir ahoma nkyekyer akatabo no ne nkaba no nsusuo egugudo no ne nkaba no mu, ma ɔmfam egugudo no ne nkyermu abɔdo a wɔawen no do, amma akatabo no aannsan emmfi egugudo no ho.

english_verse: Under his vest Aaron must wear a robe of blue wool
translation_verse: Na fa bibir ara nko yɛ egugudo no ne bataker no.

english_verse: with an opening in the center for his head. Be sure to bind the material around the collar to keep it from wearing out.
translation_verse: Na onnya tokur wɔ ne finimfin mfa mma tsir: na wɔmfa ɔtamban a wɔawen mban tokur no n’ano nhyia, ɔtse dɛ dadze atar no tokur, amma oeennsuan.

english_verse: Along the hem of the robe weave pomegranates of blue, purple, and red wool with a gold bell between each of them.
translation_verse: sika adɔmba nye pɔmegranate sika adɔmba nye pɔmegranate ntwa bataker no no mbuano no ho nhyia.

english_verse: If Aaron wears these clothes when he enters the holy place as my high priest, the sound of the bells will be heard, and his life will not be in danger.
translation_verse: Na Aaron mfa nhyɛ nsom: ama wɔaatse ne ndze wɔ aber a ɔrehɛn bea krɔnkrɔn no mu wɔ EWURADZE enyim, na aber a orifir edzi, amma oeennwu.

english_verse: On a narrow strip of pure gold engrave the words: “Dedicated to the Lord.”
translation_verse: Na fa sika mapa yɛ tɛrba, na kyerɛw do, dɛ nsɔwanokaba nkyerɛwee, dɛ KRƆNKRƆN MA EWURADZE.

english_verse: Fasten it to the front of Aaron's turban with a blue cord,
translation_verse: Na fa fam bibir ahoma do, na ɔnko asɔfokyɛw no ho; asɔfokyɛw no n’enyim na ɔmfam.

english_verse: so he can wear it on his forehead. This will show that he will take on himself the guilt for any sins the people of Israel commit in offering their gifts to me, and I will forgive them.
translation_verse: Na ɔmfam Aaron no momapɔw do, na Aaron nsoa mbusu biara a aba krɔnkrɔndze a Israelmba tsew ho yɛ no krɔnkrɔn wɔ hɔn ayɛyɛdze nyinara mu no; na ɔnda no momapɔw do daa, ama EWURADZE n’enyi asɔ hɔn.

english_verse: Since Aaron's sons are priests, they should also look dignified. So make robes, sashes, and special caps for them.
translation_verse: Na yɛ sortar ma Aaron ne mbabanyin, na yɛ abɔdo ma hɔn, na yɛ mbɔtsir bi ma hɔn, wɔ enyimnyam nye ahoɔfɛw ntsi.

english_verse: Then dress Aaron and his sons in these clothes, pour olive oil on their heads, and ordain them as my priests.
translation_verse: Na fa hyehyɛ wo nua Aaron ne mbabanyin no ka no ho; na sera hɔn ngo, na tu hɔn si nkyɛn, na tsew hɔn ho, ma wondzi m’asɔfoe.

english_verse: Make linen shorts for them that reach from the waist down to the thigh, so they won't expose themselves.
translation_verse: Na yɛ ndwera ntwontwo ma hɔn, ma ɔnkata hɔn adagyaw do, mfi asen nkodu nserɛekyir:

english_verse: Whenever they enter the sacred tent or serve at the altar or enter the holy place, they must wear these shorts, or else they will be guilty and die. This same rule applies to any of their descendants who serve as priests.
translation_verse: na Aaron nye ne mbabanyin no, sɛ wɔrokɔ ehyiadzibuw no mu, anaa sɛ wɔreba afɔrmbukyia no nkyɛn abɔsom wɔ bea krɔnkrɔn no mu a, wɔnhyehyɛ, ma woeenndzi mbusu, na woeennwu: ɔnyɛ daa ahyɛdze mma ɔno nye n’asefo a wobedzi n’ekyir no.

english_verse: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
translation_verse: Ahyɛse no Nyankopɔn bɔɔ sor nye asaase.

english_verse: The earth was barren, with no form of life; it was under a roaring ocean covered with darkness. But the Spirit of God was moving over the water. The First Day
translation_verse: Nna asaase da hɔ pan a onnyi bɔbea biara; na isum agye bun no enyi: na Nyankopɔn no sunsum butuw nsu no enyi

english_verse: God said, “I command light to shine!” And light started shining.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Ekan mbra: na ekan bae.

english_verse: God looked at the light and saw that it was good. He separated light from darkness
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn hun kan no dɛ oye: na Nyankopɔn tseew kan no fii sum no ho.

english_verse: and named the light “Day” and the darkness “Night.” Evening came, then morning—that was the first day. The Second Day
translation_verse: Na Ɔfrɛɛ ekan no Adzekyee, na isum no Adzesaa. Na adze sae na adze kyee, da a odzi kan nye no.

english_verse: God said, “I command a dome to separate the water above it from the water below it.”
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Wɔmma wimu mbra nsu no finimfin, na wɔmma ɔntsew nsu no mu ebien: na ɔyɛɛ dɛm.

english_verse: and named it “Sky.” Evening came, then morning—that was the second day. The Third Day
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn frɛɛ wimu no ɔsor. Na adze sae na adze kyee, da a otsia ebien nye no.

english_verse: God said, “I command the water under the sky to come together in one place, so there will be dry ground.” And that's what happened.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Wɔmma nsu a ɔwɔ sor n’ase no mpra nkɔboa fakor na wɔmma wewewdo nyi noho: na ɔyɛɛ dɛm.

english_verse: God named the dry ground “Land,” and he named the water “Ocean.” God looked at what he had done and saw that it was good.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn frɛɛ wewewdo no Asaase; na nsu no no mboano no ɔfrɛɛ no Epo: na Nyankopɔn hun dɛ oye.

english_verse: God said, “I command the earth to produce all kinds of plants, including fruit trees and grain.” And that's what happened.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Asaase mfifir efuw, nhaban a wɔsow aba, na ndua a wɔsow nduaba dɛ mbrɛ hɔn su tse, wɔ asaase do: na ɔyɛɛ dɛm.

english_verse: The earth produced all kinds of vegetation. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
translation_verse: Na asaase fifiir efuw, haban a ɔsow aba dɛ mbrɛ no su tse: na Nyankopɔn hun dɛ oye.

english_verse: Evening came, then morning—that was the third day. The Fourth Day
translation_verse: Na adze sae na adze kyee, da a otsia ebiasa nye no.

english_verse: God said, “I command lights to appear in the sky and to separate day from night and to show the time for seasons, special days, and years.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Nkandzea mbra wimu mbeyi adzekyee mfi adzesaa ho: na ɔnyɛ nsɛnkyerɛdze, nye mbernkyekyɛmu, nye nda nye mfe:

english_verse: I command them to shine on the earth.” And that's what happened.
translation_verse: na ɔnyɛ nkandzea wɔ wimu nhyerɛn asaase do: na ɔyɛɛ dɛm.

english_verse: God made two powerful lights, the brighter one to rule the day and the other to rule the night. He also made the stars.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn yɛɛ nkandzea akɛse ebien; kandzea kɛse no dɛ ɔnhwɛ adzekyee do, na kakraba no dɛ ɔnhwɛ adzesaa do: na ɔyɛɛ nworaba so.

english_verse: Then God put these lights in the sky to shine on the earth,
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn dze sisii wimu dɛ wɔnhyerɛn asaase do,

english_verse: to rule day and night, and to separate light from darkness. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
translation_verse: na wɔnhwɛ adzekyee nye adzesaa do, na wonyi kan mfi sum ho: na Nyankopɔn hun dɛ oye.

english_verse: Evening came, then morning—that was the fourth day. The Fifth Day
translation_verse: Na adze sae na adze kyee, da a otsia anan nye no.

english_verse: God said, “I command the ocean to be full of living creatures, and I command birds to fly above the earth.”
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Nsu no mu mbra mbowa ahorow pii na ntumbowa so nkyin asaase do wɔ sor.

english_verse: So God made the giant sea monsters and all the living creatures that swim in the ocean. He also made every kind of bird. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn bɔɔ esumbowa akɛse no, nkakramba na ahorow pii, dɛ mbrɛ hɔn su tsetsee, na ntumbowa nyinara dɛ mbrɛ hɔn su tsetsee: na Nyankopɔn hun dɛ oye.

english_verse: Then he gave the living creatures his blessing—he told the ocean creatures to increase and live everywhere in the ocean and the birds to increase everywhere on earth.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn hyiraa hɔn see dɛ, Hom nwo, na hom ndɔr, na hom nhyɛ po mu nsu mu ma, na ntumbowa ndɔr wɔ asaase do.

english_verse: Evening came, then morning—that was the fifth day. The Sixth Day
translation_verse: Na adze sae na adze kyee, da a otsia enum nye no.

english_verse: God said, “I command the earth to give life to all kinds of tame animals, wild animals, and reptiles.” And that's what happened.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Asaase ndɔr mbowa a nkwa wɔ hɔn mu dɛ mbrɛ hɔn su tsetsee, fiembowa, nye nketsebowa, nye asaase mbowadoma dɛ mbrɛ hɔn su tsetsee: na ɔyɛɛ dɛm.

english_verse: God made every one of them. Then he looked at what he had done, and it was good.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn yɛɛ asaase do mbowadoma dɛ mbrɛ hɔn su tsetsee, na Nyankopɔn hun dɛ oye.

english_verse: God said, “Now we will make humans, and they will be like us. We will let them rule the fish, the birds, and all other living creatures.”
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Wɔmma yɛnyɛ nyimpa wɔ hɛn su do, wɔ hɛn saso do: na wonya tum wɔ po mu nam, nye wimu ntumbowa, nye fiembowa, nye mbowa a wɔwea asaase do nyinara.

english_verse: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn bɔɔ nyimpa wɔ nankasa no su do, Nyew, Nyankopɔn no su do na ɔbɔɔ nyimpa; ɔbɔɔ hɔn ɔbanyin nye ɔbaa.

english_verse: God gave them his blessing and said: Have a lot of children! Fill the earth with people and bring it under your control. Rule over the fish in the ocean, the birds in the sky, and every animal on the earth.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn hyiraa hɔn: na Ɔsee hɔn dɛ, Hom nwo, na hom ndɔr, na hom nhyɛ asaase ma, na hom ndzi do tum. Hom nya tum wɔ po mu nam do, nye wimu ntumbowa do, nye asaase do mbowa nyinara do.

english_verse: I have provided all kinds of fruit and grain for you to eat.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Hwɛ, mama hom nduadzewa na nduaba a ɔwɔ asaase nyinara enyi, dɛ ɔnyɛ edziban mma hom:

english_verse: And I have given the green plants as food for everything else that breathes, including animals, both wild and tame, and birds. And so it was.
translation_verse: na asaase do mbowadoma nyinara nye wimu ntumbowa nyinara dze, mama hɔn sar na nhaban nhaban dɛ wondzi: na ɔyɛɛ dɛm.

english_verse: God looked at what he had done. All of it was very good! Evening came, then morning—that was the sixth day.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn hun adze a ɔayɛ nyinara, na hwɛ, odzi mu papaapa. Na adze sae na adzekyee, da a otsia esia nye no.

english_verse: So the heavens and the earth and everything else were created. The Seventh Day
translation_verse: Na wowiee sor nye asaase nye dza ɔwɔ mu nyinara yɛ.

english_verse: By the seventh day God had finished his work, and so he rested.
translation_verse: Na da a otsia esuon no nna Nyankopɔn ewie n’edwuma a ɔyɛɛ no nyinara; na ɔhomee fii no ndwuma no ho.

english_verse: God blessed the seventh day and made it special, because on that day he rested from his work.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn hyiraa da a otsia esuon no do na ɔtseew ho: osiandɛ ɔno mu na ɔhomee fii no ndwuma a ɔayɛ nyinara ho. Eden Ture

english_verse: That's how God created the heavens and the earth. The Garden of Eden When the Lord God made the heavens and the earth,
translation_verse: Iyi nye sor nye asaase ho nsɛm. Ber a EWURADZE Nyankopɔn yɛɛ asaase nye sor no,

english_verse: no grass or plants were growing anywhere. God had not yet sent any rain, and there was no one to work the land.
translation_verse: nna hamu dua biara nnyae mmbaa asaase do, na hamu ahaban biara nnyae mmfirii: osiandɛ EWURADZE Nyankopɔn nntɔɔ nsu nnguu asaase do, na nyimpa so nnyi hɔ a ɔbɔdɔw.

english_verse: But streams came up from the ground and watered the earth.
translation_verse: Na nsu fi asaase do bɔfɔɔw asaase enyi nyinara.

english_verse: The Lord God took some soil from the ground and made a man. God breathed life into the man, and the man started breathing.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn dze asaase do huntuma bɔɔ nyimpa, na oguu nkwahwea guu n’ahwentokura mu; na nyimpa bɛyɛɛ ɔkra tseasefo.

english_verse: The Lord made a garden in a place called Eden, which was in the east, and he put the man there.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn yɛɛ ture wɔ Eden, wɔ boka afamu; na hɔ na ɔdze nyimpa a ɔayɛ no no toe.

english_verse: The Lord God filled the garden with all kinds of beautiful trees and fruit trees. Two other trees were in the middle of the garden. One of these gave life—the other gave the wisdom to know the difference between right and wrong.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn maa ndua a ɔyɛ fɛw, na oye dɛ wodzi nyinara fifirii. Ture no ne finimfin na nkwadua no wɔ, na papa nye bɔn ho nyimdzee dua no so si.

english_verse: From Eden a river flowed out to water the garden, then it divided into four rivers.
translation_verse: Na esutsen bi fi Eden kɔree kɔfɔɔw ture no; na ofi hɔ na no mu paapaae, na ɔbɛyɛɛ nsutsir anan.

english_verse: The first one is the Pishon River that flows through the land of Havilah,
translation_verse: Dza odzi kan no ne dzin dze Pison: ɔno na otwa Havila asaase a sika kɔkɔɔ wɔ do no nyinara ho hyia.

english_verse: where pure gold, rare perfumes, and precious stones are found.
translation_verse: Na asaase no do sika ye, na bedola eduhuam nye epopobibir abo so wɔ hɔ.

english_verse: The second is the Gihon River that winds through Ethiopia.
translation_verse: Na esutsen a otsia ebien no ne dzin dze Gihon: ɔno na otwa Kus asaase nyinara ho hyia no.

english_verse: The Tigris River that flows east of Assyria is the third, and the fourth is the Euphrates River.
translation_verse: Na esutsen a otsia ebiasa no ne dzin dze Tigris: ɔno na otwa mu wɔ Asiria boka no. Esutsen a otsia anan no nye Eufrates.

english_verse: The Lord God put the man in the Garden of Eden to take care of it and to look after it.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn faa nyimpa no, na ɔdze no kɔtoo Eden ture no mu dɛ onsiesie hɔ, na ɔnhwɛ hɔ.

english_verse: But the Lord told him, “You may eat fruit from any tree in the garden,
translation_verse: Na Ɔhyɛɛ mbra maa nyimpa no dɛ, Ture yi mu ndua nyinara dzi bi:

english_verse: except the one that has the power to let you know the difference between right and wrong. If you eat any fruit from that tree, you will die before the day is over!”
translation_verse: na papa nye bɔn ho nyimdzee dua no dze, mma nndzi bi: na da ara a ibedzi bi no, ampaara dɛ ibowu.

english_verse: The Lord God said, “It isn't good for the man to live alone. I will make a suitable partner for him.”
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Onnye dɛ nyimpa no nko tsena hɔ; mebɛyɛ boafo a ɔfata no ama no.

english_verse: So the Lord God made him fall into a deep sleep, and he took out one of the man's ribs. Then after closing the man's side,
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn maa nda heii tɔɔ nyimpa no do, na ɔdae; na oyii ne mparba mu kor, na ɔdze nam hyɛɛ n’ananmu.

english_verse: the Lord made a woman out of the rib. The Lord God brought her to the man,
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn dze aparba a oyii no nyimpa no mu no yɛɛ ɔbaa, na ɔdze no brɛɛ nyimpa no.

english_verse: and the man exclaimed, “Here is someone like me! She is part of my body, my own flesh and bones. She came from me, a man. So I will name her Woman!”
translation_verse: Na nyimpa no see dɛ, Nyew! Me mbiew mu biew, na mo honam mu nam ankasa nye yi. Oyi dze, wɔbɛfrɛ no Ɔbaa, osiandɛ woyii no wɔ Banyin mu.

english_verse: That's why a man will leave his own father and mother. He marries a woman, and the two of them become like one person.
translation_verse: Dɛm ntsi na banyin begya ne na nye n’egya wɔ hɔ, na ɔdze noho akɛbata ne yer ho: na wɔayɛ honankor.

english_verse: Although the man and his wife were both naked, they were not ashamed.
translation_verse: Nna hɔn beenu no nyinara dzi adagyaw, banyin no nye ne yer no, naaso wɔammfɛr.

english_verse: The snake was sneakier than any of the other wild animals that the Lord God had made. One day it came to the woman and asked, “Did God tell you not to eat fruit from any tree in the garden?”
translation_verse: Nna abowatsena no yɛ onyitsewfo kyɛn hamu abowa biara a EWURADZE Nyankopɔn ayɛ. Na obisaa ɔbaa no dɛ, Aso ampaara dɛ Nyankopɔn ase hom dɛ, Mma hom nndzi ture yi mu ndua nyinara bi?

english_verse: The woman answered, “God said we could eat fruit from any tree in the garden,
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa no see abowatsena no dɛ, Ture yi mu ndua n’eduaba no yɛwɔ ho kwan dɛ yedzi bi;

english_verse: except the one in the middle. He told us not to eat fruit from that tree or even to touch it. If we do, we will die.”
translation_verse: mbom dua a osi ture yi ne finimfin no n’eduaba no na Nyankopɔn ase dɛ, Mma yenndzi, nkyii so mma yɛmmfa hɛn nsa nnka, amma yeennwuwu.

english_verse: “No, you won't!” the snake replied.
translation_verse: Na abowatsena no buaa ɔbaa no dɛ, Hom nnkowu bi:

english_verse: “God understands what will happen on the day you eat fruit from that tree. You will see what you have done, and you will know the difference between right and wrong, just as God does.”
translation_verse: osiandɛ Nyankopɔn nyim dɛ da a hom bedzi bi no, hom enyi bobue, na hom ayɛ dɛ Nyankopɔn na hom ehu papa nye bɔn.

english_verse: The woman stared at the fruit. It looked beautiful and tasty. She wanted the wisdom that it would give her, and she ate some of the fruit. Her husband was there with her, so she gave some to him, and he ate it too.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa no hun dɛ dua no ye dɛ wodzi, na ɔyɛ enyiwa do fɛw, yɛ akɔndɔ, na otum so dɛ obue enyi no, ɔtseew n’aba no bi dzii; na ɔmaa no kun a ɔka no ho no so bi, na odzii.

english_verse: At once they saw what they had done, and they realized they were naked. Then they sewed fig leaves together to cover themselves.
translation_verse: Na hɔn beenu no nyinara hɔn enyiwa buebuei, na wohun dɛ wodzi adagyaw; na wɔpempaam adɔmbadua n’ahataw, na wɔyɛɛ nsamenyim maa hɔnho.

english_verse: Late in the afternoon, when the breeze began to blow, the man and woman heard the Lord God walking in the garden. So they hid behind some trees. Sin Brings a Curse
translation_verse: Na ewimbir no, wɔtsee EWURADZE Nyankopɔn ne nka dɛ ɔnam ture no mu, na Adam nye ne yer no sumae fii EWURADZE Nyankopɔn n’enyim wɔ ture no mu ndua no mu.

english_verse: The Lord God called out to the man and asked, “Where are you?”
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn frɛɛ banyin no na obisaa no dɛ, Ewɔ hen?

english_verse: The man answered, “I was naked, and when I heard you walking through the garden, I was frightened and hid!”
translation_verse: Na banyin no buaa dɛ, Metsee wo ndze wɔ ture no mu, na musuroi, osiandɛ midzi adagyaw; na musumaa moho.

english_verse: “How did you know you were naked?” God asked. “Did you eat any fruit from that tree in the middle of the garden?”
translation_verse: Na obisaa no dɛ, Woana kaa kyerɛɛ wo dɛ idzi adagyaw? Ana edzi dua a mehyɛɛ wo ho mbra dɛ mma nndzi bi no bi?

english_verse: “It was the woman you put here with me,” the man said. “She gave me some of the fruit, and I ate it.”
translation_verse: Na banyin no see dɛ, ɔbaa no a edze no bɛkaa mo ho no, ɔno na ɔmaa me dua no bi, na midzii.

english_verse: The Lord God then asked the woman, “What have you done?” “The snake tricked me,” she answered, “and I ate some of that fruit.”
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn bisaa ɔbaa no dɛ, Ebɛnadze na ayɛ yi? Na ɔbaa no see dɛ, Abowatsena no na ɔdaadaa me ma midzii.

english_verse: So the Lord God said to the snake: “Because of what you have done, you will be the only animal to suffer this curse— For as long as you live, you will crawl on your stomach and eat dirt.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn see abowatsena no dɛ, Osiandɛ ayɛ iyi ntsi, wɔadom wo wɔ fiembowa nyinara mu, na hamu mbowadoma nyinara mu; wo yafun do na ebɛnantsew, na huntuma na ibedzi wo nkwa nda nyinara.

english_verse: You and this woman will hate each other; your descendants and hers will always be enemies. One of hers will strike you on the head, and you will strike him on the heel.”
translation_verse: Medze ɔtan mobɔto ɔwo nye ɔbaa no ntamu, na w’asefo nye n’asefo ntamu: ɔbɔbɔ wo tsir, na wo so asɔw n’anantsir.

english_verse: Then the Lord God said to the woman, “You will suffer terribly when you give birth. But you will still desire your husband, and he will rule over you.”
translation_verse: Ɔsee ɔbaa no dɛ, Mebɛma w’awoko mu amandzehu adɔɔ so kɛse; ɔfona mu na ebɔwo mba; na ebɛdan wo kun, na ɔbɔhwɛ wo do.

english_verse: The Lord said to the man, “You listened to your wife and ate the fruit I told you not to eat. And so, the ground will be under a curse because of what you did. As long as you live, you will have to struggle to grow enough food.
translation_verse: Na ɔsee Adam dɛ, Osiandɛ etsie wo yer ne ndze, na edzi dua a mehyɛɛ wo ho mbra dɛ, Mma nndzi bi no bi ntsi, wɔadom asaase wɔ ɔwo ntsi; ɔfona mu na ibedzidzi do wo nkwa nda nyinara;

english_verse: Your food will be plants, but the ground will produce thorns and thistles.
translation_verse: nhwirom nye nsoɛ so na obefifir ama wo; na ibedzi hamu ahaban;

english_verse: You will sweat all your life to earn a living; you were made out of soil, and you will once again turn into soil.”
translation_verse: w’enyim mfifir mu na ibedzidzi kɛpem dɛ ebɛsan akɔ daadze; osiandɛ ɔno mu na woyii wo fii: na huntuma nye wo, na huntuma mu na ebɛsan akɔ.

english_verse: The man Adam named his wife Eve because she would become the mother of all who live.
translation_verse: Na banyin no too ne yer no dzin Eve; osiandɛ ɔno na ɔyɛ atseasefo nyinara hɔn na.

english_verse: Then the Lord God made clothes out of animal skins for the man and his wife.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn yɛɛ nwoma ntar maa Adam nye ne yer, na ɔdze hyɛɛ hɔn. Wɔpam Adam na Eve fi Ture no mu

english_verse: The Lord said, “They now know the difference between right and wrong, just as we do. But they must not be allowed to eat fruit from the tree that lets them live forever.”
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Hwɛ, nyimpa no abɛyɛ dɛ hɛn mu kor, ohu papa nye bɔn; na afei bɛyɛ na ɔatsen ne nsa, na ɔatsew nkwadua no so bi edzi, na ɔatsena ase daa;

english_verse: So the Lord God sent them out of the Garden of Eden, where they would have to work the ground from which the man had been made.
translation_verse: dɛm ntsi EWURADZE Nyankopɔn tuu nyimpa fii Eden ture no mu dɛ ɔnkɔdɔw daadze a woyii no fii mu no.

english_verse: Then God put winged creatures at the entrance to the garden and a flaming, flashing sword to guard the way to the life-giving tree.
translation_verse: Na oyii nyimpa no edzi; na ɔdze Kerubim, nye gya nkrantsee a onyinanyinam, sii Eden ture no enyim boka, dɛ wɔnweɔn nkwadua no ho kwan no.

english_verse: Adam and Eve had a son. Then Eve said, “I'll name him Cain because I got him with the help of the Lord.”
translation_verse: Na banyin no nye ne yer Eve dae na onyinsenii; na ɔwoo Kain, na ɔsee dɛ, Medze EWURADZE no mboa enya banyin.

english_verse: Later she had another son and named him Abel. Abel became a sheep farmer, but Cain farmed the land.
translation_verse: Na ɔwoo babanyin bio na ɔfrɛɛ no Abel. Nna Abel yɛɛ oguanhwɛfo, na Kain dze okuafo a.

english_verse: One day, Cain gave part of his harvest to the Lord,
translation_verse: Nda guu mu no, ɔbaa dɛ Kain dze ekuadze bɔbɔɔ EWURADZE afɔr,

english_verse: and Abel also gave an offering to the Lord. He killed the first-born lamb from one of his sheep and gave the Lord the best parts of it. The Lord was pleased with Abel and his offering,
translation_verse: na Abel so dze no nguan mu mbakan na dza wɔadodɔ seradze no mu bi bae. Na EWURADZE n’enyi sɔɔ Abel nye n’afɔrbɔ:

english_verse: but not with Cain and his offering. This made Cain so angry that he could not hide his feelings.
translation_verse: na Kain nye n’afɔrbɔ dze n’enyi annsɔ. Na Kain no bo fuuw kɛsenara, na omunae.

english_verse: The Lord said to Cain: What's wrong with you? Why do you look so angry?
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE bisaa Kain dɛ, ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na wo bo efuw yi? Na ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na emuna yi?

english_verse: If you had done the right thing, you would be smiling. But you did the wrong thing, and now sin is waiting to attack you like a lion. Sin wants to destroy you, but don't let it!
translation_verse: Sɛ eyɛɛ papa a nkyɛ w’enyim nnkɛba nyam? Na dɛ annyɛ papa ntsi, bɔn kotow w’abowano rohwehwɛ w’ekyir kwan naaso ɔwɔ dɛ ehyɛ wo ho do.

english_verse: Cain said to his brother Abel, “Let's go for a walk.” And when they were out in a field, Cain attacked and killed him.
translation_verse: Na Kain kaa kyerɛɛ no nua Abel dɛ ɔmma wɔnkɔ haban mu. Na ɔbaa dɛ wɔwɔ hamu no, Kain soɛɛr no nua Abel do, na okum no.

english_verse: Afterwards the Lord asked Cain, “Where is Abel?” “How should I know?” he answered. “Am I supposed to look after my brother?”
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE bisaa Kain dɛ, Wo nua Abel wɔ hen? Na Kain buaa dɛ, Minnyim: ana emi nye mo nua no hwɛdofo?

english_verse: Then the Lord said: Why have you done this terrible thing? You killed your own brother, and his blood flowed onto the ground. Now his blood is calling out for me to punish you.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see no dɛ, Ebɛnadze na ayɛ yi? Wo nua no bɔgyaa ne ndze fi famu keka mu frɛ me.

english_verse: And so, I'll put you under a curse. Because you killed Abel and made his blood run out on the ground, you will never be able to farm it again.
translation_verse: Na afei wofi asaase a ɔahan n’anomu regye wo nua no bɔgyaa wɔ wo nsamu no do wɔadom wo:

english_verse: When you try to farm the land, it won't produce anything for you. From now on, you'll be without a home, and you'll spend the rest of your life wandering from place to place.
translation_verse: Ofi ndɛ kɔ yi, sɛ edɔw asaase a, onnkeyi no ndɔbaa ama wo; na afei so ɔkobɔfo nye okyimakyimafo na ebɛyɛ wɔ asaase do.

english_verse: “This punishment is too hard!” Cain said.
translation_verse: Na Kain see EWURADZE dɛ, Mo bɔn ho akatua so kyɛn dɛ mubotum asoa.

english_verse: “You're making me leave my home and live far from you. I will have to wander about without a home, and just anyone could kill me.”
translation_verse: Hwɛ, ndɛ eyi me edzi wɔ asaase yi do; na mubosuma efi w’enyim; na mebɛyɛ kobɔfo nye kyimakyimafo wɔ asaase do; na ɔbɛba dɛ, obiara a obohu me no oboku me.

english_verse: “No!” the Lord answered. “Anyone who kills you will be punished seven times worse than I am punishing you.” So the Lord put a mark on Cain to warn everyone not to kill him.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see no dɛ wonnkoku wo, osiandɛ obiara a oboku Kain no wobedzi no wer mpɛn esuon. Na EWURADZE yɛɛ asɛnkyerɛdze bi maa Kain, amma obi hu no a, oeennku no. Kain Asefo

english_verse: But Cain had to go far from the Lord and live in the Land of Wandering, which is east of Eden. More and More People
translation_verse: Na Kain fii EWURADZE n’enyim kɔree, na ɔkɛtsenaa Nod asaase do wɔ Eden no boka.

english_verse: Later, Cain and his wife had a son named Enoch. At the time Cain was building a town, and so he named it Enoch after his son.
translation_verse: Na Kain nye ne yer dae: na onyinsenii, na ɔwoo Enok: na ɔkyekyeer kurow, na ɔfrɛɛ kurow no ne dzin Enok, dɛ mbrɛ ne ba no ne dzin tse.

english_verse: Then Enoch had a son named Irad, who had a son named Mehujael, who had a son named Methushael, who had a son named Lamech.
translation_verse: Na Enok woo Irad; na Irad woo Mehujael: na Mehujael woo Metusael: na Metusael woo Lamek.

english_verse: Lamech and Zillah had a son named Tubal Cain who made tools out of bronze and iron. They also had a daughter, whose name was Naamah.
translation_verse: Na Zila, so woo Tubal-kain a ɔton kɔɔber nye dadze ndzɛmba a wɔdze twa adze nyinara no; na Tubal-kain n’akyerɛbaa nye Naama.

english_verse: One day, Lamech said to his two wives, “A young man wounded me, and I killed him.
translation_verse: Na Lamek see ne yernom dɛ: Hom ntsie me ndze, Ada nye Zila; Hom nyɛ aso mma me kasaa, Lamek yernom: Na meku ɔbarimba dɛ opiraa me, Na meku aberantsɛ dɛ ɔboor me,

english_verse: Anyone who tries to get even with me will be punished ten times more than anyone who tries to get even with Cain.”
translation_verse: Sɛ wobedzi Kain ho wer mpɛn esuon a, Ampa, Lamek dze, mpɛn eduosuon esuon.

english_verse: Adam and his wife had another son. They named him Seth, because they said, “God has given us a son to take the place of Abel, who was killed by his brother Cain.”
translation_verse: Na Adam nye ne yer daa bio; na ɔwoo babanyin, na ɔtoo no dzin Set: Na ɔsee dɛ, Nyankopɔn dze ba fofor ahyɛ me Abel n’ananmu; osiandɛ Kain kum no.

english_verse: Later, Seth had a son and named him Enosh. About this time people started worshiping the Lord.
translation_verse: Na Set so, wɔwoo babanyin maa no; na ɔtoo no dzin Enos: nna afei adasa hyɛɛ ase bɔɔ EWURADZE ne dzin sɔɔr no.

english_verse: and died at the age of
translation_verse: Na nda a Adam dzii nyinara ɔyɛ mfe ahaakron eduasa.

english_verse: and died at the age of
translation_verse: na Set ne nda nyinara yɛɛ mfe ahaakron nye duebien.

english_verse: and died at the age of
translation_verse: na Enos ne nda nyinara yɛɛ mfe ahaakron nye enum.

english_verse: and died at the age of
translation_verse: na Kenan ne nda nyinara yɛɛ mfe ahaakron nye du: na owui.

english_verse: and died at the age of
translation_verse: Mahalalel ne nda nyinara yɛɛ mfe ahaawɔtwe eduokron enum.

english_verse: and died at the age of
translation_verse: na Jared ne nda nyinara yɛɛ mfe ahaakron eduosia ebien na owui.

english_verse: and God took him away at the age of
translation_verse: na Enok nye Nyankopɔn nantsewee: na woennhu no bio, osiandɛ Nyankopɔn faa no kɔree.

english_verse: and died at the age of
translation_verse: Metusela ne nda nyinara yɛɛ mfe ahaakron eduosia akron: na owui.

english_verse: Lamech said, “I'll name him Noah because he will give us comfort, as we struggle hard to make a living on this land that the Lord has put under a curse.”
translation_verse: na ɔtoo no dzin Noa, dɛ, Oyiara na ɔbɛkyekye hɛn werɛ wɔ hɛn edwuma nye hɛn nsaano berɛ mu, osian asaase a EWURADZE apaa no no ntsi.

english_verse: and died at the age of
translation_verse: na Lamek ne nda nyinara yɛɛ mfe ahaesuon eduosuon esuon.

english_verse: More and more people were born, until finally they spread all over the earth. Some of their daughters were so beautiful that supernatural beings came down and married the ones they wanted.
translation_verse: Nyankopɔn ne mba hun nyimpa mbabaa no dɛ hɔn ho yɛ fɛw; na wɔwarwaar hɔn mu dza wɔpɛ nyinara.

english_verse: Then the Lord said, “I won't let my life-giving breath remain in anyone forever. No one will live for more than
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see dɛ, Mo sunsum nnkɛnye nyimpa edzi aper daa, osiandɛ ɔyɛ honam ara. Ofi dɛm ber yi ne nda bɛyɛ mfe ɔha eduonu.

english_verse: The children of the supernatural beings who had married these women became famous heroes and warriors. They were called Nephilim and lived on the earth at that time and even later.
translation_verse: Nda no mu no nna eso n’ekyir no, nna abran akɛse wɔ asaase do, osiandɛ ber a Nyankopɔn ne mba nye nyimpa mbabaa hyiae no, wɔwoo mba maa hɔn, woyinom nye tsetse etumfo no, nkorɔfo a wɔgyee dzin no.

english_verse: The Lord saw how bad the people on earth were and that everything they thought and planned was evil.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE hun dɛ nyimpa n’emumuyɛ ayɛ kɛse wɔ asaase do, na dɛ n’akoma mu nsusui nyinara yɛ bɔn ara nko daa no,

english_verse: He was sorry that he had made them,
translation_verse: EWURADZE nuu noho dɛ ɔyɛɛ nyimpa wɔ asaase do, na ɔyɛɛ no yaw wɔ n’akoma mu.

english_verse: and he said, “I'm going to destroy every person on earth! I'll even wipe out animals, birds, and reptiles. I'm sorry I ever made them.”
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see dɛ, Mebɛsɛɛ nyimpa a mabɔ no no efi asaase enyi: nyew, nyimpa, nye mbowa nyinara; osiandɛ menu moho dɛ mayɛ hɔn.

english_verse: But the Lord was pleased with Noah,
translation_verse: Naaso Noa dze, onyaa enyimpa wɔ EWURADZE enyim. Noa Adaka No

english_verse: and this is the story about him. Noah was the only person who lived right and obeyed God.
translation_verse: Iyinom nye Noa no ho asɛm. Nna Noa yɛ nyimpa tseneneenyi na odzi mu wɔ n’ebusuasantsen mu: Noa nye Nyankopɔn nantsewee.

english_verse: He had three sons: Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
translation_verse: Na Noa woo mbanyin baasa, Sem, Ham, nye Jafet.

english_verse: God knew that everyone was terribly cruel and violent.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn hwɛɛ asaase, na, hwɛ, asɛɛ; osiandɛ nyimpa asɛɛ no kwan wɔ asaase do.

english_verse: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
translation_verse: Fa dua pa pam hɛn ma woho; yɛ adan wɔ hɛn no mu, na fa ihua tar no mu nye n’ekyir.

english_verse: Noah did everything God told him to do.
translation_verse: Na Noa yɛɛ dɛ mbrɛ Nyankopɔn hyɛɛ no no nyinara pɛpɛɛpɛr.

english_verse: I'm going to send a flood that will destroy everything that breathes! Nothing will be left alive.
translation_verse: Na hwɛ, emi, medze nsuyirii reba asaase do abɛsɛɛ abɔdze a nkwahom wɔ mu nyinara efi sor ase: adze a ɔwɔ asaase do nyinara bowu.

english_verse: But I solemnly promise that you, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law will be kept safe in the boat.
translation_verse: Na menye wo bedzi ahyɛmu: na aba hɛn no mu, ɔwo, nye wo mba, nye wo yer, nye wo mba hɔn yernom ka wo ho.

english_verse: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
translation_verse: Ntumbowa ahorow, fiembowa ahorow nye mbowa ahorow nyinara hɔn mu ebien ebien bra wo nkyɛn; ama woeenya nkwa.

english_verse: Store up enough food both for yourself and for them.
translation_verse: Na fa edziban nyinara bi ma woho, na boa ano kɔ wo nkyɛn; ama ɔaayɛ edziban ama ɔwo nye hɔn.

english_verse: The Lord told Noah: Take your whole family with you into the boat, because you are the only one on this earth who pleases me.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Noa dɛ, ɔwo nye wo fie nyinara nkɔ hɛn no mu; osiandɛ ɔwo na mehu wo dɛ etsen wɔ m’enyim wɔ ber yi mu.

english_verse: Take seven pairs of every kind of animal that can be used for sacrifice and one pair of all others.
translation_verse: Mbowa a hɔn ho tsew nyinara fa hɔn mu esuon esuon, onyin nye ne ber; na mbowa a hɔn ho nntsew no mu ebien ebien, onyin na ne ber;

english_verse: Also take seven pairs of every kind of bird with you. Do this so there will always be animals and birds on the earth.
translation_verse: wimu ntumbowa mu so, esuon esuon, enyin nye aber; ama hɔn ase eenya nkwa wɔ asaase nyinara enyi.

english_verse: He obeyed God and took a male and a female of each kind of animal and bird into the boat with him.
translation_verse: ebien ebien kɔr Noa nkyɛn wɔ hɛn no mu, enyin nye aber, dɛ mbrɛ Nyankopɔn hyɛɛ Noa no.

english_verse: Seven days later a flood began to cover the earth.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ nda esuon ekyir no, nsuyirii no baa asaase do.

english_verse: The water under the earth started gushing out everywhere, the sky opened like windows, and rain poured down for
translation_verse: Na nsu tɔree guu asaase do adaduanan adzekyee nye adzesaa.

english_verse: On that day Noah and his wife went into the boat with their three sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth, and their wives.
translation_verse: Da no ara na Noa nye ne yer na ne mba Sem, Ham, nye Jafet, hɔn yernom baasa no ka hɔn ho, kɔr hɛn no mu.

english_verse: They took along every kind of animal, tame and wild, including the birds.
translation_verse: Hɔn, nye mbowa ahorow, fiembowa na hambowa, nye ntumbowa ahorow nyinara kɔr hɛn no mu.

english_verse: Noah took a male and a female of every living creature with him,
translation_verse: Na wɔkɔr Noa nkyɛn wɔ hɛn no mu, honam a nkwahom wɔ mu nyinara mu ebien ebien,

english_verse: just as God had told him to do. And when they were all in the boat, the Lord closed the door.
translation_verse: enyin nye aber na wɔkɔr mu, dɛ mbrɛ Nyankopɔn hyɛɛ Noa no: na EWURADZE toow no mu.

english_verse: Finally, the mighty flood was so deep that even the highest mountain peaks were about seven meters below the surface of the water.
translation_verse: Nsu no boor do kɔr sor basafa esuon na fa; na nkokwaa do katae.

english_verse: Not a bird, animal, reptile, or human was left alive anywhere on earth.
translation_verse: Na biribiara a nkwa wɔ mu wɔ asaase do nyinara wui, ntumbowa, nye fiembowa, nye mbowadoma, nye nketsebowa a wɔwea asaase do nyinara, nye nyimpa nyinara.

english_verse: The Lord destroyed everything that breathed. Nothing was left alive except Noah and the others in the boat.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE sɛɛ adze a nkwa wɔ mu a ɔwɔ asaase do no nyinara, nyimpa, fiembowa, nye nketsebowa, nye sor ntumbowa: na ɔkaa Noa, nye hɔn a wɔnye no wɔ hɛn no mu no.

english_verse: A hundred fifty days later, the water started going down.
translation_verse: Na nsu no yirii wɔ asaase do nda ɔha eduonum.

english_verse: God did not forget about Noah and the animals with him in the boat. So God made a wind blow, and the water started going down.
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn kaa Noa, na adze a nkwa wɔ mu a wɔnye no wɔ hɛn no mu no nyinara: na Nyankopɔn maa mframa bɔe faa asaase do, na nsu no huamee;

english_verse: God stopped up the places where the water had been gushing out from under the earth. He also closed up the sky, and the rain stopped.
translation_verse: bun no no nsunyiwa so nye ɔsor ntokura ano sisiwii, na nsu a ofi sor gyaa tɔ;

english_verse: Then on the seventeenth day of the seventh month of the year, the boat came to rest somewhere in the Ararat mountains.
translation_verse: Na ehɛn no kesii Ararat nkokwaa do wɔ bosoom a otsia esuon no mu, bosoom no ne da a otsia duesuon no mu.

english_verse: The water kept going down, and the mountain tops could be seen on the first day of the tenth month.
translation_verse: Na nsu no kɔr do twee araa dze kodur bosoom a otsia du no mu no, bosoom no ne da a odzi kan no mu, na nkokwaa etsifi dedaa hɔ.

english_verse: Forty days later Noah opened a window to send out a raven, but it kept flying around until the water had dried up.
translation_verse: na ɔsomaa akonkoran, na ofiir edzi kekyinii araa ma ɔdze kɛpeem dɛ nsu wewewee fii asaase do.

english_verse: Noah wanted to find out if the water had gone down, so he sent out a dove.
translation_verse: Na ɔsomaa ebubur fii ne nkyɛn, dɛ ɔnkɔhwɛ kyerɛ nsu no ehuam efi asaase enyi;

english_verse: Deep water was still everywhere, and when the dove could not find a place to land, it flew back to the boat. Then Noah held out his hand and helped it back in.
translation_verse: na ebubur no ennya ne nan sibew, na ɔsanee baa no hɔ wɔ hɛn no mu, osiandɛ nsu no wɔ asaase nyinara enyi: na ɔtseen ne nsa suoo no mu, na ɔdze no baa ne nkyɛn wɔ hɛn no mu.

english_verse: Seven days later Noah sent the dove out again.
translation_verse: Na ɔtweɔɔn nda esuon so bio; na ɔsomaa ebubur no fii hɛn no mu bio;

english_verse: It returned in the evening, holding in its beak a green leaf from an olive tree. Noah knew the water was finally going down.
translation_verse: na ebubur no sanee baa no hɔ ewimbir no; na, hwɛ, olive ahataw momon a ɔatsew tua n’ano: nna Noa hun dɛ nsu no atwe efi asaase do.

english_verse: He waited seven more days before sending the dove out again, and this time it did not return.
translation_verse: Na ɔtweɔɔn nda esuon so bio; na ɔsomaa ebubur no; na ɔannsan ammba no hɔ bio.

english_verse: By the twenty-seventh day of the second month, the earth was completely dry.
translation_verse: Na bosoom a otsia ebien no mu, bosoom no ne da a otsia eduonu esuon no, nna asaase do wewee.

english_verse: God said to Noah,
translation_verse: Na Nyankopɔn kasaa kyerɛɛ Noa dɛ,

english_verse: “You, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law may now leave the boat.
translation_verse: Fi hɛn no mu pue, ɔwo nye wo yer, nye wo mba, nye wo mba hɔn yernom no ka wo ho.

english_verse: After Noah and his family had left the boat,
translation_verse: Na Noa nye ne yer na ne mba, nye ne mba hɔn yernom ka no ho fiir edzi:

english_verse: the living creatures left in groups of their own kind. The Lord's Promise for the Earth
translation_verse: mbowadoma nketsebowa nyinara, nye ntumbowa nyinara, na adze biara a nkwa wɔ mu wɔ asaase do, dɛ mbrɛ hɔn mbowa ban tsetsee, fii hɛn no mu puei. Noa N’afɔrbɔ

english_verse: Noah built an altar where he could offer sacrifices to the Lord. Then he offered on the altar one of each kind of animal and bird that could be used for a sacrifice.
translation_verse: Na Noa sii afɔrmbukyia maa EWURADZE; na ɔfaa mbowa a hɔn ho tsew nyinara mu bi, na ɔdze hɔn bɔɔ ɔhyew afɔr wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do.

english_verse: The smell of the burning offering pleased the Lord, and he said: Never again will I punish the earth for the sinful things its people do. All of them have evil thoughts from the time they are young, but I will never destroy everything that breathes, as I did this time.
