english_verse: Send for your brother Aaron and his sons Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar. They are the ones I have chosen from Israel to serve as my priests.
translation_verse: Na ma wo nua Aaron, nye ne mbabanyin no nka no ho, mfi Israelmba no mu mbra wo nkyɛn, ama wɔaabɛyɛ m’asɔfoe, mekyerɛ Aaron, Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar nye Itamar, Aaron ne mbabanyin no.

english_verse: Aaron is to be dedicated as my high priest, and his clothes must be made only by persons who possess skills that I have given them.
translation_verse: Na kasa kyerɛ hɔn a wɔyɛ abadwembafo a medze nyansa sunsum mahyɛ hɔn ma nyinara, ma wɔmpam Aaron ntar no mfa ntsew no ho, ma ondzi mo sɔfoe.

english_verse: Here are the items that need to be made: a breastpiece, a priestly vest, a robe, an embroidered shirt, a turban, and a sash. These sacred clothes are to be made for your brother Aaron and his sons who will be my priests.
translation_verse: Na ntar a wɔmpam nye yi; akatabo, nye egugudo, nye bataker, nye sortar a wɔawen no dam dam, asɔfokyɛw, nye abɔdo: na wɔmpam ntar krɔnkrɔn mma wo nua Aaron, nye ne mbabanyin, ma woeedzi mo sɔfoe.

english_verse: Use only gold and fine linen, woven with blue, purple, and red wool, for making these clothes. The Vest for the High Priest (Exodus
translation_verse: Na wɔmfa sika no, nye bibir no, nye ɔberdum no, nye serdaa no, nye ndwera fɛɛfɛw no nyɛ.

english_verse: Attach two gold settings to the shoulder straps and fasten them with two braided chains of pure gold. The Breastpiece for the High Priest (Exodus
translation_verse: nye sika mapa ntweaban ebien; yɛ no dɛ ahoma, mbɛsawɔw edwuma: na fa ntweaban a wɔawɔw no mbɛsa no hyehyɛ mbraho no do.

english_verse: and engrave on each of them the name of one of the twelve tribes of Israel.
translation_verse: Na abo no nyɛ dɛ mbrɛ Israelmba hɔn edzin tsetsee, duebien, dɛ mbrɛ hɔn edzin tsetsee; dɛ nsɔwanokaba nkyerɛwee, dɛ mbrɛ baako biara ne dzin tse, wɔmfa mma ebusuakuw duebien no.

english_verse: From the same costly material make a breastpiece for the high priest to use in finding out what I want my people to do.
translation_verse: Na yɛ atsɛmbu akatabo, odwimfo pa edwinsa; dɛ mbrɛ egugudo edwuma no tse na yɛ no; sika, bibir, ɔberdum, serdaa, nye ndwera fɛɛfɛw a woefira na fa yɛ.

english_verse: with four rows of three precious stones: In the first row put a carnelian, a chrysolite, and an emerald;
translation_verse: Na fa nsisimu abo sisi mu, abo akyerɛpɛn anan: kyerɛpɛn a odzi kan no nyɛ bɔgyanambo, kratsebo, nye ahatawmomonbo kyerɛpɛn;

english_verse: in the second row a turquoise, a sapphire, and a diamond;
translation_verse: na kyerɛpɛn a otsia ebien no nyɛ nserɛmɛmbo, hoabo, nye dɛnkyɛmbo;

english_verse: in the third row a jacinth, an agate, and an amethyst;
translation_verse: na kyerɛpɛn a otsia ebiasa no nyɛ ekutuhonbo, mfrafrabo nye ɔberdumbo;

english_verse: and in the fourth row a beryl, an onyx, and a jasper. Mount the stones in delicate gold settings
translation_verse: na kyerɛpɛn a otsia anan no nyɛ sikabereebo, epopobibirbo nye ahwehwɛbo: na ne nsisimu no, sika ntwa ho nhyia.

english_verse: Attach two gold rings to the upper front corners of the breastpiece and fasten them with two braided gold chains to gold settings on the shoulder straps.
translation_verse: Na ntweaban a wɔawɔw no mbɛsa ebien no n’ano ebien a aka no fa toatoa mbraho ebien no mu, na fa hyehyɛ egugudo no n’abatsir ahoma no do wɔ n’enyim afamu.

english_verse: Then take a blue cord and tie the two lower rings on the breastpiece to those on the vest. This will keep the breastpiece in place.
translation_verse: Na wɔmfa bibir ahoma nkyekyer akatabo no ne nkaba no nsusuo egugudo no ne nkaba no mu, ma ɔmfam egugudo no ne nkyermu abɔdo a wɔawen no do, amma akatabo no aannsan emmfi egugudo no ho.

english_verse: Under his vest Aaron must wear a robe of blue wool
translation_verse: Na fa bibir ara nko yɛ egugudo no ne bataker no.

english_verse: with an opening in the center for his head. Be sure to bind the material around the collar to keep it from wearing out.
translation_verse: Na onnya tokur wɔ ne finimfin mfa mma tsir: na wɔmfa ɔtamban a wɔawen mban tokur no n’ano nhyia, ɔtse dɛ dadze atar no tokur, amma oeennsuan.

english_verse: Along the hem of the robe weave pomegranates of blue, purple, and red wool with a gold bell between each of them.
translation_verse: sika adɔmba nye pɔmegranate sika adɔmba nye pɔmegranate ntwa bataker no no mbuano no ho nhyia.

english_verse: If Aaron wears these clothes when he enters the holy place as my high priest, the sound of the bells will be heard, and his life will not be in danger.
translation_verse: Na Aaron mfa nhyɛ nsom: ama wɔaatse ne ndze wɔ aber a ɔrehɛn bea krɔnkrɔn no mu wɔ EWURADZE enyim, na aber a orifir edzi, amma oeennwu.

english_verse: On a narrow strip of pure gold engrave the words: “Dedicated to the Lord.”
translation_verse: Na fa sika mapa yɛ tɛrba, na kyerɛw do, dɛ nsɔwanokaba nkyerɛwee, dɛ KRƆNKRƆN MA EWURADZE.

english_verse: Fasten it to the front of Aaron's turban with a blue cord,
translation_verse: Na fa fam bibir ahoma do, na ɔnko asɔfokyɛw no ho; asɔfokyɛw no n’enyim na ɔmfam.

english_verse: so he can wear it on his forehead. This will show that he will take on himself the guilt for any sins the people of Israel commit in offering their gifts to me, and I will forgive them.
translation_verse: Na ɔmfam Aaron no momapɔw do, na Aaron nsoa mbusu biara a aba krɔnkrɔndze a Israelmba tsew ho yɛ no krɔnkrɔn wɔ hɔn ayɛyɛdze nyinara mu no; na ɔnda no momapɔw do daa, ama EWURADZE n’enyi asɔ hɔn.

english_verse: Since Aaron's sons are priests, they should also look dignified. So make robes, sashes, and special caps for them.
translation_verse: Na yɛ sortar ma Aaron ne mbabanyin, na yɛ abɔdo ma hɔn, na yɛ mbɔtsir bi ma hɔn, wɔ enyimnyam nye ahoɔfɛw ntsi.

english_verse: Then dress Aaron and his sons in these clothes, pour olive oil on their heads, and ordain them as my priests.
translation_verse: Na fa hyehyɛ wo nua Aaron ne mbabanyin no ka no ho; na sera hɔn ngo, na tu hɔn si nkyɛn, na tsew hɔn ho, ma wondzi m’asɔfoe.

english_verse: Make linen shorts for them that reach from the waist down to the thigh, so they won't expose themselves.
translation_verse: Na yɛ ndwera ntwontwo ma hɔn, ma ɔnkata hɔn adagyaw do, mfi asen nkodu nserɛekyir:

english_verse: Whenever they enter the sacred tent or serve at the altar or enter the holy place, they must wear these shorts, or else they will be guilty and die. This same rule applies to any of their descendants who serve as priests.
translation_verse: na Aaron nye ne mbabanyin no, sɛ wɔrokɔ ehyiadzibuw no mu, anaa sɛ wɔreba afɔrmbukyia no nkyɛn abɔsom wɔ bea krɔnkrɔn no mu a, wɔnhyehyɛ, ma woeenndzi mbusu, na woeennwu: ɔnyɛ daa ahyɛdze mma ɔno nye n’asefo a wobedzi n’ekyir no.

english_verse: Each day you must offer a bull as a sacrifice for sin and as a way of purifying the altar. In addition, you must smear the altar with olive oil to make it completely holy.
translation_verse: Na daa nyina fa nantwisaa bɔ bɔn ho afɔr no ma ɔnyɛ mpatadze: na tsew afɔrmbukyia no ho, aber a erepata wɔ ne tsir no; na sera ho ngo, fa tsew ho.

english_verse: Then from your finest flour make three batches of dough without yeast. Shape some of it into larger loaves, some into smaller loaves mixed with olive oil, and the rest into thin wafers brushed with oil.
translation_verse: nye epitsi boodoo, nye epitsi tatar a wɔdze ngo aforɔw, na epitsi tatar ntrɛtraba a wɔasera ho ngo: ewifua pa isiam na fa yɛ.

english_verse: Put all of this bread in a basket and bring it when you come to sacrifice the three animals to me.
translation_verse: Na fa gu kɛntsɛn kor mu, na ongu kɛntsɛn no mu ma fa bra, ɔnye nantwisaa no nye nguannyin ebien no.

english_verse: Bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the sacred tent and tell them to wash themselves.
translation_verse: Na fa Aaron nye ne mbabanyin no bra ehyiadzibuw no n’abowano, na fa nsu hohor hɔn ho.

english_verse: Dress Aaron in the priestly shirt, the robe that goes under the sacred vest, the vest itself, the breastpiece, and the sash.
translation_verse: Na fa ntar no, na fa sortar no, nye egugudo ne bataker no, nye egugudo no, nye akatabo no hyɛ Aaron, na fa egugudo abɔdo a wɔawen no nkyermu no bɔ no mu:

english_verse: and then ordain him by pouring olive oil on his head.
translation_verse: Na nkyii fa ɔserango no, na hue gu ne tsir do, na fa sera no.

english_verse: Lead the bull to the entrance of the sacred tent, where Aaron and his sons will lay their hands on its head.
translation_verse: Na fa ɔnantwisaa no bra ehyiadzibuw no enyim: na Aaron nye ne mbabanyin no mfa hɔn nsa ngu nantwisaa no etsifi.

english_verse: Kill the bull near my altar in front of the tent.
translation_verse: Na ku nantwisaa no wɔ EWURADZE enyim, wɔ ehyiadzibuw no n’abowano.

english_verse: Use a finger to smear some of its blood on each of the four corners of the altar and pour out the rest of the blood on the ground next to the altar.
translation_verse: Na fa nantwisaa no no bɔgyaa bi, na fa wo nsatseaba bɔ mu sera afɔrmbukyia no ne mmbɛn ho; na hue bɔgya no nyinara gu afɔrmbukyia no ase.

english_verse: But the meat, the skin, and the food still in the bull's stomach must be burned outside the camp as an offering to ask forgiveness for the sins of the priests.
translation_verse: Na ɔnantwisaa no ne nam, nye no nwoma, nye n’egyanan dze, fa gya hyew no wɔ nseraban no ekyir: ɔyɛ bɔn ho afɔr.

english_verse: Bring one of the rams to Aaron and his sons and tell them to lay their hands on its head.
translation_verse: Eso fa oguannyin kor; na Aaron nye ne mbabanyin no mfa hɔn nsa ngu n’etsifi.

english_verse: Kill the ram and splatter its blood against all four sides of the altar.
translation_verse: Na ku oguannyin no, na fa no bɔgyaa, na fa petse afɔrmbukyia no ho hyia.

english_verse: Cut up the ram, wash its insides and legs, and lay all of its parts on the altar, including the head.
translation_verse: Na twitwa oguannyin no esinesin, na hohor n’ayamudze, nye no ntwɛr ho, na fa ka n’esinesin, na ɔnye ne tsir no ho.

english_verse: Then make sure that the whole animal goes up in smoke with a smell that pleases me.
translation_verse: Na hyew oguannyin no no nyinara wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do: ɔyɛ ɔhyew afɔr ma EWURADZE: ɔyɛ adze huanhuam, afɔr a wɔdze gya bɔ ma EWURADZE a.

english_verse: Bring the other ram to Aaron and his sons and tell them to lay their hands on its head.
translation_verse: Na fa oguannyin kor no so; na Aaron nye ne mbabanyin no mfa hɔn nsa ngu oguannyin no etsifi.

english_verse: Kill the ram and place some of its blood on Aaron's right ear lobe, his right thumb, and the big toe of his right foot. Do the same for each of his sons and splatter the rest of the blood against the four sides of the altar.
translation_verse: Na nkyii ku oguannyin no, na fa no mbɔgyaa bi, na fa ka Aaron n’aso nyimfa ano, nye ne mbabanyin no hɔn aso nyimfa ano, nye hɔn nsa nyimfa kokrobetsir ano, nye hɔn anan nyimfa kokrobetsir ano, na fa bɔgya no petse afɔrmbukyia no ho hyia.

english_verse: Then take some of the blood from the altar, mix it with the oil used for ordination, and sprinkle it on Aaron and his clothes, and also on his sons and their clothes. This will show that they and their clothes have been dedicated to me.
translation_verse: Na fa afɔrmbukyia no do bɔgya no bi, nye ɔserango no bi, na fa petse Aaron do, nye ne ntar do, nye ne mbabanyin no do, nye ne mbabanyin no hɔn ntar do ka no ho, ma ɔntsew ɔno nye ne ntar, nye ne mbabanyin no, nye ne mbabanyin no hɔn ntar ho nka no ho.

english_verse: This ram is part of the ordination service. So remove its right hind leg, its fat tail, the fat on its insides, as well as the lower part of the liver and the two kidneys with their fat.
translation_verse: Nkyii so fa oguannyin no ne seradze, nye ne seradze dua no, nye seradze a ɔkata n’ayamudze ho no, nye adze a ɔsensɛn ne mberɛbo ho no, nye nsɛbea ebien no, nye no do seradze no, nye aserɛ nyimfa dze no; osiandɛ ɔyɛ etuhoakyɛ oguannyin:

english_verse: and put this bread, together with the meat, into the hands of Aaron and his sons. Then they will lift it all up to show that it is dedicated to me.
translation_verse: na fa ne nyina gu Aaron nsamu, nye ne mbabanyin no nsamu; na hihim no yɛ EWURADZE enyim ohim afɔr.

english_verse: After this, put the meat and bread on the altar and send them up in smoke with a smell that pleases me.
translation_verse: Na gyegye no wɔ hɔn nsamu, na hyew no wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do wɔ ɔhyew afɔr no do, ma ɔnyɛ EWURADZE enyim adze huanhuam: ɔyɛ afɔr a wɔdze gya bɔ ma EWURADZE.

english_verse: You may eat the choice ribs from this second ram, but you must first lift them up to show that this meat is dedicated to me.
translation_verse: Na fa Aaron n’etuhoakyɛ oguannyin no ne nyan, na hihim yɛ EWURADZE enyim ohim afɔr: na ɔnyɛ wo kyɛpɛn.

english_verse: After Aaron's death, his priestly clothes are to be handed down to each descendant who succeeds him as high priest, and these clothes must be worn during the seven-day ceremony of ordination.
translation_verse: Nda esuon na ɔbabanyin a odzi ɔsɔfo wɔ n’ananmu no nhyɛ, aber a ɔreba ehyiadzibuw no mu abɔsom wɔ bea krɔnkrɔn no.

english_verse: Boil the meat of the ordination ram in a sacred place,
translation_verse: Na fa etuhoakyɛ oguannyin no, na noa ne nam no wɔ bea a ɔyɛ krɔnkrɔn.

english_verse: then Aaron and his sons are to eat it together with the three kinds of bread at the entrance to the sacred tent.
translation_verse: Na Aaron nye ne mbabanyin no ndzi oguannyin no ne nam, nye boodoo a ɔwɔ kɛntsɛn no mu no, wɔ ehyiadzibuw no n’abowano.

english_verse: At their ordination, a ceremony of forgiveness was performed for them with this sacred food, and only they have the right to eat it.
translation_verse: Na wondzi ndzɛmba no a wɔdze yɛɛ mpata, ma wɔdze eetu hɔn esi nkyɛn, na wɔatsew hɔnho no: mbom mma nyimpa biara nndzi bi, osiandɛ ɔyɛ krɔnkrɔn.

english_verse: If any of the sacred food is left until morning, it must be completely burned.
translation_verse: Na sɛ etuhoakyɛ nam no, anaa boodoo no bi ka ma adze kye do a, ɔno fa gya hyew dza aka no: mma wonndzi, osiandɛ ɔyɛ krɔnkrɔn.

english_verse: Repeat this ordination ceremony for Aaron and his sons seven days in a row, just as I have instructed you.
translation_verse: Na dɛm na fa yɛ Aaron nye ne mbabanyin no dɛ mbrɛ mahyɛ wo no nyinara: nda esuon na tu hɔn si nkyɛn.

english_verse: Do this for seven days, and the altar will become so holy that anyone who touches it will become holy. Daily Sacrifices (Leviticus
translation_verse: Nda esuon na pata wɔ afɔrmbukyia no ne tsir, na tsew ho: na afɔrmbukyia no nyɛ krɔnkrɔn mu krɔnkrɔn; biribiara a ɔka afɔrmbukyia no ɔnyɛ krɔnkrɔn.

english_verse: Each day you must sacrifice two lambs a year old,
translation_verse: Afei iyi nye dza fa bɔ afɔr wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do; daa daa nyina nguambaa ebien a woedzi afrenhyia afrenhyia.

english_verse: one in the morning and one in the evening.
translation_verse: Eguambaa kor na fa bɔ afɔr anapa; na eguambaa kor so fa bɔ afɔr ewimbir:

english_verse: With each lamb offer one kilogram of your finest flour mixed with a liter of pure olive oil, and also pour out a liter of wine as an offering. The smell of this sacrifice on the fires of the altar will be pleasing to me.
translation_verse: Na eguambaa kor no so fa bɔ afɔr ewimbir, na yɛ no dɛ mbrɛ anapa edzidzi afɔr no, na mbrɛ no nom afɔr no tse, ma ɔnyɛ adze huanhuam, afɔr a wɔdze gya bɔ ma EWURADZE.

english_verse: Because of who I am, the tent will become sacred, and Aaron and his sons will become worthy to serve as my priests.
translation_verse: Na mebɛtsew ehyiadzibuw no, nye afɔrmbukyia no ho: Aaron so nye ne mbabanyin no mebɛtsew hɔn ho, ma woeedzi m’asɔfoe.

english_verse: I will live among you as your God,
translation_verse: Na mebɛtsena Israelmba no mu, na mayɛ hɔn Nyankopɔn.

english_verse: and you will know that I am the Lord your God, the one who rescued you from Egypt, so that I could live among you.
translation_verse: Na wobohu dɛ emi nye EWURADZE hɔn Nyankopɔn a miyii hɔn fii Egypt asaase do bae, dɛ mebɛtsena hɔn mu no: emi nye EWURADZE hɔn Nyankopɔn.

english_verse: It is truly holy because it is dedicated to me, so don't ever make any for yourselves.
translation_verse: Na eduhuam hyewee a erebɛyɛ no, mma hom nnsusu no mforaforae do nnyɛ bi mma homankasa homho: ɔnyɛ wo krɔnkrɔn mma EWURADZE.

english_verse: the altar for sacrifices, and the large bowl with its stand.
translation_verse: nye ɔhyew afɔr afɔrmbukyia no nye no ho ndzɛmba no nyinara, nye eguarsɛn no nye ne ntaease.

english_verse: Build an altar of acacia wood where you can burn incense.
translation_verse: Na yɛ afɔrmbukyia a wɔhyew do eduhuam: akeesia dua na fa yɛ.

english_verse: Cover it with pure gold and put a gold edging around it.
translation_verse: Na fa sika mapa dura ho, n’etsifi, nye ne nkyɛn nkyɛnmu hyia, nye ne mmbɛn no; na yɛ ho sika hankra fa hyia.

english_verse: Then below the edging on opposite sides attach two gold rings through which you can put the poles for carrying the altar.
translation_verse: Na yɛ ho sika nkaba ebien wɔ ne hankra no ase, wɔ ne mparba ebien do, ne nkyɛnmu ebien no na yɛ no wɔ do; na ɔnyɛ mpoma anan mu na wɔdze aasoa.

english_verse: These poles are also to be made of acacia wood covered with gold.
translation_verse: Na fa akeesia dua sinsian mpoma no, na duradura ho sika.

english_verse: Put the altar in front of the inside curtain of the sacred tent. The chest with the place of mercy is kept behind that curtain, and I will talk with you there.
translation_verse: Na fa si ntwamutam a ɔwɔ dase adaka no ho no enyim, wɔ mbɔbɔrhu egua no a ɔwɔ dase no do no enyim, mbrɛ menye wo behyia no.

english_verse: Burn only the proper incense on the altar and never use it for grain sacrifices or animal sacrifices or drink offerings.
translation_verse: Mma hom nnhyew do eduhuam fofor bi, anaa ɔhyew afɔr, anaa edzidzi afɔr; na mma hom nnhue nom afɔr nngu do.

english_verse: Once a year Aaron must purify the altar by smearing on its four corners the blood of an animal sacrificed for sin, and this practice must always be followed. The altar is sacred because it is dedicated to me. The Money for the Sacred Tent
translation_verse: Na Aaron mpata wɔ ne mmbɛn do afrenhyia mu prɛko: bɔn ho afɔr mpata bɔgyaa no na ɔmfa mpata ne tsir afrenhyia mu prɛko wɔ hom mbasantsen nyina mu: ɔyɛ krɔnkrɔn mu krɔnkrɔn ma EWURADZE.

english_verse: The Lord said to Moses:
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE kasaa kyerɛɛ Moses dɛ,

english_verse: Find out how many grown men there are in Israel and require each of them to pay me to keep him safe from danger while you are counting them.
translation_verse: Sɛ erohwehwɛ Israelmba hɔn dodow ano ebu, dɛ mbrɛ hɔn mu hɔn a wɔkan hɔn no tse a, ɔno nyimpa biara mma ne kra tsir apondze mma EWURADZE, sɛ erekan hɔn a; ama sɛ erekan hɔn a, ɔyardɔm bi eennsi hɔn mu.

english_verse: , whether rich or poor, must pay me the same amount of money, weighed according to the official standards.
translation_verse: Na Aaron nye ne mbabanyin no mfa nhohor hɔn nsa ho nye hɔn anan ho wɔ hɔ:

english_verse: This money is to be used for the upkeep of the sacred tent, and because of it, I will never forget my people. The Large Bronze Bowl (Exodus
translation_verse: Na gyegye mpata sika no wɔ Israelmba no hɔ, na fa ma ehyiadzibuw no mu som no; ma ɔaayɛ nkaa ama Israelmba no wɔ EWURADZE enyim, dze aayɛ hɔn akra tsir mpatadze.

english_verse: If you ever make any of it to use as perfume, you will no longer belong to my people.
translation_verse: Obiara a ɔbɛyɛ bi a ɔtse dɛm, ehua ahwɛ no, wobotwa no efi no nkorɔfo mu akyen.

english_verse: The Lord said to Moses:
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE kasaa kyerɛɛ Moses dɛ,

english_verse: By dedicating them in this way, you will make them so holy that anyone who even touches them will become holy.
translation_verse: Na tsew ho ma ɔnyɛ krɔnkrɔn mu krɔnkrɔn: biribiara a ɔka no nyɛ krɔnkrɔn.

english_verse: The Lord said to Moses:
translation_verse: Nkyii so EWURADZE kasaa kyerɛɛ Moses dɛ,

english_verse: for dedicating the tent and chest,
translation_verse: Na fa sera ehyiadzibuw no, nye dasedaka no,

english_verse: the table with its equipment, the lampstand with its equipment, the incense altar with all its utensils,
translation_verse: na ɔpon no nye no ho ndzɛmba no nyinara, nye kandzeadua no nye no ho ndzɛmba no, nye eduhuamhyew afɔrmbukyia no,

english_verse: When you ordain Aaron and his sons as my priests, sprinkle them with some of this oil,
translation_verse: Na sera Aaron nye ne mbabanyin no ngo, na tsew hɔn ho, ma wondzi m’asɔfoe.

english_verse: and say to the people of Israel: “This oil must always be used in the ordination service of a priest. It is holy because it is dedicated to the Lord.
translation_verse: Na kasa kyerɛ Israelmba no dɛ, Iyi nyɛ ɔserango krɔnkrɔn mma me wɔ hom mbasantsen nyina mu.

english_verse: So treat it as holy! Don't ever use it for everyday purposes or mix any for yourselves.
translation_verse: Nyimpa honam do na mma wonnhue nngu, nkyii so mma hom nnyɛ bi a ɔtse dɛm, dɛ mbrɛ no mforafora mu tse: ɔyɛ krɔnkrɔn, na ɔnyɛ krɔnkrɔn mma hom.

english_verse: If you do, you will no longer belong to the Lord's people.” The Sweet-Smelling Incense
translation_verse: Obiara a ɔbɔforafora bi dɛm no, anaa obiara a ɔdze bi bɛka ɔhɔho ho no, wobotwa no efi no nkorɔfo mu akyen.

english_verse: Mix equal amounts of the costly spices stacte, onycha, galbanum, and pure frankincense, then add salt to make the mixture pure and holy.
translation_verse: na fa yɛ eduhuam hyewee, eduhuam a wɔafora no oduhuamforafo nyansaa mu a wɔaposa a ɔyɛ gyenengyenen nye krɔnkrɔn:

english_verse: Pound some of it into powder and sprinkle it in front of the sacred chest, where I meet with you. Be sure to treat this incense as something very holy.
translation_verse: na siw no fekɔfekɔ, na fa bi gu dase no enyim wɔ ehyiadzibuw no mu, mbrɛ menye wo behyia no; ɔnyɛ krɔnkrɔn mu adzekrɔnkrɔn mma hom.

english_verse: The Lord said to Moses:
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE kasaa kyerɛɛ Moses dɛ,

english_verse: I have chosen Bezalel from the Judah tribe to make the sacred tent and its furnishings.
translation_verse: Hwɛ, mabɔ Bezalel, Uri ba no, Hur ba no a ofi Juda ebusuakuw mu no ne dzin mafrɛ no:

english_verse: I have appointed Oholiab from the tribe of Dan to work with him, and I have also given skills to those who will help them make everything exactly as I have commanded you:
translation_verse: Na, hwɛ, mara mafa Oholiab, Ahisamak ba a ofi Dan ebusuakuw mu, aka no ho; na hɔn a wɔwɔ nyansa akoma nyinara medze nyansa ahyɛ hɔn akoma mu ma wɔaayɛ dza mahyɛ wo nyinara:

english_verse: Moses told the Israelites that the Lord had said: The Sabbath belongs to me. Now I command you and your descendants to always obey the laws of the Sabbath. By doing this, you will know that I have chosen you as my own.
translation_verse: Eso kasa kyerɛ Israelmba no dɛ, Ampaara, m’ahomgyenda no na hom nsie: osiandɛ ɔyɛ emi nye hom ntamu asɛnkyerɛdze wɔ hom mbasantsen nyina mu; ma hom eehu dɛ emi nye EWURADZE a metsew hom ho no.

english_verse: Keep the Sabbath holy. You have six days to do your work, but the Sabbath is mine, and it must remain a day of rest. If you work on the Sabbath, you will no longer be part of my people, and you will be put to death.
translation_verse: Nda esia na wɔmfa nyɛ edwuma; na da a otsia esuon no ɔyɛ ahomgye mu ahomgyeda a ɔyɛ krɔnkrɔn ma EWURADZE: obiara a ɔyɛ edwuma biara wɔ ahomgyeda no mu no, oku na wonku no.

english_verse: This day will always serve as a reminder, both to me and to the Israelites, that I made the heavens and the earth in six days, then on the seventh day I rested and relaxed.
translation_verse: Ɔyɛ emi nye Israelmba ntamu daa daa asɛnkyerɛdze: na nda esia na EWURADZE dze yɛɛ ɔsor nye asaase, na da a otsia esuon no ɔhomee na ɔdwedwee noho.

english_verse: When God had finished speaking to Moses on Mount Sinai, he gave him the two flat stones on which he had written all his laws with his own hand.
translation_verse: Na aber a ɔnye Moses wiee nkɔmbɔ twetwe wɔ bepɔw Sinai do no, ɔdze dasepon ebien no maa no, abopon a, wɔdze Nyankopɔn nsatseaba akyerɛw do.

english_verse: Moses, I have seen how stubborn these people are,
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Mehu ɔman yi, na, hwɛ, wɔyɛ ɔman akɔnkyefo.

english_verse: The people got up early the next morning and killed some animals to be used for sacrifices and others to be eaten. Then everyone ate and drank so much that they began to carry on like wild people.
translation_verse: Na wɔsoɛɛr ahanamakye, na wɔbobɔɔ ɔhyew afɔr, na wɔdzedze asomdwee afɔr bae; na ɔman no tsenaa famu dzidzii, na wɔnomee, na wɔsoɛree goree.

english_verse: Moses said to them, “You obeyed the Lord and did what was right, and so you will serve as his priests for the people of Israel. It was hard for you to kill your own sons and brothers, but the Lord has blessed you and made you his priests today.”
translation_verse: Na Moses see dɛ, Hom ntu homho nsi nkyɛn mma EWURADZE ndɛ, nyew, nyimpa biara ntsia ne ba, na ontsia no nua; ma ɔdze nhyira aama hom ndɛ da yi.

english_verse: After the people saw that Moses had been on the mountain for a long time, they went to Aaron and said, “Make us an image of a god who will lead and protect us. Moses brought us out of Egypt, but nobody knows what has happened to him.”
translation_verse: Na aber a ɔman no hun dɛ Moses ekyinkyim dɛ obesian efi bepɔw no do aba no, ɔman no boaa hɔn ano wɔ Aaron nkyɛn, na wɔsee no dɛ, Soɛr, yɛ enyame ma hɛn, ma wondzi hɛn enyim; na Moses yi, nyimpa a ɔdze hɛn fi Egypt asaase do bae no dze, yennyim dza ayɛ no.

english_verse: Aaron told them, “Bring me the gold earrings that your wives and sons and daughters are wearing.”
translation_verse: Na Aaron see hɔn dɛ, Hom nyiyi sika nkaba a ɔhyehyɛ hom yernom, hom mbabanyin, nye hom mbabaa asowa mu no, na wɔmfa mbrɛ me.

english_verse: then he melted them and made an idol in the shape of a young bull. All the people said to one another, “This is the god who brought us out of Egypt!”
translation_verse: Na ɔgyee no wɔ hɔn nsamu, na ɔdze edwindadze huorii, na ɔyɛɛ no nantwiba a woegu: na wɔsee dɛ, Israel, iyinom nye w’enyame a woyii wo fii Egypt asaase do bae.

english_verse: When Aaron saw what was happening, he built an altar in front of the idol and said, “Tomorrow we will celebrate in honor of the Lord.”
translation_verse: Na aber a Aaron hun hɔn enyigye wɔ ho no, osii afɔrmbukyia wɔ n’enyim; na Aaron bɔɔ dawur, na ɔsee dɛ, Ɔkyena nye EWURADZE afahyɛ.

english_verse: The Lord said to Moses: Hurry back down! Those people you led out of Egypt are acting like fools.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE kaa kyerɛɛ Moses dɛ, Sian famu, kɔ; na wo man a edze hɔn fi Egypt asaase do bae no, asɛɛ hɔnho:

english_verse: They have already stopped obeying me and have made themselves an idol in the shape of a young bull. They have bowed down to it, offered sacrifices, and said that it is the god who brought them out of Egypt.
translation_verse: wɔadan efi kwan a mekyerɛɛ hɔn no do ntsɛmara afa nkyɛn: wɔayɛ nantwiba a woegu ama hɔn ho, na wɔasɔr no, na wɔabɔ afɔr ama no, na wɔase dɛ, Israel, iyinom nye w’enyame a woyii wo wɔ Egypt asaase do bae.

english_verse: and I'm angry enough to destroy them, so don't try to stop me. But I will make your descendants into a great nation.
translation_verse: Dɛm ntsi afei gyaa me, ma m’ebufuw nhur nhye hɔn, na menhyew hɔn: na menyɛ wo ɔman kɛse.

english_verse: Moses tried to get the Lord God to change his mind: Our Lord, you used your mighty power to bring these people out of Egypt. Now don't become angry and destroy them.
translation_verse: Na Moses paa EWURADZE ne Nyankopɔn kyɛw see dɛ, EWURADZE, ebɛnadze ntsi na w’ebufuw nhur nhye wo man a edze ahoɔdzen kɛse nye tum nsa yii hɔn wɔ Egypt asaase do bae no yi?

english_verse: If you do, the Egyptians will say that you brought your people out here into the mountains just to get rid of them. Please don't be angry with your people. Don't destroy them!
translation_verse: Ebɛnadze ntsi na Egyptfo nka dɛ, Bɔn ntsi na okeyii hɔn fiir edzi, ma oeekoku hɔn wɔ mbepɔw no mu, na ɔahyew hɔn efi asaase enyi yi? Dan fi w’ebufuhyew no mu, na ma bɔn yi a edze rebɛyɛ wo man yi nyɛ wo yaw.

english_verse: Remember the solemn promise you made to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. You promised that someday they would have as many descendants as there are stars in the sky and that you would give them land.
translation_verse: Kaa Abraham, nye Isaak, nye Israel, nye w’akowaa a ekaa hɔn wankasa wo ho ntam, na esee hɔn dɛ, Mebɛma hom asefo adɔr dɛ ɔsor nworaba, na asaase a maka ho asɛm yi nyinara medze bɛma hom asefo, na wobenya edzi daapem.

english_verse: So even though the Lord had threatened to destroy the people, he changed his mind and let them live.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE nuu no ho wɔ bɔn a ɔkaa dɛ ɔdze rekɛyɛ ne man no ho.

english_verse: Moses went back down the mountain with the two flat stones on which God had written all of his laws with his own hand, using both sides of the stones.
translation_verse: Na apon no yɛ Nyankopɔn edwinsa, na nkyerɛwee no yɛ Nyankopɔn nkyerɛwee a wɔakyerɛw no apon no do.

english_verse: When Joshua heard the noisy shouts of the people, he said to Moses, “A battle must be going on down in the camp.”
translation_verse: Na aber a Josua tsee ɔman no hɔn dede dɛ wɔrobɔ ose no, ɔsee Moses dɛ, Ɔko ne dede esi nsera no mu.

english_verse: But Moses replied, “It doesn't sound like they are shouting because they have won or lost a battle. It sounds more like a wild party!”
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Nnyɛ hɔn a woedzi konyim esebɔ ndze, nkyii so nnyɛ hɔn a wɔako egu ntseatseamu ndze: mbom hɔn a wɔrotow ndwom ne dede na metse.

english_verse: As Moses got closer to the camp, he saw the idol, and he also saw the people dancing around. This made him so angry that he threw down the stones and broke them to pieces at the foot of the mountain.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ, ɔbɛɛn nsera no ara pɛ, nna ohun nantwiba nye asaw no: na Moses no bo fuwii, na ɔtoow ne nsamu apon no gui, na ɔbobɔɔ no wɔ bepɔw no enyiadze.

english_verse: He melted the idol the people had made, and he ground it into powder. He scattered it in their water and made them drink it.
translation_verse: Na ɔfaa nantwiba no a wɔyɛe no, na ɔdze gya hyeew no, na ɔyaam no mokomoko, na ɔdze petsee nsu enyi, na ɔmaa Israelmba no nomee.

english_verse: Moses asked Aaron, “What did these people do to harm you? Why did you make them sin in this terrible way?”
translation_verse: Na Moses see Aaron dɛ, Ebɛnadze na ɔman yi yɛɛ wo, na edze bɔn aba hɔn do yi?

english_verse: Aaron answered: Don't be angry with me. You know as well as I do that they are determined to do evil.
translation_verse: Na Aaron see dɛ, Mma mo wura no bo mmfuw: woara nyim ɔman yi dɛ bɔn ara na osi hɔn enyi do.

english_verse: They even told me, “That man Moses led us out of Egypt, but now we don't know what has happened to him. Make us a god to lead us.”
translation_verse: Na wɔsee me dɛ, Yɛ hɛn enyame a wobedzi hɛn enyim: na Moses yi, nyimpa a ɔdze hɛn fi Egypt asaase do bae no dze, yennyim dza ayɛ no.

english_verse: Then I asked them to bring me their gold earrings. They took them off and gave them to me. I threw the gold into a fire, and out came this bull.
translation_verse: Na mesee hɔn dɛ, Obiara a ɔwɔ sika biara no, wɔmma wonyi; nna wɔdze maa me: na motowee guu gya mu, na ɔnantwiba yi fii mu bae.

english_verse: Moses knew that the people were out of control and that it was Aaron's fault. And now they had made fools of themselves in front of their enemies.
translation_verse: Na aber a Moses hun dɛ ɔman no ayɛ bisibisibasaa no; osiandɛ Aaron ama wɔayɛ bisibisibasaa abɔ adapaa wɔ hɔn atamfo mu:

english_verse: So Moses stood at the gate of the camp and shouted, “Everyone who is on the Lord's side come over here!” Then the men of the Levi tribe gathered around Moses,
translation_verse: nna Moses gyinaa nseraban no ano, na ɔsee dɛ, Obiara a ɔwɔ EWURADZE afa ɔmbra me nkyɛn. Na Levi mbabanyin nyinara boaboaa hɔnho ano baa ne nkyɛn.

english_verse: and he said to them, “The Lord God of Israel commands you to strap on your swords and go through the camp, killing your relatives, your friends, and your neighbors.”
translation_verse: Na ɔsee hɔn dɛ, Mbrɛ EWURADZE, Israel Nyankopɔn, se nye yi, Hom mu nyimpa biara mfa n’afona mbɔ n’asen, na ondzi akɔnaaba, mfi nseraban no ano nkɔ n’ano, na nyimpa biara nku no nua, nye nyimpa biara ne nyɛnko, nye nyimpa biara ne fisamenanyi.

english_verse: The men of the Levi tribe followed his orders, and that day they killed about
translation_verse: Na Levi mbabanyin no yɛɛ dɛ mbrɛ Moses n’asɛm no tse no ara: na ɔman no mu nyimpa bɛyɛ mpem ebiasa totɔr da no ara.

english_verse: The next day Moses told the people, “This is a terrible thing you have done. But I will go back to the Lord to see if I can do something to keep this sin from being held against you.”
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ adze kyee no, Moses see ɔman no dɛ, Hom afom mfom kɛse: na afei mobɔfow akɔ EWURADZE nkyɛn; gyangyanbiara mubotum mapata wɔ hom bɔn no ho.

english_verse: Moses returned to the Lord and said, “The people have committed a terrible sin. They have made a gold idol to be their god.
translation_verse: Na Moses sanee kɔr EWURADZE nkyɛn, na ɔsee dɛ, Ao, mfom kɛse na ɔman yi afom, na wɔayɛ sika enyame ama hɔnho.

english_verse: But I beg you to forgive them. If you don't, please wipe my name out of your book.”
translation_verse: Naaso afei, sɛ edze hɔn bɔn no bɛkyɛ a; na sɛ annyɛ dɛm a, mepa wo kyɛw, pepa mo so fi wo nwoma a akyerɛw no mu.

english_verse: The Lord replied, “I will wipe out of my book the name of everyone who has sinned against me.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Obiara a ɔayɛ bɔn etsia me no, ɔno na mebɛpepa no efi mo nwoma no mu.

english_verse: Now take my people to the place I told you about, and my angel will lead you. But when the time comes, I will punish them for this sin.”
translation_verse: Na afei kɔ, kegya ɔman no kɔ beebi mbrɛ maka akyerɛ wo no: hwɛ, mo bɔfoe bedzi w’enyim akɔ: naaso da a mebɛba abɛsera no, mebɛsera hɔn bɔn wɔ hɔn do.

english_verse: So the Lord punished the people of Israel with a terrible disease for talking Aaron into making the gold idol.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE haaw ɔman no, osian nantwiba a Aaron yɛe no ntsi.

english_verse: The Lord said to Moses: You led the people of Israel out of Egypt. Now get ready to lead them to the land I promised their ancestors Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE kaa kyerɛɛ Moses dɛ, Si mu, fi ha kɔ, ɔwo nye ɔman a edze hɔn fi Egypt asaase do bae no, kɔ asaase a mekaa ho ntam kyerɛɛ Abraham, Isaak, nye Jakob, dɛ W’asefo na medze mebɛma hɔn no do:

english_verse: It is a land rich with milk and honey, and I will send an angel to force out those people who live there—the Canaanites, the Amorites, the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites. But I will not go with my people. They are so rebellious that I would destroy them before they get there.
translation_verse: kɔ asaase a numfonsu nye ewo pem wɔ do no do: na mirinndzi wo mu nnkɔ; osiandɛ ɔman akɔnkyefo nye wo: bɛyɛ na mahyew wo wɔ ɔkwan no mu.

english_verse: Even before the Lord said these harsh things, he had told Moses, “These people really are rebellious, and I would kill them at once, if I went with them. But tell them to take off their fancy jewelry, then I'll decide what to do with them.” So the people started mourning,
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Se Israelmba no dɛ, Ɔman akɔnkyefo nye hom: sɛ miridzi wo mu mber kakraabi menye wo akɔ a, nkyɛ mebɛhyew wo: dɛm ntsi afei yiyi w’ahyehyɛdze no wɔ wo ho, ma munhu dza medze bɛyɛ wo.

english_verse: and after leaving Mount Sinai, they stopped wearing fancy jewelry. The Lord Is with His People
translation_verse: Na Israelmba no yiyii hɔn ho ahyehyɛdze no fi bepɔw Horeb ho araa dze kɔ no. EWURADZE N’ehyiadzibuw

english_verse: Moses used to set up a tent far from camp. He called it the “meeting tent,” and whoever needed some message from the Lord would go there.
translation_verse: Afei Moses ɔtɔ dɛ ɔfa buw no na ɔdze kesi nseraban no ekyir, ekyirekyir fi nseraban no ho; na ɔfrɛɛ no Ehyiadzibuw. Na ɔbaa dɛ obiara a ɔrohwehwɛ EWURADZE no fir edzi kɔ ehyiadzibuw no a ɔwɔ nseraban no ekyir no ho.

english_verse: Each time Moses went out to this tent, everyone would stand at the entrance to their own tents and watch him enter.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ, aber a Moses fiir edzi rokɔ Buw no ho no, ɔman no nyinara soɛrsoɛree, na wogyinagyinae, nyimpa biara wɔ no buw abowano, na wɔhwɛɛ Moses n’etsikɔ araa kodur dɛ ɔkɛhɛɛn Buw no mu.

english_verse: Then they would bow down because a thick cloud would come down in front of the tent, and the Lord would speak to Moses face to face, just like a friend. Afterwards, Moses would return to camp, but his young assistant Joshua would stay at the tent. The Lord Promises To Be with His People
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE kasaa kyerɛɛ Moses enyim nye enyim, dɛ nyimpa rekasa kyerɛ ne nyɛnko. Na ɔsanee baa nseraban no mu: mbom no somfo Josua, Nun ne babanyin, aberantsɛ no, emmfi Buw no mu emmfi. Moses Hu Nyankopɔn N’enyimnyam

english_verse: Moses said to the Lord, “I know that you have told me to lead these people to the land you promised them. But you have not said who will go along to help me. You have said that you are my friend and that you are pleased with me.
translation_verse: Na Moses see EWURADZE dɛ, Hwɛ, ese me dɛ, Fa ɔman yi kɔ: na emmaa munnhun nyia ebɔsoma no ma ɔnye me aakɔ. Naaso aka dɛ, Medze wo dzin minyim wo, na enya m’enyim enyimnyam so.

english_verse: If this is true, let me know what your plans are, then I can obey and continue to please you. And don't forget that you have chosen this nation to be your own.”
translation_verse: Dɛm ntsi afei, mepa wo kyɛw, sɛ menya w’enyim enyimnyam a, afei kyerɛ me w’akwan, ama meehu wo, ma meenya w’enyim enyimnyam: na hwɛ ɔman yi dɛ ɔyɛ wo man.

english_verse: The Lord said, “I will go with you and give you peace.”
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see dɛ, Mara ankasa bedzi w’enyim nye wo akɔ, na mama wo ahomgye.

english_verse: Then Moses replied, “If you aren't going with us, please don't make us leave this place.
translation_verse: Na ɔsee no dɛ, Sɛ warankasa nnye hɛn annkɔ dze a, ɔno mma mmfa hɛn mfi ha nnkɔ.

english_verse: But if you do go with us, everyone will know that you are pleased with your people and with me. That way, we will be different from the rest of the people on earth.”
translation_verse: Na ebɛnadze mu na wɔdze bohu dɛ menya w’enyim enyimnyam, emi nye wo man? Ana nnyɛ kɔ a enye hɛn bɔkɔ no mu bi a, ma yɛaatse hɛnho, emi nye wo man, wɔ amanaman a wɔwɔ asaase enyi nyinara mu bi a?

english_verse: So the Lord told him, “I will do what you have asked, because I am your friend and I am pleased with you.”
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Dza aka yi so mebɛyɛ: osiandɛ enya m’enyim enyimnyam, na medze wo dzin minyim wo.

english_verse: Then Moses said, “I pray that you will let me see you in all of your glory.”
translation_verse: Na ɔsee no dɛ, Mepa wo kyɛw, kyerɛ me w’enyimnyam.

english_verse: The Lord answered: All right. I am the Lord, and I show mercy and kindness to anyone I choose. I will let you see my glory and hear my holy name,
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Mebɛma me yie nyinara etwa mu wɔ w’enyim, na mapaa EWURADZE ne dzin wɔ w’enyim, na madom nyia mobɔdom no, na mehu nyia mubohu no mbɔbɔr, mbɔbɔr.

english_verse: but I won't let you see my face, because anyone who sees my face will die.
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Innkotum ahwɛ m’enyim: osiandɛ nyimpa nnkɔhwɛ me na ɔatsena ase.

english_verse: There is a rock not far from me. Stand beside it,
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see dɛ, Hwɛ, ɔkwan wɔ me nkyɛn ha, na gyina ɔbotan no do:

english_verse: and before I pass by in all of my shining glory, I will put you in a large crack in the rock. I will cover your eyes with my hand until I have passed by.
translation_verse: na ɔbɛba dɛ sɛ m’enyimnyam no rutwa mu a, medze wo bɛhyɛ ɔbotan no ne mpaae bi mu, na medze me nsa akata wo do ma meetwa mu ansaana:

english_verse: Then I will take my hand away, and you will see my back. You will not see my face.
translation_verse: na meeyi me nsa, ma ehu m’ekyir: naaso m’enyim dze, irunnhu.

english_verse: The Lord said: I promise to perform miracles for you that have never been seen anywhere on earth. Neighboring nations will stand in fear and know that I was the one who did these marvelous things.
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Hwɛ, miridzi ahyɛmu: wo man nyinara enyim na mebɛyɛ anwanwadze, dza wɔnnyɛɛ bi da wɔ asaase nyinara do, anaaso ɔman biara mu: na nkorɔfo a ewɔ hɔn mu nyinara ehu EWURADZE edwuma, osiandɛ adze a medze wo rebɛyɛ no yɛ hu.

english_verse: Be ready tomorrow morning to come up Mount Sinai and meet me at the top.
translation_verse: Na siesie woho sie anapa, na fow bra bepɔw Sinai do anapa no, na yi woho wɔ hɔ kyerɛ me wɔ bepɔw no etsifi.

english_verse: No one is to come with you or to be on the mountain at all. Don't even let the sheep and cattle graze at the foot of the mountain.”
translation_verse: Na mma nyimpa biara nnka wo ho mmfow mmba, nkyii so mma wonnhu nyimpa biara wɔ bepɔw no do beebiara; na mma nguan anaa anantwi so nndzidzi wɔ bepɔw no ho.

english_verse: So Moses cut two flat stones like the first ones, and early the next morning he carried them to the top of Mount Sinai, just as the Lord had commanded.
translation_verse: Na ɔseen abopon ebien a ɔtse dɛ kan no dze no; na Moses soɛɛr ahanamakye, na ɔfowee kɔr bepɔw Sinai do, dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛɛ no no na ɔfaa abopon ebien no kitsaa ne nsamu.

english_verse: Then he passed in front of Moses and called out, “I am the Lord God. I am merciful and very patient with my people. I show great love, and I can be trusted.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE twaa mu wɔ n’enyim, na ɔpaa dɛ, EWURADZE, EWURADZE, Nyankopɔn a ayamuhyehye nye adom ahyɛ no ma, ɔfa ebufuw nyaa, na no mbɔbɔrhu nye no nokwar dɔɔ so;

english_verse: Moses quickly bowed down to the ground and worshiped the Lord.
translation_verse: Na Moses yɛɛ ntsɛm, na ɔbɔɔ ne tsir adze wɔ famu, na ɔsɔree.

english_verse: He prayed, “Lord, if you really are pleased with me, I pray that you will go with us. It is true that these people are sinful and rebellious, but forgive our sin and let us be your people.” A Promise and Its Demands (Exodus
translation_verse: Na ɔsee dɛ, Sɛ afei menya w’enyim enyimnyam a, EWURADZE, mepa wo kyɛw, ma EWURADZE ndzi hɛn mu nkɔ; osiandɛ wɔyɛ ɔman akɔnkyefo; na fa hɛn emumuyɛ nye hɛn bɔn kyɛ hɛn, na fa hɛn yɛ w’egyapadze. Wodzi Ahyɛmu No Fofor (Eksodɔs

english_verse: Your men must come to worship me three times a year, because I am the Lord God of Israel.
translation_verse: Afrenhyia mu mpɛn ebiasa na wo mbabanyin nyinara mbefir wɔ EWURADZE Nyankopɔn, Israel Nyankopɔn no enyim.

english_verse: I demand your complete loyalty—you must not worship any other god!
translation_verse: na mma nnsɔr onyame fofor bi: osiandɛ EWURADZE, nyia ne dzin nye Onyinkufo No, ɔyɛ Nyame nyinkufo:

english_verse: The first-born males of your families and of your flocks and herds belong to me.
translation_verse: Dza obue yafun mu nyinara medze a; nye wo fiembowa nyinara mu mbakan a wɔyɛ enyin no, ɔnantwi o, oguan o.

english_verse: Don't make metal images of gods.
translation_verse: Mma nnyɛ enyame a woegu mma woho.

english_verse: I will force out the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites, but you must do what I command you today.
translation_verse: Dwen dza merehyɛ wo ndɛ yi do: hwɛ, murutu Amorinyi, Keenannyi, Hitinyi, Periznyi, Hivinyi, nye Jebusnyi no wɔ w’enyim.

english_verse: Don't make treaties with any of those people. If you do, it will be like falling into a trap.
translation_verse: Hwɛ woho yie, amma ennye asaase a erokɔ do no do atsefo no eenndzi ahyɛmu:

english_verse: Instead, you must destroy their altars and tear down the sacred poles they use in the worship of the goddess Asherah.
translation_verse: mbom hom mbubu hɔn afɔrmbukyia, wondwuruw hɔn edum, na wontwitwa hɔn Aserim no ngu:

english_verse: Don't make treaties with the people there, or you will soon find yourselves worshiping their gods and taking part in their sacrificial meals.
translation_verse: ɔno bɛyɛ na enye asaase no do atsefo no edzi ahyɛmu, na woekedzi hɔn enyame ekyir abɔ ndwamambra, na wɔabɔ afɔr ama hɔn enyame, na obi afrɛ wo na wo so ekedzi n’afɔrbɔdze no bi;

english_verse: Don't fail to observe the Festival of Thin Bread in the month of Abib. Obey me and eat bread without yeast for seven days during Abib, because that is the month you left Egypt.
translation_verse: Sie epitsi afahyɛ no. Nda esuon na dzi epitsi, dɛ mbrɛ mehyɛɛ wo no, bosoom Abib mu wɔ mber a wɔahyɛ no mu: osiandɛ bosoom Abib mu na ifii Egypt bae.

english_verse: You can save the life of a first-born donkey by sacrificing a lamb; if you don't, you must break the donkey's neck. You must save every first-born son. Bring an offering every time you come to worship.
translation_verse: Na asoasopɔnkɔ abakan dze fa eguambaa pon: na sɛ ammpon no a, ɔno bu no kɔn mu. Wo mbabanyin mu mbakan nyinara dze sesa pon hɔn. Na mma obiara mmfa nsapan mmbefir m’enyim.

english_verse: Work for six days and rest on the seventh day, even during the seasons for plowing and harvesting.
translation_verse: Nda esia na yɛ edwuma, na da a otsia esuon no dze, gye w’ahom: funtumber mu nye twaber mu na hom.

english_verse: Celebrate the Harvest Festival each spring when you start harvesting your wheat, and celebrate the Festival of Shelters each autumn when you pick your fruit.
translation_verse: Na dwen adapɛn afahyɛ no do, ewifua twaber mu nduadzewakan no, nye anoboaboa afahyɛ no wɔ afe n’ewiei no.

english_verse: As you advance, I will force the nations out of your land and enlarge your borders. Then no one will try to take your property when you come to worship me these three times each year.
translation_verse: Na mubotutu amanaman wɔ w’enyim, na matsɛw w’ahe mu: nkyii so sɛ erofow akɔ ekefir wɔ EWURADZE wo Nyankopɔn enyim afrenhyia mu mpɛn ebiasa a, nyimpa biara n’enyi nnkɛber w’asaase.

english_verse: When you sacrifice an animal on the altar, don't offer bread made with yeast. And don't save any part of the Passover meal for the next day.
translation_verse: Mma mmfa mbɔrkaw nnka m’afɔr bɔgyaa ho; nkyii so mma adze nnkye apaho afahyɛ afɔr no do.

english_verse: I am the Lord your God, and you must bring the first part of your harvest to the place of worship. Don't boil a young goat in its mother's milk.
translation_verse: W’asaase mu nduadzewakan a odzi kan no fa bra EWURADZE wo Nyankopɔn fie. Mma nnoa apɔnkyeba wɔ ne na no numfonsu mu.

english_verse: The Lord told Moses to put these laws in writing, as part of his agreement with Israel.
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE see Moses dɛ, Kyerɛw asɛm yi: na nsɛm yi do na megyina menye wo na ɔnye Israel edzi ahyɛmu.

english_verse: Moses came down from Mount Sinai, carrying the Ten Commandments. His face was shining brightly because the Lord had been speaking to him. But Moses did not know at first that his face was shining.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ, aber a Moses fi bepɔw Sinai do sianee no, nna dasepon ebien no dze ne nsamu, na aber a Moses fi bepɔw no do sianee no, nna onnyim dɛ osiandɛ ɔnye EWURADZE kasae ntsi n’enyim wer ahyerɛn.

english_verse: When Aaron and the others looked at Moses, they saw this, and they were afraid to go near him.
translation_verse: Na aber a Aaron nye Israelmba no nyinara hun Moses no, hwɛ, nna n’enyim wer hyerɛn: na wosuroo dɛ wɔbɛbɛn no.

english_verse: Moses called out for Aaron and the leaders to come to him, and he spoke with them.
translation_verse: Na Moses frɛɛ hɔn; na Aaron nye mboanofo no mu mpanyimfo no nyinara sanee baa ne nkyɛn: na Moses kasaa kyerɛɛ hɔn.

english_verse: Then the rest of the people of Israel gathered around Moses, and he gave them the laws that the Lord had given him on Mount Sinai.
translation_verse: Na n’ekyir no Israelmba no nyinara twee bɛnee: na ɔdze dza EWURADZE kaa kyerɛɛ no wɔ bepɔw Sinai do no nyinara hyɛɛ hɔn mbra.

english_verse: The face of Moses kept shining, and after he had spoken with the people, he covered his face with a veil.
translation_verse: Na aber a Moses nye hɔn wiee kasa no, ɔdze nkataenyim kataa n’enyim.

english_verse: Moses would always remove the veil when he went into the sacred tent to speak with the Lord. And when he came out, he would tell the people everything the Lord had told him to say.
translation_verse: Na aber a Moses rokɔ EWURADZE enyim nye no akɛkasa no, oyii nkataenyim no, dze kodur dɛ ofii ne nkyɛn; na ofii ne nkyɛn na ɔbɛkaa dza wɔdze hyɛɛ no no kyerɛɛ Israelmba no;

english_verse: They could see that his face was still shining. So after he had spoken with them, he would put the veil back on and leave it on until the next time he went to speak with the Lord.
translation_verse: na Israelmba no hun Moses n’enyim dɛ Moses n’enyim wer no hyerɛn: na Moses dze nkataenyim kataa n’enyim bio, kodur dɛ ɔkɔr mu nye EWURADZE kɛkasae.

english_verse: You have six days in which to do your work. But the seventh day must be dedicated to me, your Lord, as a day of rest. Whoever works on the Sabbath will be put to death.
translation_verse: Nda esia na wɔmfa nyɛ edwuma, na da a otsia esuon no nyɛ da krɔnkrɔn mma hom, ahomgyeda pa mma EWURADZE: obiara a ɔbɛyɛ edwuma biara wɔ mu no woboku no.

english_verse: Don't even build a cooking fire at home on the Sabbath. Offerings for the Sacred Tent (Exodus
translation_verse: Mma hom nnsɔ gya wɔ hom tsenabea nyinara ahomgyeda no. Ntamadan Krɔnkrɔn No Ho Mbra (Eksodɔs

english_verse: The women who were good at weaving cloth brought the blue, purple, and red wool and the fine linen they had made.
translation_verse: Na mbaa a wɔwɔ nyansa akoma nyinara wɔdze hɔn nsa toow asaaba, na wɔdze dza wɔtowee no bae, bibir, nye ɔberdum, nye serdaa, nye ndwera pa.

english_verse: onyx stones or other gems for the sacred vest and breastpiece.
translation_verse: nye epopobibirbo, nye nsisimu abo a wɔdze besisi asɔfontar egugudo no nye akatabo no mu.

english_verse: And he has given them all kinds of artistic skills, including the ability to design and embroider with blue, purple, and red wool and to weave fine linen.
translation_verse: Hɔn na ɔdze akoma mu nyansa ahyɛ hɔn ma, dɛ wondzi edwin horow nyinara, ɔbosiamfo, nye duadwimfo, nye ɔwemfo a odzi bibir, nye ɔberdum, serdaa, nye ndwera fɛɛfɛw mu edwin, nye ɔtanwemfo dze no, mekyerɛ hɔn a wodzi edwin biara, nye hɔn a wɔdwen hɔn tsir mu dzi edwin horow.

english_verse: If you have any skills, you should use them to help make what I have commanded:
translation_verse: Na wɔmma hom mu onyansafo biara mbra, na ɔmbɛhyɛ dza EWURADZE ahyɛ nyinara;

english_verse: Moses told the people of Israel that the Lord had said:
translation_verse: Na Moses kasaa kyerɛɛ Israelmba mboanofo no nyinara dɛ, Iyi nye dza EWURADZE hyɛe no, dɛ,

english_verse: I will welcome an offering from anyone who wants to give something. You may bring gold, silver, or bronze;
translation_verse: Hom nyiyi ayɛyɛdze wɔ hom mu mma EWURADZE: obiara a n’akoma pɛ no, ɔmfa EWURADZE n’ayɛyɛdze mbra; sika, nye dwetɛ, nye boran;

english_verse: blue, purple, or red wool; fine linen; goat hair;
translation_verse: nye bibir, nye ɔberdum, nye serdaa, nye ndwera fɛɛfɛw, nye mpɔnkyenhwi;

english_verse: tanned ram skin or fine leather; acacia wood;
translation_verse: nye nguannyin nwoma a wɔahyɛ no memen, nye nsukraman nwoma, nye akeesia dua;

english_verse: olive oil for the lamp; sweet-smelling spices for the oil of dedication and for the incense; or
translation_verse: nye kandzea no ho ngo, nye ɔserango no nye ɔhyeweduhuam no ho nduhuam;

english_verse: the sacred chest with its carrying poles, its place of mercy, and the curtain in front of it;
translation_verse: adaka no, nye no ho mpoma no, mbɔbɔrhu egua no, nye nsensɛnho ntwamutam no;

english_verse: the table with its carrying poles and all that goes on it, including the sacred bread;
translation_verse: ɔpon no, nye no mpoma no, nye no ho ndzɛmba nyinara, nye ɔkyerɛ edziban no:

english_verse: the incense altar with its carrying poles and sweet-smelling incense; the ordination oil; the curtain for the entrance to the sacred tent;
translation_verse: nye eduhuamhyew afɔrmbukyia no, nye no mpoma, nye ɔserango no, nye ɔhyeweduhuam no nye ntamadan abow no, n’abowano nsensɛnho no;

english_verse: the altar for sacrifices with its bronze grating, its carrying poles, and its equipment; the large bronze bowl with its stand;
translation_verse: ɔhyew afɔr afɔrmbukyia no, nye boran serɛnkye no, no mpoma, nye no ho ndzɛmba nyinara, eguarsɛn no nye ne ntaease;

english_verse: the curtains with the posts and stands that go around the courtyard and the curtain at the entrance;
translation_verse: dampan no ne nsensɛnho no, n’edum no, nye ne ngyinangyinado no, nye dampan no n’abotsen ano nkataano no:

english_verse: the pegs and ropes for the tent and the courtyard;
translation_verse: ntamadan no ho mbɔfondua, nye dampan no ho mbɔfondua, nye ne ntampehoma;

english_verse: Moses finished speaking, and everyone left.
translation_verse: Na Israelmba mboanofo no nyinara fii Moses enyim kɔree.

english_verse: Then those who wanted to bring gifts to the Lord, brought them to be used for the sacred tent, the worship services, and the priestly clothes.
translation_verse: Na wɔbae, obiara a n’akoma maa no do, nye obiara a no sunsum kaa no no, na wɔdze EWURADZE ayɛyɛdze no baa dɛ wɔmfa nyɛ ehyiadzibuw no, nye no ho som ndzɛmba nyinara, nye ntar krɔnkrɔn no.

english_verse: Everyone brought their blue, purple, and red wool, their fine linen, and their cloth made of goat hair, as well as their ram skins dyed red and their fine leather.
translation_verse: Na nyimpa biara a ne nsamu wɔ bibir nye ɔberdum, nye serdaa, nye ndwera fɛɛfɛw, nye mpɔnkyenhwi, nye nguannyin nwoma a wɔahyɛ no memen, nye nsukraman nwoma, ɔdze bae.

english_verse: Anyone who had silver or bronze or acacia wood brought it as a gift to the Lord.
translation_verse: Obiara a ɔbɔɔ dwetɛ nye boran ayɛyɛdze afɔr no ɔdze EWURADZE ayɛyɛdze no bae: na nyimpa biara a ne nsamu wɔ akeesia dua a wɔdze bɛyɛ som edwuma no biara no, ɔdze bae.

english_verse: And the women who knew how to make cloth from goat hair were glad to do so.
translation_verse: Na mbaa a hɔn akoma kenyaan hɔn wɔ nyansa mu nyinara toow mpɔnkyenhwi no.

english_verse: The leaders brought different kinds of jewels to be sewn on the special clothes and the breastpiece for the high priest.
translation_verse: Na mpanyimfo no dze epopobibirbo, nye nsisimu abo a wɔdze besisi egugudo no, nye akatabo no bae;

english_verse: They also brought sweet-smelling spices to be mixed with the incense and olive oil that were for the lamps and for ordaining the priests.
translation_verse: nye eduhuam, nye ngo; kandzea ho dze, nye ɔserango ho dze, nye ɔhyew eduhuam ho dze.

english_verse: Moses had told the people what the Lord wanted them to do, and many of them decided to bring their gifts. Bezalel and Oholiab (Exodus
translation_verse: Israelmba no dze ayamuyi ayɛyɛdze brɛɛ EWURADZE; ɔbanyin nye ɔbaa biara a hɔn akoma maa hɔn do dɛ wɔmfa edwuma nyinara a EWURADZE nam Moses ne nsa do hyɛɛ dɛ wɔnyɛ no no ho biribi mbra no a. Edwumayɛfo a Wɔbɛyɛ EWURADZE Ne Ntamadan (Eksodɔs

english_verse: Moses said to the people of Israel: The Lord has chosen Bezalel of the Judah tribe.
translation_verse: Na Moses see Israelmba no dɛ, Hom nhwɛ, EWURADZE abɔ Bezalel, Uri ba no, Hur ba no a ofi Juda ebusuakuw mu no ne dzin afrɛ no:

english_verse: The Lord is urging him and Oholiab from the tribe of Dan to teach others.
translation_verse: Na ɔdze ahyɛ n’akoma mu dɛ ɔmfa nkyerɛkyerɛ, ɔno, nye Oholiab, Ahisamak ne babanyin no a ofi Dan ebusuakuw mu no, beenu.

english_verse: and he gave them the gifts that the people of Israel had donated for building the place of worship. In fact, so much was being given each morning,
translation_verse: na wɔgyee Moses hɔ ayɛyɛdze no a Israelmba no dze baa dɛ wɔmfa nyɛ krɔnkrɔnbea no som ho edwuma no nyinara, dɛ wɔdze bɛyɛ. Na wɔwɔɔw do ara dze ayamuyi ayɛyɛdze brɛɛ no anapa daa.

english_verse: and said, “Moses, there is already more than we need for what the Lord has assigned us to do.”
translation_verse: na wɔbɛkaa kyerɛɛ Moses dɛ, Ɔman no dza wɔdze ba dɔɔ so sen dza ɔbɔso edwuma a EWURADZE hyɛɛ dɛ wɔnyɛ no yɛ.

english_verse: But there was already more than enough to do what needed to be done. The Curtains and Coverings for the Sacred Tent (Exodus
translation_verse: Osiandɛ dza wonyae no ara dɔɔ so ma edwuma no nyinara dɛ wɔdze bɛyɛ ma bi aka.

english_verse: The skilled workers got together to make the sacred tent and its linen curtains that were woven with blue, purple, and red wool and embroidered with figures of winged creatures. Each of the ten panels was twelve meters long and two meters wide,
translation_verse: Nsensɛnhotam no kor ne tsentsen yɛ basafa duanan, na nsensɛnhotam no kor ne tɛtɛr yɛ basafa ebien: nsensɛnhoatam no nyinara no nsusui gyinaa kor.

english_verse: These eleven sections were joined to make two panels, one with five and the other with six sections.
translation_verse: Na ɔdze nsensɛnho tam enum toatoae yɛɛ no kor, na ɔyɛɛ nsensɛnho tam esia so kor.

english_verse: and the two panels were fastened at the loops with
translation_verse: Na ɔyɛɛ boran nkɔtɔkorba eduonum dze eesusuo buw no mu atoa mu, ma aayɛ kor.

english_verse: Two other coverings were made—one with fine leather and the other with ram skins dyed red. The Framework for the Sacred Tent (Exodus
translation_verse: Na ɔdze nguannyin nwoma a wɔahyɛ no memen yɛɛ buw no ne nkatado, na ɔdze nsukraman nwoma yɛɛ no sor akatado.

english_verse: Altogether, along the back wall there were eight frames with two silver stands under each of them.
translation_verse: Na ɔyɛɛ ntaabow awɔtwe, nye no dwetɛ ngyinado, ngyinado duesia; ngyinado ebien hyehyɛ taabow biara n’ase.

english_verse: Six frames were used for the back wall along the west side
translation_verse: Na ntamadan no n’ekyir a odzi anee afamu no ɔyɛɛ ntaabow esia.

english_verse: with two more at the southwest and northwest corners. These corner frames were joined from top to bottom.
translation_verse: Na ɔpaam ho wɔ ase, na ɔyɛɛ mu dɛmara kodur no sor nkaba kor ho: dɛm na ɔdze yɛɛ n’ebien nyina wɔ kutuw ebien no do.

english_verse: Five crossbars were made for each of the wooden frames, with the center crossbar running the full length of the wall.
translation_verse: Na ɔyɛɛ finimfin mbeamu a ɔwɔ ntaabow no ne finimfin no ma otwaa mu fi tsir kodur tsir.

english_verse: The frames and crossbars were covered with gold, and gold rings were attached to the frames to run the crossbars through. The Inside Curtain for the Sacred Tent (Exodus
translation_verse: Na ɔdze sika duraduraa ntaabow no ho, ɔdze sika yɛɛ no ho nkaba no ma ɔyɛɛ mbeabeamu no hyehyɛbea, na ɔdze sika duraduraa mbeabeamu no ho.

english_verse: For the entrance to the tent, they used a curtain of fine linen woven with blue, purple, and red wool and embroidered with fancy needlework.
translation_verse: Na ɔdze bibir, nye ɔberdum, nye serdaa, nye ndwera fɛɛfɛw a woefira ween nkyermu dze yɛɛ ntamadan no n’abowano nkataano;

english_verse: They made the inside curtain of fine linen woven with blue, purple, and red wool, and embroidered with figures of winged creatures.
translation_verse: Na ɔdze bibir, nye ɔberdum, nye serdaa, nye ndwera fɛɛfɛw a woefira yɛɛ ntwamutam no: ɔyɛɛ mu kerubim a ɔyɛ odwimfo pa edwinsa.

english_verse: They also made four acacia wood posts and covered them with gold. Then gold rings were fastened to the posts, which were set on silver stands.
translation_verse: Na ɔyɛɛ ho akeesia edum anan, na ɔdze sika duraduraa ho: no nkwaba yɛ sika; na oguu ase dwetɛ ngyinado anan.

english_verse: They made five posts, covered them completely with gold, and set each of them on a gold-covered bronze stand. Finally, they attached hooks for the curtain.
translation_verse: na ɔyɛɛ n’edum enum no nye no nkwaba: na oduraduraa n’etsir nye no mbɔtsir ho sika: na ne ngyinado enum no yɛ boran.

english_verse: He covered it inside and out with pure gold and put a gold edging around the top.
translation_verse: na ɔdze sika mapa duraa no mu nye n’ekyir, na ɔyɛɛ sika hankra twaa ho hyiae.

english_verse: He made four gold rings and attached one of them to each of the four legs of the chest.
translation_verse: Na oguu no ho sika nkaba anan, dze sisii n’anan anan no ho; nkaba ebien wɔ n’afa kor, na nkaba ebien wɔ n’afa kor so.

english_verse: Then he made two poles of acacia wood, covered them with gold,
translation_verse: Na ɔdze akeesia dua senseen mpoma, na oduraduraa ho sika.

english_verse: The entire lid of the chest, which was made of pure gold, was the place of mercy.
translation_verse: Na ɔdze sika mapa yɛɛ mbɔbɔrhu egua: ne tsentsen yɛ bɛyɛ basafa kor nye fa, na ne tɛtɛr bɛyɛ anamɔn ebien nye fa.

english_verse: On each of the two ends of the chest he made a winged creature of hammered gold. They faced each other, and their wings covered the place of mercy. The Table for the Sacred Bread (Exodus
translation_verse: Na kerubim no tserɛtserɛɛw hɔn ntsaban mu wɔ sor, dze hɔn ntsaban no kataa mbɔbɔrhu egua no do, hɔn enyim dzi abɔenyim; mbɔbɔrhu egua no do na kerubfo no hɔn enyim bedzii. Pon a Wɔdze EWURADZE Paanoo Si Do No Ne Nyɛɛ (Eksodɔs

english_verse: He covered it with pure gold and put a gold edging around it with a border
translation_verse: Na ɔyɛɛ ho ntotoano a ɔbɛyɛ nsatseama hyiae, na ɔyɛɛ no ntotoano no ho sika hankra hyiae.

english_verse: He made four gold rings and attached one to each of the legs
translation_verse: Na oguu no ho sika nkaba anan, na ɔdze nkaba no sisii kutuw anan a ɔwowɔ n’anan anan no ho no do.

english_verse: near the edging. The poles for carrying the table were placed through these rings
translation_verse: Ntoatoano no nkyɛn papaapa na nkaba no dzii, beebi mbrɛ wɔdze mpoma no bɛhyehyɛ dze asoa ɔpon no.

english_verse: and were made of acacia wood covered with gold.
translation_verse: Na ɔdze akeesia dua sinsiaan mpoma no, na oduraduraa ho sika, ma wɔdze aasoa ɔpon no.

english_verse: Everything that was to be set on the table was made of pure gold—the bowls, plates, jars, and cups for wine offerings. The Lampstand (Exodus
translation_verse: Na ɔdze sika mapa yɛɛ ɔpon no no do ndzɛmba no, nkwansɛn, nye ne ntser, nye no nkorbaa, nye no nkɔɔpow a wɔdze bohue.

english_verse: Bezalel made a lampstand of pure gold. The whole lampstand, including its decorative flowers, was made from a single piece of hammered gold,
translation_verse: Na ɔdze sika mapa yɛɛ kandzeadua no: ɔbor edwuma na ɔdze yɛɛ kandzeadua no, n’anan, nye no dua; no ho mpokuwa, nye nkukuduuduw, nye ne nhyiren, fi sin kor no ara mu:

english_verse: There was also a blossom where each pair of branches came out from the stem.
translation_verse: na kukuduuduw hyɛ abaw ebien a otuatua ho no mu biara ase, wɔ abaw esia a efifir mu no ase.

english_verse: The lamp and its equipment, including the tongs and trays, were made of about
translation_verse: Sika mapa kyɛpɛn kor na ɔdze yɛe, nye no ho ndzɛmba no nyinara. Eduhuamhyew Afɔrmbukyia Ne Nyɛɛ

english_verse: He covered it with pure gold and put a gold edging around it.
translation_verse: Na ɔdze sika mapa duraa ho fi n’etsifi, nye ne nkyɛn nkyɛn hyiae, nye ne mmbɛn no: na ɔyɛɛ ho sika hankra dze hyiae.

english_verse: Then below the edging on opposite sides he attached two gold rings through which he put the poles for carrying the altar.
translation_verse: Na ɔyɛɛ no ho sika nkaba ebien wɔ ne hankra no ase, wɔ ne mparba ebien no do, wɔ ne nkyɛnmu ebien no do, beebi mbrɛ wɔdze mpoma no bɛhyehyɛ dze asoa.

english_verse: These poles were also made of acacia wood and covered with gold. The Oil for Dedication and the Incense (Exodus
translation_verse: Na ɔdze akeesia dua senseen mpoma no, na oduraduraa ho sika.

english_verse: Bezalel mixed the sacred oil for dedication and the pure spices for the sweet-smelling incense.
translation_verse: Na ɔyɛɛ ɔserango krɔnkrɔn no, nye nduhuanhuam hyewee gyenengyenen no, dɛ mbrɛ oduhuamforafo n’edwuma tse.

english_verse: Bezalel made a large bowl and a stand out of bronze from the mirrors of the women who helped at the entrance to the sacred tent. The Courtyard around the Sacred Tent (Exodus
translation_verse: Na ɔdze boran yɛɛ eguarsɛn no, na ne ntaease no so boran a ofi mbaa asomfo a wɔsom wɔ ehyiadzibuw no n’abowano no hɔn nhwehwɛ no mu.

english_verse: and the silver that was collected when the people were counted came to
translation_verse: Na mboanofo no mu hɔn a wɔkaan hɔn no hɔn dwetɛ yɛ kyɛpɛn ɔha, nye dwetɛbona apem ahaesuon eduosuon enum, dɛ mbrɛ krɔnkrɔnbea dwetɛbona no tse:

english_verse: Then he attached a bronze ring beneath the ledge at the four corners to put the poles through.
translation_verse: Na oguu nkaba anan tuatuaa boran serɛnkye no no kutuw anan no do, dɛ ɔnyɛ mpoma no ne hyɛbew.

english_verse: put them through the rings for carrying the altar, which was shaped like an open box. The Large Bronze Bowl (Exodus
translation_verse: Na ɔdze poma no hyehyɛɛ nkaba no mu wɔ afɔrmbukyia no ne nkyɛn nkyɛn, ma wɔdze aasoa; ɔdze ntaabow yɛe, no mu da kwan.

english_verse: And it was used to make the stands for the entrance to the tent, the altar and its grating, the equipment for the altar,
translation_verse: Na ɔno na ɔdze yɛɛ ehyiadzibuw no n’abowano ngyinado no, nye boran afɔrmbukyia no, nye no ho boran serɛnkye no, nye afɔrmbukyia no ho ndzɛmba nyinara,

english_verse: For the entrance to the courtyard, Bezalel made a curtain nine meters long, which he hung on four bronze posts that were set on bronze stands. This curtain was
translation_verse: Na edum no yɛ anan, nye ne ngyinado anan, boran a; no nkwaba dwetɛ a, na n’etsir nduraho nye no mbɔtsir dwetɛ a.

english_verse: Bezalel had worked closely with Oholiab, who was an expert at designing and engraving, and at embroidering blue, purple, and red wool. The two of them completed the work that the Lord had commanded to be done. Moses put Aaron's son Ithamar in charge of the Levites who kept record of the metals used for the sacred tent.
translation_verse: Na nyia ɔka no ho nye Oholiab, Ahisamak ne ba no a ofi Dan ebusuakuw mu no, ɔbosiamfo, nye nduadwimfo, nye ɔwemfo a odzi bibir, nye ɔberdum, nye serdaa, nye ndwera fɛɛfɛw mu edwin.

english_verse: Two thousand four hundred and twenty-five kilograms of bronze were given.
translation_verse: Na oyima boran no ɔyɛ dwetɛ kyɛpɛn eduosuon, nye dwetɛbona mpemebien ahaanan.

english_verse: the stands for the posts that surrounded the courtyard, including those at the entrance to the courtyard, and the pegs for the tent and the courtyard.
translation_verse: nye dampan no ne ngyinado a etwa ho ehyia, nye dampan no n’abotsen ano ngyinado, nye ntamadan no ho mbɔfondua nyinara, nye dampan no no mbɔfondua a etwa ho ehyia nyinara.

english_verse: Beautiful priestly clothes were made of blue, purple, and red wool for Aaron to wear when he performed his duties in the holy place. This was done exactly as the Lord had commanded Moses.
translation_verse: Na wɔdze bibir, nye ɔberdum, nye serdaa, yɛɛ ntar fɛɛfɛw a wɔdze bɔsom wɔ bea krɔnkrɔn hɔ no, na wɔyɛɛ Aaron ntar krɔnkrɔn no dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛɛ Moses no.

english_verse: and on each of them was engraved the name of one of the twelve tribes of Israel.
translation_verse: Na abo no wɔyɛɛ no dɛ mbrɛ Israelmba no hɔn edzin tsetsee, duebien, dɛ mbrɛ hɔn edzin tsetsee; dɛ nsɔwanonkaba nkyerɛwee, baako biara mbrɛ ne dzin tse, dze ma ebusuakuw duebien no.

english_verse: The entire priestly vest was made of fine linen, woven with blue, purple, and red wool. Thin sheets of gold were hammered out and cut into threads that were skillfully woven into the vest.
translation_verse: Na wɔboor sika no tɛtraba, na wotwitwaa no ntseatseaba, dɛ wɔdze robɔfora bibir, nye ɔberdum, nye serdaa, nye ndwera fɛɛfɛw no mu edzi no edwin.

english_verse: Onyx stones were placed in gold settings, and each one was engraved with the name of one of Israel's sons.
translation_verse: Na wosiaan epopobibirbo, sisii sika mbraho mu a, wɔdze nsɔwanokaba nkyerɛwee kyerɛwee, dɛ mbrɛ Israelmba no hɔn edzin tsetsee.

english_verse: Then these were attached to the shoulder straps of the vest, so the Lord would never forget his people. Everything was done exactly as the Lord had commanded Moses. The Breastpiece (Exodus
translation_verse: Na ɔdze bobɔɔ egugudo no n’abatsir ahoma no do ma wɔaayɛ nkaa abo ama Israelmba no; dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛɛ Moses no. Akatabo No Ne Nyɛɛ (Eksodɔs

english_verse: The words “Dedicated to the Lord” were engraved on a narrow strip of pure gold,
translation_verse: Na wɔdze sika mapa yɛɛ abɔtsir krɔnkrɔn no ne tɛrba no, na wɔkyerɛɛw do nkyerɛwee dɛ nsɔwanokaba do nkyerɛwee dɛ, KRƆNKRƆN MA EWURADZE.

english_verse: The breastpiece was made with the same materials and designs as the priestly vest.
translation_verse: Na ɔyɛɛ akatabo no, odwimfo pa edwinsa, dɛ mbrɛ egugudo edwuma no tse; sika, bibir, ɔberdum, serdaa, nye ndwera fɛɛfɛw a woefira.

english_verse: The priestly robe was made of blue wool
translation_verse: Na ɔyɛɛ egugudo no ne bataker no tanwemfo edwinsa, ne nyinara bibir ara nko;

english_verse: a turquoise, a sapphire, and a diamond were in the second row;
translation_verse: Na kyerɛpɛn a otsia ebien no, nserɛmɛmbo, hoabo, nye ɔdɛnkyɛmbo a.

english_verse: a jacinth, an agate, and an amethyst were in the third row;
translation_verse: Na kyerɛpɛn a otsia ebiasa no, ekutuhonbo, aforaforabo nye ɔberdumbo a.

english_verse: and a beryl, an onyx, and a jasper were in the fourth row. They were mounted in a delicate gold setting,
translation_verse: Na kyerɛpɛn a otsia anan no, sikabereebo, epopobibirbo, nye ahwehwɛbo a: ne nsisimu no wɔyɛɛ ho sika mbraho hyiae.

english_verse: Two gold rings were attached to the upper front corners of the breastpiece and fastened with two braided gold chains to gold settings on the shoulder straps.
translation_verse: Na ntweaban a wɔawɔw no mbɛsa ebien no n’ano ebien a aka no, wɔdze toatoaa mbraho ebien no mu, na wɔdze hyehyɛɛ egugudo no n’abatsir ahoma no do wɔ n’enyim afamu.

english_verse: To keep the breastpiece in place, a blue cord was used to tie the two lower rings on the breastpiece to those on the vest. These things were done exactly as the Lord had commanded Moses. The Clothes for the Priests (Exodus
translation_verse: Na wɔdze bibir ahoma kyekyeer akatabo no ne nkaba no susuoo egugudo no ne nkaba no mu, dɛ ɔmfam egugudo no ne nkyermu abɔdo a wɔawen no do, amma akatabo no aannsan emmfi egugudo no ho; dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛɛ Moses no. Asɔfontar Nkaa No (Eksodɔs

english_verse: which was fastened to Aaron's turban. These things were done exactly as the Lord had commanded Moses. The Work Is Completed (Exodus
translation_verse: Na wɔdze bibir ahoma kyekyeree dze faam asɔfokyɛw no ho sor; dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛɛ Moses no. Moses Kɔ Dwumadzi No Ho Nsera (Eksodɔs

english_verse: When Moses saw that the people had done everything exactly as the Lord had commanded, he gave them his blessing.
translation_verse: Na Moses hwɛɛ edwuma no nyinara, na, hwɛ, wɔayɛ; dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛe no, dɛmara so pɛ na wɔyɛe: na Moses hyiraa hɔn.

english_verse: with an opening in the center for the head. The material around the collar was bound so as to keep it from wearing out.
translation_verse: na bataker no ne finimfin otokur no, ɔtse dɛ dadze atar no tokur, wɔdze ɔtamban a wɔawen aban otokur no n’ano ehyia, amma woeennsuan.

english_verse: Along the hem of the robe were woven pomegranates of blue, purple, and red wool with a bell of pure gold between each of them. This robe was to be worn by Aaron when he performed his duties.
translation_verse: adɔmba nye pɔmegranate, adɔmba nye pɔmegranate, wɔ bataker no no mbuano no do ara hyiae, ma wɔdze aasom; dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛɛ Moses no.

english_verse: Everything that Aaron and his sons wore was made of fine linen woven with blue, purple, and red wool, including their robes and turbans, their fancy caps and underwear, and even their sashes that were embroidered with needlework.
translation_verse: na abɔdo no a wɔdze ndwera fɛɛfɛw a woefira, nye bibir, nye ɔberdum, nye serdaa yɛe, nkyermuwemfo edwinsa a; dɛ mbrɛ Ewuradze hyɛɛ Moses no.

english_verse: the sacred chest with its carrying poles and the place of mercy;
translation_verse: dasedaka no, nye no mpoma no, nye mbɔbɔrhu egua no;

english_verse: So the people of Israel finished making everything the Lord had told Moses to make.
translation_verse: Ehyiadzibuw no ntamadan no ho edwuma nyinara mbrɛ wosii wiei nye yi: na Israelmba no yɛɛ dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛɛ Moses no nyinara.

english_verse: Then they brought it all to Moses: the sacred tent and its equipment, including the hooks, the framework and crossbars, and its posts and stands;
translation_verse: Na wɔdze ntamadan no brɛɛ Moses, Buw no, nye no ho ndzɛmba nyinara, no nkɔtɔkorba, ne ntaabow, ne mbeabeamu, nye n’edum, nye ne ngyinangyinado;

english_verse: the covering of tanned ram skins and fine leather; the inside curtain;
translation_verse: na nguannyin nwoma a wɔahyɛ no memen nkatado, nye nsukraman nwoma nkatado, nye ntwamutam nsensɛnho no;

english_verse: the table with all that goes on it, including the sacred bread;
translation_verse: pon no, no ho ndzɛmba nyinara, nye kyerɛ edziban no;

english_verse: the lampstand of pure gold, together with its equipment and oil;
translation_verse: kandzeadua krɔnkrɔn no, ne nkandzea, mekyerɛ nkandzea a wobesiesie esisi do, nye no ho ndzɛmba nyinara, nye kandzea no ngo;

english_verse: the gold-covered incense altar; the ordination oil and the sweet-smelling incense; the curtain for the entrance to the tent;
translation_verse: na sika afɔrmbukyia no, nye ɔserango no, nye eduhuam hyewee huanhuam no, nye Buw no n’abowano nsensɛnho no;

english_verse: the bronze altar for sacrifices with its bronze grating, its carrying poles, and its equipment; the large bronze bowl with its stand;
translation_verse: boran afɔrmbukyia no, nye no boran serɛnkye, no mpoma, nye no ho ndzɛmba nyinara, eguarsɛn no nye ne ntaease;

english_verse: the curtain with its posts and cords, and its pegs and stands that go around the courtyard; everything needed for the sacred tent;
translation_verse: dampan no ne nsensɛnho no, n’edum, nye ne ngyinangyinado, nye dampan no n’abotsenano nsensɛnho, n’ahoma, nye no mbɔfondua, nye ntamadan no no som ho ndzɛmba nyinara, dze aama ehyiadzibuw no;

english_verse: The Lord said to Moses:
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE kasaa kyerɛɛ Moses dɛ,

english_verse: Set up my tent on the first day of the year
translation_verse: Bosoom a odzi kan no ne da a odzi kan no si ehyiadzibuw no ne ntamadan no.

english_verse: and put the chest with the Ten Commandments behind the inside curtain.
translation_verse: Na fa ahyɛmudaka no si mu, na fa ntwamutam no kata adaka no enyim.

english_verse: Bring in the table and set on it those things that are made for it. Also bring in the lampstand and attach the lamps to it.
translation_verse: Na fa ɔpon no kɔ mu, na siesie ndzɛmba a wɔdze sisi do no; na fa kandzeadua no kɔ mu, na sosɔ ne nkandzea no.

english_verse: Then place the gold altar of incense in front of the sacred chest and hang a curtain at the entrance to the tent.
translation_verse: Na fa sika eduhuamhyew afɔrmbukyia no kesi ahyɛmudaka no enyim, na fa ntamadan no n’abowano nsensɛnho no sɛn.

english_verse: Set the altar for burning sacrifices in front of the entrance to my tent.
translation_verse: Na fa ɔhyew afɔr afɔrmbukyia no si ehyiadzibuw no ne ntamadan no n’abow enyim.

english_verse: Put the large bronze bowl between the tent and the altar and fill the bowl with water.
translation_verse: Na fa eguarsɛn no si ehyiadzibuw no nye afɔrmbukyia no ntamu, na fa nsu gu mu.

english_verse: Surround the tent and the altar with the wall of curtains and hang the curtain that was made for the entrance.
translation_verse: Na sisi dampan no hyia, na fa dampan no n’abotsenano nsensɛnho no sɛn.

english_verse: Use the sacred olive oil to dedicate to me the tent and everything in it.
translation_verse: Na fa ɔserango no, na fa sera ntamadan no, nye no mu adze nyinara, na yɛ no krɔnkrɔn, ɔnye no ho ndzɛmba nyinara; na ɔnyɛ krɔnkrɔn.

english_verse: Do the same thing with the altar for offering sacrifices and its equipment
translation_verse: Na sera ɔhyew afɔr afɔrmbukyia no, nye no ho ndzɛmba nyinara ngo, na yɛ afɔrmbukyia no krɔnkrɔn: na afɔrmbukyia no nyɛ krɔnkrɔndze mu adzekrɔnkrɔn.

english_verse: and with the bowl and its stand.
translation_verse: Na sera eguarsɛn no nye ne ntaease no ngo, na yɛ no krɔnkrɔn.

english_verse: Bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the tent and tell them to wash themselves.
translation_verse: Na fa Aaron nye ne mbabanyin no bra ehyiadzibuw no n’abowano, na fa nsu hohor hɔn ho.

english_verse: Dress Aaron in the priestly clothes, then use the sacred olive oil to ordain him and dedicate him to me as my priest.
translation_verse: Na fa ntar krɔnkrɔn hyɛ Aaron; na sera no ngo, na yɛ no krɔnkrɔn ma ondzi mo sɔfoe.

english_verse: Put the priestly robes on Aaron's sons
translation_verse: Na fa ne mbabanyin no bra, na hyehyɛ hɔn sorntar:

english_verse: and ordain them in the same way, so they and their descendants will always be my priests.
translation_verse: na sera hɔn ngo dɛ mbrɛ eseraa hɔn egya ngo no, ma wondzi m’asɔfoe: na hɔn sera no nyɛ hɔn daadaa asɔfodzi nkɔ hɔn ebusuasantsen nyina mu.

english_verse: And on the first day of the first month of the second year, the sacred tent was set up.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ afe a otsia ebien no mu bosoom a odzi kan no mu, da a odzi bosoom no ne kan no, na wosii ntamadan no.

english_verse: The stones with the Ten Commandments written on them were stored in the sacred chest, the place of mercy was put on top of it, and the carrying poles were attached.
translation_verse: Na ɔfaa dase no dze hyɛɛ adaka no, na ɔdze mpoma no hyehyɛɛ adaka no ho, na ɔdze mbɔbɔrhu egua no sii adaka no do ɔsor;

english_verse: The chest was brought into the tent and set behind the curtain in the most holy place. These things were done exactly as the Lord had commanded Moses.
translation_verse: na ɔdze adaka no kɔr ntamadan no mu, na ɔdze nsensɛnho ntwamutam no sensɛnee, na ɔdze kataa ahyɛmudaka no enyim; dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛɛ Moses no.

english_verse: The table for the sacred bread was put along the north wall of the holy place,
translation_verse: Na ɔdze ɔpon no sii ehyiadzibuw no mu, wɔ ntamadan no nkyɛn mu kwaamu afamu, wɔ ntwamutam no ekyir.

english_verse: after which the bread was set on the table.
translation_verse: Na osiesiee edziban no wɔ do wɔ EWURADZE enyim; dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛɛ Moses no.

english_verse: then the lamps were attached to it there in the presence of the Lord.
translation_verse: Na ɔsosɔɔ nkandzea no wɔ EWURADZE enyim; dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛɛ Moses no.

english_verse: The gold incense altar was set up in front of the curtain,
translation_verse: Na ɔdze sika afɔrmbukyia no sii ehyiadzibuw no mu wɔ ntwamutam no enyim:

english_verse: and sweet-smelling incense was burned on it. These things were done exactly as the Lord had commanded Moses.
translation_verse: na ɔhyeew nduhuam wɔ do; dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛɛ Moses no.
