english_verse: The curtain was hung at the entrance to the sacred tent.
translation_verse: Na ɔdze nsensɛnho no sɛɛn ntamadan no n’abow no ano.

english_verse: Then the altar for offering sacrifices was put in front of the tent, and animal sacrifices and gifts of grain were offered there.
translation_verse: Na ɔdze ɔhyew afɔr afɔrmbukyia no sii ehyiadzibuw ntamadan no n’abow no ano, na ɔbɔɔ do ɔhyew afɔr nye edziban afɔr; dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛɛ Moses no.

english_verse: The large bronze bowl was placed between the altar and the entrance to the tent. It was filled with water,
translation_verse: Na ɔdze eguarsɛn no sii ehyiadzibuw no nye afɔrmbukyia no ntamu, na ɔdze nsu guu mu dɛ wɔmfa nhohor hɔnho.

english_verse: then Moses and Aaron, together with Aaron's sons, washed their hands and feet.
translation_verse: Na Moses nye Aaron nye ne mbabanyin no hohoor hɔn nsa ho nye hɔn anan ho wɔ hɔ;

english_verse: In fact, they washed each time before entering the tent or offering sacrifices at the altar. These things were done exactly as the Lord had commanded Moses.
translation_verse: aber a wɔrokɔ ehyiadzibuw no mu, na aber a wɔrebɛbɛn afɔrmbukyia no, wɔhohoor hɔnho: dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛɛ Moses no.

english_verse: Finally, Moses had the curtains hung around the courtyard and at the entrance. The Glory of the Lord
translation_verse: Na osii dampan no dze twaa ntamadan no nye afɔrmbukyia no ho hyiae, na ɔdze dampan no n’abotsenano nsensɛnho no sɛnee. Nna Moses wiee edwuma no yɛ.

english_verse: Suddenly the sacred tent was covered by a thick cloud and filled with the glory of the Lord.
translation_verse: Nna mununkum no bɛkataa ehyiadzibuw no do, na EWURADZE enyimnyam bɛhyɛɛ ntamadan no ma.

english_verse: Whenever the cloud moved from the tent, the people would break camp and follow;
translation_verse: Na aber a wɔama mununkum no do wɔ ntamadan no do no, nna Israelmba etu kɔ kan, wɔ hɔn akwantutu no nyina mu;

english_verse: then they would set up camp and stay there, until it moved again.
translation_verse: na sɛ mununkum no wɔamma do a, ɔnodze wonntu kwan araa kodur da a wɔama do no.

english_verse: No matter where the people traveled, the Lord was with them. Each day his cloud was over the tent, and each night a fire could be seen in the cloud.
translation_verse: Osiandɛ EWURADZE no mununkum no wɔ ntamadan no do adzekyee, na ogya wɔ mu adzesaa, wɔ Israel fi no nyinara enyim, wɔ hɔn akwantutu no nyina mu.

english_verse: Lay your hand on its head, and I will accept the animal as a sacrifice for taking away your sins.
translation_verse: Na ɔmfa ne nsa nto ɔhyew afɔr no n’etsifi; na ɔbɔsɔ enyi dɛ mpata ama no.

english_verse: After the bull is killed in my presence, some priests from Aaron's family will offer its blood to me by splattering it against the four sides of the altar.
translation_verse: Na onku nantwisaa no wɔ EWURADZE enyim: na Aaron mbabanyin, asɔfo no, mfa bɔgya no mbɛkyerɛ, na wɔmfa bɔgya no mpetse afɔrmbukyia no a ɔwɔ ehyiadzibuw no n’abowano no do nhyia.

english_verse: Skin the bull and cut it up,
translation_verse: Na ɔnyer abowa no, na ontwitwa no esinesin.

english_verse: while the priests pile wood on the altar fire to make it start blazing.
translation_verse: Na ɔsɔfo Aaron ne mbabanyin no mfa nyena ngu afɔrmbukyia no do, na wɔnsɔ gya wɔ do.

english_verse: Wash the bull's insides and hind legs, so the priests can lay them on the altar with the head, the fat, and the rest of the animal. A priest will then send all of it up in smoke with a smell that pleases me.
translation_verse: n’ayamudze nye ne ntwɛr dze ɔmfa nsu nhohor ho ana: na ɔsɔfo no nhyew ne nyinara wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do, ma ɔnyɛ ɔhyew afɔr, mekyerɛ ogya afɔr a oyi nka huanhuam ma EWURADZE.

english_verse: If you sacrifice a ram or a goat, it must not have anything wrong with it.
translation_verse: Na sɛ n’ayɛyɛdze fi nguankuw, nkɔsɔw anaa mpɔnkye mu a, ɔmfa onyin a onndzii dzɛm mbra.

english_verse: Lead the animal to the north side of the altar, where it is to be killed in my presence. Then some of the priests will splatter its blood against the four sides of the altar.
translation_verse: Na onku no wɔ afɔrmbukyia no ne nkyɛn mu kwaamu afamu wɔ EWURADZE enyim: na Aaron mbabanyin, asɔfo no, mfa no bɔgyaa mpetse afɔrmbukyia no do nhyia.

english_verse: Cut up the animal and wash its insides and hind legs. A priest will put these parts on the altar with the head, the fat, and the rest of the animal. Then he will send all of it up in smoke with a smell that pleases me.
translation_verse: ayamudze nye ntwɛr no dze ɔmfa nsu nhohor ho ana: na ɔsɔfo no mfa ne nyinara mbra, na ɔmbɛhyew no wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do: ɔyɛ ɔhyew afɔr, mekyerɛ ogya afɔr a oyi nka huanhuam ma EWURADZE.

english_verse: If you offer a bird for this kind of sacrifice, it must be a dove or a pigeon.
translation_verse: Na sɛ n’ayɛyɛdze fi nnoma mu na ɔdze robɔbɔ EWURADZE ɔhyew afɔr a, ɔno ɔmfa ntamram, anaa mborɔnomamba bi nyɛ n’ayɛyɛdze mbra.

english_verse: A priest will take the bird to the bronze altar, where he will wring its neck and put its head on the fire. Then he will drain out its blood on one side of the altar,
translation_verse: Na ɔsɔfo no mfa mbra afɔrmbukyia no ho, na ɔntsew ne tsir, na ɔnhyew no wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do; na no bɔgyaa no nkyi ngu afɔrmbukyia no ho:

english_verse: remove the bird's craw with what is in it, and throw them on the ash heap at the east side of the altar.
translation_verse: na onyi no totuaa nka no mu ifi no ho, na ɔntow nkyen afɔrmbukyia no nkyɛn wɔ boka afamu, wɔ mbrɛ nso no gu no:

english_verse: Finally, he will take the bird by its wings, tear it partially open, and send it up in smoke with a smell that pleases me.
translation_verse: na onsuan mu wɔ n’atsaban ho, na mbom mma ɔnntsew mu: na ɔsɔfo no nhyew no wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do, wɔ nyena no a ɔwɔ gya no do no do: ɔyɛ ɔhyew afɔr, mekyerɛ ogya afɔr a oyi nka huanhuam ma EWURADZE.

english_verse: When you offer sacrifices to give thanks to me, you must use only your finest flour. Put it in a dish, sprinkle olive oil and incense on the flour,
translation_verse: Na sɛ obiara dze edziban afɔr ayɛyɛdze rebrɛ EWURADZE a, n’ayɛyɛdze no nyɛ isiam pa; na onhue ngo ngu do, na ɔmfa krɔbɔw nto do:

english_verse: and take it to the priests from Aaron's family. One of them will scoop up the incense together with a handful of the flour and oil. Then, to show that the whole offering belongs to me, the priest will lay this part on the bronze altar and send it up in smoke with a smell that pleases me.
translation_verse: na ɔmfa mbrɛ Aaron mbabanyin asɔfo no: na ɔmfa isiam pa, nye no ngo no ne nsapuw, ɔnye no krɔbɔw no nyinara: na ɔsɔfo no nhyew no dɛ nkaadze wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do, ogya afɔr a oyi nka huanhuam ma EWURADZE:

english_verse: The rest of this sacrifice is for the priests; it is very holy because it was offered to me.
translation_verse: na edziban afɔr no mu dza ɔbɛka no nyɛ Aaron nye ne mbabanyin dze: ɔyɛ EWURADZE ogya afɔr mu krɔnkrɔndze mu adzekrɔnkrɔn.

english_verse: If you bake bread in an oven for this sacrifice, use only your finest flour, but without any yeast. You may make the flour into a loaf mixed with olive oil, or you may make it into thin wafers and brush them with oil.
translation_verse: Na sɛ edze edziban afɔr ayɛyɛdze a wɔto no wɔ fononoo mu bi rebɛba a, fa isiam pa epitsi tatar a wɔafrɔw no ngo, anaa epitsi tatar ntrɛtraba a wɔasera ho ngo.

english_verse: If you cook bread in a shallow pan for this sacrifice, use only your finest flour. Mix it with olive oil, but do not use any yeast.
translation_verse: Na sɛ w’ayɛyɛdze no yɛ ɔtosɛn mu edziban afɔr a, ma ɔnyɛ isiam pa epitsi a wɔafɔw no ngo.

english_verse: Then break the bread into small pieces and sprinkle them with oil.
translation_verse: Bubu mu esinesin, na hue ngo gu do: ɔyɛ edziban afɔr.

english_verse: If you cook your bread in a pan with a lid on it, you must also use the finest flour mixed with oil.
translation_verse: Na sɛ w’ayɛyɛdze no yɛ ɔkyesɛn mu edziban afɔr a, fa isiam pa nye ngo yɛ.

english_verse: You may prepare sacrifices to give thanks in any of these three ways. Bring your sacrifice to a priest, and he will take it to the bronze altar.
translation_verse: Na fa edziban afɔr a wɔdze ndzɛmba yinom ayɛ no brɛ EWURADZE: wɔmfa mma ɔsɔfo no, ma ɔmfa mbra afɔrmbukyia no ho.

english_verse: Then, to show that the whole offering belongs to me, the priest will lay part of it on the altar and send it up in smoke with a smell that pleases me.
translation_verse: Na ɔsɔfo no nyi edziban afɔr no do nkaadze no, na ɔnhyew no wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do: ɔyɛ ogya afɔr a oyi nka huanhuam ma EWURADZE.

english_verse: The rest of this sacrifice is for the priests; it is very holy because it was offered to me.
translation_verse: Na edziban afɔr no mu dza ɔbɛka no nyɛ Aaron nye ne mbabanyin dze: ɔyɛ EWURADZE ogya afɔr mu krɔnkrɔndze mu adzekrɔnkrɔn.

english_verse: Yeast and honey must never be burned on the altar, so don't ever mix either of these in a grain sacrifice.
translation_verse: Edziban afɔr biara a hom dze bɛbrɛ EWURADZE no, mma wɔmmfa mbɔrkaw nnyɛ: na mma hom nnhyew mbɔrkaw, anaa ewo biara, dɛ ogya afɔr mma EWURADZE.

english_verse: You may offer either of them separately, when you present the first part of your harvest to me, but they must never be burned on the altar.
translation_verse: Hom mfa iyinom mbrɛ EWURADZE, dɛ nduadzewakan ayɛyɛdze: na afɔrmbukyia no do dze, na mma bi mmba mmbeyi nka huanhuam.

english_verse: Salt is offered when you make an agreement with me, so sprinkle salt on these sacrifices.
translation_verse: Na w’edziban afɔr ayɛyɛdze biara fa nkyen to mu; nkyii so mma wo Nyankopɔn ahyɛmu nkyen no mmpa w’edziban afɔr mu: w’ayɛyɛdze nyinara mu fa nkyen ka ho bra.

english_verse: Freshly cut grain, either roasted or coarsely ground, must be used when you offer the first part of your grain harvest.
translation_verse: Na sɛ edze nduadzewakan ayɛyɛdze rebrɛ EWURADZE a, fa eburow mbɛtsem a wɔatoto no ogya mu, eburow mbɛtsem nomanoma a wɔapose, yɛ wo nduadzewakan mu ayɛyɛdze bra.

english_verse: You must mix in some olive oil and put incense on top, because this is a grain sacrifice.
translation_verse: Na fa ngo gu do, na fa krɔbɔw to do: ɔyɛ edziban afɔr.

english_verse: A priest will sprinkle all of the incense and some of the grain and oil on the altar and send them up in smoke to show that the whole offering belongs to me.
translation_verse: Na ɔsɔfo no nhyew ne nkaadze no, n’eburow mposee no bi, nye no ngo no bi, nye no krɔbɔw no nyinara; ɔyɛ ogya afɔr ma EWURADZE.

english_verse: When you offer sacrifices to ask my blessing, you may offer either a bull or a cow, but there must be nothing wrong with the animal.
translation_verse: Na sɛ n’ayɛyɛdze yɛ asomdwee afɔr afɔrbɔdze; sɛ ofi anantwi mu, dɛ onyin o, ɔber o, na ɔdze reba a, dza onndzii dzɛm na ɔmfa no mbra EWURADZE enyim.

english_verse: Lead it to the entrance of the sacred tent, lay your hand on its head, and have it killed there. A priest from Aaron's family will splatter its blood against the four sides of the altar.
translation_verse: Na ɔmfa ne nsa nto n’ayɛyɛdze no etsifi, na onku no wɔ ehyiadzibuw no n’abowano: na Aaron mbabanyin asɔfo no mfa bɔgya no mpetse afɔrmbukyia no do nhyia.

english_verse: as well as the lower part of the liver and the two kidneys with their fat.
translation_verse: nye nsɛbɛ ebien no, na seradze a ɔwɔ ho no, dza ɔwɔ sisiw mu, nye berɛbo do ntentan no mfi hɔ.

english_verse: Some of the priests will lay these pieces on the altar and send them up in smoke with a smell that pleases me, together with the sacrifice that is offered to please me.
translation_verse: Na Aaron mbabanyin no nhyew no wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do ɔhyew afɔr no a ɔwɔ nyena no a ɔwɔ ogya no do no do: ɔyɛ ogya afɔr a oyi nka huanhuam ma EWURADZE.

english_verse: Instead of a bull or a cow, you may offer any sheep or goat that has nothing wrong with it.
translation_verse: Na sɛ n’ayɛyɛdze a ɔdze rebɛyɛ asomdwee afɔr afɔrbɔdze ama EWURADZE no fi nguan mu a; onyin o, ɔber o, dza onndzii dzɛm na ɔmfa no mbra.

english_verse: If you offer a sheep, you must present it to me at the entrance to the sacred tent.
translation_verse: Sɛ ɔdze eguambaa rebɛyɛ n’ayɛyɛdze a, ɔno ɔmfa no mbra EWURADZE enyim:

english_verse: Lay your hand on its head and have it killed there. A priest will then splatter its blood against the four sides of the altar.
translation_verse: na ɔmfa ne nsa nto n’ayɛyɛdze no etsifi, na onku no wɔ ehyiadzibuw no enyim: na Aaron mbabanyin no mfa no bɔgyaa mpetse afɔrmbukyia no do nhyia.

english_verse: as well as the lower part of the liver and the two kidneys with their fat.
translation_verse: nye nsɛbɛ ebien no, nye no do seradze no a ɔwɔ sisiw mu no, nye berɛbo do ntentan no mfi hɔ.

english_verse: One of the priests will lay these pieces on the altar and send them up in smoke as a food offering for me.
translation_verse: Na ɔsɔfo no nhyew no wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do: ɔyɛ ogya afɔr edziban ma EWURADZE.

english_verse: If you offer a goat, you must also present it to me
translation_verse: Na sɛ n’ayɛyɛdze yɛ apɔnkye a, ɔno ɔmfa no mbra EWURADZE enyim:

english_verse: at the entrance to the sacred tent. Lay your hand on its head and have it killed there. A priest will then splatter its blood against the four sides of the altar.
translation_verse: na ɔmfa ne nsa nto n’etsifi, na onku no wɔ ehyiadzibuw no enyim; na Aaron ne mbabanyin no mfa no bɔgyaa mpetse afɔrmbukyia no do nhyia.

english_verse: as well as the lower part of the liver and the two kidneys with their fat.
translation_verse: nye nsɛbɛ ebien no, nye no do seradze no a ɔwɔ sisiw mu no, nye berɛbo do ntentan no dze, onyi mfi hɔ.

english_verse: One of the priests will put these pieces on the altar and send them up in smoke as a food offering with a smell that pleases me. All fat belongs to me.
translation_verse: Na ɔsɔfo no nhyew no wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do: ɔyɛ ogya afɔr edziban a oyi nka huanhuam: seradze no nyinara yɛ EWURADZE dze.

english_verse: So you and your descendants must never eat any fat or any blood, not even in the privacy of your own homes. This law will never change.
translation_verse: Ɔnyɛ daa ahyɛdze wɔ hom ebusuasantsen mu wɔ hom tsenabea nyinara mu, dɛ hom nndzi seradze anaa bɔgya biara.

english_verse: dip a finger in the blood, and sprinkle some of it seven times toward the sacred chest behind the curtain.
translation_verse: na ɔsɔfo no mfa ne nsatseaba mbɔ bɔgya no mu, na ɔmfa bɔgya no bi mpetse mpɛn esuon wɔ EWURADZE enyim, wɔ krɔnkrɔnbea no no ntwamutam no enyim.

english_verse: This is a sacrifice for sin. So he will lay his hand on the animal's head, before having it killed in my presence at the north side of the bronze altar.
translation_verse: na ɔmfa ne nsa nto apɔnkye no n’etsifi, na onku no wɔ beebi mbrɛ woku ɔhyew afɔr no wɔ EWURADZE enyim no. Iyi yɛ bɔn ho afɔr.

english_verse: Then a priest will dip a finger in the blood; he will smear some of it on each of the four corners of the altar and pour out the rest at the foot of the altar.
translation_verse: Na ɔsɔfo no mfa ne nsatseaba mfa bɔgya no bi, na ɔmfa nkeka ɔhyew afɔr afɔrmbukyia no ne mmbɛn ho, na onhue no bɔgyaa no nyinara ngu afɔrmbukyia no ase.

english_verse: The Lord told Moses
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE kasaa kyerɛɛ Moses dɛ,

english_verse: to say to the community of Israel: Offer a sacrifice to ask forgiveness when you sin by accidentally doing something I have told you not to do. When the High Priest Sins The Lord said:
translation_verse: Kasa kyerɛ Israelmba, dɛ, Sɛ nyimpa biara yɛ bɔn a ɔnndze enyidaho wɔ ndzɛmba biara a EWURADZE ahyɛ ho mbra dɛ mma wɔnnyɛ no mu, na ɔkɛyɛ no mu kor biara a, dza wɔnyɛ nye iyi:

english_verse: When the high priest sins, he makes everyone else guilty too. And so, he must sacrifice a young bull that has nothing wrong with it.
translation_verse: sɛ ɔsɔfo a wɔasera no no kɛyɛ bɔn ma ɔdze asodzi ba ɔman no do a: ɔno no bɔn a ɔayɛ no ntsi, ma ɔmfa ɔnantwisaa eburwa a onndzii dzɛm mbrɛ EWURADZE mfa nyɛ bɔn ho afɔr.

english_verse: The priest will lead the bull to the entrance of the sacred tent, lay his hand on its head, and kill it there.
translation_verse: Na ɔmfa nantwisaa no mbra ehyiadzibuw no n’abowano wɔ EWURADZE enyim; na ɔmfa ne nsa nto nantwisaa no n’etsifi, na onku no wɔ EWURADZE enyim.

english_verse: He will take a bowl of the blood inside the tent,
translation_verse: Na ɔsɔfo a wɔasera no no mfa nantwisaa no no bɔgyaa no bi, na ɔmfa mbra ehyiadzibuw no mu:

english_verse: Then, in my presence, he will smear some of the blood on each of the four corners of the incense altar, before pouring out the rest at the foot of the bronze altar near the entrance to the tent.
translation_verse: Na ɔsɔfo no mfa bɔgya no bi nkeka eduhuamhyew afɔrmbukyia no a ɔwɔ EWURADZE enyim no a ɔwɔ ehyiadzibuw no mu no ne mbɛn no ho; na onhue nantwisaa no no bɔgyaa nyinara ngu ɔhyew afɔr afɔrmbukyia no a ɔwɔ ehyiadzibuw no n’abowano no ase.

english_verse: The skin and flesh of the bull, together with its legs, insides, and the food still in its stomach, are to be taken outside the camp and burned on a wood fire near the ash heap. When the Whole Nation Sins The Lord said:
translation_verse: mekyerɛ ɔnantwisaa mu no nyinara na ɔnsoa nkɔ nseraban no ekyir beebi a hɔ tsew, mbrɛ wohue nso gu no, na ɔmfa ogya nhyew no nyena do. Beebi a wohue nso gu no ara na wɔnhyew no. Nyimpakuw No Hɔn Bɔn

english_verse: When the nation of Israel disobeys me without meaning to, the whole nation is still guilty.
translation_verse: Na sɛ Israel mboanofo nyinara fom, a wɔammfa enyidaho annyɛ, na wɔyɛ ndzɛmba a EWURADZE ahyɛ ho mbra dɛ mma wɔnnyɛ no mu bi, na wodzi so;

english_verse: Once you realize what has happened, you must sacrifice a young bull to ask my forgiveness. Lead the bull to the entrance of the sacred tent,
translation_verse: na ekyir yi wohu bɔn a wɔafom wɔ mu no a, ɔno bagua no mfa nantwisaa eburwa mbra mbɛyɛ bɔn ho afɔr, na wɔmfa no mbra ehyiadzibuw no enyim.

english_verse: where your tribal leaders will lay their hands on its head, before having it killed in my presence.
translation_verse: Na mboanofo no mu mpanyimfo no mfa hɔn nsa ngu nantwisaa no n’etsifi na wonku nantwisaa no wɔ EWURADZE enyim.

english_verse: The priest will take a bowl of the animal's blood inside the sacred tent,
translation_verse: Na ɔsɔfo a wɔasera no no mfa nantwisaa no no bɔgyaa no bi mbra ehyiadzibuw no mu.

english_verse: dip a finger in the blood, and sprinkle some of it seven times toward the sacred chest behind the curtain.
translation_verse: Na ɔsɔfo no mfa ne nsatseaba mbɔ bɔgya no mu, na ɔmfa mpetse mpɛn esuon wɔ EWURADZE enyim wɔ ntwamutam no enyim.

english_verse: Then, in my presence, he must smear some of the blood on each of the four corners of the incense altar, before pouring out the rest at the foot of the bronze altar near the entrance to the tent.
translation_verse: Na ɔmfa bɔgya no bi nkeka afɔrmbukyia no a ɔwɔ EWURADZE enyim no a ɔwɔ ehyiadzibuw no mu no ne mbɛn ho, na onhue bɔgya no nyinara ngu ɔhyew afɔr afɔrmbukyia no a ɔwɔ ehyiadzibuw no n’abowano ho ase.

english_verse: After this, the priest will remove the fat from the bull and send it up in smoke on the altar. Finally, he will burn its remains outside the camp, just as he did with the other bull. By this sacrifice the sin of the whole nation will be forgiven. When a Tribal Leader Sins The Lord God said:
translation_verse: Na ɔmfa nantwisaa no mfir edzi nkɔ nseraban no ekyir, na ɔnhyew no dɛ mbrɛ ɔhyeew asɔpɔn nantwisaa no. Iyi yɛ bagua no hɔn bɔn ho afɔr. Ɔman No Mu Akandzifo No Hɔn Bɔn

english_verse: As soon as the leader realizes what has happened, he must sacrifice a goat that has nothing wrong with it.
translation_verse: na sɛ wɔma ohu no bɔn a ɔafom wɔ mu no a, ɔmfa n’ayɛyɛdze a ɔyɛ apɔnkye mbra, onyin a onndzii dzɛm;

english_verse: The priest will dip a finger in the blood, smear some of it on each of the four corners of the altar, and pour out the rest at the foot of the altar.
translation_verse: Na ɔsɔfo no mfa ne nsatseaba mfa bɔn ho afɔr bɔgyaa no bi, na ɔmfa nkeka ɔhyew afɔr afɔrmbukyia no ne mmbɛn ho, na onhue no bɔgyaa no ngu ɔhyew afɔr afɔrmbukyia no ase.

english_verse: Then he must send all of the fat up in smoke, just as he does when a sacrifice is offered to ask my blessing. By this sacrifice the leader's sin will be forgiven. When Ordinary People Sin The Lord said:
translation_verse: Na ɔnhyew ne seradze no nyinara wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do, dɛ asomdwee afɔr ayɛyɛdze seradze no: na ɔsɔfo no mpata mma no wɔ no bɔn no ho, na wɔdze bɛkyɛ no. Ankorankor No Hɔn Bɔn

english_verse: When any of you ordinary people disobey me without meaning to, you are still guilty.
translation_verse: Na sɛ ɔman mba no bi ammfa enyidaho na ɔyɛ bɔn wɔ ndzɛmba nyinara a EWURADZE ahyɛ ho mbra dɛ mma wɔnnyɛ no mu biara a, odzi so.

english_verse: As soon as you realize what you have done, you must sacrifice a female goat that has nothing wrong with it.
translation_verse: Sɛ wɔma ohu no bɔn a ɔafom no a ɔno ɔmfa n’ayɛyɛdze, apɔnkyeber a onndzii dzɛm, mbra mbɛma, wɔ no bɔn a ɔayɛ no ntsi.

english_verse: Lead the goat to the north side of the bronze altar and lay your hand on its head, before having it killed.
translation_verse: Na ɔmfa ne nsa nto bɔn ho afɔr no n’etsifi, na onku bɔn ho afɔr no wɔ ɔhyew afɔr bea no do.

english_verse: After this, the priest will remove all of the fat, just as he does when an animal is sacrificed to ask my blessing. The priest will then send the fat up in smoke with a smell that pleases me. This animal is sacrificed so that I will forgive you ordinary people when you sin.
translation_verse: Na onyi ne seradze no nyinara, dɛ mbrɛ woyi asomdwee afɔr seradze no fi ho no; na ɔsɔfo no nhyew no wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do ma onyi nka huanhuam mma EWURADZE; na ɔsɔfo no mpata mma no, na wɔdze bɛkyɛ no.

english_verse: If you offer a lamb instead of a goat as a sacrifice for sin, it must be a female that has nothing wrong with it.
translation_verse: Na sɛ ɔdze eguambaa ba dɛ n’ayɛyɛdze wɔ bɔn ho afɔr ntsi a, ɔmfa ɔber a onndzii dzɛm mbra.

english_verse: Lead the lamb to the altar and lay your hand on its head, before having it killed.
translation_verse: Na ɔmfa ne nsa nto bɔn no afɔr abowa no n’etsifi, na onku no bɔn ho afɔr wɔ beebi mbrɛ woku ɔhyew afɔr no.

english_verse: The priest will dip a finger in the blood, smear some of it on each of the four corners of the altar, and pour out the rest at the foot of the altar.
translation_verse: Na ɔsɔfo no mfa ne nsatseaba mfa bɔn ho afɔr bɔgyaa no bi, na ɔmfa nkeka ɔhyew afɔr afɔrmbukyia no ne mbɛn ho, na onhue no bɔgyaa no nyinara ngu afɔrmbukyia no ase:

english_verse: After this, all of the fat must be removed, just as when an animal is sacrificed to ask my blessing. Then the priest will send it up in smoke to me, together with a food offering, and your sin will be forgiven.
translation_verse: na onyi ne seradze no nyinara, dɛ mbrɛ woyi asomdwee afɔr eguambaa seradze no fi ho no; na ɔsɔfo no nhyew no wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do, wɔ EWURADZE ogya afɔr no do: na ɔsɔfo no mpata mma no wɔ no bɔn a ɔayɛ no ho, na wɔdze bɛkyɛ no.

english_verse: You are guilty if you find out that you have accidentally touched anything unclean that comes from a human body.
translation_verse: anaa sɛ ɔka nyimpa bi ne fi, wɔ adze fi biara a nyimpa dze gu no ho fi no mu, ber a onnyim mpo na sɛ ekyir yi ohu a, ɔno odzi so:

english_verse: You are guilty the moment you realize that you have made a hasty promise to do something good or bad.
translation_verse: anaa sɛ obi ka ntanhun dɛ ɔbɛyɛ bɔn, anaa ɔbɛyɛ papa, wɔ asɛnhun biara mu, na n’adwen annkɔ do na sɛ ekyir yi n’enyi ba noho do a, ɔno odzi ndzɛmba iyinom mu biara ho so:

english_verse: As soon as you discover that you have committed any of these sins, you must confess what you have done.
translation_verse: Na sɛ ɔba dɛ, odzi ndzɛmba iyinom mu biara ho so a, ɔnka dza ɔayɛ mu bɔn no:

english_verse: Then you must bring a female sheep or goat to me as the price for your sin. A priest will sacrifice the animal, and you will be forgiven.
translation_verse: na no bɔn a ɔayɛ no ntsi ɔmfa n’asodzii ho afɔr mbrɛ EWURADZE, nguan mu ɔber, eguambaa anaa apɔnkye, mfa nyɛ bɔn ho afɔr; na ɔsɔfo no mpata mma no wɔ no bɔn ho.

english_verse: Give both birds to the priest, who will offer one as a sacrifice to ask my forgiveness. He will wring its neck without tearing off its head,
translation_verse: Na ɔmfa mbrɛ ɔsɔfo no, ma ondzi kan mfa dza ɔyɛ bɔn ho afɔr no, na ɔntsew ne tsir wɔ no kɔn do, na mbom mma ɔmmpaa mu koraa:

english_verse: splatter some of its blood on one side of the bronze altar, and drain out the rest at the foot of the altar.
translation_verse: na ɔmfa bɔn ho afɔr bɔgyaa no bi mpetse afɔrmbukyia no nkyɛn; na bɔgya no ne nkaa no ɔnsɔn ngu afɔrmbukyia no ase: ɔyɛ bɔn ho afɔr.

english_verse: If you are so poor that you cannot afford doves or pigeons, you may bring one kilogram of your finest flour. This is a sacrifice to ask my forgiveness, so don't sprinkle olive oil or sweet-smelling incense on it.
translation_verse: Na sɛ ne nsa annso ntamram ebien, anaa mborɔnomamba ebien do a, ɔno ɔmfa isiam pa efa ebupɛn du mu kor nyɛ no bɔn a ɔayɛ no ho ayɛyɛdze mbra mbɛyɛ bɔn ho afɔr; mma ɔmmfa ngo nngu do, nkyii so mma ɔmmfa krɔbɔw bi nnto do: na ɔyɛ bɔn ho afɔr.

english_verse: Give the flour to a priest, who will scoop up a handful and send it up in smoke together with the other offerings. This is a reminder that all of the flour belongs to me.
translation_verse: Na ɔmfa mbrɛ ɔsɔfo no, na ɔsɔfo no mfa mu ne nsapuw dɛ ne nkaadze, na ɔnhyew no wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do, wɔ EWURADZE ogya afɔr no do; ɔyɛ bɔn ho afɔr.

english_verse: By offering this sacrifice, the priest pays the price for any of these sins you may have committed. The priest gets to keep the rest of the flour, just as he does with grain sacrifices. Sacrifices To Make Things Right (Leviticus
translation_verse: Na ɔsɔfo no mpata mma no wɔ no bɔn a ɔayɛ no wɔ ndzɛmba iyinom mu biara mu no ho, na wɔdze bɛkyɛ no: na nkaa no nyɛ ɔsɔfo no dze, dɛ edziban afɔr no. Asodzi Ho Afɔrbɔ

english_verse: The Lord told Moses what the people must do to make things right when they find out they have cheated the Lord without meaning to: If this happens, you must either sacrifice a ram that has nothing wrong with it or else pay the price of a ram with the official money used by the priests.
translation_verse: Sɛ obiara to mbra, dɛ ɔannyɛ dza ɔfata, wɔ EWURADZE ndzɛmba krɔnkrɔn no mu wɔ ber a ɔnndze enyidaho a; ɔno ɔmfa n’asodzii ho afɔr mbrɛ EWURADZE, nguan mu oguannyin a onndzii dzɛm, dɛ mbrɛ ebo a etwa wɔ dwetɛbona mu wɔ dwɛɛtɛɛ mu tse, dɛ krɔnkrɔnbea dwetɛbona nyɛ asodzi ho afɔr:

english_verse: The next morning you will dress in your priestly clothes, including your linen underwear. Then clean away the ashes left by the sacrifices and pile them beside the altar.
translation_verse: Na ɔsɔfo no nhyɛ no ndwera atar, na ɔnhyɛ no ndwera ntwontwo nkata noho; na ɔnsesaw nso no a ogya a ɔhyeew ɔhyew afɔr no gyae wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do no, na ɔmfa ngu afɔrmbukyia no nkyɛn.

english_verse: and told him what sacrifices the priests must offer on the morning and evening of the day they are ordained: It is the same as the regular morning and evening sacrifices—half a kilogram of flour
translation_verse: Iyi nye Aaron nye ne mbabanyin ayɛyɛdze a wɔmfa mbrɛ EWURADZE wɔ da a wɔasera no ngo no mu; isiam pa efa ebupɛn du mu kor nyɛ daa edziban afɔr, anapa dze, fa, na ewimbir dze, fa.

english_verse: In addition, you must either bring to the priest a ram that has nothing wrong with it or else pay him for one. The priest will then offer it as a sacrifice to make things right, and you will be forgiven for what you did wrong. Daily Sacrifices (Exodus
translation_verse: na ɔsɔfo no mpata mma no wɔ EWURADZE enyim, na wɔdze adze biara a ɔayɛ a odzi ho so no bɛkyɛ no. Ɔhyew Afɔr Ho Nhyehyɛɛ

english_verse: This sacrifice is very holy, and only the priests may have any part of it.
translation_verse: Banyin biara a ofi asɔfo ebusuakuw mu no ndzi bi: ɔyɛ krɔnkrɔndze mu adzekrɔnkrɔn.

english_verse: If the meat was cooked in a clay pot, the pot must be destroyed, but if it was cooked in a bronze pot, the pot must be scrubbed and rinsed with water.
translation_verse: Mbom dɛtsesɛn a wɔanoa no wɔ mu no wɔmbɔ no: na sɛ wɔnoa no boransɛn mu a, wontwuw mu, na wɔmfa nsu nhohor mu.

english_verse: The Lord told Moses to tell Aaron and his sons how to offer the daily sacrifices that are sent up in smoke to please the Lord: You must put the animal for the sacrifice on the altar in the evening and let it stay there all night. But make sure the fire keeps burning.
translation_verse: Hyɛ Aaron nye ne mbabanyin no dɛ, Iyi nye ɔhyew afɔr ho mbra: ɔhyew afɔr no nko mbukyia no do wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do anadwe araa nkodu anapa; na afɔrmbukyia no do ogya no nsuo mu ndzɛw.

english_verse: Change into your everyday clothes, take the ashes outside the camp, and pile them in the special place.
translation_verse: Na onyi ne ntar no, na ɔnhyɛ ntar fofor, na ɔmfa nso no mfir edzi nkɔ nseraban no ekyir nkogu beebi mbrɛ ɔtsew.

english_verse: The fire must never go out, so put wood on it each morning. After this, you are to lay an animal on the altar next to the fat that you sacrifice to ask my blessing. Then send it all up in smoke to me.
translation_verse: Na afɔrmbukyia no do ogya no nda ho ndzɛw wɔ do, mma onndum ara da; na ɔsɔfo no nhyew do nyena anapa daa: na onsiesie ɔhyew afɔr no yie wɔ do, na ɔnhyew asomdwee afɔr no seradze no wɔ do.

english_verse: The altar fire must always be kept burning—it must never go out. Sacrifices To Give Thanks to the Lord The Lord said:
translation_verse: Ogya nkɔ do ndzɛw afɔrmbukyia no do daa; mma onndum ara da. Edziban Afɔr Ho Mbra

english_verse: When someone offers a sacrifice to give thanks to me, the priests from Aaron's family must bring it to the front of the bronze altar,
translation_verse: Na iyi nye edziban afɔr ho mbra: Aaron ne mbabanyin no mfa mbra EWURADZE enyim, wɔ afɔrmbukyia no enyim.

english_verse: where one of them will scoop up a handful of the flour and oil, together with all the incense on it. Then, to show that the whole offering belongs to me, he will lay all of this on the altar and send it up in smoke with a smell that pleases me.
translation_verse: Na ɔmfa mu ne nsapuw, edziban afɔr isiam pa no bi, nye no ngo no bi, nye edziban afɔr no do krɔbɔw no nyinara, na ɔnhyew no wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do, nyi nka huanhuam, dɛ ne nkaadze, mma EWURADZE.

english_verse: The rest of it is to be baked without yeast and eaten by the priests in the sacred courtyard of the sacred tent. This bread is very holy, just like the sacrifices for sin or the sacrifices for making things right, and I have given this part to the priests from what is offered to me on the altar.
translation_verse: Mma wɔmmfa mbɔrkaw nnka ho nnto. Hɔn kyɛfa a medze ama wɔ me gya afɔr mu nye no; ɔyɛ krɔnkrɔndze mu adzekrɔnkrɔn, dɛ bɔn ho afɔr, na asodzi ho afɔr no.

english_verse: Only the men in Aaron's family are allowed to eat this bread, and they must go through a ceremony to be made holy before touching it. This law will never change. When Priests Are Ordained
translation_verse: Aaron ne mba mu banyin biara ndzi bi, dɛ EWURADZE ogya afɔr ho daa ahyɛdze, nkodu hom ebusuasantsen do: obiara a ɔka no no nyɛ krɔnkrɔn. Ngosera Ho Afɔr

english_verse: The Lord spoke to Moses
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE kasaa kyerɛɛ Moses dɛ,

english_verse: mixed with olive oil and cooked in a shallow pan. The bread must then be crumbled into small pieces and sent up in smoke with a smell that pleases me.
translation_verse: Wɔmfa ngo nka ho nyɛ no wɔ ɔtosɛn mu; sɛ ɔhon a, fa bra: yɛ no dɛ edziban afɔr a wɔato ebubu mu esinesin bra, ma onyi nka huanhuam mma EWURADZE.

english_verse: The Lord told Moses
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE kasaa kyerɛɛ Moses dɛ,

english_verse: how the priests from Aaron's family were to offer the sacrifice for sin: This sacrifice is very sacred, and the animal must be killed in my presence at the north side of the bronze altar.
translation_verse: Kasa kyerɛ Aaron nye ne mbabanyin no dɛ, Iyi nye bɔn ho afɔr abowa ho mbra: beebi mbrɛ woku ɔhyew abowa afɔr no hɔ ara na wonku bɔn ho afɔr no wɔ EWURADZE enyim: Ɔyɛ krɔnkrɔndze mu adzekrɔnkrɔn.

english_verse: The priest who offers this sacrifice must eat it in the sacred courtyard of the sacred tent,
translation_verse: Ɔsɔfo a ɔbɔ bɔn ho afɔr no ndzi: wondzi no wɔ mbrɛ ɔyɛ krɔnkrɔn wɔ ehyiadzibuw no ne pampamu.

english_verse: and anyone or anything that touches the meat will be holy. If any of the animal's blood is splattered on the clothes of the priest, they must be washed in a holy place.
translation_verse: Biribiara a ɔka ne nam no nyɛ krɔnkrɔn: na sɛ no bɔgyaa petse atar biara do a, hor dza apetse do no wɔ mbrɛ ɔyɛ krɔnkrɔn.

english_verse: None of the meat may be eaten from the sacrifices for sin that require blood to be brought into the sacred tent. These sacrifices must be completely burned.
translation_verse: Na bɔn ho afɔr biara a wɔdze no bɔgyaa bi ba ehyiadzibuw no mu bɛpata wɔ bea krɔnkrɔn no, mma wonndzi: wɔmfa ogya nhyew no.

english_verse: One of the priests will lay these pieces on the altar and send them up in smoke to me.
translation_verse: na ɔsɔfo no nhyew no wɔ afɔrmbukyia do nyɛ ogya afɔr mma EWURADZE: ɔyɛ asodzi ho afɔr.

english_verse: Give me one loaf or wafer from each of these four kinds of bread, after which they will belong to the priest who splattered the blood against the bronze altar.
translation_verse: Na ɔmfa ayɛyɛdze biara mu kor mbrɛ mbɛyɛ ɔmado afɔr mma EWURADZE; ɔsɔfo a ɔdze asomdwee afɔr bɔgyaa petse no na ɔnyɛ nedze.

english_verse: Don't eat any of the meat that has touched something unclean. Instead, burn it. The rest of the meat may be eaten by anyone who is clean and acceptable to me.
translation_verse: Na nam a aka biribiara a no ho nntsew no, mma wonndzi; wɔmfa ogya nhyew no. Na nam no dze, obiara a no ho tsew ndzi bi:

english_verse: The sacrifice to make things right is very sacred.
translation_verse: Na iyi nye asodzi ho afɔr ho mbra: ɔyɛ krɔnkrɔndze mu adzekrɔnkrɔn.

english_verse: The animal must be killed in the same place where the sacrifice to please me is killed, and the animal's blood must be splattered against the four sides of the bronze altar.
translation_verse: Beebi mbrɛ woku ɔhyew afɔr abowa no, hɔ ara so na wonku asodzi ho afɔr abowa no; na ɔmfa no bɔgyaa mpetse afɔrmbukyia no ho nhyia.

english_verse: Offer all of the animal's fat, including the fat on its tail and on its insides,
translation_verse: Na onyi ne seradze nyinara mbra, seradze dua no, nye seradze a ɔkata ayamudze no ho no,

english_verse: as well as the lower part of the liver and the two kidneys with their fat.
translation_verse: nye nsɛbɛ ebien no, nye seradze a ɔwɔ ho wɔ sisiw mu no, nye berɛbo do ntentan no, nye nsɛbɛ no, onyi mfi hɔ:

english_verse: This sacrifice for making things right is very holy. Only the priests may eat it, and they must eat it in a holy place.
translation_verse: Asɔfo ebusuakuw no mu ɔbanyin biara ndzi bi: wondzi no wɔ mbrɛ ɔyɛ krɔnkrɔn: ɔyɛ krɔnkrɔndze mu adzekrɔnkrɔn.

english_verse: The ceremony for this sacrifice and the one for sin are the same, and the meat may be eaten only by the priest who performs this ceremony of forgiveness.
translation_verse: Dɛ mbrɛ bɔn ho afɔr tse no, dɛmara na asodzi ho afɔr tse: mbra kor na ɔda ho: ɔsɔfo no a ɔdze pata no, na ɔnyɛ nedze.

english_verse: In fact, the priest who offers a sacrifice to please me may keep the skin of the animal,
translation_verse: Na ɔsɔfo a ɔbɔ nyimpa biara ne hyew afɔr no, ɔhyew afɔr a ɔabɔ no no nwoma no nyɛ ɔsɔfo no dze.

english_verse: just as he may eat the bread from a sacrifice to give thanks to me.
translation_verse: Na edziban afɔr biara a wɔto wɔ fononoo mu, nye dza wɔyɛ no wɔ kyesɛn mu, nye ɔtosɛn mu nyina, nyɛ ɔsɔfo a ɔdze ba no dze.

english_verse: All other grain sacrifices—with or without olive oil in them—are to be divided equally among the priests of Aaron's family. Sacrifices To Ask the Lord's Blessing The Lord said:
translation_verse: Na edziban afɔr biara a wɔdze ngo afrɔw, anaa wesee no, nyɛ Aaron ne mbabanyin nyinara dze, baako nya dɛ mbrɛ baako so nya. Asomdwee Afɔr

english_verse: Here are the instructions for offering a sacrifice to ask my blessing:
translation_verse: Na iyi nye asomdwee afɔr a obi bɔ ma EWURADZE no ho mbra.

english_verse: You must also make some bread with yeast.
translation_verse: Mbɔrkaw boodoo tatar na ɔmfa nka n’ayɛyɛdze nye n’asomdwee afɔr ho mbra mbɛyɛ aseda.

english_verse: When you offer an animal to ask a blessing from me or to thank me, the meat belongs to you, but it must be eaten the same day.
translation_verse: Na n’aseda asomdwee afɔr no nam no wondzi no wɔ n’ayɛyɛdze da no ara; mma onngya mu bi ma onnkodu adzekyee.

english_verse: It is different with the sacrifices you offer when you make me a promise or voluntarily give me something. The meat from those sacrifices may be kept and eaten the next day,
translation_verse: Mbom sɛ n’ayɛyɛdze yɛ anohoba, anaa ayamuyi afɔr a, wondzi no da no ara a ɔbɔ n’afɔr no: na no mu dza ɔka ma adze kye do no wondzi:

english_verse: but any that is left over must be destroyed. If you eat any of it after the second day, your sacrifice will be useless and unacceptable, and you will be both disgusting and guilty.
translation_verse: Na sɛ wodzi n’asomdwee afɔr no ne nam biara da a otsia ebiasa no a, wɔremmpen do, nkyii so worummbu mma nyia ɔdze ba no: ɔyɛ ekyiwadze, na ɔkra a obedzi bi no bɔsoa no mbusu.

english_verse: But don't eat any of this meat if you have become unclean by touching something unclean from a human or an animal or from any other creature. If you do, you will no longer belong to the community of Israel.
translation_verse: Na sɛ nyimpa biara ka biribiara a no ho nntsew nyimpa n’efifidze, anaa abowa a no ho nntsew, anaa ekyiwadze biara a no ho nntsew, na odzi asomdwee afɔr a ɔyɛ EWURADZE dze no ne nam bi no, ɔkra no wobotwa no efi ne man mu akyen. Bɔgya Na Nam Seradze Ho Ekyiwadze

english_verse: The Lord told Moses
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE kasaa kyerɛɛ Moses dɛ,

english_verse: to say to the people: Don't eat the fat of cattle, sheep, or goats.
translation_verse: Kasa kyerɛ Israelmba dɛ, Ɔnantwi, anaa ɔkɔsɔw, anaa apɔnkye seradze no, mma hom nndzi.

english_verse: If one of your animals dies or is killed by some wild animal, you may do anything with its fat except eat it.
translation_verse: Na dza noara ewu ne seradze, nye dza abowa atsetsew no ne seradze no, wɔmfa nyɛ edwuma biara: mbom dzi na mma hom nndzi bi.

english_verse: If you eat the fat of an animal that can be used as a sacrifice to me, you will no longer belong to the community of Israel.
translation_verse: Na obiara a odzi abowa a adasa dze bi bɔ ogya afɔr ma EWURADZE no ne seradze bi no, mekyerɛ ɔkra a odzi no wobotwa no efi ne man mu akyen.

english_verse: And no matter where you live, you must not eat the blood of any bird or animal,
translation_verse: Na mma hom nndzi bɔgya biara, anoma anaa aketsebowa dze, wɔ hom tsenabew biara,

english_verse: or you will no longer belong to the community of Israel.
translation_verse: Obiara a odzi bɔgya biara no, no wobotwa no efi ne man mu akyen. Asɔfo No Kyɛfa

english_verse: The Lord also told Moses
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE kasaa kyerɛɛ Moses dɛ,

english_verse: to say to the people of Israel: If you want to offer a sacrifice to ask my blessing, you must bring the part to be burned and lay it on the bronze altar. But you must first lift up the choice ribs with their fat to show that the offering is dedicated to me.
translation_verse: ɔmfa noara ne nsa mfa EWURADZE gya afɔr no mbra: seradze no nye nyan no na ɔmfa mbra, ama nyan no wɔaatow no ayɛ ɔtow afɔr wɔ EWURADZE enyim.

english_verse: A priest from Aaron's family will then send the fat up in smoke, but the ribs belong to the priests.
translation_verse: Na ɔsɔfo no nhyew seradze no wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do: na nyan no nyɛ Aaron nye ne mbabanyin dze.

english_verse: The upper joint of the right hind leg is for the priest who offers the blood and the fat of the animal.
translation_verse: Aaron ne mbabanyin mu nyia ɔdze asomdwee afɔr no bɔgyaa, nye seradze ba no, nye aserɛ nyimfa no nyɛ ne kyɛfa.

english_verse: I have decided that the people of Israel must always give the choice ribs and the upper joint of the right hind leg to Aaron's descendants
translation_verse: Na ɔtow nyan no nye ɔmado serɛ no mafa wɔ Israelmba hɔn asomdwee afɔr no mu, na medze mama ɔsɔfo Aaron nye ne mbabanyin dɛ daa ahyɛdze a ofi Israelmba hɔ.

english_verse: These are the ceremonies for sacrifices to please the Lord, to give him thanks, and to ask for his blessing or his forgiveness, as well as the ceremonies for those sacrifices that demand a payment and for the sacrifices that are offered when priests are ordained.
translation_verse: Iyi nye mbra a ɔda hɔ ma ɔhyew afɔr, edziban afɔr, nye bɔn ho afɔr, nye asodzi ho afɔr, nye etuhoakyɛ afɔr, nye asomdwee afɔr a

english_verse: While Moses and the people of Israel were in the desert at Mount Sinai, the Lord commanded them to start offering these sacrifices.
translation_verse: EWURADZE hyɛɛ Moses wɔ bepɔw Sinai do, wɔ da a ɔhyɛɛ Israelmba dɛ wɔmfa hɔn ayɛyɛdze mbrɛ EWURADZE, wɔ Sinai sar do no.

english_verse: Moses lifted up the choice ribs of the ram to show that they were dedicated to the Lord. This was the part that the Lord had said Moses could have.
translation_verse: Na Moses faa nyan no, na ɔtowee yɛɛ ɔtow afɔr wɔ EWURADZE enyim: ɔyɛ Moses ne kyɛfa wɔ etuhoakyɛ afɔr oguannyin no mu; dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛɛ Moses no ara.

english_verse: The Lord said to Moses:
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE kasaa kyerɛɛ Moses dɛ,

english_verse: Send for Aaron and his sons, as well as their priestly clothes, the oil for ordination, the bull for the sin offering, the two rams, and a basket of bread made without yeast.
translation_verse: Fa Aaron nye ne mbabanyin no ka no ho, na ntar no, nye ɔserango no, nye bɔn ho afɔr nantwisaa no, nye nguannyin ebien no, nye epitsi boodoo kɛntsɛn no,

english_verse: Then bring the whole community of Israel together at the entrance to the sacred tent.
translation_verse: na mboanofo no nyinara mbɔ ngua wɔ ehyiadzibuw no n’abowano.

english_verse: Moses obeyed the Lord, and when everyone had come together,
translation_verse: Na Moses yɛɛ dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛɛ no no; na mboanofo bɔɔ ngua wɔ ehyiadzibuw no n’abowano.

english_verse: he said, “We are here to follow the Lord's instructions.”
translation_verse: Na Moses see mboanofo no dɛ, Iyi nye adze a EWURADZE ahyɛ dɛ wɔnyɛ.

english_verse: After Moses told Aaron and his sons to step forward, he told them to wash themselves.
translation_verse: Na Moses dze Aaron nye ne mbabanyin no bae, na ɔdze nsu hohoor hɔn ho.

english_verse: He put the priestly shirt and robe on Aaron and wrapped the sash around his waist. Then he put the sacred vest on Aaron and fastened it with the finely woven belt.
translation_verse: Na ɔdze sortar no hyɛɛ no, na ɔdze abɔyamu no bɔɔ no do, na ɔdze bataker no hyɛɛ no, na ɔdze egugudo no guu no, na ɔdze egugudo ne nkyermu nkyekyermu no bɔɔ no do, na ɔdze kyekyeer no.

english_verse: Next, he put on Aaron the sacred breastpiece that was used in finding out what the Lord wanted his people to do.
translation_verse: Na ɔdze akatabo no hyɛɛ no: na ɔdze Urim nye Tummim no hyɛɛ akatabo no mu.

english_verse: He placed the turban on Aaron's head, and on the front of the turban was the narrow strip of thin gold as a sign of his dedication to the Lord.
translation_verse: Na ɔdze asɔfokyɛw no hyɛɛ ne tsir: na ɔdze sika abɔtsir, abɔenyim krɔnkrɔn no, bɔɔ asɔfokyɛw no ho, wɔ n’enyim afamu; dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛɛ Moses no.

english_verse: Moses then dedicated the sacred tent and everything in it to the Lord by sprinkling them with some of the oil for ordination.
translation_verse: Na Moses faa ɔserango, na ɔdze seraa ntamadan no nye dza ɔwɔ mu nyinara, na ɔyɛɛ no krɔnkrɔn.

english_verse: He sprinkled the bronze altar seven times, and he sprinkled its equipment, as well as the large bronze bowl and its base.
translation_verse: Na ɔdze bi petsee afɔrmbukyia no do mpɛn esuon, na ɔseraa afɔrmbukyia no nye no ho ndzɛmba nyinara, nye eguarsɛn no nye ne ntaease no ngo dze yɛɛ no krɔnkrɔn.

english_verse: He also poured some of the oil on Aaron's head to dedicate him to the Lord.
translation_verse: Na ohuee ɔserango no bi guu Aaron n’etsifi, na ɔseraa no ngo dze yɛɛ no krɔnkrɔn.

english_verse: Next, Moses dressed Aaron's sons in their shirts, then tied sashes around them and put special caps on them, just as the Lord had commanded.
translation_verse: Na Moses dze Aaron mbabanyin no bae, na ɔdze sorntar hyehyɛɛ hɔn, na ɔdze abɔyamu bobɔɔ hɔn do, na ɔdze abɔtsir bobɔɔ hɔn; dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛɛ Moses no.

english_verse: Moses led out the bull that was to be sacrificed for sin, and Aaron and his sons laid their hands on its head.
translation_verse: Na ɔdze bɔn ho afɔr nantwisaa no bae: na Aaron nye ne mbabanyin no dze hɔn nsa guu bɔn ho afɔr nantwisaa no n’etsifi.

english_verse: After it was killed, Moses dipped a finger in the blood and smeared some of it on each of the four corners of the bronze altar, before pouring out the rest at the foot of the altar. This purified the altar and made it a fit place for offering the sacrifice for sin.
translation_verse: Na Moses kum no; na ɔdze ne nsatseaba faa mbɔgya no bi, na ɔdze kekaa afɔrmbukyia no ne mmbɛn ho hyiae, na ɔtseew afɔrmbukyia no ho, na ohuee bɔgya no guu afɔrmbukyia no ase, na ɔyɛɛ no krɔnkrɔn, dze yɛɛ mpatadze.

english_verse: Moses then took the fat on the bull's insides, as well as the lower part of the liver and the two kidneys with their fat, and sent them up in smoke on the altar fire.
translation_verse: Na ɔfaa seradze a ɔwɔ ayamudze no ho no nyinara, nye berɛbo do ntentan no, nye nsɛbɛ ebien no, nye no ho seradze no, na Moses hyeew no wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do.

english_verse: Finally, he took the skin and the flesh of the bull, together with the food still in its stomach, and burned them outside the camp, just as the Lord had commanded.
translation_verse: Na ɔnantwisaa no, nye no nwoma, nye ne nam, nye n’egyanan, ɔdze ogya hyeew no wɔ nseraban no ekyir; dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛɛ Moses no.

english_verse: Moses led out the ram for the sacrifice to please the Lord. After Aaron and his sons had laid their hands on its head,
translation_verse: Na ɔdze ɔhyew afɔr oguannyin no bae: na Aaron nye ne mbabanyin dze hɔn nsa guu oguannyin no etsifi.

english_verse: Moses killed the ram and splattered its blood against the four sides of the altar.
translation_verse: Na Moses kum no: na ɔdze bɔgya no petsee afɔrmbukyia no do hyiae.

english_verse: Moses had the animal cut up, and he washed its insides and hind legs. Then he laid the head, the fat, and the rest of the ram on the altar and sent them up in smoke with a smell that pleased the Lord. All this was done just as the Lord had commanded.
translation_verse: Na ɔdze nsu hohoor ayamudze nye ntwɛr no ho; na afei ɔhyeew oguannyin mu no nyinara wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do: ɔyɛ ɔhyew afɔr a oyi nka huanhuam: ɔyɛ EWURADZE ogya afɔr; dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛɛ Moses no.

english_verse: Moses led out the ram for the ceremony of ordination. Aaron and his sons laid their hands on its head,
translation_verse: Na ɔdze oguannyin kor no so, etuhoakyɛ afɔr oguannyin no, bae: na Aaron nye ne mbabanyin no dze hɔn nsa guu oguannyin no etsifi.

english_verse: and it was killed. Moses smeared some of its blood on Aaron's right earlobe, some on his right thumb, and some on the big toe of his right foot.
translation_verse: Na Moses kum no; na ɔfaa no bɔgyaa no bi, na ɔdze kaa Aaron n’aso nyimfa ano, na ne nsa nyimfa kokrobetsir, nye ne nan nyimfa kokonyin no ano.

english_verse: Moses did the same thing for Aaron's sons, before splattering the rest of the blood against the four sides of the altar.
translation_verse: Na ɔdze Aaron mbabanyin no bae, na Moses dze bɔgya no bi kekaa hɔn aso nyimfa ano, nye hɔn nsa nyimfa kokrobetsir, nye hɔn anan nyimfa kokonyin no ano: na Moses dze bɔgya no petsee afɔrmbukyia no do hyiae.

english_verse: He took the animal's fat tail, the fat on its insides, and the lower part of the liver and the two kidneys with their fat, and the right hind leg.
translation_verse: Na ɔfaa seradze no, nye seradze dua no, nye nsɛbɛ ebien no, nye no ho seradze no, nye aserɛ nyimfa no:

english_verse: Then he took from a basket some of each of the three kinds of bread that had been made without yeast and had been dedicated to the Lord.
translation_verse: na ɔfaa epitsi boodoo kɛntsɛn a osi EWURADZE enyim no mu epitsi tatar kor, nye ngo boodoo tatar kor, nye tatar trɛtraba kor, na ɔdze guu seradze no nye aserɛ nyimfa no do:

english_verse: Moses placed the bread on top of the meat and gave it all to Aaron and his sons, who lifted it up to show that it was dedicated to the Lord.
translation_verse: na ɔdze ne nyinara hyɛɛ Aaron na ne mbabanyin no nsa, na ɔtowee yɛɛ ɔtow afɔr wɔ EWURADZE enyim.

english_verse: After this, Moses placed it on the fires of the altar and sent it up in smoke with a smell that pleased the Lord. This was part of the ordination ceremony.
translation_verse: Na Moses gyee no wɔ hɔn nsamu, na ɔhyeew no wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do wɔ ɔhyew afɔr no do: dze yɛɛ etuhoakyɛ afɔr a oyi nka huanhuam: ɔyɛ ogya afɔr ma EWURADZE.

english_verse: Finally, Moses sprinkled the priestly clothes of Aaron and his sons with some of the oil for ordination and with some of the blood from the altar. So Aaron and his sons, together with their priestly clothes, were dedicated to the Lord.
translation_verse: Na Moses faa ɔserango no bi, nye afɔrmbukyia no do bɔgya no bi, na ɔdze petsee Aaron, nye ne ntar, nye ne mbabanyin, nye ne mbabanyin ntar do; dze yɛɛ Aaron nye ne ntar, nye ne mbabanyin, nye ne mbabanyin ntar no, krɔnkrɔn.

english_verse: Moses said to Aaron and his sons: The Lord told me that you must boil this meat at the entrance to the sacred tent and eat it there with the bread.
translation_verse: Na Moses see Aaron, nye ne mbabanyin no dɛ, Hom nnoa nam no wɔ ehyiadzibuw no n’abowano: na hom ndzi no wɔ hɔ ara ɔnye etuhoakyɛ afɔr kɛntsɛn no mu boodoo no.

english_verse: and stay near the entrance to the sacred tent until the ordination ceremony ends seven days from now.
translation_verse: Na mma hom mmfi ehyiadzibuw no n’abowano nda esuon, kyerɛ hom etuhoakyɛ afɔr nda no aso ana: na nda esuon na ɔdze rutu hom akyɛ.

english_verse: We have obeyed the Lord in everything that has been done today, so that your sins may be forgiven.
translation_verse: Dɛ mbrɛ wɔayɛ no ndɛ da yi no, dɛmara na EWURADZE ahyɛ dɛ wɔnyɛ mfa mpata mma hom.

english_verse: The Lord has told me that you must stay near the entrance to the tent for seven days and nights, or else you will die.
translation_verse: Na hom ntsena ehyiadzibuw no n’abowano adzekyee nye adzesaa nda esuon, na hom nhwɛ EWURADZE ahwɛdze no, amma hom eennwuwu: na dɛm na wɔdze ahyɛ me mbra.

english_verse: Aaron and his sons obeyed everything that the Lord had told Moses they must do.
translation_verse: Na Aaron nye ne mbabanyin no yɛɛ ndzɛmba a EWURADZE nam Moses nsa do hyɛe no nyinara.

english_verse: Eight days later Moses called together Aaron, his sons, and Israel's leaders.
translation_verse: Na ɔbaa dɛ da a otsia awɔtwe no, Moses frɛɛ Aaron nye ne mbabanyin no, na Israel mpanyimfo no;

english_verse: Then he said to Aaron: Find a young bull and a ram that have nothing wrong with them. Offer the bull to the Lord as a sacrifice for sin and the ram as a sacrifice to please him.
translation_verse: na ɔsee Aaron dɛ, Fa nantwiba onyin a wɔdze bɛyɛ bɔn ho afɔr, nye oguannyin a wɔdze bɛyɛ ɔhyew afɔr a wonndzii dzɛm, na fa bra EWURADZE enyim.

english_verse: Tell the people of Israel that they must offer sacrifices as well. They must offer a goat as a sacrifice for sin, and a bull and a ram as a sacrifice to please the Lord. The bull and the ram must be a year old and have nothing wrong with them.
translation_verse: Na kasa kyerɛ Israelmba no dɛ, Hom mfa apɔnkyenyin mbɛyɛ bɔn ho afɔr; na nantwiba nye eguambaa a hɔn beenu nyina woedzi afrenhyia afrenhyia a wonndzii dzɛm, mbɛyɛ ɔhyew afɔr;

english_verse: Then the people must offer a bull and a ram as a sacrifice to ask the Lord's blessing and also a grain sacrifice mixed with oil. Do this, because the Lord will appear to you today.
translation_verse: na ɔnantwinyin nye oguannyin mbɛyɛ asomdwee afɔr, mfa mbɔ afɔr wɔ EWURADZE enyim; na edziban afɔr a wɔdze ngo afrɔw: na ndɛ na EWURADZE yi noho kyerɛ hom.

english_verse: After the animals and the grain had been brought to the front of the sacred tent, and the people were standing there in the presence of the Lord,
translation_verse: Na wɔdze dza Moses hyɛe no baa ehyiadzibuw no enyim: na mboanofo no nyinara twee bɛnee begyinaa EWURADZE enyim.

english_verse: Moses said: The Lord has ordered you to do this, so that he may appear to you in all of his glory.
translation_verse: Na Moses see dɛ, Iyi nye adze a EWURADZE hyɛɛ dɛ hom nyɛ: na EWURADZE enyimnyam nyi noho nkyerɛ hom.

english_verse: Aaron, step up to the altar and offer the sacrifice to please the Lord, then offer the sacrifices for the forgiveness of your sins and for the sins of the people, just as the Lord has commanded.
translation_verse: Na Moses see Aaron dɛ, Twe bɛn afɔrmbukyia no ho, na bɔ wo bɔn ho afɔr, nye wo hyew afɔr, na pata ma woho, nye ɔman no: na fa ɔman no hɔn ayɛyɛdze bra, na pata ma hɔn; dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛe no.

english_verse: Aaron stepped up to the altar and killed the bull that was to be the sacrifice for his sins.
translation_verse: Nna Aaron twee bɛɛn afɔrmbukyia no ho, na okum bɔn ho afɔr nantwiba no a ɔyɛ nankasa nedze no.

english_verse: His sons brought him the blood. He dipped a finger in it, smeared some on the four corners of the bronze altar, and poured out the rest at its foot.
translation_verse: Na Aaron ne mbabanyin no dze bɔgya no kɛmaa no; na ɔdze ne nsatseaba bɔɔ bɔgya no mu, na ɔdze kekaa afɔrmbukyia no ne mmbɛn no, na ohuee bɔgya no guu afɔrmbukyia no ase:

english_verse: But he sent up in smoke the fat, the kidneys, and the lower part of the liver, just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
translation_verse: na bɔn ho afɔr no mu seradze no, nye nsɛbɛ no, nye berɛbo do ntentan no, ɔhyeew no wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do; dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE hyɛɛ Moses no.

english_verse: Then Aaron burned the skin and the flesh outside the camp.
translation_verse: Na nam no nye nwoma no dze, ɔdze gya hyeew no wɔ nseraban no ekyir.

english_verse: After Aaron had killed the ram that was sacrificed to please the Lord, Aaron's sons brought him the blood, and he splattered it against all four sides of the altar.
translation_verse: Na okum ɔhyew afɔr no; na Aaron ne mbabanyin no dze bɔgya no kɛmaa no, na ɔdze petsee afɔrmbukyia no do hyiae.

english_verse: They brought him each piece of the animal, including the head, and he burned them all on the altar.
translation_verse: Na wɔdze ɔhyew afɔr no esinesin, nye tsir no, kɛmaa no: na ɔhyeew no wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do.

english_verse: He washed the insides and the hind legs and also sent them up in smoke.
translation_verse: Na ɔhohoor ayamudze nye ntwɛr no ho, na ɔhyeew no wɔ ɔhyew afɔr no do wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do.

english_verse: Next, Aaron sacrificed the goat for the sins of the people, as he had done with the sacrifice for his own sins.
translation_verse: Na ɔdze ɔman no hɔn ayɛyɛdze kɛmae, na ɔfaa bɔn ho afɔr apɔnkye a ɔyɛ ɔman no dze no, na okum no, na ɔyɛɛ no bɔn ho afɔr, dɛ asɔpɔn dze no.

english_verse: And so, he burned this sacrifice on the altar in the proper way.
translation_verse: Na ɔdze edziban afɔr no kɛmae, na ɔyɛɛ no dɛ mbrɛ mbra no tse no ara.

english_verse: He also presented the grain sacrifice and burned a handful of the flour on the altar as part of the morning sacrifice.
translation_verse: Na ɔdze edziban afɔr no kɛmae, na ɔfaa mu ne nsapuw, na ɔhyeew no wɔ afɔrmbukyia no do, dze kaa anapa ɔhyew afɔr no ho.

english_verse: Finally, he killed the bull and the ram as a sacrifice to ask the Lord's blessing on the people. Aaron's sons brought him the blood, and he splattered it against the four sides of the altar.
translation_verse: Na eso okum nantwinyin nye oguannyin no asomdwee afɔr a ɔyɛ ɔman no dze no: na Aaron mbabanyin no dze bɔgya no kɛmaa no, na ɔdze petsee afɔrmbukyia no do hyiae,

english_verse: His sons placed all the fat, as well as the kidneys and the lower part of the liver
translation_verse: na ɔnantwinyin nye oguannyin no seradze no; seradze dua no, nye dza ɔkata ayamudze no ho, nye nsɛbɛ no, nye berɛbo do ntentan no:

english_verse: Then Aaron burned the fat on the altar and lifted up the ribs and the right hind leg to show that these were dedicated to the Lord. This was done just as the Lord had instructed Moses.
translation_verse: na nyan no nye aserɛ nyimfa no Aaron towee dze yɛɛ ɔtow afɔr wɔ EWURADZE enyim; dɛ mbrɛ Moses hyɛe no.

english_verse: Aaron held out his hand and gave the people his blessing, before coming down from the bronze altar where he had offered the sacrifices.
translation_verse: Na Aaron memaa ne nsa do wɔ ɔman no do, na ohyiraa hɔn; na ɔbɔɔ bɔn ho afɔr nye ɔhyew afɔr, nye asomdwee afɔr wiee no, osianee.

english_verse: He and Moses went into the sacred tent, and when they came out, they gave the people their blessing. Then the Lord appeared to the people in all of his glory.
translation_verse: Na Moses nye Aaron kɛhɛɛn ehyiadzibuw no mu, na wopuei, na wohyiraa ɔman no; na EWURADZE enyimnyam yii noho kyerɛɛ ɔman no nyinara.

english_verse: The Lord sent fiery flames that burned up everything on the altar, and when everyone saw this, they shouted and fell to their knees to worship the Lord.
translation_verse: Na ogya fi EWURADZE enyim bae, na ɔbɛhyeew afɔrmbukyia no do hyew afɔr nye seradze no: na ɔman no nyinara hunii no, wɔbɔɔ ose, na wɔdze hɔn enyim butubutuuw famu.

english_verse: Nadab and Abihu were two of Aaron's sons, but they disobeyed the Lord by burning incense to him on a fire pan, when they were not supposed to.
translation_verse: Na Aaron ne mbabanyin no, Nadab nye Abihu, fefaa hɔn nduhuamsɛn, na wɔdze ogya guu mu, na wɔdze eduhuam guguu do, na wɔdze ananagya a ɔnnhyɛɛ hɔn ho mbra, baa EWURADZE enyim.

english_verse: Suddenly the Lord sent fiery flames and burned them to death.
translation_verse: Na ogya fi EWURADZE enyim bae, na ɔbɛmeen hɔn, na wowuwui wɔ EWURADZE enyim.

english_verse: Then Moses told Aaron that this was exactly what the Lord had meant when he said: “I demand respect from my priests, and I will be praised by everyone!” Aaron was speechless.
translation_verse: Nna Moses see Aaron dɛ, Iyi nye dza EWURADZE kae no, dɛ hɔn a wɔtwe bɛn me no mu na mebɛyɛ moho krɔnkrɔn, na ɔman no nyinara enyim na mebɛhyɛ moho enyimnyam. Na Aaron muaa n’ano.

english_verse: Moses sent for Mishael and Elzaphan, the two sons of Aaron's uncle Uzziel. Then he told them, “Take these two dead relatives of yours outside the camp far from the entrance to the sacred tent.”
translation_verse: Na Moses frɛɛ Misael nye Elzafan, Aaron n’egya no nua banyin Uziel ne mbabanyin no, na ɔsee hɔn dɛ, Hom ntwe mbɛn, hom nsoasoa hom nuanom mfi krɔnkrɔnbea no enyim nkɔ nseraban no ekyir.

english_verse: So they dragged the dead men away by their clothes.
translation_verse: Nna wɔtwee bɛnee, na wɔsoasoaa hɔn wɔ hɔn sorntar mu kɔr nseraban no ekyir; dɛ mbrɛ Moses kae no.

english_verse: Then Moses told Aaron and his other two sons, Eleazar and Ithamar: Don't show your sorrow by messing up your hair and tearing your priestly clothes, or the Lord will get angry. He will kill the three of you and punish everyone else. It's all right for your relatives, the people of Israel, to mourn for those he destroyed by fire.
translation_verse: Na Moses kaa kyerɛɛ Aaron, nye ne mbabanyin Eleazar nye Itamar, dɛ, Mma hom mmbɔ hom tsinhwi mpɛsɛw, nkyii so mma hom nnsunsuan hom ntar mu; amma hom eennwuwu, amma EWURADZE n’ebufuw ammba mboanofo no nyinara do; mbom hom mma hom nuanom, Israel fi nyinara, ngyam ɔhyew a EWURADZE dze gya ahyew hɔn yi.

english_verse: But you are the Lord's chosen priests, and you must not leave the sacred tent, or you will die. Aaron and his two sons obeyed Moses.
translation_verse: Na mma hom mmfi ehyiadzibuw no n’abowano, amma hom eennwuwu: na EWURADZE ɔserango no wɔ hom do. Na wɔyɛɛ dɛ mbrɛ Moses kae no. Asɔfo No Hɔn Mbra

english_verse: The Lord said to Aaron:
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE kasaa kyerɛɛ Aaron dɛ,

english_verse: When you or your sons enter the sacred tent, you must never drink beer or wine. If you do, you will die right there! This law will never change.
translation_verse: Mma nnom wenyin anaa nsa dzendzen, ɔwo nye wo mbabanyin nyinara, aber a hom rokɔ ehyiadzibuw no mu no, amma hom eennwuwu: ɔnyɛ daa ahyɛdze wɔ hom ebusuasantsen nyinara mu:

english_verse: You must learn the difference between what is holy and what isn't holy and between the clean and the unclean.
translation_verse: na hom dze he aato adzekrɔnkrɔn nye adze a ɔnnyɛ krɔnkrɔn ntamu, nye adze a no ho nntsew nye dza no ho tsew ntamu;

english_verse: You must also teach the people of Israel everything that I commanded Moses to say to them.
translation_verse: na hom aakyerɛkyerɛ Israelmba ahyɛdze a EWURADZE nam Moses nsa do aka akyerɛ hɔn no nyinara.

english_verse: Moses told Aaron and his two sons, Eleazar and Ithamar: The grain sacrifice that was offered to give thanks to the Lord is very holy. So make bread without yeast from the part that wasn't sent up in smoke and eat it beside the altar.
translation_verse: Na Moses kasaa kyerɛɛ Aaron, na Eleazar nye Itamar, ne mbabanyin a wɔkae no dɛ, Hom mfa edziban afɔr a ɔka a ofi EWURADZE ogya afɔr do no, na hom nkwetsi mbɔrkaw ndzi no wɔ afɔrmbukyia ho: osiandɛ ɔyɛ krɔnkrɔndze mu adzekrɔnkrɔn:

english_verse: The Lord has said that this belongs to you and your sons, and that it must be eaten in a holy place.
translation_verse: na hom ndzi no wɔ bea krɔnkrɔn do, osiandɛ ɔyɛ w’ahyɛdze, nye wo mbabanyin ahyɛdze, wɔ EWURADZE ogya afɔr do: na dɛm na wɔahyɛ.

english_verse: But the choice ribs and the hind leg that were lifted up may be eaten by your entire family, as long as you do so in an acceptable place. These parts are yours from the sacrifices that the people offer to ask the Lord's blessing. This is what the Lord has commanded, and it will never change.
translation_verse: Ɔmado aserɛ no nye ɔtow nyan no wɔmfa nka ogya afɔr seradze no ho mbra, mbɔtow no ɔtow afɔr wɔ EWURADZE enyim; na ɔnyɛ wodze, nye wo mbabanyin a wɔka wo ho no dze, dɛ daa ahyɛdze; dɛ mbrɛ EWURADZE ahyɛ no.

english_verse: When Moses asked around and learned that the ram for the sin sacrifice had already been burned on the altar, he became angry with Eleazar and Ithamar and said,
translation_verse: Na Moses hwehwɛɛ bɔn ho afɔr apɔnkye no enyiberdo, na hwɛ, wɔahyew no: na no bo fuuw Eleazar na ɔnye Itamar, Aaron ne mbabanyin a wɔkae no, dɛ,

english_verse: “Why didn't you eat the meat from this sacrifice in an acceptable place? It is very holy, and the Lord has given you this sacrifice to remove Israel's sin and guilt.
translation_verse: Ebɛn ntsi na hom enndzi bɔn ho afɔr no wɔ beebi mbrɛ ɔyɛ bea krɔnkrɔn no, osiandɛ ɔyɛ krɔnkrɔndze mu adzekrɔnkrɔn, na ɔdze ama hom dɛ, hom nsoa mboanofo no hɔn mbusu, nyɛ mpata mma hɔn wɔ EWURADZE enyim?

english_verse: Whenever an animal's blood isn't brought into the sacred tent, I commanded you to eat its meat in an acceptable place, but you burned it instead.”
translation_verse: Hwɛ, wɔammfa no bɔgyaa no ammba krɔnkrɔnbea no no mu: ampaara ɔsee dɛ nkyɛ hom ndzi no wɔ krɔnkrɔn bea no do hɔ, dɛ mbrɛ mehyɛe no.

english_verse: Their father Aaron replied, “Today two of my sons offered the sacrifice for sin and the sacrifice to please the Lord, and look what has happened to me! Would the Lord have approved if I had eaten the sacrifice for sin?”
translation_verse: Na Aaron kasaa kyerɛɛ Moses dɛ, Hwɛ, ndɛ da yi na wɔdze hɔn bɔn ho afɔr nye hɔn ɔhyew afɔr aba EWURADZE enyim; na ndzɛmba a ɔtsetse dɛm yi aba mo do yi: na sɛ midzi bɔn ho afɔr ndɛ a, ana nkyɛ ɔbɛyɛ enyisɔ wɔ EWURADZE enyim?

english_verse: Moses was satisfied with Aaron's reply.
translation_verse: Na Moses tsee no, ɔyɛɛ no abodwe.

english_verse: any animal that has divided hoofs and chews the cud.
translation_verse: Anansambowa mu biara a wɔapaa n’anansa mu, na ɔyɛ anansapaae koraa, na ɔkosa adzepuwii no, ɔno na hom ndzi.

english_verse: The Lord told Moses and Aaron
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE kasaa kyerɛɛ Moses nye Aaron, see hɔn dɛ,

english_verse: to say to the community of Israel: You may eat
translation_verse: hom nkasa nkyerɛ Israelmba dɛ, Iyinom nye mbowa a hom ndzi wɔ asaase do mbowa nyinara mu.

english_verse: The only winged insects you may eat are locusts, grasshoppers, and crickets. All other winged insects that crawl are too disgusting for you to eat.
translation_verse: Na ntumbowa menyamenya nyinara a wɔwɔ anan anan anan no, yɛ ekyiwadze ma hom.

english_verse: Moles, rats, mice, and all kinds of lizards are unclean.
translation_verse: nye ban yer abamba, nye ɔmampam, nye ɔsamanapa, nye anhweaketsew, nye abosomanketsew.

english_verse: Anyone who touches their dead bodies or anything touched by their dead bodies becomes unclean until evening.
translation_verse: Iyinom nye hɔn a hɔn ho nntsew mma hom wɔ dza wɔwea nyinara mu: sɛ wowu a, obiara a ɔka hɔn no, no ho nntsew araa kodu ewimbir.

english_verse: If something made of wood, cloth, or leather touches one of their dead bodies, it must be washed, but it is still unclean until evening.
translation_verse: Na adze biara a hɔn mu dza oewu atɔ do no, no ho nntsew; dua adze o, atar o, nwoma o, bɔtɔ o, adze biara a wɔdze yɛ edwuma biara, wɔmfa nhyɛ nsu mu, na no ho nntsew araa kodu ewimbir; ansaana no ho atsew.

english_verse: If any of these animals is found dead in a clay pot, the pot must be broken to pieces, and everything in it becomes unclean.
translation_verse: Na dɛtsesɛn biara a hɔn mu bi bɔtɔ mu no, adze biara a ɔwɔ mu no no ho nntsew, na sɛn no hom mbɔ no.

english_verse: If you pour water from this pot on any food, that food becomes unclean, and anything drinkable in the pot becomes unclean.
translation_verse: No mu edziban a wodzi biara a nsu bogu mu no, no ho nntsew: na anondze nyinara a wɔnom wɔ dɛm adze biara mu no, no ho nntsew:

english_verse: If the dead body of one of these animals touches anything else, including ovens and stoves, that thing becomes unclean and must be destroyed.
translation_verse: Na adze biara a hɔn fun no bi bɔtɔ do no no ho nntsew; fononoo o, mbukyia o, wombubu no esinesin: hɔn ho nntsew, na hɔn ho nntsew mma hom.

english_verse: A spring or a cistern where one of these dead animals is found is still clean, but anyone who touches the animal becomes unclean.
translation_verse: Naaso nsubura anaa amona a nsu egyina mu no dze, no ho tsew: mbom dza ɔka hɔn fun no, no ho nntsew.

english_verse: If the dead body of one of these animals is found lying on seeds that have been set aside for planting, the seeds remain clean.
translation_verse: Na sɛ hɔn fun bi tɔ ekuadze a woedua biara do a, no ho tsew.

english_verse: But seeds that are soaking in water become unclean, if the dead animal is found in the water.
translation_verse: Mbom sɛ wɔdze nsu gu ekuadze no do a, na hɔn fun bi tɔ do a, ekuadze no ho nntsew mma hom.

english_verse: If an animal that may be eaten happens to die, and you touch it, you become unclean until evening.
translation_verse: Na sɛ abowa biara a hom dzi wu a, nyia ɔka no fun no, no ho nntsew araa kodu ewimbir.

english_verse: If you eat any of its meat or carry its body away, you must wash your clothes, but you are still unclean until evening.
translation_verse: Na nyia odzi no fun bi no, ɔnhor ne ntar, na no ho nntsew araa kodu ewimbir: onyia so a ɔsoa no fun no, nhor ne ntar, na no ho nntsew araa kodu ewimbir.

english_verse: If you eat any of them, you will become just as disgusting and unclean as they are.
translation_verse: Mma hom nnyɛ homho ekyiwadze wɔ aweaweabowa biara a ɔwea ho, nkyii so mma hom nnyɛ homho fi a ɔmma hom ho nntsew wɔ hɔn ho.

english_verse: I am the Lord your God, and you must dedicate yourselves to me and be holy, just as I am holy. Don't become disgusting by eating any of these unclean creatures.
translation_verse: Na emi nye EWURADZE hom Nyankopɔn: dɛm ntsi hom ntsew homho, na hom nyɛ krɔnkrɔn; osiandɛ meyɛ krɔnkrɔn. Nkyii so mma hom nngu homho fi wɔ aweaweabowa biara a otwuw noho adze wɔ asaase do no ho.

english_verse: I brought you out of Egypt so that I could be your God. Now you must become holy, because I am holy!
translation_verse: Na emi nye EWURADZE a miyii hom fii Egypt asaase do bae, dɛ meyɛ hom Nyankopɔn no; dɛm ntsi hom nyɛ krɔnkrɔn, na meyɛ krɔnkrɔn.

english_verse: I have given these laws so that you will know what animals, birds, and fish are clean and may be eaten, and which ones are unclean and may not be eaten.
translation_verse: ma wɔdze apa adze a no ho nntsew nye dza no ho tsew mu, na adze a nkwa wɔ mu a wodzi nye dza nkwa wɔ mu a wonndzi mu.

english_verse: The Lord told Moses
translation_verse: Na EWURADZE kasaa kyerɛɛ Moses dɛ,

english_verse: to say to the community of Israel: If a woman gives birth to a son, she is unclean for seven days, just as she is during her monthly period.
translation_verse: Kasa kyerɛ Israelmba dɛ, Sɛ ɔbaa bi nyinsen na ɔwo banyimba a, ɔno no ho nntsew araa nda esuon; dɛ mbrɛ no ho nntsew wɔ n’asabu nda no mu no.

english_verse: Her son must be circumcised on the eighth day,
translation_verse: Na da a otsia awɔtwe no wontwa ne banyinwer no twatsia.

english_verse: but her loss of blood keeps her from being completely clean for another
translation_verse: Na ɔnko ntandan mu adaduasa nda ebiasa wɔ n’ahoguar bɔgya mu; mma ɔnnka adzekrɔnkrɔn biara, nkyii so ɔmmba krɔnkrɔn bea, kyerɛ n’ahoguar nda ewie du ana.

english_verse: Any woman who gives birth to a daughter is unclean for two weeks, just as she is during her period. And she won't be completely clean for another
translation_verse: Mbom sɛ ɔwo basiaba a, ɔno no ho nntsew araa adapɛn ebien, dɛ n’asabu mu: na ɔnko ntandan mu adaduosia nda esia wɔ n’ahoguar bɔgya mu.

english_verse: When the mother has completed her time of cleansing, she must come to the front of the sacred tent and bring to the priest a year-old lamb as a sacrifice to please me and a dove or a pigeon as a sacrifice for sin.
translation_verse: Na sɛ n’ahoguar nda no wie du, ma ɔbanyin, anaa ɔbabaa a, ɔmfa eguambaa a oedzi afrenhyia a wɔdze bɔbɔ ɔhyew afɔr, nye aborɔnoma ba, anaa atamram a wɔdze bɔbɔ bɔn ho afɔr, mbra ehyiadzibuw no n’abowano mbɛma ɔsɔfo no:

english_verse: After the priest offers the sacrifices to me, the mother will become completely clean from her loss of blood, whether her child is a boy or a girl.
translation_verse: na ɔmfa mbɔ afɔr wɔ EWURADZE enyim, na ɔmpata mma no; na no ho ntsew wɔ no bɔgyaagu no ho. Iyi nye mbra a ɔwɔ hɔ ma nyia ɔwo ɔbanyin anaa ɔbaa.

english_verse: If she cannot afford a lamb, she can offer two doves or two pigeons, one as a sacrifice to please me and the other as a sacrifice for sin.
translation_verse: Na sɛ ne nsa annso eguambaa do a, ɔno ɔmfa ntamram ebien; kor nyɛ ɔhyew afɔr, na kor so nyɛ bɔn ho afɔr: na ɔsɔfo no mpata mma no, na no ho ntsew.
